  - Dense Field        | Buff/Risk  | While Crushing Aura is active, your own movement speed is reduced 10% but your melee damage is increased by 20% — you embrace the weight

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Tectonic Blow      | Attack   | Slam both fists down; creates a shockwave in a 10m line dealing 180% damage and leaving a Gravity Scar (terrain that slows movement 30% for 8s)
  - Gravitational Spike| CC       | Launch a focused spike of compressed gravity at a target; deals 140% damage and pins them to the ground (cannot stand for 3s)
  - Weight of Worlds   | Passive  | Each stack of Weighty on a target also reduces their damage output by 5%; at 5 stacks, the target is considered Pinned and takes 25% more damage from all sources

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Event Horizon Fist | Attack   | Charge for 2s, then deliver a punch that creates a localized gravity implosion at the point of impact; pulls all enemies within 8m toward that point, dealing 300% damage to the primary and 150% to pulled enemies
  - Absolute Mass      | Buff     | For 10s, your attacks cannot be blocked, parried, or dodged — gravity does not care about evasion; cooldown 60s

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Planet Breaker     | Channel  | Channel for 3s, condensing gravity into your fists; release to deliver a strike that deals 600% damage to the target and radiates crushing force in a 15m radius — enemies in the radius are pinned for 5s and take 200% damage; the impact leaves a Gravity Scar zone for 20s

>> Your notes on CRUSH:
>> The fantasy here is "inevitability" — the Crush archetype should feel like a slow avalanche. Reward patient players who stack debuffs methodically.
>> The Gravity Scar terrain feature creates interesting map control even in a melee-focused tree.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Orbit (Pull/Push, Positioning, Orbital Mechanics)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A dynamic battlefield manipulator who treats enemies as satellites — pulling them into orbit, slingshotting them into each other, and controlling spacing with gravitational finesse.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Gravitational Pull | Active   | Yank a single target up to 12m toward you; deals minor impact damage (60% weapon) if they collide with terrain
  - Repulse Burst      | Active   | Emit a directional gravity pulse; pushes all enemies in a 6m cone away from you with enough force to interrupt casts
  - Orbital Awareness  | Passive  | Gain a passive radar showing gravitational mass of nearby entities; you always know the location of enemies within 20m (through walls)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Slingshot          | Active   | Grab a target, spin them in a 3m orbit for 1.5s, then release — they fly in the chosen direction, dealing damage to the first enemy they collide with equal to 200% of their max HP percentage
  - Lagrange Field     | Utility  | Create a gravitational stable point; allies standing within 4m of the point cannot be pulled, pushed, or knocked back for 8s
  - Counterweight      | Reactive | When you are pulled or pushed by any effect, automatically redirect that force to a nearby enemy within 6m instead

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Binary Pull        | Active   | Select two targets; pull them toward each other at high speed — they collide at the midpoint, each taking 220% weapon damage and being stunned for 2s
  - Orbital Strike     | Attack   | Launch an enemy into a low arc and crash them back into the ground at a target location up to 15m away; deals 180% on launch, 240% on impact
  - Tidal Locking      | Debuff   | Lock a target's facing direction relative to you — they are always forced to face you for 6s, making them unable to flee or attack allies behind them

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Gravity Lattice    | Active   | Place up to 4 gravitational nodes in the environment; enemies passing between two nodes are automatically pulled to the midpoint and suspended for 2s; lasts 15s
  - Orrery             | Buff     | For 12s, you gain the ability to have up to 3 targets in "Orbit" simultaneously — each orbiting target takes 15% increased damage from all sources and can be released as projectiles at will

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Solar Dominion     | Transform| For 20s, become the gravitational center of the battlefield — all enemies within 25m are constantly pulled toward you (resistible with movement), and any enemy you designate with an attack is launched into orbit around you, helpless, for up to 6s before you can choose to slam them into the ground or into another enemy

>> Your notes on ORBIT:
>> This archetype rewards spatial intelligence and creativity. Binary Pull and Slingshot are the "wow" moments — design encounters with enough enemies to make them shine.
>> Solar Dominion is intentionally chaotic; the GM/system should anticipate players using it in unexpected ways.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Singularity (AoE Implosion, Black Hole, Zone Control)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A slow-building devastator who seeds gravitational anomalies across the battlefield, culminating in catastrophic implosions that devour everything in a zone.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Micro Collapse     | Active   | Detonate a small singularity at target location (18m range); pulls enemies within 4m toward the center and deals 80% weapon damage
  - Vacuum Seal        | Debuff   | Create a localized low-gravity void around a target; they float slightly and suffer -20% accuracy and cannot use ground-based movement abilities for 4s
  - Residual Gravity   | Passive  | After any of your singularity abilities detonate, a Gravity Remnant lingers for 3s — enemies who walk through it are Slowed 20%

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Dark Matter Mine   | Utility  | Place an invisible gravitational mine; when an enemy steps within 2m, it detonates pulling everything within 6m to the point and dealing 120% AoE damage
  - Collapse Field     | Active   | Designate a 6m radius zone; for 5s the zone has increased gravity — enemies inside move 30% slower and projectiles curve toward the center
  - Hawking Radiation  | Passive  | Your singularity effects deal 3% of a target's max HP as bonus damage per second they remain within the collapse radius

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Twin Collapse      | Active   | Spawn two micro singularities 10m apart; they slowly drift toward each other over 4s — when they merge, they explode for 350% weapon damage in a 10m radius
  - Gravitational Lens | Utility  | Warp space in a 12m corridor; all projectiles (ally and enemy) that pass through are bent toward the center, redirecting their trajectory — useful for making "impossible" shots
  - Tidal Disruption   | CC       | Create a gravitational shear around a target; they are stretched and compressed rapidly, dealing 160% damage and interrupting all channeled abilities (cannot channel for 5s)

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Proto Black Hole   | Active   | Summon a stable micro black hole at target location for 8s; it continuously pulls enemies within 14m toward it at increasing speed, dealing 60% weapon damage per second to those within 4m; cooldown 45s
  - Relativistic Crush | Active   | Slow time locally around a target for 3s — they act at 25% speed but take full damage; when the effect ends, all the "stored" time collapses and they take an additional 400% damage burst

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Omega Singularity  | Channel  | Channel for 4s to birth a true singularity at target location (20m range); it persists for 12s, growing in radius every 3s (4m -> 8m -> 12m -> 16m); enemies inside take 100% weapon damage per second and are pulled inexorably inward; at the end of the duration, the singularity collapses in a final explosion dealing 800% damage to everything within 20m

>> Your notes on SINGULARITY:
>> This is the "big brain" slow-burn archetype. Combo with Twin Collapse into a Proto Black Hole for satisfying cascades.
>> Gravitational Lens is the sleeper utility pick — in the right encounter, it completely changes projectile dynamics.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON GRAVITON]
> The Graviton class works best when the three archetypes are mixed — a Crush/Orbit hybrid plays like a grappler, while a Orbit/Singularity hybrid is a chaos conductor.
> Consider giving the class a "Gravitational Mass" resource bar that charges through hits and is spent on heavy abilities.
> The sci-fi theme should feel grounded in physics metaphors even when the effects are fantastical — language like "mass," "orbit," "collapse," and "radiation" keeps the flavor consistent.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 13: Nanomancer (Nanotech Mage)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A brilliant techno-mage who commands clouds of microscopic machines — consuming organic matter to fuel swarms, rebuilding allies at the cellular level, and assembling autonomous nanite constructs that evolve and replicate mid-combat.

ARCHETYPE A: Grey Goo (Swarm Damage, Consume, Dissolution)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: An aggressive attrition specialist who floods targets with consuming nanite swarms, dissolving armor, siphoning vitality, and growing stronger the more they devour.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Nanite Swarm       | Attack   | Release a cloud of consuming nanites at target; deals 90% weapon damage over 4s and reduces armor by 10% per second of contact
  - Dissolution        | Debuff   | Inject nanites that break down organic material; target takes 5% of their max HP as damage per second for 6s and cannot regenerate HP during this time
  - Feast Protocol     | Passive  | Each kill by your nanites recovers 5% of your max HP and temporarily increases your swarm damage by 8% for 8s (stacks up to 3 times)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Consumption Wave   | Active   | Unleash a 10m cone of nanites that latch onto all enemies; each enemy hit feeds your Nanite Pool, increasing damage of your next Grey Goo ability by 15% per enemy consumed
  - Adaptive Enzymes   | Passive  | Your nanites learn from defenses — after attacking the same enemy 3 times, they bypass 25% of that enemy's resistances permanently for the encounter
  - Recursive Hunger   | Toggle   | While toggled on, each tick of Dissolution also spreads a weaker copy (50% potency) to an adjacent enemy within 4m; costs 2% max HP per second to maintain

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Grey Tide          | Active   | Flood a 8m radius zone with nanites; all enemies inside take 70% weapon damage per second for 5s; enemies who die inside the zone become Nanite Fuel (boosts your next ability by 50% damage)
  - Molecular Shred    | Attack   | Focus nanites on a single target's structural weak points; deals 300% weapon damage and removes all damage-reducing buffs from the target
  - Contagion Protocol | Debuff   | Infect a target with self-replicating dissolution nanites; when the target dies, all enemies within 6m are immediately infected with a full stack of Dissolution

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Omega Swarm        | Summon   | Release a semi-autonomous mega-swarm that hunts the nearest enemy; deals 200% weapon damage per second for 8s, ignores armor, and heals you for 50% of damage dealt
  - Voracious Upgrade  | Passive  | Each time your nanites consume a living entity, your max nanite count increases by 5% for the remainder of the encounter — no cap; this stacks into a terrifying late-fight crescendo

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Grey Goo Event     | Channel  | Channel for 2s to release a catastrophic grey goo cascade in a 20m radius; all enemies take 150% weapon damage per second for 10s; enemies who die are consumed and their mass is added to the swarm, increasing radius by 2m and duration by 1s per death; the cascade cannot be stopped once initiated

>> Your notes on GREY GOO:
>> This archetype is designed to snowball hard — it should feel almost scary at full ramp. Balance through long setup times and high visibility (enemies see the swarm coming).
>> Contagion Protocol is the standout "oh no" skill for enemies; make sure it has counterplay (cleanse, fast kills, etc.).
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Repair Protocol (Nano Healing, Restoration, Maintenance)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A precision support engineer who deploys nanite healers to restore allies, purge toxins, rebuild damaged tissue over time, and maintain structural integrity under fire.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Nano Suture        | Heal     | Inject a target ally with repair nanites; restores 120% of your Tech Power as HP instantly and leaves a HoT (30% Tech Power per second for 4s)
  - Diagnostic Sweep   | Active   | Scan an ally; remove 1 debuff and gain insight into their status — your next heal on them is 30% more effective
  - Maintenance Field  | Aura     | Passively emit repair nanites in a 6m radius; allies in range regenerate 15% of their max HP per minute passively (does not stack with other regen auras)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Emergency Rebuild  | Heal     | Instantly restore an ally at critical HP (below 20%) to 40% max HP; cooldown 30s per target
  - Nanobot Lattice    | Buff/Defense     | Weave nanite scaffolding into an ally's body; they gain 20% damage reduction and are immune to bleeding/poison for 8s
  - Triage Protocol    | Heal   | Simultaneously send basic repair nanites to up to 3 allies; each receives a small heal (50% Tech Power) and has 1 debuff removed

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Regenerative Core  | Passive/Heal  | Allies you have healed in the last 10s gain a Nanite Core — if they are reduced to 0 HP while the Core is active, they are instead restored to 15% HP (once per encounter per ally)
  - Full Rebuild       | Channel  | Channel on an ally for 3s; at completion, restore them to full HP and remove all debuffs; they also gain a 20% HP shield for 6s
  - Adaptive Shielding | Reactive/Defense | When an ally within 10m takes a hit that would deal more than 30% of their max HP, automatically deploy a nanite barrier absorbing 40% of the overflow damage; cooldown 15s per ally

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Nanite Resurrection| Active   | Target a recently defeated ally (within 10s of death); revive them with 50% HP and 50% of their resource; cooldown 90s
  - Distributed Repair | Passive/Heal  | Your Maintenance Field now also clears 1 debuff from allies every 5s; additionally, allies in the field deal 10% increased healing to themselves

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Omega Repair Grid  | Channel  | Channel for 2s to deploy a full-battlefield nanite repair grid for 15s; all allies are continuously healed for 80% Tech Power per second, immune to all damage-over-time effects, and any ally who falls below 25% HP is automatically restored to 50% HP once during the duration

>> Your notes on REPAIR PROTOCOL:
>> The "Regenerative Core" passive is the emotional anchor of this tree — it creates a safety net that good players will rely on and bad players won't realize is protecting them.
>> Nanite Resurrection is intentionally limited to 90s cooldown; it should feel like a genuine second chance, not a routine button.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Replicator (Nano Constructs, Drones, Self-Replication)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A drone architect who assembles autonomous nanite constructs mid-battle, commanding a growing fleet of self-replicating machines that adapt their function to battlefield needs.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Assemble Drone     | Summon   | Construct a basic combat drone (15% of your max HP, 40% weapon damage per attack); up to 2 active simultaneously
  - Replication Seed   | Active   | Attach a replication module to an existing drone; after 6s it splits into 2 copies at 60% of the original's stats
  - Swarm Intelligence | Passive  | Each active drone increases your Tech Power by 4%; this bonus is lost when the drone is destroyed

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Forge Sentinel     | Summon   | Construct a heavier Sentinel drone (25% max HP, 60% weapon damage, taunts enemies within 4m); counts as 2 drone slots
  - Overclock          | Buff/Risk     | Choose a drone; it attacks 100% faster and deals 50% more damage for 8s, then self-destructs in a 5m explosion dealing 180% weapon damage
  - Nanite Recycler    | Passive  | When any of your drones is destroyed, you recover 8% of your max HP and gain 1 Nanite Charge (resource for Replicator abilities)

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Adaptive Platform  | Summon   | Deploy a configurable platform drone; choose its role at deployment — Shield (intercepts projectiles), Repair (heals nearby allies), or Assault (AoE damage); can be reconfigured once per 10s
  - Exponential Growth | Summon   | Trigger a replication cascade on all active drones simultaneously; each drone spawns one copy at 50% stats; copies cannot themselves replicate; lasts until destroyed
  - Drone Uplink       | Passive  | You can directly inhabit a drone's senses — see from its perspective and direct it with full manual control for up to 5s without breaking your own actions; cooldown 20s

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Constructor Array  | Active   | Deploy a stationary Constructor unit that assembles 1 basic combat drone every 8s for 30s (up to 3 drones built); Constructor itself has 50% of your max HP
  - Nanobot Gestalt    | Active   | Merge all active drones into a single giant Gestalt construct; Gestalt HP equals the sum of all merged drones plus 20%, damage equals combined damage plus 30%; lasts 15s before dispersing back into individual drones (at 50% HP each)

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Infinite Replication| Toggle  | While active, whenever a drone is destroyed, two new basic drones are automatically assembled at the destruction site after a 3s delay; toggle costs 5% max HP per second to maintain; the battlefield gradually floods with nanite constructs — the swarm never truly dies

>> Your notes on REPLICATOR:
>> Nanobot Gestalt is the "Voltron moment" of this archetype — it should feel satisfying to combine 5 drones into one massive construct.
>> Infinite Replication is deliberately overpowered against attrition fights and weak against burst/AoE; design encounters that test both.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON NANOMANCER]
> The three archetypes represent three philosophies: Grey Goo (consume the enemy), Repair Protocol (preserve allies), Replicator (build an army). A mixed build could create a self-sustaining nano-ecosystem.
> Consider a shared "Nanite Pool" resource that is generated by Grey Goo kills and spent by Replicator constructions — this creates a natural tension between the two offensive/creative trees.
> The class should feel like playing a mad scientist whose experiments get out of hand in the best possible way.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 14: Psi-Blade (Psychic Warrior)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A warrior who has forged their mind into a weapon — bending physical reality with telekinetic force, shattering enemy will with psychic assault, and erecting barriers of pure mental energy that make them a fortress on the battlefield.

ARCHETYPE A: Telekinetic (Force Push/Pull, Object Throw, Environmental)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A battlefield environmentalist who weaponizes the world itself — hurling debris, slamming enemies with invisible force, and reshaping combat terrain through raw telekinetic power.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Force Push         | Active   | Emit a focused telekinetic blast; pushes a single target up to 8m and deals 80% weapon damage; deals double damage if they impact terrain
  - Telekinetic Grip   | Active   | Seize a target within 15m and hold them suspended for 2s; they cannot act but you must concentrate (no other actions during grip)
  - Object Pull        | Utility  | Instantly retrieve any unattended object or weapon within 20m; if used on an enemy's held weapon, force a disarm check (contested roll)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Debris Storm       | Attack   | Rip loose material from the environment and hurl it at a target in a rapid flurry; deals 5 hits of 50% weapon damage each; more debris available in cluttered environments
  - Repulsor Wave      | Active   | Release an omnidirectional telekinetic shockwave; all enemies within 8m are pushed back and knocked prone; cooldown 12s
  - Psionic Leverage   | Passive  | When you throw an object or push an enemy into terrain, the collision damage is increased by 50%; you instinctively find the best angle

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Vehicle Throw      | Active   | Lift a large environmental object (boulder, car, pillar) and hurl it at a target location; deals 350% weapon damage in a 6m AoE and creates impassable terrain rubble; requires a large object nearby
  - Suspension Field   | Active   | Freeze all projectiles (friendly and enemy) within a 12m radius in midair for 4s; release them simultaneously in the direction of your choice
  - Kinetic Siphon     | Passive/Utility  | When an enemy is pushed or thrown by your abilities, you recover 8% of your max Psi Energy; heavy throw abilities restore 20%

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Crushing Vise      | Active   | Grip a target with overwhelming telekinetic force; they take 80% weapon damage per second for 5s and are completely immobilized; at the end of the duration, the vise snaps and the compressed force explodes for 250% damage
  - Psionic Hurricane  | Channel  | Channel for 3s; rip everything loose within 15m into a spiraling vortex that deals 120% weapon damage per second to all enemies caught inside; objects hurl outward at the end dealing 200% AoE damage

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Telekinetic Supremacy | Transform | For 20s, your telekinetic power reaches its absolute limit — you can simultaneously grip up to 5 targets, throw them at each other or the environment freely, and all Force abilities cost no Psi Energy; gravity and momentum become your personal tools; the environment within 30m becomes a weapon

>> Your notes on TELEKINETIC:
>> The "requires a large object nearby" caveat on Vehicle Throw is a feature, not a bug — design encounter spaces thoughtfully so this ability shines.
>> Suspension Field is one of the most creative tactical abilities in the class; it rewards forethought and can reverse ambushes dramatically.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Mindbreaker (Psychic Damage, Debuff, Will Destruction)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A psychological predator who invades enemy minds, shattering concentration, implanting crippling fears, and eroding mental fortitude until opponents crumble from the inside out.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Psi Lance          | Attack   | Fire a focused beam of psychic energy; deals 100% weapon damage as psychic (ignores physical armor) and interrupts the target's current action
  - Disorient          | Debuff   | Scramble a target's spatial sense; they suffer -25% accuracy and have a 20% chance to attack a random nearby target (including allies) for 5s
  - Mental Pressure    | Passive  | Enemies you have hit with psychic abilities have their Willpower reduced by 5% per hit (max -25%); lower Willpower increases effectiveness of all Mindbreaker debuffs

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Nightmare Insertion| Debuff   | Implant a waking nightmare; target is Feared for 3s (flees at max speed in a random direction); if they were already affected by Disorient, duration doubles
  - Mind Spike         | Attack   | Drive a psychic spike through the target's concentration; deals 150% psychic damage and removes their active buffs (up to 2)
  - Psychic Feedback   | Reactive | When an enemy uses a mental ability (magic, psi, or command) within 10m, reflect 60% of its effect back at them and deal 80% psychic damage

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Shatter Will       | Active   | Target an enemy with reduced Willpower (below 50%); their will breaks — they become Broken for 8s (cannot use abilities, reduced to basic attacks only, -30% damage)
  - Cascade Break      | Debuff   | Implant a psychic time bomb; after 5s the bomb detonates, dealing 200% psychic damage and spreading Disorient to all enemies within 6m of the target
  - Paranoia Field     | Aura     | Emanate psychic interference within 10m; enemies in range cannot benefit from ally buffs (cut off from coordination) and have their senses dulled (-15% to all perception checks)

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Mind Wipe          | Active   | Target an enemy; wipe their short-term combat memory — they forget all ability cooldowns are active but they are still on cooldown, they lose current targeting, and they lose track of ally positions for 6s; bosses are immune
  - Psychic Maelstrom  | Attack   | Unleash cascading psychic energy through a chained assault; bounces between up to 4 targets dealing 180% psychic damage each hit; each consecutive bounce deals 20% more damage

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Total Collapse     | Active   | Select a target; over 3s, systematically destroy their mental architecture — first Disorient (instant), then Fear (1s), then Broken (2s), then at 3s their mind shatters completely: they are Stunned for 5s, take 500% psychic damage, and are left with 1% Willpower for the rest of the encounter (making them trivially affected by all subsequent debuffs); cannot be used on the same target twice per encounter

>> Your notes on MINDBREAKER:
>> Willpower as a secondary defense stat (reduced by Mental Pressure) creates a satisfying depth loop — the longer you attack a target, the more helpless they become.
>> Total Collapse should feel like an execution, not just a damage ability. The staged breakdown animation should be unmistakable.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Thought Shield (Psychic Barriers, Tank, Mind Fortress)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A defensive titan who externalizes mental discipline as impenetrable psychic barriers — protecting allies, reflecting attacks, and becoming an immovable anchor of pure willpower.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Psi Ward           | Buff/Shield     | Erect a psychic barrier on yourself or an ally; absorbs damage equal to 150% of your Tech/Psi Power before breaking; lasts 8s or until depleted
  - Mental Fortress    | Passive/Immunity  | Your own Willpower is exceptionally high — you are immune to Fear, Disorient, and Mind Wipe effects; reduces duration of all CC by 30%
  - Barrier Pulse      | Active   | Expand your active Psi Ward as a shockwave; pushes all enemies within 5m back 4m and deals 60% weapon damage; consumes the ward on use

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Shared Bulwark     | Buff/Shield     | Project a psychic barrier simultaneously on yourself and up to 2 allies; each barrier absorbs 100% Psi Power in damage; barriers share remaining absorption pool
  - Reflect Aegis      | Reactive | When your Psi Ward absorbs a hit exceeding 20% of its capacity, reflect 40% of that hit's damage back at the attacker as psychic damage
  - Iron Mind          | Passive/Utility  | Each time your Psi Ward successfully absorbs damage, your Psi Power increases by 3% for 10s (stacks up to 5 times); strong defense empowers stronger wards

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Dome of Will       | Active   | Project a stationary 8m radius psychic dome at target location; the dome blocks all incoming projectiles and reduces melee damage from outside by 30% for 10s; allies can shoot out freely
  - Counter-Psyche     | Reactive | When an enemy targets you or an ally with a mental debuff, automatically apply Psi Ward to the target before the debuff lands (absorbs the damage component if any) and reflect the debuff back at the caster; cooldown 20s
  - Resonant Barrier   | Passive/Defense  | Your barriers now pulse with psychic energy — enemies who attack a barrier-protected target take 30% of the absorbed damage as psychic feedback automatically

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Inviolate Mind     | Buff/Immunity     | For 12s, become completely immune to all forms of damage — physical, elemental, and psychic; cannot be moved, knocked back, or CC'd; can still act freely; cooldown 90s
  - Fortress of One    | Stance   | Enter a defensive stance; you become a living barrier — take 50% reduced damage, immune to knock-back, and generate a persistent Psi Ward every 5s; movement speed reduced by 40% while active

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Mind Over Matter   | Channel  | Channel for 2s to extend your psychic dominion over a 20m radius for 15s; within the radius you decide what forces are allowed — you can negate all projectiles, all AoE damage, or all melee damage (choose one per second, cycling options); allies inside the radius also benefit from Resonant Barrier and Iron Mind passives at full potency

>> Your notes on THOUGHT SHIELD:
>> Inviolate Mind is the "oh shit" button — 90s cooldown means it's a real decision. Consider rewarding players who use it proactively rather than reactively.
>> Mind Over Matter at cap makes the Psi-Blade feel like a final-form villain. The choice of which damage type to negate each second is genuinely engaging moment-to-moment.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON PSI-BLADE]
> The three archetypes cover the three ways to "win" with a mind: force others around (Telekinetic), break others down (Mindbreaker), make yourself unbreakable (Thought Shield).
> A Telekinetic/Mindbreaker multispec plays like a sadistic puppeteer; a Mindbreaker/Thought Shield build is an unstoppable juggernaut who crushes wills and endures all reprisal.
> Consider a "Psi Energy" resource that is spent by all three trees — make players choose between offense, utility, and defense with each resource expenditure.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 15: Shadowweaver (Shadow/Darkness)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A master of living darkness who calls shadow entities from the void between worlds, slips through enemy lines like a ghost cast from pure night, and drowns the battlefield in lightless terror that blinds and disorients all who oppose them.

ARCHETYPE A: Shade Caller (Shadow Entity Summons, Void, Fear)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A sinister summoner who tears shadow entities from the dark between dimensions, commanding a menagerie of fear-inducing void creatures to overwhelm, terrify, and consume enemies.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Summon Shade       | Summon   | Tear a basic shadow entity from the void; it has 20% of your max HP, attacks for 50% weapon damage, and inflicts Minor Fear (5% reduced accuracy) on enemies it strikes
  - Shadow Bond        | Passive/Utility  | Your summoned Shades share a portion of your senses — they cannot be surprised, flank automatically, and deal 15% increased damage to enemies engaged with you
  - Void Whisper       | Debuff   | Channel dark voices into a target's mind; they suffer Unnerved (-20% damage) for 6s and have a 30% chance to hesitate on their next ability use

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Shade Pack         | Summon   | Summon 3 lesser shadow wisps simultaneously; individually weak (10% HP, 25% damage) but collectively devastating — increase max simultaneous Shades by 3 for 15s
  - Consuming Shadow   | Active   | Command a Shade to sacrifice itself, exploding in a burst of darkness that deals 150% weapon damage in a 5m radius and inflicts Blind (30% miss chance) for 3s
  - Void Tether        | Passive  | Shades you summon are tethered to a target at summon-time; tethered Shades deal 25% increased damage to their tethered target and follow them relentlessly through any teleport or movement ability

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Greater Shade      | Summon   | Call forth a powerful Greater Shade (50% max HP, 100% weapon damage, can CC enemies with a grasping attack); counts as 2 Shade slots
  - Eclipse Aura       | Aura     | Your Shades radiate a field of terror; enemies within 4m of any Shade suffer -15% to all stats and have a 10% chance per second to gain the Fleeing status for 2s
  - Rift of Shades     | Active   | Tear open a void rift at target location (15m range); 4 Shades pour through over 4s; rift remains open for 8s and can be used as a shadow-step teleport destination by you

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Void Matriarch     | Summon   | Summon the Void Matriarch, a massive shadow entity (75% max HP, 150% weapon damage, AoE slashing attacks, radiates Major Fear in 8m); can only have 1 Matriarch active; cooldown 60s
  - Shadow Army        | Active   | For 12s, every enemy that dies within 20m of you rises as a temporary Shade under your control (20% original HP, 40% original damage); the battlefield of the dead serves you

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Hunger         | Channel  | Channel for 3s to open a permanent void rift for 20s; the rift continuously spills entities that overwhelm your control — every 2s, 2 random shadow entities emerge (basic Shades, Greater Shades, or unique void horrors) and attack all non-summoner targets; the void hungers and you have simply given it a door

>> Your notes on SHADE CALLER:
>> The "Rift of Shades as teleport destination" detail is important — it makes Shade Caller feel connected to Umbral Step's theme without duplicating it.
>> Shadow Army is the dark fantasy power fantasy moment: let the player feel like a necromancer of darkness as the fallen become their soldiers.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Umbral Step (Shadow Teleport, Assassin, Flank)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A ghost-step assassin who uses darkness as a highway — vanishing from sight, reappearing behind prey, and delivering strikes of lethal precision before dissolving back into shadow.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Shadow Step        | Movement | Instantly teleport up to 12m in any direction; you briefly become incorporeal during transit (immune to damage for 0.3s); must arrive in shadow or dim light
  - Umbral Strike      | Attack   | Strike from the shadow; if you attacked from Stealth or within 1s of a Shadow Step, deal 200% weapon damage and apply a stacking Shadow Mark (+10% damage taken per stack, max 5)
  - Dark Adaptation    | Passive/Evasion  | You see perfectly in all darkness conditions and gain +15% movement speed in dim or dark environments; enemies in darkness have a 20% miss chance against you

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Backstab Protocol  | Passive  | Attacks made while flanking or from behind deal an additional 50% weapon damage and have a 15% chance to apply Stunned (1.5s); your strikes find the gap between armor plates instinctively
  - Shadow Anchor      | Active   | Mark a location; at any point within 12s, activate again to teleport back to that exact position instantly; useful for escape or tactical repositioning
  - Cloak of Night     | Stealth  | Enter deep stealth for up to 8s; while Cloaked, your movement speed is not reduced and you can still Shadow Step; Cloak breaks on attack or taking damage

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Chain Step         | Movement | Shadow Step gains 2 charges, each usable within 2s of the last; each consecutive step deals 20% bonus damage to the next attack (stacks); the shadow trail you leave deals minor damage to enemies passing through it
  - Void Slip          | Movement | Become incorporeal for 3s — pass through walls, enemies, and hazards freely; cannot attack while incorporeal but can reposition for a devastating emergence; cooldown 25s
  - Executioner's Mark | Debuff   | Apply a mark to a target that persists for 15s; while marked, all your attacks against them gain 30% critical chance and your Shadow Steps are not limited by light level when targeting them

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Shadow Clone       | Active   | Create a shadow duplicate of yourself at your current position; the clone mimics your last 3 attacks for 6s before dissolving; enemies cannot distinguish clone from original until it takes damage
  - Thousand Cuts      | Attack   | Blink through a target 5 times in 2s, dealing 80% weapon damage each pass; each pass applies one stack of Shadow Mark; final pass deals 200% weapon damage

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Wraith Form        | Transform| For 15s, fully merge with the shadow plane — you become a living shadow; ignore all terrain, pass through enemies dealing 60% weapon damage per pass, are immune to all non-light-based damage, and your Shadow Step has unlimited range (any shadow within line of sight); at the end of duration, emerge in a burst of shadow energy dealing 400% weapon damage to all nearby enemies

>> Your notes on UMBRAL STEP:
>> Chain Step's "shadow trail" is a wonderful secondary pressure element — it punishes enemies who try to follow your teleport path.
>> Wraith Form is the ultimate assassin fantasy. The "any shadow within line of sight" range makes indoor environments and nighttime maps dramatically more dangerous with this build.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Nightfall (Darkness Zone Control, Blind, Area Denial)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A zone controller who extinguishes light itself — blanketing areas in supernatural darkness that blinds enemies, empowers allies, and transforms the battlefield into a nightmare landscape where only the Shadowweaver truly sees.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Snuff Light        | Active   | Extinguish all light sources within 10m for 8s; affected enemies with normal vision suffer -30% accuracy; you and allies with Dark Adaptation are unaffected
  - Shadow Veil        | Utility  | Cast a 6m radius zone of deep shadow at target location (20m range); the zone lasts 12s, provides soft cover to allies inside, and grants them the Shadowborn buff (+10% damage while inside)
  - Creeping Dark      | Passive/Utility  | The shadow zones you create slowly expand by 1m per 3s; enemies within your shadows regenerate HP 50% slower as the darkness saps vitality

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Midnight Cascade   | Active   | Rapidly expand an existing shadow zone to 3x its radius in 1s; the sudden darkness expansion causes all enemies caught in the expansion to be Blinded for 3s (cannot see; miss all attacks)
  - Shadow Maze        | Utility  | Create a network of shadow walls in a 10m area; enemies inside are disoriented (move 30% slower, cannot use dash abilities) and their minimap goes dark; lasts 10s
  - Dusk Resonance     | Passive  | Your shadow zones now deal 20% of your weapon damage per second to enemies inside; darkness itself becomes corrosive to those who should not stand within it

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Eternal Night      | Active   | Convert all light within a massive 20m radius to supernatural darkness for 15s; this is true magical darkness — even magical light sources are suppressed; only those with Shadowweaver's blessing can see clearly
  - Terror in the Dark | Debuff   | Target an enemy inside one of your shadow zones; the darkness shows them their deepest fear — they are Terrified (cannot approach enemies, must flee) for 5s and take 150% psychic damage
  - Living Darkness    | Active   | Animate a shadow zone as a semi-autonomous entity; it slowly pursues enemies (3m/s), dealing 100% weapon damage per second of contact; lasts 10s or until destroyed

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Void Vortex        | Active   | Create a massive darkness sink at target location; it pulls shadows from the entire map, creating a 15m radius zone of absolute darkness and drawing all enemies within 20m inward (as a pull effect) for 4s; enemies pulled in are Blinded and Slowed for 6s
  - Shade of the World | Aura     | Passively darken your surroundings — the ambient light level within 15m of you is always reduced by 2 categories (bright becomes dim, dim becomes dark, dark becomes absolute); this is always active and cannot be suppressed below Rare light-generation abilities

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Nightfall Protocol | Channel  | Channel for 3s to trigger Nightfall — supernatural darkness sweeps across the entire encounter area regardless of size for 25s; all enemies are Blinded and their movement speed is reduced 40%; every 5s, your darkness pulses and deals 200% weapon damage to all enemies; allies under your blessing are unaffected; the battlefield becomes your domain alone

>> Your notes on NIGHTFALL:
>> "Shade of the World" as a persistent passive is a design statement: this player changes the environment just by existing. Other players should feel the darkness creeping in.
>> Living Darkness is intentionally unsettling mechanically — a darkness that chases you is a horror movie staple translated into gameplay.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON SHADOWWEAVER]
> The three archetypes create three darkness fantasies: Shade Caller (I am served by darkness), Umbral Step (I move through darkness), Nightfall (I am the darkness).
> These trees synergize beautifully — Nightfall creates the perfect environment for Umbral Step's vision advantages, and Shade Callers thrive in zones their summoned Shades help darken.
> Mechanically, a "Light Level" system that the Shadowweaver manipulates would make this class feel genuinely impactful on the world state.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 16: Crystal Sage (Mineral/Gem Magic)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A lithomancer who has unlocked the inner light of the earth — splitting sunlight through living prisms to lance enemies with focused beams, forging armor of crystal resonance that shatters attackers, and attuning to different gemstones to unleash wildly different elemental manifestations.

ARCHETYPE A: Prism (Light Refraction, Beam Splitting, Photonic Attacks)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A precision ranged specialist who generates and splits beams of crystallized light, bouncing attacks off surfaces, piercing multiple targets, and dazzling enemies with blinding brilliance.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Prism Shot         | Attack   | Fire a focused light beam (20m range); deals 90% weapon damage; if it passes through a Crystal Shard in the environment, it splits into 3 beams at 60% damage each
  - Crystal Shard      | Utility  | Plant a floating Crystal Shard at target location (15m range); Shards persist for 20s and serve as beam splitters/reflectors for Prism abilities
  - Light Refraction   | Passive  | Your beam abilities automatically seek the most efficient path — they can bounce off Crystal Shards without aiming manually, finding targets around corners

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Prismatic Flare    | Attack   | Fire a wide-angle burst of scattered light; deals 70% weapon damage to all enemies in a 10m cone and applies Dazzled (-20% accuracy) for 4s to those hit
  - Mirror Array       | Utility  | Place 3 mirror Crystal Shards simultaneously in a triangle formation; beams bouncing through all 3 are amplified by 50% damage and gain piercing; lasts 15s
  - Focused Lens       | Passive  | When a beam hits the same target twice in a single ability use (from splits or bounces), each subsequent hit on that target deals 30% more damage

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Laser Lattice      | Channel  | Channel for up to 4s; emit a sustained beam that sweeps in a 90-degree arc; deals 80% weapon damage per second of contact; Crystal Shards in the arc explode in secondary beams
  - Rainbow Burst      | Attack   | Fire a beam that hits the first Crystal Shard encountered and explodes in all possible directions simultaneously; hits all enemies in the environment for 100% weapon damage if Shards are optimally placed
  - Coherent Light     | Passive  | After firing 3 consecutive beam abilities, your next beam is Coherent — it ignores all cover, pierces through all targets in its path, and deals 50% bonus damage

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Grand Prism        | Summon   | Summon a massive floating Grand Prism crystal for 12s; it automatically splits and reflects any beam ability fired within 20m, tripling its effective hits; also illuminates the area (removing darkness penalties)
  - Sunfire Convergence| Attack   | Command all active Crystal Shards to fire beams simultaneously converging on a single target; each Shard contributes 150% weapon damage; cooldown 30s (devastating with Mirror Array active)

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Perfect Spectrum   | Transform| For 15s, become a living prism — every damaging ability you use spawns 3 light beams that independently seek the nearest 3 enemies for 100% weapon damage each; Crystal Shards within 25m become permanently active amplifiers; your light beams ignore armor entirely, dealing pure radiant damage

>> Your notes on PRISM:
>> Crystal Shards are the class's "setup" mechanic — reward players who pre-position Shards before big pulls. The Mirror Array/Rainbow Burst combo should feel like solving a geometry puzzle.
>> Coherent Light's "3 consecutive beams" tracker creates a rhythm for experienced players to exploit.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Geode (Crystal Armor, Defense, Damage Reflection)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A crystalline tank who encases themselves in layered mineral armor — reflecting physical punishment back at attackers, growing harder the more they are struck, and ultimately becoming an immovable geode of living stone.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Crystal Shell      | Buff/Shield     | Encase yourself in a crystal shell; absorbs damage equal to 200% weapon stat before shattering; shattered Shell deals 60% weapon damage to all adjacent enemies
  - Mineral Hardening  | Passive/Defense  | Each time your Crystal Shell absorbs a hit, your physical damage reduction permanently increases by 2% for the encounter (max +20%)
  - Quartz Spike       | Reactive | When struck in melee, automatically sprout a quartz spike from your armor; deals 80% weapon damage to the attacker; cannot trigger more than once per 2s

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Geode Form         | Stance   | Assume the Geode Stance; movement speed -20%, damage output -10%, but all incoming damage is reduced by 25% and Crystal Shell recharges twice as fast; toggle on/off
  - Refraction Plate   | Passive/Resistance  | Your crystal armor refracts incoming beam, laser, and radiant attacks — they deal 50% less damage and a weak version of the beam is reflected back at the source (30% of original damage)
  - Crystal Barricade  | Active   | Rapidly grow a wall of crystals up to 6m long and 2m tall; blocks movement and projectiles; lasts 10s or until destroyed (HP: 300% your weapon stat)

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Diamond Shell      | Buff/Shield     | Upgrade your Crystal Shell to Diamond quality; absorbs 400% weapon stat in damage, does not shatter on depletion (instead resets to 100% capacity after 5s), and reflects 30% of all absorbed damage at attackers
  - Resonant Armor     | Reactive  | Your crystal armor vibrates sympathetically with damage — after absorbing 500% weapon stat in total damage, release a resonance burst dealing 200% weapon damage to all enemies within 6m; this resets after each burst
  - Living Stone       | Passive/Immunity  | Your body partially converts to mineral — you gain immunity to bleed, poison, and disease; you also gain tremorsense (detect all ground movement within 15m through vibration)

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Geode Eruption     | Active   | Detonate your crystal armor outward in a lethal spray; deals 300% weapon damage to all enemies within 8m and stuns them for 2s; you immediately begin regenerating a new Crystal Shell over 3s; cooldown 30s
  - Impervious Lattice | Buff/Immunity     | For 10s, become completely immune to all forced movement (knockback, pull, push) and CC effects while your Crystal Shell is active; additionally, all damage absorbed by your Shell is stored and released in a final detonation when the buff ends

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Heart of the Mountain | Transform | For 20s, become one with the living stone — your body fully crystallizes; gain 60% damage reduction, immunity to all non-magical damage, and your Crystal Shell capacity is tripled; every attack against you causes a crystal shockwave dealing 100% weapon damage to all nearby attackers; you cannot be moved, toppled, or displaced; you are the mountain

>> Your notes on GEODE:
>> Diamond Shell replacing Crystal Shell at T3 is satisfying progression — players feel their defense maturing. The "5s reset instead of shatter" is a dramatic power spike.
>> Living Stone's tremorsense is flavor that doubles as genuine utility in stealth encounters — a sleeping power players discover naturally.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Facet (Gem Attunement, Elemental Switching, Multi-Element)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A mercurial elemental adaptor who attunes to different precious gems to unlock radically different elemental power sets — switching gems mid-combat to exploit weaknesses and create multi-element combinations.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Attune: Ruby        | Stance   | Attune to Ruby (Fire element); your attacks deal bonus fire damage (+30% weapon as fire), you gain fire resistance (25%), and fire-themed skills become available
  - Attune: Sapphire    | Stance   | Attune to Sapphire (Ice element); your attacks deal bonus ice damage (+30% weapon as ice), you gain ice resistance (25%), and ice-themed skills become available; switching Attunements takes 1s
  - Gem Sense          | Passive  | You instinctively detect elemental vulnerabilities — gain automatic knowledge of elemental resistances and weaknesses for all enemies within 20m

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Attune: Emerald     | Stance   | Attune to Emerald (Lightning element); attacks deal bonus lightning damage (+30% weapon), you gain lightning resistance (25%), and chaining abilities arc to 1 additional target for 50% damage
  - Facet Strike        | Attack   | Strike with your current gem's element at 160% weapon damage; apply the element's signature status (Fire: Burning 3s, Ice: Chilled 3s, Lightning: Shocked 3s, more from later gems)
  - Prism Cycle         | Passive  | Switching Attunement now takes only 0.5s; additionally, each Attunement switch generates 1 Facet Charge (max 5); Facet Charges are consumed by higher-tier abilities

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Attune: Amethyst    | Stance   | Attune to Amethyst (Arcane/Void element); attacks deal arcane damage, you gain magic resistance (25%), and your spells pierce 20% of magical resistance; applies Arcane Vulnerability (target takes +15% magic damage for 6s) on hit
  - Gemstone Cascade   | Attack   | Rapidly cycle through all known Attunements, firing one Facet Strike per gem in sequence; each strike deals 120% weapon damage in its element; costs 3 Facet Charges
  - Crystal Resonance  | Passive  | Enemies affected by 2 or more different elemental status effects simultaneously take 25% increased damage from all sources; you excel at applying the combination

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Attune: Onyx        | Stance   | Attune to Onyx (Shadow/Necrotic element); attacks drain 15% of damage dealt as HP, deal necrotic damage, and apply Shadow Mark (reduce target healing received by 50%)
  - Prismatic Detonation| Active   | Detonate all elemental status effects on a target simultaneously; each active effect deals 200% weapon damage in its element; a target with 4+ effects takes 50% bonus damage from the detonation; costs 4 Facet Charges

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Perfect Facet       | Transform| For 20s, attune to all gems simultaneously — you deal all elemental types at once with every attack (each contributing 25% weapon damage in its element), apply all status effects with each Facet Strike, and your Gemstone Cascade fires all elements simultaneously rather than in sequence; Crystal Resonance's bonus increases to 50% for the duration; you are every element and none — the perfect jewel

>> Your notes on FACET:
>> The Attunement system is the mechanical heart of this archetype — the joy is in reading enemy weaknesses and switching efficiently. More attunements unlock across tiers to give a sense of growing mastery.
>> Prismatic Detonation is the "setup and explode" payoff ability. Encourage players to spread multiple status effects rather than stacking one.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON CRYSTAL SAGE]
> Crystal Sage is the most visually spectacular class in this set — every ability should be accompanied by brilliant light, crystalline sounds, and geometric formations.
> Cross-tree synergy: Prism Crystal Shards enhance Geode's Crystal Barricade (both are crystal terrain); Facet Attunements can imbue Prism beams with elemental damage.
> Consider a "Crystal Clarity" resource — a clarity meter that fills as you use gem magic, granting bonus damage when fully attuned to a single gem type, and unlocking multi-gem powers at the cost of clarity.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 17: Beastlord (Animal Companion)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A primal sovereign who commands the animal kingdom — running with a slavering wolf pack, merging their own soul with a beast companion to become a hybrid predator, or bonding with a single supreme creature to evolve it into an apex killing machine beyond anything nature intended.

ARCHETYPE A: Pack Alpha (Multi-Pet Swarm, Wolf Pack, Coordinated Hunt)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A commanding pack leader who controls a mob of coordinated beasts, using positioning and tactical commands to overwhelm enemies through sheer numbers and coordinated attacks.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Call Wolf          | Summon   | Summon a wolf companion (25% max HP, 45% weapon damage, fast attack speed); up to 3 wolves active simultaneously
  - Pack Tactics       | Passive  | Each wolf attacking the same target as another wolf gains +15% damage; this stacks per additional wolf on the same target (up to +45% with 3 wolves on 1 target)
  - Alpha Howl         | Buff     | Release a commanding howl; all active animal companions gain +30% attack speed and +20% damage for 6s; affects all companion types

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Summon Boar        | Summon   | Summon a heavily armored boar (45% max HP, 60% weapon damage, charges in a straight line on command, knocking enemies aside); counts as 1 companion slot
  - Hunt Command       | Active   | Designate a target; all companions immediately stop current actions and focus entirely on the designated target, gaining +25% damage and ignoring other threats; lasts 8s or until target is dead
  - Bloodlust Frenzy   | Passive  | When any companion kills an enemy, all other companions gain Bloodlust for 6s (+20% attack speed, +10% damage); Bloodlust stacks from multiple kills

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Summon Raptor      | Summon   | Summon a swift raptor (20% max HP, 70% weapon damage, extremely fast, can leap 6m to attack); raptors flank automatically and always attack from the enemy's rear
  - Pack Surround      | Active   | Command: all companions spread to maximally surround the target enemy; the target is Surrounded (cannot flee, takes +20% damage from all sources); lasts until companions move away
  - Nature's Numbers   | Passive  | For each companion you have active beyond the first, you gain +5% damage and +3% max HP yourself; your strength grows with your pack

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Apex Pack          | Buff   | For 15s, your companion limit is doubled and all existing companions are Empowered (stats increased by 50%); new companions summoned during Apex Pack begin at Empowered stats
  - Call of the Wild   | Active   | Send out a primal call that attracts 1 random wild animal from the local environment to serve you permanently for the encounter; the animal type is appropriate to the biome (bear in forests, shark in water, etc.)

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Pack Sovereign     | Aura     | While this ability is active (permanent toggle), your pack never numbers fewer than 3 wolves — if wolves are killed, they respawn after 10s; additionally, all companions gain Pack Mind (they share sensory information with each other and you, cannot be flanked, and are immune to Fear); your pack is not a collection of animals, it is one organism with many bodies

>> Your notes on PACK ALPHA:
>> The companion type variety (wolf speed, boar durability, raptor flanking) is key — reward players who mix companions strategically rather than spamming one type.
>> Call of the Wild is the "discovery" ability — make the biome-appropriate animal genuinely surprising and useful (a cave spider, a dire elk, an albatross that can aerial dive).
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Chimera Bond (Fuse with Pet, Hybrid Transform, Bestial Power)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A shape-shifting hybrid who merges their own body with their animal companion, gaining bestial traits, hybrid forms, and the ability to temporarily become something between human and beast.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Bond Companion     | Active   | Establish a deep soul bond with one animal companion; the bonded companion gains +30% HP and damage and will not flee when you are downed
  - Bestial Instinct   | Passive/Evasion  | Your bond with animals sharpens your senses — you gain +15% evasion, tremorsense (detect movement within 10m), and can communicate intent with any bonded animal
  - Feral Strike       | Attack   | Channel your bonded companion's fighting spirit; attack with unnatural ferocity dealing 160% weapon damage and applying Lacerate (bleed: 30% weapon damage per second for 6s)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Partial Merge      | Transform| Partially merge with your bonded companion for 10s; gain the companion's primary physical trait (wolf: claw attacks, eagle: flight, bear: massive HP boost); you retain all your own abilities during the merge
  - Soul Tether        | Passive/Heal  | When your bonded companion would be killed, you can expend 20% of your own max HP to keep them alive at 1 HP; this is automatic if toggled on; your lives are linked
  - Predator Sense     | Passive  | Inherit your bonded companion's sensory modality — wolf: superior smell (detect invisible enemies), eagle: superior sight (doubled vision range), bear: vibration sense (no blind spots); changes with companion type

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Full Merge         | Transform| Completely merge with your bonded companion for 12s; become a full hybrid creature (4 legs, natural weapons, animal senses + full intelligence); your base stats are replaced by average of yours and companion's, multiplied by 1.4; cooldown 45s
  - Bestial Surge      | Active   | Tap into your merged companion's primal energy; for 6s your attacks become Bestial — triple attack speed, each hit applies a random beast status (Lacerate, Hobble, Stun 1s); usable only during Full Merge
  - Chimeric Adaptation| Passive/Utility  | Each time you perform a Full Merge, you permanently retain one minor trait from the companion (wolf: +5% speed, eagle: +5% evasion, bear: +5% HP); these stack across multiple encounters

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Apex Chimera       | Transform| The ultimate merge — you and your companion fuse into a true Chimera with features from multiple beasts for 20s; gain the abilities of up to 2 companion types simultaneously, all stats increased by 60%, and you gain the unique Chimera Roar (AoE Fear, 8m, 4s); cooldown 90s
  - Bond Beyond Death  | Passive  | If your bonded companion dies, you absorb their essence — you gain their stat bonus (+30% to companion's primary stat) permanently for the encounter and are consumed with Grief Rage (+40% damage, -20% defense) for 15s

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Eternal Chimera    | Transform| Permanently merge with your bonded companion — the merge never ends; you become a true hybrid creature for the rest of the encounter; gain all companion traits simultaneously, your HP is combined with companion HP, all your attacks are Bestial, and you can shift between human-dominant and beast-dominant modes as a free action (each mode emphasizes different abilities); the bond is complete

>> Your notes on CHIMERA BOND:
>> The companion type should meaningfully change the Partial Merge and Full Merge experience — a wolf merge plays very differently from a bear merge. Invest in the differentiation.
>> Chimeric Adaptation's permanent trait accumulation is a wonderful long-campaign mechanic — a veteran Chimera Bond player looks subtly different from a new one.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Apex Predator (Single Powerful Beast, Enhancement, Evolution)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A focused beast master who bonds with a single legendary animal companion, pouring all investment into evolving it to supernatural power and becoming an indomitable duo of unmatched lethality.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Bond Apex Beast    | Summon   | Summon and bond with a single powerful beast (50% max HP, 80% weapon damage, 1 active companion only); this companion persists between encounters and levels with you
  - Prime Training     | Passive  | Your apex beast is highly trained — it obeys complex commands, never misinterprets orders, and deals 20% bonus damage to any enemy type you designate it to specialize against
  - Shared Resolve     | Passive/Defense  | You and your apex beast share a resilience link — when either of you is reduced below 30% HP, both gain +15% damage and +20% damage reduction for 8s

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Evolve: Speed      | Passive  | Permanently evolve your apex beast's movement and attack speed; it gains +30% speed, can sprint 60m without fatigue, and its attacks trigger once more per second (faster auto-attack)
  - Beast Empowerment  | Buff     | Channel beast energy into your companion for 3s; they gain +50% damage and +30% HP for 12s; cooldown 25s
  - Tag Team Strike    | Attack   | You and your apex beast attack the same target simultaneously; the combined strike deals 200% weapon damage each (400% total) and has a 30% chance to Stun (2s)

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Evolve: Armored    | Passive/Defense  | Permanently evolve your apex beast's hide into natural armor; it gains 30% damage reduction, immunity to bleed and poison, and generates thorns (30% of melee damage reflected to attackers)
  - Apex Mark          | Active   | Designate a target as prey for your apex beast; the beast deals 40% increased damage to the marked target and gains 10% lifesteal against them; mark persists until target is dead
  - Alpha Evolution    | Passive  | Your apex beast grows stronger with each kill — each confirmed kill grants +2% permanent damage and +1% permanent HP for the encounter; after 10 kills, it reaches Evolved status (special abilities unlock)

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Evolve: Predator   | Passive  | Your apex beast evolves into a supernatural predator — gains the ability to track prey through any terrain, turns invisible for 3s before attacks (true stealth), and its critical hits deal 100% bonus damage
  - Legendary Bond     | Passive/Defense  | The bond between you and your apex beast reaches legendary depth — your companion shares 50% of the damage reduction and resistances you have, and you share 50% of its physical power bonuses; you are one unit, not two

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Apex Ascension     | Transform| Your apex beast undergoes a final evolution — for 30s, it transforms into a Legendary Form (triple HP, double damage, gains flight or burrowing depending on species, immune to CC); during Apex Ascension your Tag Team abilities gain no cooldown and your beast can use two special abilities simultaneously; when the Ascension ends, the beast retains 10% of its Legendary Form power as a permanent upgrade

>> Your notes on APEX PREDATOR:
>> The persistent, leveling apex beast is the emotional anchor of this archetype — players name it, protect it, and feel genuine loss when it's threatened. Make Bond Beyond Death (from Chimera Bond) cross-pollinate here for tragic players.
>> Alpha Evolution's kill-counting snowball is satisfying against hordes and meaningless against single bosses — design for both by giving the beast other bonuses in single-target fights.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON BEASTLORD]
> Three companion philosophies: many weak (Pack Alpha), one merged self (Chimera Bond), one evolved champion (Apex Predator). Each rewards a fundamentally different emotional relationship with animals.
> A multi-tree Beastlord could run Pack Alpha for the wolves while maintaining an apex beast for boss fights — let the builds breathe together.
> Consider a "Beast Bond" resource that charges through companion kills and is spent on enhancement abilities — this ties all three trees to the shared fantasy of empowering beasts.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 18: Runecarver (Inscription Magic)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A methodical magic-crafter who inscribes reality with runes of power — etching glyphs into the earth as deadly defensive traps, charging weapons and terrain with explosive sigil strikes, and composing runes that rewrite the behavior of other runes, creating cascading magical systems of terrifying complexity.

ARCHETYPE A: Wardcraft (Defensive Rune Traps, Glyphs, Territory Control)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A patient trap-setter who inscribes the battlefield with protective runes and deadly wards before enemies arrive, punishing aggression and turning any terrain into a fortified killing ground.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Inscribe Ward      | Utility  | Carve a basic ward glyph into the ground at target location (12m range); when an enemy steps within 1.5m, it triggers — dealing 80% weapon damage and applying Root (2s); lasts 60s or until triggered
  - Rune Sense         | Passive/Utility  | You always know the status of your active runes — their triggers, charges remaining, and enemy proximity; runes you set cannot be detected by enemies unless they have specific magical detection abilities
  - Reinforcing Glyph  | Buff/Defense     | Inscribe a rune on an ally or yourself; reduces all incoming damage by 15% for 30s; up to 3 Reinforcing Glyphs active simultaneously

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Alarm Rune         | Utility  | Place an invisible detection rune; alerts you when any creature crosses it (60m range); creature is also marked (visible through walls for 8s); lasts until triggered or 10 minutes
  - Rune Wall          | Utility  | Inscribe a line of linked runes up to 8m long; any enemy crossing the line is knocked back 4m and takes 100% weapon damage; the line triggers 3 times before expiring
  - Sympathetic Ward   | Passive  | When one of your wards is triggered, all wards within 6m of it pulse with energy, gaining 1 Empowered charge (next trigger deals 30% bonus damage and has enhanced effect)

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Sanctuary Glyph    | Active   | Inscribe a 6m radius sanctuary circle; within the circle, allies take 25% less damage, cannot be pushed or pulled, and regenerate 5% max HP per second; lasts 20s; enemies who enter the circle are Slowed 30%
  - Runic Minefield    | Utility  | Rapidly inscribe 5 wards in a 10m radius scatter pattern; all trigger simultaneously if any single ward is triggered (chain detonation); deals 150% weapon damage each to caught enemies
  - Binding Rune       | Debuff   | Inscribe a tracking rune on a target; they are Bound (movement speed -40%, cannot teleport or shadow step) for 12s; if they exceed 15m from the rune's inscription point, the rune detonates for 200% weapon damage

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Fortress of Runes  | Active   | Spend 3s inscribing a comprehensive ward network in a 15m radius around you; the network contains 8 wards of various types placed intelligently at chokepoints; this acts as a temporary runic fortress; the entire network shares Sympathetic Ward bonuses
  - Anti-Magic Glyph   | Utility  | Inscribe a powerful null-rune at a location; any magical ability used within 5m of it is suppressed — spells fizzle, magical summons are banished, enchantments are temporarily suppressed; lasts 20s; cooldown 60s

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Living Inscription | Channel  | Channel for 5s to inscribe a self-evolving master ward across the entire encounter area; the master ward persists indefinitely, senses all enemy movement, and adaptively creates new wards at chokepoints every 10s; it cannot be fully destroyed — destroying individual wards only delays the network; the battlefield itself becomes your warded domain

>> Your notes on WARDCRAFT:
>> Pre-combat inscription time is the fantasy — Wardcraft rewards players who scout ahead, plan, and set up. Runic Minefield's chain detonation should feel like a "they walked right into it" moment.
>> Anti-Magic Glyph is the surprise anti-caster tool. Make sure it has counterplay (physical movement, ranged attacks from beyond 5m range).
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Sigil Strike (Offensive Rune Explosions, Charged Attacks, Rune Weapons)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: An aggressive rune-warrior who charges weapons and attacks with volatile explosive sigils, detonating inscribed power on contact for massive burst damage with satisfying magical pyrotechnics.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Charge Weapon      | Buff     | Inscribe your weapon with an explosive sigil; your next attack deals bonus 80% weapon damage as magic explosion and creates a 3m AoE shockwave of the same damage
  - Sigil Bolt         | Attack   | Hurl a compact inscribed sigil as a projectile (20m range); detonates on impact for 100% weapon damage in a 4m AoE; can be inscribed mid-combat
  - Runic Resonance    | Passive  | When two of your sigils detonate within 2s of each other, they resonate — both deal 20% bonus damage; three detonations within 3s deal 40% bonus damage (Triresonance)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Overcharge         | Buff/Risk     | Pour excess magical energy into your weapon inscription; the next Charge Weapon attack deals 200% bonus damage instead of 80%, but the explosion radius doubles to 6m (hits allies); use wisely
  - Sigil Chain        | Attack   | Fire a sigil that bounces between up to 4 enemies (10m range each bounce) before detonating; each hit deals 70% weapon damage, and the final target receives the full detonation (150% damage)
  - Master Inscription | Passive  | Your sigils are permanently more volatile — Charge Weapon and Sigil Bolt each gain +15% crit chance, and critical sigils cause Secondary Detonation (50% damage in the same AoE 1s after the initial blast)

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Sigil Burst Array  | Attack   | Spend 2s inscribing a rapid array of 4 sigils in a fan pattern; all 4 launch simultaneously, each dealing 90% weapon damage on contact; enemies hit by multiple sigils take 20% bonus damage per additional hit
  - Volatile Rune      | Active   | Inscribe an unstable rune on a target (enemy or terrain); after 3s or when struck by another sigil, it detonates for 350% weapon damage in a 8m AoE; the detonation is Triresonant by default
  - Runic Armor-Break  | Passive  | Sigil explosions leave Runic Burns on targets — each burn reduces armor by 8% for 8s; stacks up to 5 times for a maximum -40% armor reduction

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Grand Sigil        | Channel  | Channel for 3s to inscribe a massive Grand Sigil at target location (20m range); when detonated (manually or by Volatile Rune), it deals 500% weapon damage in a 12m AoE and stuns all targets for 3s; can be prepared in advance during safe moments
  - Inscription Frenzy | Buff     | For 10s, Charge Weapon becomes free action (no cooldown, can be applied after every attack) and all Sigil detonations trigger Runic Resonance regardless of timing; cooldown 60s

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Rune of Annihilation | Channel | Channel for 4s, inscribing the most complex and dangerous rune in existence at target location; after a 2s visual warning, it detonates in a 25m radius for 800% weapon damage with Secondary Detonation at 400% damage 2s later; enemies in the outer 10m of the radius receive 50% damage; the explosion leaves the area Runed (enemies in it take 15% more damage from all sigil effects for 30s)

>> Your notes on SIGIL STRIKE:
>> Runic Resonance is the combo system — train players to set up multiple sigils in quick succession by making Triresonance feel dramatically powerful.
>> Grand Sigil's "can be prepared in advance" language is intentional: reward players who inscribe it during the dialogue phase of a boss fight or between combat waves.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Meta Script (Runes That Modify Runes, Inscription Combos, Rune Language)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A rune scholar of unprecedented sophistication who writes runes that alter the behavior of other runes — compounding effects, changing triggers, linking distant glyphs, and ultimately composing a living rune language that cascades beyond prediction.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Amplifier Rune     | Utility  | Inscribe an amplifier modifier on any existing rune; that rune's damage and effect potency increase by 30%; this modification counts as a separate inscription
  - Trigger Link       | Utility  | Inscribe a link between two existing runes (must be within 20m of each other); when one is triggered, the other triggers simultaneously as well; one use per pair
  - Inscription Speed  | Passive  | Your rune inscriptions are significantly faster — all inscription times reduced by 30%; additionally, you can inscribe while moving (at 50% speed)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Delay Script       | Utility  | Modify an existing rune to delay its trigger; choose a delay from 1-10s after trigger condition is met; useful for setting ambushes with precise timing
  - Repeating Rune     | Utility  | Modify an existing rune to trigger multiple times (choose 2-4 times); each trigger has a 3s cooldown; the rune expires after all triggers are used
  - Counter-Script     | Reactive | When an enemy attempts to disrupt or destroy one of your runes, automatically inscribe a retaliating secondary rune on the attacker (50% weapon damage + 1 random debuff) without spending an action

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Reversal Glyph     | Utility  | Write a meta-rune that inverts an existing rune's function — a damage ward becomes a healing ward for allies; a slow rune becomes a speed boost; a binding rune becomes a teleport; 8s duration of inversion
  - Cascade Script     | Passive  | When a Trigger Link fires both runes, each rune's effect is Empowered; additionally, you can now chain up to 3 runes in a single Trigger Link cascade (each fires in sequence 0.5s apart)
  - Absorption Rune    | Utility  | Write a meta-rune near a hostile spell or trap; your rune absorbs the incoming magical effect and stores its energy; you can release the stored energy as one of your sigil strikes at full potency; cooldown 15s

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Recursive Loop     | Utility  | Write a complex meta-script that causes two existing runes to trigger each other alternately — they fire in sequence continuously until either expires or an enemy disrupts the loop; each loop cycle deals 60% combined damage and stacks debuffs rapidly
  - Master Grammar     | Passive  | You have mastered the complete runic language — you can now add 2 modifiers (not 1) to any single rune; modifier combinations create unique compound effects (Amplifier + Repeating = each repeated trigger is stronger than the last; Delay + Trigger Link = time-shifted cascade)

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Living Manuscript | Channel | Channel for 5s to inscribe a master-level recursive meta-script across the entire battlefield; the script begins generating new runes from the grammar of your existing inscriptions — it learns from each rune triggered, adapts to enemy behavior, and evolves new variations autonomously over its 30s lifetime; by the end of its duration, the battlefield is covered in a self-authored rune language that you only partially understand; it represents magic exceeding the Runecarver's own conscious design

>> Your notes on META SCRIPT:
>> The Reversal Glyph is the most creative ability in this tree — it inverts enemy traps or your own runes. The possibility space is enormous and should be intentionally underspecified to let clever players discover uses.
>> The Living Manuscript is the "the apprentice becomes the sorcerer's broom" moment — the rune language grows beyond the caster's control in a narratively meaningful way.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON RUNECARVER]
> The three archetypes represent three philosophies of inscription: Wardcraft (defense/preparation), Sigil Strike (offense/explosion), Meta Script (recursion/systems).
> Multi-tree Runecarvers who combine Wardcraft's wards with Meta Script's Trigger Links and Cascade Scripts create genuinely emergent "rune machines" — this is the class's highest expression.
> Consider a "Ink" resource (limited per encounter) that must be managed across all inscription abilities — forcing prioritization between wards, strikes, and meta-modifications.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 19: Tide Caller (Water/Ocean)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A sovereign of the ocean's full fury — riding crushing wave bursts to obliterate everything in their path, commanding the abyssal pressure of the deep to crush and drown all who dare oppose them, and raising living coral towers and sea creature guardians to reshape the battlefield into a coral reef domain.

ARCHETYPE A: Tsunami (Wave Burst Damage, Surge, Flood)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A relentless offensive force who builds wave momentum and releases it in devastating surges — riding crests of destruction across the battlefield and hammering lines of enemies with unstoppable torrential force.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Wave Strike        | Attack   | Launch a focused wave blast at target (15m range); deals 95% weapon damage and pushes the target back 4m; deals +20% damage to targets already Soaked
  - Surge              | Movement | Ride a self-generated wave 10m in a straight line, becoming briefly untargetable during transit; enemies in your path are knocked aside (50% weapon damage)
  - Soaked             | Passive  | All water-damage abilities you use apply the Soaked condition (lasts 8s); Soaked targets take +15% damage from all your water abilities and take 10% extra lightning damage from any source

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Wave Crash         | Attack   | Conjure a massive wave and crash it down on an 8m line (20m range); deals 140% weapon damage to all targets in the line and knocks them prone; leaves shallow water (difficult terrain, Soaks enemies) for 6s
  - Riptide Pull       | Active   | Create a powerful water current at target location (18m range, 6m radius); pulls all enemies toward the center over 2s; enemies pulled into each other collide for 80% weapon damage each
  - Current Rider      | Passive  | After using Surge, your next attack deals +40% weapon damage and triggers an additional minor wave burst (50% damage) for free; you harness momentum

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Tidal Cascade      | Channel  | Channel for 3s; generate a sustained tidal surge in a 12m cone, dealing 90% weapon damage per second to all enemies inside and continuously pushing them back; enemies pinned against terrain take +50% damage per second
