  - Wall of Water      | Active   | Raise a 10m wide, 4m tall wall of crashing water (20m range); enemies who pass through take 150% weapon damage and are Knocked Down (2s); the wall lasts 6s and can be detonated early as a burst (200% damage to all nearby)
  - Breaking Point     | Passive  | Each consecutive wave ability that hits the same target builds a Pressure Stack (+10% damage from water abilities, max 5); when a target reaches 5 Stacks, the next hit triggers a Breaking Wave (bonus 200% weapon damage AoE)

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Leviathan Wave     | Attack   | Summon a colossal wave at the far edge of the encounter area; it sweeps across the entire battlefield (up to 30m wide) over 3s, dealing 300% weapon damage to all enemies in its path and heavily Soaking everything; terrain is flooded for 15s after
  - Tide Lock          | Buff     | For 12s, your wave abilities generate twice the normal number of Pressure Stacks and Soaked duration is doubled; cooldown 45s

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Omega Tsunami      | Channel  | Channel for 3s while the ocean answers your call; release a 40m wide mega-tsunami that sweeps the field for 600% weapon damage to everything in its path; it then pulls back as a massive undertow (all enemies dragged 20m toward you), then crashes forward again for 300% damage in the secondary wave; the encounter area remains deeply flooded for 30s, treating all ground as difficult water terrain

>> Your notes on TSUNAMI:
>> Pressure Stacks and Soaked condition work together as the damage amplification system — players who hit the same target repeatedly get Breaking Wave payoffs. Design bosses with enough health to make this satisfying.
>> Leviathan Wave is an encounter-transforming ability — the 15s flood aftermath completely changes mobility dynamics. Lean into this with terrain design.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Abyssal Depths (Pressure, Crush, Drown, Deep Water)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A terrifying cold-depths specialist who weaponizes oceanic pressure itself — immobilizing enemies in crushing water columns, drowning them in place, and dragging them into the lightless deep where they are slowly annihilated by forces beyond comprehension.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Pressure Point     | Attack   | Focus oceanic pressure on a target (18m range); deals 80% weapon damage and applies Pressurized (target feels crushing weight: -15% speed, -10% attack speed) for 6s
  - Deep Pull          | Debuff   | Create a downward pull on a target, pinning them to the ground; they cannot jump, fly, or be knocked back for 4s and suffer -20% movement speed; ground-based abilities deal +15% damage to them
  - Abyssal Cold       | Aura     | Radiate supernatural cold from the deep ocean; enemies within 8m suffer -10% attack speed and -5% damage due to numbing cold; cold worsens with proximity (at 4m: -20% attack speed, -10% damage)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Crush Depth        | Active   | Dramatically increase pressure on a target for 3s; they take 100% weapon damage per second and their armor value is reduced by 20% as it buckles under pressure; enemies near the target are also Pressurized
  - Drown              | CC       | Encase a target in a sphere of deep water; they are unable to act (drowning state) for 3s and take 80% weapon damage per second; they can spend 1 action to break free but remain Soaked and Pressurized
  - Thermal Vent       | Utility  | Open a superheated vent from the ocean floor; creates a 5m radius pillar of boiling water for 6s; enemies inside take 60% weapon damage per second from scalding water and steam; the extreme temperature contrast with Abyssal Cold creates Thermal Shock (stun 1.5s) if both debuffs are active

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Abyssal Descent    | Active   | Drag a target toward the abyss — they phase partially into the deep ocean; for 6s they are Entombed (cannot take normal actions, take 100% weapon damage per second from pressure, cannot be targeted by allies or enemies); they return to the battlefield at the end of duration, heavily Pressurized; bosses phase for only 2s
  - Pressure Burst     | Attack   | Detonate all Pressurized stacks on a target simultaneously; each stack deals 80% weapon damage in a 4m AoE and the detonation inflicts Shattered Armor (-30% armor for 10s)
  - Midnight Zone      | Active   | Create a 10m radius zone of abyssal conditions at target location (20m range) for 12s; inside the zone, all enemies are Pressurized at double speed, Abyssal Cold is tripled in severity, and Deep Pull automatically applies to any non-flying enemy

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Crushing Deep      | Channel  | Channel for 4s while increasing pressure on all enemies within 15m; each second of channel increases damage by 50% cumulatively; at full channel, all affected enemies are Crushed (40% HP damage, Shattered Armor, immobilized for 5s); cooldown 60s
  - Void of the Abyss  | Active   | Tear open a rift to the absolute deep at target location; for 8s it creates an irresistible suction pulling all enemies within 20m toward it; enemies caught at the center are partially Abyssal Descended (2s) and take massive pressure damage (200% weapon per second)

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Marianas Call  | Transform| For 20s, become an avatar of the absolute deep — you exist at abyssal pressure, immune to all physical and cold damage; all enemies within 25m are under Abyssal Descent conditions at all times (partial phasing, crushing pressure); Thermal Shock triggers automatically whenever any enemy near you has both cold and heat effects; at the end of duration, release all accumulated pressure in a single catastrophic implosion (800% damage to all enemies in 15m)

>> Your notes on ABYSSAL DEPTHS:
>> Drown and Abyssal Descent create two levels of "out of play" mechanics — Drown is shorter and breakable, Descent is longer and inescapable. Use this difference meaningfully.
>> Thermal Vent's interaction with Abyssal Cold is the archetype's hidden depth — players who discover the Thermal Shock combo feel exceptionally clever.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Coral Warden (Water Summons, Coral Terrain, Ocean Domain)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A domain-creating summoner who raises coral reefs and living ocean constructs to transform any battlefield into an ocean floor — growing a living fortress of sea creatures and crystalline coral that makes the Tide Caller's domain impenetrable.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Raise Coral        | Utility  | Grow a coral formation at target location (15m range); coral is 3m tall, provides cover, and blocks movement; lasts 30s; enemies who strike the coral take 30% weapon damage from its jagged surface
  - Summon Seahorse    | Summon   | Summon a giant seahorse mount/companion (30% max HP, 50% weapon damage, water jet attack that pushes enemies back 3m); the seahorse can serve as a mount in flooded terrain
  - Coral Growth       | Passive/Utility  | All coral formations you create grow by 1m per 10s and develop 1 new feature per 20s (spine barbs: +15% damage; filter fans: heal allies in 3m; bioluminescence: reveals invisible enemies nearby)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Summon Shark       | Summon   | Summon a Great White Shark companion (50% max HP, 90% weapon damage, can move through flooded terrain at full speed, Bleeding Bite: targets bleed for 30% damage per second for 8s); highly effective in flooded environments
  - Coral Barrier Wall | Utility  | Raise a 12m long coral wall up to 6m tall; it blocks movement and projectiles, has substantial HP (400% weapon stat), and harms enemies who climb or break through it (150% weapon damage on breach)
  - Ocean's Bounty     | Passive/Heal  | When an ally stands within 4m of your coral formations, they receive a continuous benefit: minor HP regeneration (3% max HP per second), Soaked condition immunity, and +10% damage to water abilities

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Summon Kraken Arm  | Summon   | Raise a massive Kraken tentacle from the floor (or water); it lashes a 10m radius dealing 120% weapon damage, can grab a target (Grappled: cannot act, takes 80% damage per second), and serves as an impassable barrier; 1 active; lasts 12s
  - Reef Fortress      | Active   | Rapidly grow a 10m radius coral reef fortress in 3s; the fortress contains 4 coral walls and 2 coral spire towers (which automatically fire water bolts at enemies for 60% weapon damage every 3s); lasts 20s
  - Tide Pool Trap     | Utility  | Grow a shallow tide pool (6m radius) at target location that looks like terrain; enemies who walk into it are revealed (Soaked), move at 50% speed, and can be targeted by Kraken Arm from any water nearby; lasts 30s

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Summon Leviathan   | Summon   | Call a juvenile Leviathan from the deep (75% max HP, 150% weapon damage, massive AoE tail swipe, swallow smaller enemies whole: instant kill on enemies below 20% HP); only 1 active; cooldown 90s; the Leviathan returns to the sea when slain
  - Living Reef        | Active   | For 20s, animate all coral formations in the encounter area into Living Coral — they sprout tentacles that attack nearby enemies (80% weapon damage each, once every 3s), slowly reposition themselves, and rebuild at 1m per 3s if damaged; the reef fights alongside you

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Ocean's Sovereignty| Transform| For 25s, declare full sovereignty over the battlefield — the entire encounter area floods (treated as open ocean, all terrain is underwater); your coral grows to fill the space (12 formations in 5s), your summons operate at double speed and power, all enemies are Soaked and Pressurized by default, and you can breathe freely and move in all directions at full speed; all non-water-adapted allies receive water breathing; the enemy team has come to fight you in your ocean now

>> Your notes on CORAL WARDEN:
>> The coral growth passive is the secret delight of this archetype — players plant coral early and come back to find it has grown features they didn't explicitly build. Design the growth features to feel like discovering gifts.
>> Ocean's Sovereignty is the ultimate terrain transformation: it should be visually spectacular and mechanically transformative. The combat rhythm shifts completely when the field floods.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON TIDE CALLER]
> Three ocean aspects: surface fury (Tsunami), abyssal horror (Abyssal Depths), living reef (Coral Warden). Each captures a different relationship with the sea — the storm, the darkness, and the ecosystem.
> The Soaked condition is the shared cross-tree glue — Tsunami applies it for damage, Abyssal Depths deepens its effects with Pressurized, and Coral Warden benefits from allies' and enemies' Soaked status in flood terrain.
> Consider a "Tide" resource that rises and falls — high Tide empowers Tsunami, low Tide activates Abyssal Depths bonuses, and stable Tide benefits Coral Warden's sustained growth; the cycle creates natural ability rhythm.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 20: SAND WRAITH (Desert / Sand)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A ghost born from the desert's cruelty — patient, erosive, and utterly merciless. They do not strike with the raw power of fire or water but with the slow inevitability of erosion, the madness of mirages, and the suffocating totality of a sandstorm that devours everything it touches.

ARCHETYPE A: EROSION (Armor / Stat Destruction)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Applies stacking Erosion debuffs to enemies that permanently (for the encounter) degrade their defensive stats, making every fight a war of attrition where the longer enemies survive, the more vulnerable they become.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Sand Abrasion       | Attack   | Blast target with abrasive sand; deal light damage and apply 1 Erosion stack (each stack reduces armor by 3% and magic resistance by 2%)
  - Grinding Winds      | Aura     | Aura: all enemies within 6m accumulate 1 Erosion stack every 4s from constant sand exposure
  - Deep Erosion        | Passive  | At 5 Erosion stacks, the degradation deepens — each additional stack reduces attack damage by 2% in addition to defensive stats

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Sandblast           | Attack   | Fire a concentrated sand bolt; deal moderate damage and apply 3 Erosion stacks simultaneously
  - Corrode             | Debuff   | Apply a rust-and-sand curse; target's weapon or primary attack deals 20% less damage for 8s (their equipment is visibly degraded)
  - Erosion Cascade     | Passive  | When a target reaches 8 Erosion stacks, their accumulated erosion affects nearby enemies — all enemies within 4m gain 2 Erosion stacks from the debris scatter

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Structural Failure  | Active   | Trigger a catastrophic erosion event on target with 8+ Erosion stacks; all stacks are consumed for a burst of damage equal to 20 per stack, and target's armor is set to 0 for 6s (completely stripped)
  - Desert Patience     | Passive  | Erosion stacks you apply never expire during combat; the desert always wins through persistence
  - Dust to Dust        | Attack   | Target a Corroded or highly Eroded enemy; deal damage proportional to how many defensive stat points they have lost (high-armor enemies take enormous damage from this)

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Abrasion      | Channel  | Channel for 3s; apply 1 Erosion stack per 0.5s to all enemies within 6m — at channel end all Erosion stacks increase by 2 from the sustained bombardment
  - Erosion Mastery     | Passive  | Erosion stacks now also reduce movement speed by 2% per stack and reduce the target's healing received by 2% per stack; the sand gets into everything

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Scouring        | Active   | Trigger a catastrophic erosion event affecting all enemies simultaneously — apply 5 Erosion stacks to every enemy on the battlefield, then immediately trigger Structural Failure on every enemy who has 8+ stacks, and set all remaining enemies' armor reduction to maximum for 10s; nothing resists the desert's patience long enough

>> Your notes on Erosion:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: MIRAGE (Illusion / Misdirection)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Creates convincing sand mirages that confuse, misdirect, and waste enemy actions — not dealing damage directly but causing the enemy to fight phantoms while the real Wraith flanks from safety.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Sand Clone          | Active   | Create a visual mirage of yourself at target location; it mimics your appearance and draws enemy attention for 6s before dissolving
  - Heat Haze           | Debuff   | Distort the air around target enemy with heat shimmer; they have 20% miss chance on all attacks for 5s
  - Mirage Passive      | Passive/Evasion  | You passively emit minor heat distortion; enemies targeting you from beyond 10m have 10% miss chance at all times

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - False Oasis         | Active   | Create a mirage of a safe retreat point; enemies with a flee or retreat behavior are drawn toward it for 4s before it dissolves (lures fleeing enemies back into range)
  - Army of Mirages     | Active   | Create 4 sand clones simultaneously, all mimicking you; enemies must attack each one to determine which is real (you deal 15% less damage while maintaining the illusion)
  - Illusory Charge     | Active   | Send a mirage charging at an enemy; the mirage deals no damage but causes the enemy to dodge or retaliate, wasting their action — you attack from the opposite direction simultaneously

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Perfect Double      | Active   | Create a fully convincing mirage that uses one of your abilities (visually); the ability appears to deal damage and apply effects but nothing actually happens — expensive but costs the enemy a reaction
  - Sand Substitution   | Reactive | When you are hit, replace yourself with a sand clone at the last moment; you teleport 5m away while the clone takes the hit and dissolves, negating the damage; 20s cooldown
  - Mirage Field        | Active   | Fill a 10m area with heat mirage distortion for 8s; all enemies inside have 30% miss chance and cannot target beyond 5m

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Grand Illusion      | Active   | Create a massive mirage battlefield overlay — for 6s, the entire terrain appears shifted (walls seem open, gaps seem closed, distances seem wrong); enemies without high mental resistance cannot navigate effectively
  - Identity Theft      | Active   | Create a perfect mirage of one of the enemies; for 8s the mirage fights alongside you dealing 50% damage and drawing attacks from confused enemies who cannot identify which is the original

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Desert's Lie    | Active   | For 15s, completely invert perceived reality for all enemies — their allies appear as enemies, you appear as one of their allies, your sand clones appear as their party members; the resulting confusion causes enemies to attack each other for the duration while you are effectively invisible to them; when the effect ends, all enemies are Dazed for 3s

>> Your notes on Mirage:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: SIMOOM (Sandstorm AoE / Blind)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Generates and directs devastating sandstorm weather systems across the battlefield, using wind-driven sand to blind, suffocate, and physically batter everything in the storm's path.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Sand Gust           | Attack   | Hurl a burst of sand at target; deal light damage and apply Sand (1 stack; at 5 stacks: Blinded for 3s)
  - Dust Devil          | Summon   | Summon a small whirlwind at target location for 6s; it moves 1m per second in a direction and applies 1 Sand stack to enemies it touches
  - Storm Sight         | Passive/Immunity  | You are immune to all sand-based blindness and Erosion effects; the desert is your home

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Sandstorm Veil      | Active   | Create a 8m radius sandstorm cloud that persists for 10s; all enemies inside gain Sand stacks every 2s and have 25% miss chance
  - Simoom Breath       | Attack   | Exhale a concentrated hot sand wind in a cone; apply 3 Sand stacks to all enemies hit and deal heat damage
  - Sand Suffocation    | Passive  | At 5 Sand stacks (Blinded), enemies also begin Suffocating (1% max HP damage per second) as sand fills their airways

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Direct Simoom       | Active   | Summon a fast-moving sandstorm column 4m wide and 15m long; it sweeps across the battlefield in 2s dealing damage and applying 2 Sand stacks to all in its path
  - Wall of Sand        | Active   | Raise a solid wall of compressed sand 8m wide and 4m tall; it blocks movement and projectiles but degrades over 10s — as it degrades it periodically blasts Sand stacks at nearby enemies
  - Scourwind           | Active   | Dramatically increase wind speed across the entire field for 6s; all Sand stacks accumulate twice as fast, all Sand-based zones deal 50% more damage, and enemy accuracy is globally reduced by 15%

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Eye of the Storm    | Active   | Create a large swirling sandstorm 15m across with a calm center (4m); enemies in the storm wall take continuous damage and Sand stacks while you and allies in the center are unaffected and gain 20% damage bonus from the dramatic contrast
  - Sandstorm Apex      | Passive  | When an enemy is Blinded by Sand stacks, the blindness spreads — any enemy who moves within 2m of a Blinded enemy gains 3 Sand stacks from the aura of swirling sand

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Great Simoom    | Active   | Call down the legendary desert killing wind for 15s — a catastrophic sandstorm covers the entire battlefield; all enemies gain 2 Sand stacks per second, all Blinded enemies also Suffocate at triple speed, visibility for enemies drops to 1m, and a directional Simoom wave rolls across the field every 3s dealing damage to everything in its path; you glide through the storm untouched

>> Your notes on Simoom:
>>
>>

################################################################################
CLASS 21: PLAGUE DOCTOR (Disease / Alchemy)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A macabre physician who has devoted themselves to understanding disease not to cure it, but to weaponize it with the same clinical detachment they once used to treat it. Behind the beaked mask lies a brilliant and terrifying mind that sees every living body as a petri dish and every battlefield as a laboratory.

ARCHETYPE A: CONTAGION (Disease Spreading)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Infects enemies with diseases that spread between targets on contact, managing multiple active strains simultaneously with each disease having a unique spread condition and symptom profile.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Patient Zero        | Attack   | Infect target with Strain A (Wasting Fever): deals 3% max HP as disease damage per second and spreads to adjacent enemies when target takes damage
  - Quarantine Break    | Active   | Forcibly trigger the spread condition of one active disease on a target; it immediately attempts to spread to all adjacent enemies
  - Virulence           | Passive  | Diseases you apply have a 20% chance to apply at double intensity (dealing double damage per second)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Strain B: Plague    | Attack   | Infect target with a bubonic-style plague; deals moderate instant damage, applies Weakness (20% stat reduction), and spreads when the target moves within 3m of another enemy
  - Cross-Contamination | Passive  | When a target has two different disease strains simultaneously, they interact — each disease's spread rate doubles and damage is increased by 30%
  - Epidemic Tracking   | Passive  | You can see all diseased enemies through walls and obstacles; you also see the disease spread network in real time

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Strain C: Necrosis  | Attack   | Infect target with a tissue-death disease; deals escalating damage (doubles every 4s) and spreads on death — when the infected target dies, all enemies within 5m are immediately infected at full intensity
  - Super Spreader      | Active   | Designate one infected target as a Super Spreader; their spread radius doubles and their movement generates a disease trail that infects enemies who walk through it for 8s
  - Mutation Event      | Active   | Force a mutation in one active disease strain; randomly upgrade the disease's spread condition and damage type (unpredictable but always an improvement)

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Pandemic Protocol   | Active   | Combine all active disease strains in your arsenal into a single super-disease and unleash it in a 12m radius AoE; the combined disease carries all active strain symptoms simultaneously
  - Final Stage         | Passive  | Any enemy who has been infected for 15+ seconds total enters the Final Stage — they take 50% more damage from all sources as their body breaks down

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Black Harvest   | Active   | For 15s, all diseases in your arsenal become airborne — they spread passively from every infected target to every target within 8m every 2s without requiring physical contact; any enemy who reaches Final Stage immediately spreads all their strains to all enemies within 10m on death; the pandemic becomes unstoppable

>> Your notes on Contagion:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: APOTHECARY (Cure / Buff Potions)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Brews and throws alchemical potions during combat from a limited Flask inventory, managing a pre-combat preparation system where the potions selected before the fight determine support options during it.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Healing Flask       | Heal     | Throw a healing flask at target ally; restore moderate HP immediately on impact
  - Antidote Flask      | Cleanse  | Throw an antidote flask at target ally; remove all poison and disease effects
  - Flask Preparation   | Passive  | Begin each combat with 6 Flask charges distributed among your prepared potion types (set before combat); charges refill between encounters

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Fortifying Tonic    | Buff/Defense     | Throw a fortifying tonic; target ally gains 25% damage reduction for 10s
  - Stimulant Flask     | Buff/Movement     | Throw a combat stimulant; target ally gains 30% attack speed and 20% movement speed for 8s
  - Master Brewer       | Passive  | All flask effects are 20% stronger; you also carry 2 additional Flask charges per combat

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Resurrection Reagent| Heal     | Throw a powerful revival compound at a fallen ally; revive them at 35% HP — uses 2 Flask charges
  - Immunizing Agent    | Buff/Immunity     | Throw a disease immunity compound; target ally is immune to all disease, poison, and Contagion effects for 12s
  - Area Flask          | Active   | Throw any flask as an area burst (4m radius) rather than single-target; uses 2 Flask charges instead of 1

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Grand Tincture      | Buff/Heal     | Throw a master alchemical compound; target ally gains ALL flask buffs simultaneously (healing, fortification, stimulant) at 70% effectiveness — uses 3 Flask charges
  - Emergency Stockpile | Active   | Activate once per combat; immediately restore 4 Flask charges from emergency reserves carried in the coat lining

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Magnum Opus     | Active   | Throw a legendary alchemical masterpiece (uses all remaining Flask charges, minimum 3); creates a 10m radius area of transformative reagent that lasts 12s — all allies inside continuously regenerate HP, have all debuffs cleansed every 2s, deal 25% bonus damage, and move 20% faster; enemies inside take continuous alchemical damage and have healing received reduced by 50%

>> Your notes on Apothecary:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: MUTAGEN (Self-Transformation Via Mutation)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Injects experimental mutagens into themselves to gain monstrous new traits, with each injection permanently altering their body for the encounter in unpredictable but generally powerful ways.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Basic Mutagen       | Active   | Inject a low-grade mutagen; gain a random Minor Mutation (regeneration, increased strength, enhanced senses, or natural armor) for this encounter
  - Stable Formula      | Passive  | Your first mutagen injection each combat always provides the most beneficial available mutation with no risk of side effects
  - Mutation Synergy    | Passive  | When you have 2 or more mutations active, they interact — gain a 10% bonus to all stats from the compound effect of altered biology

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Combat Stimulant    | Buff/Risk   | Inject an adrenaline-enhancing mutagen; gain 40% attack speed and 20% damage for 8s but take 5% of max HP as self-damage from the metabolic strain
  - Toxic Blood         | Reactive/Defense   | Inject a venom-producing mutagen; any enemy who hits you in melee takes poison damage equal to 5% of your max HP per second for 4s
  - Adaptive Immunity   | Passive/Resistance  | After suffering a disease or poison effect, your mutated immune system adapts — gain 30% resistance to that specific effect permanently for the encounter

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Apex Mutagen        | Active   | Inject a high-grade mutagen; gain a random Major Mutation (bone spurs that deal melee damage, bioluminescent acid spray, massive size increase, or regenerative healing factor) — chance of a negative side effect (10%)
  - Controlled Mutation | Active   | Select a specific mutation from your known catalog rather than rolling randomly; costs double the standard injection resource
  - Stacked Biology     | Passive  | You can maintain up to 4 mutations simultaneously; each additional mutation beyond 2 provides an additional 10% bonus to all stats from biological complexity

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Unstable Compound   | Active   | Inject a dangerously unstable mutagen; gain a random Catastrophic Mutation for 10s — these mutations are enormously powerful (triple stats, limb replacement with living weapons, regeneration at 10% HP per second) but the compound expires catastrophically, dealing 20% max HP damage at the end
  - Perfect Specimen    | Passive  | All mutations you apply now trend toward positive outcomes; chance of side effects reduced to 0% and random mutations always roll from the top tier available to you

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Final Formula   | Transform| Inject the ultimate mutagen compound — over 3s your body rewrites itself completely; gain ALL currently known mutations simultaneously at full potency (no side effects), your size increases dramatically, and for 20s you are a true chimeric horror with every mutation working in concert — the compound is permanent for the remainder of the encounter

>> Your notes on Mutagen:
>>
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON PLAGUE DOCTOR]
>
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 22: PUPPETEER (Control / Manipulation)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A controller whose entire philosophy is that no enemy is truly an obstacle — only a resource not yet fully exploited. They reduce living beings to mechanisms, pulling strings, installing control chips, and linking minds until the battlefield of enemies becomes an army of reluctant allies fighting their own cause.

ARCHETYPE A: MARIONETTE (Directly Control Enemy)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Seizes direct control of a single enemy, spending Control Time to command their actions — a high-skill archetype that rewards knowledge of enemy abilities and positioning.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Puppet Wire         | Active   | Attach control wires to target enemy for 3s; their movement is reduced 50% and they are pulled 1m toward you per second
  - Mind Hook           | Active   | Establish a brief mental connection with target; for 2s you can redirect their next auto-attack at a target of your choice
  - Control Threshold   | Passive  | Enemies below 40% HP are easier to control; all Marionette abilities last 50% longer against targets below this threshold

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Puppet Walk         | Active   | Take full control of target enemy for 3s (full Marionette control — movement and basic attacks); stronger enemies require more Control charges
  - Forced Retreat      | Active   | Force target enemy to flee from their current position for 3s as if Feared; they move at full speed away from you
  - Sympathetic Pain    | Passive  | While controlling a Marionette, 30% of damage dealt to them is also felt by nearby enemies (they share the puppet's suffering)

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Deep Control        | Active   | Take full control of target enemy for 6s including ability use; you can activate their special abilities against their allies
  - Puppet Shield       | Active   | While controlling a Marionette, force them to stand between you and the nearest enemy — they act as your human shield, absorbing attacks meant for you
  - Transfer Control    | Active   | Release current Marionette and instantly attach to a new target within 8m; the released Marionette is Dazed for 2s from the severed connection

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Perfect Marionette  | Active   | Achieve complete control of target for 8s with no restrictions — full movement, all abilities (including their ultimates), and they act at your mental speed (no input delay); 90s cooldown
  - Residual Strings    | Passive  | After control expires, target retains puppet residue for 10s — they have 20% chance to attack their own allies with each action from the lingering commands

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Grand Puppeteer | Active   | For 10s, simultaneously control up to 3 enemies — each receives independent commands as if you had three puppets; this requires significant mental effort but allows you to turn the entire enemy force against itself with no cooldown on individual commands for the duration

>> Your notes on Marionette:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: DOLLMAKER (Create Puppet Summons)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Crafts artificial puppet constructs from collected materials, building an army of obedient dolls that fight with the Puppeteer while also serving as control-relay stations for enhanced abilities.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Basic Doll          | Summon   | Assemble a simple combat puppet from available materials; it fights enemies at basic effectiveness and has low HP
  - String Network      | Passive/Defense  | Dolls within 6m of each other share a string network — if one is hit, the impact force is distributed among all connected dolls (reducing damage to each)
  - Puppet Materials    | Active   | Harvest material from a corpse or object; gain 2 Materials (used to build higher-quality dolls)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Voodoo Doll         | Summon   | Create a Voodoo Doll linked to a specific living enemy; spend 3 Materials — any damage dealt to the doll is also felt by the linked enemy at 50% effectiveness
  - Soldier Doll        | Summon   | Assemble a combat-specialized doll; costs 3 Materials, has significantly better stats than a Basic Doll and a charge attack
  - Doll Repair         | Heal     | Spend 1 Material to repair a damaged doll, restoring 30% of its max HP instantly

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Commander Doll      | Summon   | Build a Commander Doll that buffs all nearby dolls (+20% stats); costs 5 Materials and has moderate combat ability itself
  - Explosive Doll      | Summon   | Build a doll packed with alchemical explosive; costs 3 Materials; command it to self-destruct at any time, dealing AoE damage equal to 20 per Material originally invested
  - String Puppet       | Passive/Utility  | When a doll is destroyed, release its strings — all dolls within 4m gain 15% stats from absorbing the severed string energy

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Grand Doll          | Summon   | Construct a large, powerful Grand Doll; costs 8 Materials; has enormous HP, powerful attacks, and acts as a mobile relay — all Marionette controls used within 10m of it have doubled duration
  - Doll Army           | Summon   | Spend 10 Materials to instantly summon 6 Basic Dolls simultaneously; they arrive in formation around a target location

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Masterwork      | Summon   | Spend 15 Materials to construct the ultimate doll — a near-perfect replica of yourself with 80% of your stats and access to all your Basic and Uncommon abilities; it fights autonomously and intelligently for 20s, and while it is active, all your other dolls gain 30% stats from the presence of the Masterwork inspiring them

>> Your notes on Dollmaker:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: HIVEMIND (Link and Control Groups)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Links multiple enemies together into a psychic collective, causing effects applied to one to propagate across the entire network — a crowd-control archetype that scales with group size.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Neural Link         | Active   | Establish a psychic link between two enemies; damage dealt to either is shared with the other at 30% efficiency
  - Link Sense          | Passive  | You can feel the edges of all Neural Links you have established; linked enemies glow visibly to you regardless of distance or obstacles
  - Cascade Thought     | Passive  | CC effects applied to a Linked enemy have a 25% chance to propagate to all enemies sharing a link with them at 50% duration

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Group Link          | Active   | Establish a Neural Link between up to 4 enemies simultaneously; all linked enemies share damage at 20% efficiency per member
  - Shared Agony        | Active   | Apply a debuff to one Linked enemy; it instantly propagates at full effectiveness to all enemies sharing that link
  - Link Overload       | Active   | Overload a Neural Link — force it to transmit too much psychic data; all Linked enemies take psychic damage equal to 15% of their max HP and are Dazed for 2s

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Hive Directive      | Active   | Issue a behavioral directive through the Hivemind link; all Linked enemies follow a simple command for 3s (stand still, attack nearest ally, move to a location)
  - Empathy Weapon      | Active   | Force all Linked enemies to share the emotional state of the most injured member; the healthy ones suffer Weakness debuffs (20% stat reduction) from the shared pain of the wounded
  - Permanent Link      | Passive  | Neural Links you establish no longer have a time limit; they persist until you cancel them or an enemy is killed; maximum total links increases to 8

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Collective Punishment| Active  | Deal damage to one Linked enemy equal to 40% of their max HP; this damage propagates at 50% to every enemy in the link — with a large group, this is devastating
  - Hivemind Control    | Active   | For 5s, assert absolute collective control — all Linked enemies act as a unit under your command (they fight their unlinked allies together); the link must have 4+ members for this to activate

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Grand Collective | Active  | For 12s, link every enemy on the battlefield into a single Hivemind — all damage dealt to any enemy is distributed at 20% among all others, all CC propagates at 100% effectiveness, all debuffs are shared, and once per 3s you can issue a Hive Directive to the entire battlefield-spanning collective simultaneously

>> Your notes on Hivemind:

################################################################################
CLASS 23: GUNSLINGER (Sci-fi Ranged)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A hyperkinetic combat specialist who has turned the art of the draw into something approaching the supernatural — part soldier, part gambler, part physicist. They shoot faster than thought, at angles that defy geometry, and with a diverse enough arsenal that no two fights ever look the same.

ARCHETYPE A: QUICKDRAW (Speed / Crit)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Builds Draw Speed stacks through rapid consecutive attacks that increase firing rate and critical chance — the faster they go, the faster they can go, until they blur into a storm of gunfire.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Quick Shot          | Attack   | Fire a fast snapshot; deals moderate damage and generates 1 Draw Speed stack (max 10; each stack = 3% attack speed and 2% crit chance)
  - Fan the Hammer      | Attack   | Rapidly fire 3 shots at reduced accuracy; generates 3 Draw Speed stacks regardless of hits
  - Hair Trigger        | Passive  | At 5+ Draw Speed stacks, your reload time is eliminated — magazine size is effectively infinite for the duration

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Lightning Draw      | Attack   | Spend 5 Draw Speed stacks; perform an instantaneous draw-and-fire that cannot be dodged — deals heavy crit damage automatically
  - Speed Cascade       | Passive  | Each critical hit generates 2 additional Draw Speed stacks instead of 1
  - Shoot Through       | Attack   | Fire a penetrating shot that passes through multiple enemies in a line; each enemy hit generates 1 Draw Speed stack

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Full Auto Burst     | Attack   | Spend 8 Draw Speed stacks; unleash a 10-shot burst at one target over 1.5s — each shot benefits from the crit bonus earned from the spent stacks
  - Reflexes of the Draw| Passive  | Draw Speed stacks no longer decay between shots as long as you keep firing at least once every 2s — sustaining fire sustains speed
  - Afterimage          | Passive/Evasion  | At max Draw Speed (10 stacks), briefly create an afterimage that draws one attack per 4s from enemies as they can't track your real position

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Apex Speed          | Active   | Instantly jump to max Draw Speed stacks (10) regardless of current count; 45s cooldown — the legendary quick draw
  - Bullet Time         | Active   | Enter a 4s focus state where your subjective time slows but you still move at full speed; fire at triple rate while enemies appear to move at 30% speed

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Fastest Gun     | Active   | For 10s, enter a legendary draw state — Draw Speed stacks are permanently maxed and cannot decrease, all shots automatically crit, fire rate doubles beyond the stack bonus, and once per second you fire a free additional shot at the lowest HP enemy in range as automatic targeting

>> Your notes on Quickdraw:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: TRICK SHOT (Ricochet / Geometry)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Uses environmental geometry and ricochet physics to hit targets behind cover, attack multiple enemies from impossible angles, and set up elaborate multi-bounce kill shots.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Ricochet            | Attack   | Fire a shot designed to bounce; it ricochets off one surface and hits target behind cover — deals 80% damage after bounce
  - Geometry Read       | Passive  | You can visualize ricochet trajectories — the game shows predicted bounce paths for 2s when aiming; all ricochets deal 5% more damage per bounce
  - Skip Shot           | Attack   | Fire a shot low that skips off the floor; it travels 12m at ground level, ignoring chest-height cover and hitting leg-level targets

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Double Bounce       | Attack   | Fire a shot that bounces twice before reaching target; can bypass two walls of cover, deals 65% damage at final target
  - Ricochet Array      | Attack   | Fire 3 simultaneous shots designed to all converge on the same target from different angles after bouncing; all three deal full damage if they connect
  - Splitting Round     | Attack   | Fire a round that splits into 2 fragments on its first bounce; each fragment continues to a different target, both dealing 60% damage

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Impossible Angle    | Attack   | Fire around a corner at 90+ degrees — this shot curves through the air rather than bouncing; targets completely behind cover can still be hit at 70% damage
  - Chain Ricochet      | Attack   | Fire a shot that bounces between enemies (not terrain) — it arcs from one enemy to the next, hitting up to 5 targets, dealing 85% damage per bounce
  - Geometric Mastery   | Passive  | All bounced/ricocheted shots deal 15% bonus damage per bounce accumulated; a triple-bounce shot deals 45% bonus damage at the end

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Bullet Labyrinth    | Active   | Over 2s, fire a complex shot that bounces 8 times between terrain and enemies in a predetermined path you visualize; each bounce deals damage to any enemy touched
  - Orbital Shot        | Attack   | Fire a round on a curved trajectory that arcs around a target and hits them from behind regardless of facing; ignores directional damage bonuses of the target and bypasses shields that face forward

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Impossible Shot | Active   | Fire a single legendary round that the physics engine has no right to support — it bounces up to 15 times, targeting every enemy on the battlefield at least once, gaining 5% damage per bounce, and returning to you after completing its path as a visual flourish; total damage depends entirely on enemy count and geometry

>> Your notes on Trick Shot:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: ARSENAL (Weapon Switching / Variety)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Carries 4 distinct weapon types with unique mechanics, switching between them as a tactical decision — mastery lies in knowing which weapon to deploy and when to switch for maximum effect.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Plasma Pistol       | Attack   | Active weapon: Plasma Pistol — rapid fire energy bolts that deal reliable moderate damage and apply Heat stacks (at 5 stacks: target is Burned for fire DoT)
  - Cryo Rifle          | Attack   | Active weapon: Cryo Rifle — slow shots that deal heavy damage and apply Chill stacks (synergizes with any cold-based effects in the encounter)
  - Weapon Cycle        | Active   | Switch to the next weapon in your Arsenal; 0.3s swap animation (much faster than combat reload); each weapon has independent ammo

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Scatter Cannon      | Attack   | Active weapon: Scatter Cannon — short-range shotgun burst hitting 5 pellets in a cone; each pellet applies 1 stack of Stagger (at 5 stacks: target is briefly Stunned)
  - Rail Gun            | Attack   | Active weapon: Rail Gun — single-shot devastating penetrating beam that punches through all enemies in a line; very slow reload but enormous damage
  - Arsenal Mastery     | Passive  | Switching weapons generates 1 Draw Speed stack (from Quickdraw cross-training) and increases the first shot from the new weapon's damage by 20%

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Combo Protocol      | Passive  | When you switch weapons and immediately fire, the combination of two weapon types deals a bonus effect (Plasma > Cryo = Superchilled Plasma: slows and burns; Cryo > Scatter = Shatter Shot: frozen pellets cause Brittle; all 6 combos documented)
  - Overload Charge     | Active   | Spend 4s charging any weapon to its maximum; fire an overloaded shot that deals 300% of normal damage for that weapon type and applies all associated debuffs at maximum stacks
  - Dual Wield          | Active   | For 8s, equip two weapons simultaneously; fire both with each attack (one from each hand) at 70% damage each — the weapon combo effect triggers every shot

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Signature Round     | Active   | Load a unique signature round into current weapon; the next shot fires a legendary version of that weapon's attack at 250% damage with an enhanced version of its debuff
  - Full Arsenal Barrage| Active   | Fire one shot from every weapon in your Arsenal in rapid sequence (4 shots in 2s); each shot applies its full effect and triggers the Combo Protocol between adjacent weapon types

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Maximum Arsenal     | Active   | For 12s, all four weapons are simultaneously loaded and ready with no switching required — every shot you fire is from a random weapon in the Arsenal, all Combo Protocol bonuses are always active regardless of switch order, all weapon ammo is unlimited, and weapon overloads charge 3x faster

################################################################################
CLASS 24: WARFORGED (Mech / Power Armor)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A soldier encased in a cutting-edge powered exoskeleton that blurs the line between human and machine — capable of transforming between combat modes that prioritize mobility, defense, or raw destructive output, each mode representing a different philosophy of mechanized warfare.

ARCHETYPE A: SIEGE MODE (Stationary Heavy Damage)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Locks the mech's legs into a stationary firing platform, trading all movement for dramatically increased weapon damage and energy efficiency — a glass cannon that rewards positioning.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Deploy              | Stance   | Anchor your mech legs into Siege Mode; become immobile but gain 30% bonus damage and 20% energy efficiency on all weapons
  - Heavy Cannon        | Attack   | Fire the mech's primary heavy cannon; deals heavy damage to a single target in Siege Mode (40% less damage if not in Siege Mode)
  - Reinforced Anchor   | Passive/Defense  | While in Siege Mode, gain 15% damage reduction from stabilized footing and become immune to knockback and displacement effects

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Suppression Fire    | Channel  | Channel Siege Mode's chain guns for 3s; fire continuously at a target zone, dealing damage every 0.3s and applying Suppressed (enemies cannot move toward you for duration)
  - Howitzer Shell      | Attack   | Load and fire a high-explosive shell; deals massive AoE damage in a 8m radius, but has 4s reload time in Siege Mode (8s outside)
  - Overwatch           | Passive  | While in Siege Mode, automatically fire at any enemy who attempts to move within 6m — a free auto-attack that doesn't interrupt other actions

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Siege Barrage       | Active   | In Siege Mode, fire all available weapons simultaneously in a 3s burst; combined fire deals enormous total damage and suppresses the entire target area
  - Extended Platform   | Passive  | Siege Mode duration is unlimited (it was already toggle-based, but now the energy cost per second is reduced to 0 — pure efficiency)
  - Target Painter      | Active   | Mark a target in Siege Mode; for 8s all subsequent shots against them deal 30% bonus damage from the firing solution established by your stationary vantage

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Nuclear Option      | Active   | While in Siege Mode, charge for 3s then fire a single devastating orbital strike-equivalent shot; deals the highest single-target damage available to the class — requires remaining stationary for the entire charge
  - Siege Network       | Passive  | If there are other Warforged in Siege Mode within 15m (ally Warforged or your own deployed turrets), all Siege Mode damage bonuses increase by 10% per adjacent Siege unit

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Siege      | Active   | Enter Maximum Siege Protocol for 15s — your mech becomes a fixed installation; you cannot exit or be forced out of Siege Mode, all weapons deal triple damage, Howitzer cooldown drops to 2s, Overwatch fires twice per trigger, and a target automatically painted at the start of the protocol takes quadruple all damage from your attacks

>> Your notes on Siege Mode:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: FORTRESS PROTOCOL (Mech Tank)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Activates defensive sub-systems to become an unkillable mobile fortress, generating Shield Integrity as a second HP pool and using threat management to protect allies behind the mech's bulk.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Fortress Mode       | Stance   | Activate defensive plating; gain a Shield Integrity pool equal to 40% of your max HP that must be depleted before real HP is touched
  - Taunt Protocol      | Active   | Broadcast an aggression signal; all enemies within 10m are forced to target you for 4s
  - Bulwark             | Passive/Defense  | Allies directly behind you (within 6m) gain 20% damage reduction from your mech body intercepting fire

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Shield Recharge     | Active   | Trigger emergency shield recharge; restore 30% of max Shield Integrity over 3s — must not take damage during recharge or it is interrupted
  - Reflective Plating  | Passive/Defense  | 15% of damage absorbed by Shield Integrity is reflected back at the attacker as energy discharge
  - Ablative Armor      | Passive/Defense  | Even after Shield Integrity is depleted, retain 15% damage reduction from structural mech plating

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Deploy Shield Wall  | Active   | Extend a physical barrier from your mech 4m wide; it blocks all projectiles from one direction for 8s and allies can stand behind it freely
  - Emergency Systems   | Reactive | When HP drops below 20%, automatically trigger Shield Recharge and gain 20% damage reduction for 6s from emergency protocols
  - Fortress Walk       | Passive/Immunity  | In Fortress Mode you walk 20% slower but become immune to all forced movement; you cannot be pushed, pulled, or knocked back

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Invincible Fortress | Active   | For 8s, become completely invulnerable — all damage dealt to you during this window is stored as Fortress Debt, then converted into Shield Integrity when the effect ends (gain back up to 50% of damage taken as shield)
  - Reactive Plating    | Passive/Defense  | When an enemy crits you, the crit triggers Reflective Plating at 50% efficiency (reflecting 50% of the crit bonus damage) — critical hits are actively punished

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Unbreakable Wall | Active  | For 15s, achieve maximum Fortress Protocol — Shield Integrity regenerates at 5% per second, Taunt Protocol is always active (all enemies must target you), all damage to allies within 10m is reduced by 40% as you interpose yourself, and any attack that would deplete the last of your Shield Integrity is negated once every 3s

>> Your notes on Fortress Protocol:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: OVERCLOCK (Speed / Burst at Risk of Overheating)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Pushes the mech's systems beyond safe operating parameters, managing a Heat meter that enables tremendous speed and power but risks Overheat shutdown if not carefully managed.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Overclock           | Toggle   | Push mech systems to 120% capacity; gain 25% movement speed and 15% damage but generate 5 Heat per second (max 100 Heat; at 100: Overheat Shutdown for 4s)
  - Coolant Vent        | Active   | Release coolant; reduce Heat by 30 instantly — 20s cooldown; the vent deals minor cold damage to adjacent enemies
  - Thermal Management  | Passive  | At under 50 Heat, Heat decay rate doubles during Overclock (naturally bleeds off faster when not near overheat)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Turbo Dash          | Movement | Boost jets to maximum; dash 12m instantly and generate 15 Heat — can be used in rapid succession at the risk of quick overheat
  - Overdrive Strike    | Attack   | Overclock the weapon arm for a single massive strike; deals 200% weapon damage and generates 25 Heat — high risk, high reward
  - Redline             | Passive  | Between 75 and 99 Heat (Redline zone), all damage increases by 25% — rewarding the risky play of running near overheat

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Emergency Purge     | Active   | Dump all Heat instantly in a directed thermal explosion; deals Heat-equal damage to all enemies in a 6m cone (1 damage per Heat point dumped), then Heat resets to 0 — deliberate overheat weaponized
  - Cooling Protocols   | Passive  | After an Overheat Shutdown ends, gain 10s of Overcooled (generate 50% less Heat from all sources)
  - Beyond the Limit    | Active   | Temporarily disable safety limiters; for 6s Heat cannot cause Overheat Shutdown (it still accumulates); systems crash when this expires, venting all accumulated Heat as Emergency Purge

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Maximum Overclock   | Active   | Push systems to 150% for 8s; gain 50% movement speed, 40% damage, and 30% attack speed — Heat generates at triple rate during this window; survival requires precise coolant management
  - Thermal Armor       | Passive/Defense  | High Heat levels harden mech plating via thermal expansion; above 60 Heat, gain damage reduction equal to 0.3% per Heat point (max 18% at 60 Heat)

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Overclock  | Active   | Override all safety systems for 12s — Heat cannot increase beyond 95 (preventing Shutdown), movement speed triples, all abilities generate 50% less Heat, and Redline bonuses apply as if at 99 Heat for the entire duration; when the effect ends, forced Coolant Vent fires automatically at triple strength

>> Your notes on Overclock:
>>
>>

################################################################################
CLASS 25: INK MAGE (Art / Creation Magic)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A sorcerer whose power flows through a brush and an inkwell filled with the fundamental substance of reality — they do not cast spells in the traditional sense but create them, illustrating creatures into existence, writing words that become law, and erasing things from the world as casually as crossing out a mistake on parchment.

ARCHETYPE A: ILLUSTRATOR (Draw Creatures to Life)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Spends Ink to illustrate creatures onto surfaces or the air; the quality of the illustration (determined by channel time) determines the creature's power — rushed sketches versus masterwork paintings.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Quick Sketch        | Summon   | Spend 2 Ink; rapidly draw a simple creature in 1s (low quality, low HP and damage) — a sketch soldier that fights for 15s
  - Ink Reserve         | Passive/Utility  | Passively regenerate 1 Ink per 4s; carry a maximum of 20 Ink at a time; some abilities require high Ink investment
  - Artistic Eye        | Passive  | Creatures you draw in 3+ seconds of channel time have 20% bonus HP and deal 10% bonus damage from improved craftsmanship

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Illustrated Beast   | Summon   | Spend 4 Ink and channel 2s; draw a recognizable predatory animal that fights with moderate stats and applies a species-appropriate debuff on hit
  - Living Mural        | Summon   | Spend 3 Ink to draw a creature directly on a wall; it springs from the surface and fights, but remains anchored to surfaces (can climb walls, move on ceiling, etc.)
  - Artist's Haste      | Passive  | You can draw two creatures simultaneously using both hands — second creature costs 1 additional Ink but is drawn at the same time

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Masterwork Creature | Summon   | Spend 8 Ink and channel 4s; draw a detailed, powerful creature with high stats, special abilities, and 2x the HP of a Quick Sketch — worth the investment
  - Erase               | Active   | Spend 2 Ink to draw an eraser stroke over one of your summoned creatures; it disappears but detonates in an ink explosion dealing damage to nearby enemies
  - Color Theory        | Passive  | Using different colored Inks (red = fire damage, blue = ice, gold = holy) changes the damage type of illustrated creatures; switch colors as a free action

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Legendary Portrait  | Summon   | Spend 15 Ink and channel 6s; illustrate a magnificent, unique creature of your design — a true artistic masterpiece with enormous stats, a unique special ability, and the ability to use simple tactics intelligently
  - Animate Environment | Active   | Spend 5 Ink; animate all existing illustrations in the environment (decorative paintings, murals, tattoos on enemies) — they spring to life for 10s as temporary fighting allies

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Grand Illustration | Summon| Spend all remaining Ink (minimum 15); spend 4s creating a masterwork battle scene — illustrate an entire army of 8 creatures simultaneously, all at Masterwork quality, and for 20s they fight with perfect coordination as a united force while your Ink regenerates at triple speed

>> Your notes on Illustrator:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: CALLIGRAPHER (Word / Glyph Spells)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Writes words and glyphs in the air that become literal reality — the word "FIRE" bursts into flame, "SLOW" crawls into an enemy's legs, "WALL" erects a barrier — a system of linguistic magic limited only by vocabulary and Ink.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Write: FIRE         | Attack   | Inscribe the word FIRE in the air; it erupts into flames dealing fire damage to target and applying Burning
  - Write: SLOW         | Debuff   | Inscribe the word SLOW on target; reduce their movement speed by 40% for 6s as the word crawls into their being
  - Calligrapher's Flow | Passive  | Writing speed increases with practice; frequently used words cost 1 less Ink after being written 5 times in a session

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Write: SHIELD       | Buff/Shield     | Inscribe the word SHIELD around an ally; create a protective barrier that absorbs moderate damage for 8s
  - Write: SILENCE      | CC       | Inscribe the word SILENCE over target's mouth area; they cannot cast spells or use verbal abilities for 5s
  - Compound Words      | Passive/Utility  | You can write two-word combinations for enhanced effects — FIRE + SLOW = HEATSTROKE (deals fire damage AND slows); SHIELD + HEAVY = FORTIFY (stronger shield)

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Write: DEATH        | Attack   | Inscribe the word DEATH — deals a large burst of dark damage; against enemies below 25% HP, DEATH deals triple damage as the word resonates with their condition
  - Write: HASTE        | Buff/Movement     | Inscribe the word HASTE on an ally; they gain 40% attack and movement speed for 8s as the word empowers their motion
  - Living Grammar      | Passive  | Written words persist for 3s after being cast — enemies who walk through the lingering script take the word's effect as well (FIRE lingers as a damaging zone briefly)

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Write: NEGATE       | Active   | Inscribe the word NEGATE over an enemy's active ability; cancel it mid-use — can interrupt even powerful channeled abilities; costs 5 Ink
  - Sentence of Power   | Active   | Write a full sentence (3 words combined) over 2s; a grammatically complete magical sentence has 200% the power of individual words — complex but devastating

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Final Word      | Active   | Spend 15 Ink and 3s to inscribe a cosmic command across the sky — write one of three ultimate words: "STOP" (all enemies are Frozen for 6s), "FALL" (all enemies are knocked prone and take fall damage), or "END" (all enemies below 30% HP are instantly killed by the absolute authority of the word)

>> Your notes on Calligrapher:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: ERASURE (Unmake / Delete Reality)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Uses a special destructive ink to erase things from existence — removing enemy buffs, deleting terrain features, erasing parts of enemies' bodies, and ultimately threatening to delete targets entirely.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Erase Buff          | Active   | Draw an erasure stroke through target's buff icons (metaphysically); remove 1 buff from any enemy
  - Blot               | Attack   | Apply a large ink blot to target; deal moderate damage as the blot erases a patch of their outer layer — also reduces their armor by 10% for 6s
  - Deletion Passive    | Passive  | Your erasure effects cannot be resisted by targets below 50% HP; the weakened have less reality to hold onto

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Erase Terrain       | Active   | Draw erasure strokes over a terrain feature (wall, pillar, cover object); delete it from existence permanently — this opens paths, removes cover, and disrupts enemy positioning
  - Partial Deletion    | Attack   | Erase part of target's body — randomly remove a limb equivalent effect: erased leg (cannot move for 3s), erased arm (cannot attack for 3s), erased eyes (Blind for 4s)
  - Reality Smudge      | Debuff   | Smear reality around target; they become partially erased — they take 20% more damage as their physical form is compromised for 8s

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Mass Erasure        | Active   | Draw sweeping erasure strokes across a 6m area; remove all buffs from all enemies in range and deal light damage as their reinforcing effects are deleted
  - Erase Identity      | Debuff   | Attempt to erase target's sense of self; they forget one of their abilities for 6s (randomly selected) — that ability becomes greyed out and unusable
  - Undraw              | Reactive | When an enemy summons a creature or creates a construct, use Undraw as a reaction to immediately erase the summon before it can act; costs 4 Ink

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Erasure       | Attack   | Channel for 2s erasing larger and larger portions of target; at channel end deal massive damage equal to 30% of target's max HP as a significant portion of them is simply removed from reality — does not kill but is very difficult to recover from
  - Erase the Moment    | Active   | Retroactively erase the last 2s of combat for a target — undo all buffs they gained, all healing they received, and all cooldowns they reset in those 2 seconds; costs 8 Ink

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The White Canvas    | Active   | For 10s, wield absolute erasure authority — erase all terrain cover in the battlefield instantly, all enemy buffs are continuously deleted every 2s as fast as they can apply them, Partial Deletion hits all enemies simultaneously every 3s, and for the final 2s channel a Complete Erasure on one target that deals damage equal to 60% of their max HP as most of their physical form ceases to exist

>> Your notes on Erasure:
>>
>>

################################################################################
CLASS 26: OATHKEEPER (Holy Warrior)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A warrior bound to a sacred oath whose power scales with the purity of their conviction — they fight not for themselves but for something larger, and the divine responds to that selflessness with radiant fire, inexorable judgment, and ultimately the willingness to sacrifice everything so that others may live.

ARCHETYPE A: RADIANCE (Holy Damage / Burn Undead)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Channels holy light as an offensive weapon, with abilities dealing dramatically increased damage to undead and shadow entities while maintaining respectable damage against all enemies through stacking Radiance.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Holy Strike         | Attack   | Charge a melee strike with radiant energy; deals physical + holy damage and generates 1 Radiance stack (max 10; each stack = 3% holy damage bonus)
  - Sacred Flame        | Attack   | Launch a projectile of holy fire; deals fire + holy damage; against undead deals triple damage
  - Light of the Oath   | Passive  | Whenever you attack an undead, shadow, or evil-aligned enemy, gain 2 Radiance stacks instead of 1

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Radiant Burst       | Attack   | Release stored Radiance; spend 5 stacks to release a burst dealing heavy holy damage to all nearby enemies — undead take 50% bonus damage from the burst
  - Consecrated Ground  | Active   | Spend 3 Radiance stacks; create a 6m area of holy ground for 10s — undead cannot enter, and all allies inside gain 10% damage as holy bonus damage
  - Blinding Light      | Active   | Release a flash of radiance; all enemies in 8m are Blinded for 2s from the overwhelming holy light

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Holy Condemnation   | Debuff   | Mark target as Condemned by the holy oath; they take 25% more holy damage for 8s and cannot be healed by dark or shadow sources
  - Radiant Armor       | Aura     | Aura: emit radiant light — all allies within 6m gain 10% holy damage and 15% resistance to dark/shadow damage; undead adjacent to you take ongoing holy burn
  - Smite              | Attack   | Spend all Radiance stacks; fire a direct divine smite dealing 25 holy damage per stack consumed — guaranteed crit against undead

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Solar Nova          | Active   | For 3s, become a living star — constant AoE holy damage radiating from you in a 10m radius; undead caught in the nova take 10x damage per second
  - Oath's Fury         | Passive  | When any ally is killed, immediately gain 10 Radiance stacks and a 20% damage increase for 10s — the oath demands retribution

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Divine Revelation   | Active   | For 12s, become a vessel of pure divine radiance — Radiance stacks are permanently maxed, all attacks deal full holy damage to any enemy type (not just undead), Consecrated Ground covers the entire battlefield, and undead enemies must spend 2s of animation before taking any action as the holy aura overwhelms their animation

>> Your notes on Radiance:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: JUDGMENT (Mark and Execute)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Marks enemies with a divine Judgment that builds a sentence against them — the more evidence gathered through combat actions, the more devastating the eventual execution strike.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Mark of Judgment    | Debuff   | Brand a target with the Mark of Judgment; they accumulate Verdict stacks from subsequent attacks (1 Verdict per hit on marked target)
  - Prosecute           | Attack   | Strike a Marked target; deals normal damage and applies 2 Verdict stacks — designed to rapidly build the case
  - Just Cause          | Passive  | Your first hit on a Marked target each combat encounter always applies 3 Verdict stacks (establishing grounds for judgment)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Charge the Mark     | Active   | Apply 3 Verdict stacks directly without attacking; the evidence mounts through divine observation
  - Verdict: Guilty     | Active   | When target has 8+ Verdict stacks, declare Guilty — deal burst damage equal to 15 per Verdict stack consumed and reduce their armor by 30% for 6s
  - Double Jeopardy     | Passive  | After a target is found Guilty once, all subsequent Verdict stacks applied to them deal 50% of their judgment damage immediately as they are already in the divine record

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Mass Indictment     | Active   | Simultaneously apply 2 Verdict stacks to all enemies within 10m; divine law reaches broadly
  - Sentence: Death     | Active   | Requires 15 Verdict stacks; instantly kill target if they are below 25% HP — the sentence is carried out; if above 25% HP, deal 40% max HP damage instead
  - Judge and Jury      | Passive  | You gain Verdict stacks against yourself for damage you absorb protecting allies (from Martyrdom cross-training or any protective ability) — your sacrifice is evidence of righteousness, enhancing your next judgment

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Divine Tribunal     | Active   | Summon a spiritual court for 8s; for the duration, all attacks on marked targets generate double Verdict stacks and the execution threshold for Sentence: Death increases to 40% HP
  - Condemn             | Debuff   | Apply a permanent Condemnation for this encounter; the target's Verdict stacks can never decrease (normally they halve on Guilty verdict) — evidence is eternal

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Final Judgment  | Active   | Trigger the ultimate divine reckoning — apply 20 Verdict stacks to every enemy on the battlefield simultaneously, then over 5s call down individual divine judgments on each enemy in sequence, dealing damage equal to 20 per stack to each; enemies killed by the Judgment cannot be revived by any means

>> Your notes on Judgment:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: MARTYRDOM (Sacrifice Self for Allies)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Intercepts harm intended for allies and converts their own suffering into divine power — the more damage they absorb, the more powerfully they protect and empower their team.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Shield of the Oath  | Reactive | Interpose yourself between an attack and a nearby ally; absorb up to 30% of the incoming damage — the redirected damage generates 1 Martyr Point per 10 damage absorbed
  - Burden Bearer       | Passive  | Passively redirect 15% of all damage any nearby ally takes to yourself; this generates Martyr Points at the standard rate
  - Stoic Endurance     | Passive/Defense  | You gain 10% bonus maximum HP from your oath's reinforcement; your commitment to martyrdom strengthens your body

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Holy Transfer       | Heal     | Spend 5 Martyr Points; convert accumulated suffering into healing — restore HP to target ally equal to 150% of the Martyr Points spent
  - Selfless Aegis      | Buff/Shield     | Spend 3 Martyr Points; grant target ally a divine shield that absorbs a large amount of damage — the aegis is fueled by your suffering
  - Pain into Power     | Passive  | Each time you absorb 50 damage for allies, gain a stacking buff (1% bonus damage, max 20 stacks) — your self-sacrifice fuels righteous fury

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Absorb Debuff       | Cleanse  | Transfer one debuff from target ally to yourself; gain bonus Martyr Points equal to the debuff's severity
  - Radiant Suffering   | Passive/Heal  | While below 30% HP, all Martyrdom abilities cost 50% fewer Martyr Points and healing provided is 50% more effective — near death, you serve best
  - Holy Transference   | Buff/Shield     | Spend 8 Martyr Points; grant target ally 30% of your current HP as a temporary bonus HP pool for 8s

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Take My Place       | Active   | Spend 10 Martyr Points; completely swap HP percentage with a nearby ally — if they are at 10% HP and you are at 80%, they jump to 80% and you drop to 10%; you accept their suffering
  - Undying Oath        | Passive/Defense  | If you would die while absorbing damage for an ally, instead survive at 1 HP; this triggers once per combat and generates 20 Martyr Points from the near-death experience

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Great Sacrifice | Active   | Spend all Martyr Points (minimum 20) and reduce yourself to 1 HP voluntarily; in exchange, all allies are fully healed to maximum HP, all debuffs are cleansed from allies, all allies gain a divine aegis equal to 50% of their max HP, and all allies receive a 30% damage bonus for 15s from the power of your sacrifice — you cannot die for 5s after activating this, giving you time to recover

>> Your notes on Martyrdom:
>>
>>

################################################################################
CLASS 27: HEXBLADE (Cursed Weapon Fighter)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A warrior whose weapon is not merely a tool but an entity — cursed, hungry, and alive with dark purpose. They have entered into a pact with a weapon that feeds on death and grows with each kill, while projecting spectral blades and heaping calamitous hexes on those who face them.

ARCHETYPE A: HUNGERING BLADE (Lifesteal / Growth)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: The blade grows in power as it feeds on enemies' life force — kills permanently increase its stats for the encounter while lifesteal sustains the wielder through aggressive offense.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Hungry Strike       | Attack   | Strike with the hungering blade; deals weapon damage and heals you for 8% of damage dealt (lifesteal)
  - Blood Feeding       | Passive  | On each kill, the blade feeds — permanently gain 2% weapon damage for this encounter (max 40 kills = 80% bonus)
  - Voracious           | Passive/Heal  | Lifesteal from Hungry Strike increases to 12% while below 50% HP; the blade fights harder when its host is threatened

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Drain Strike        | Attack   | Perform a deep, draining slash; deals heavy damage and heals for 20% of damage dealt — higher lifesteal but slower attack
  - Blade Hunger        | Active   | Activate the blade's feeding frenzy for 6s; all lifesteal is doubled and each strike heals for a bonus equal to 2% of target's max HP regardless of damage dealt
  - Living Weapon       | Passive  | The blade becomes partially autonomous — it occasionally attacks (once per 5s) for a free auto-attack that deals 50% weapon damage and triggers lifesteal

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Soul Drink          | Attack   | On kill, perform a soul drain — restore 15% of your max HP instantly from the departing soul; can only trigger on the killing blow
  - Overfed             | Passive/Shield  | When healed beyond max HP via lifesteal, gain a temporary HP buffer (overheal can accumulate up to 20% of max HP as a temporary shield)
  - Hunger Madness      | Buff/Risk   | Temporarily lose control of the blade — for 5s it attacks automatically at triple speed but hits the nearest target (friend or foe); lifesteal is quadrupled during madness

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Feast Mode          | Active   | The blade demands a feast; for 10s all damage you deal is converted to 100% lifesteal (you heal instead of dealing direct damage — but the healing is enormous and the targets still take damage); 90s cooldown
  - Satiated Power      | Passive  | When at maximum HP (or with full overheal buffer), all blade attacks deal 30% bonus damage from the well-fed, empowered state of the weapon

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Infinite Hunger | Active   | Surrender fully to the blade's hunger for 12s — the blade enters an absolute feeding state: all lifesteal is at 100% efficiency, Blood Feeding stacks gain at 3x speed, all kills restore 25% max HP, and the blade extends your melee range to 4m as it reaches outward for more sustenance; when the state ends, the blade's permanent bonus is locked in at its current value

>> Your notes on Hungering Blade:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: CURSE MARK (Hex Debuffs)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Inscribes Hex Marks on enemies that stack into increasingly severe curse states — building a catalog of unique hexes that interact and compound as more are applied simultaneously.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Hex: Weakness       | Debuff   | Brand a Weakness hex on target; reduce their physical damage output by 15% for 8s
  - Hex: Slowmark       | Debuff   | Brand a Slowmark hex on target; reduce their movement speed by 30% for 8s
  - Curse Reader        | Passive  | You can see all active hexes on all enemies at a glance; hexes you apply have 20% longer duration

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Hex: Vulnerability  | Debuff   | Brand a Vulnerability hex; target takes 20% more damage from all sources for 8s
  - Hex: Misfortune     | Debuff   | Brand a Misfortune hex; target has 25% chance for any ability they use to have a random negative side effect (miss, deal less damage, affect themselves)
  - Compound Curse      | Passive  | Each additional hex applied to a target with 2+ existing hexes applies at 130% effectiveness; hexes compound on each other

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Hex: Ruin           | Debuff   | Brand a Ruin hex; reduce all of target's stats by 15% for 8s (comprehensive debilitation)
  - Hex: Unraveling     | Debuff   | Brand an Unraveling hex; the target's max HP decreases by 2% per second for 6s as the curse degrades their vitality ceiling
  - Curse Explosion     | Active   | Consume all hexes on a target simultaneously; deal burst damage equal to 20 per hex consumed and stun for 0.5s per hex consumed

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Grand Hex           | Active   | Apply 3 hexes of your choice simultaneously as a single action; the combined curse deals immediate damage equal to 10 per hex applied
  - The Withering       | Passive  | Enemies with 4+ hexes suffer The Withering — they take ongoing curse damage equal to 3% max HP per second just from the weight of accumulated misfortune

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Curse      | Active   | Brand target with the ultimate curse — apply all 6 hex types simultaneously, all at double potency, and for 10s any hex that expires immediately reapplies at half potency automatically; the target is a walking catastrophe of layered misfortune for the encounter

>> Your notes on Curse Mark:
>>
