ARCHETYPE C: Mutagen Brewer (Self-Buff Potions, Mutation, Side Effects)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A reckless self-experimenter who drinks their own dangerously unstable creations to
mutate their body for combat — gaining extraordinary powers with unpredictable side effects.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Iron Hide Serum    | Buff/Defense     | Drink a skin-hardening mutagen; gain +20% physical damage reduction for 10 seconds but move 15% slower as muscles stiffen.
  - Adrenaline Spike   | Buff/Risk     | Inject a combat stimulant; gain +25% attack speed and +15% movement speed for 8 seconds; after it wears off, suffer 5 seconds of -10% attack speed as withdrawal.
  - Metabolic Surge    | Passive/Utility  | Your mutagens metabolize faster than normal; all self-buff potions last 25% longer and their negative side effects are 10% less severe.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Hemotoxin Cocktail | Buff/Risk     | Drink a blood-altering formula; your attacks inject hemotoxin into enemies on hit for 8 seconds, dealing damage over time; side effect: you lose 3% max HP per attack you make.
  - Muscle Hypertrophy | Transform| Rapidly grow your muscles to abnormal size for 10 seconds; physical damage doubles and you gain knockback immunity, but you cannot use ranged attacks or fine-motor skills (items, complex spells) during the mutation.
  - Adaptive Serum     | Passive/Utility  | Your body partially adapts to side effects the more potions you drink in a fight; each potion consumed reduces the severity of the next potion's side effect by 10% (max 50% reduction).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Ectofluid Extract  | Buff/Risk     | Drink a phase-shifting solution; for 8 seconds, dodge through solid objects and enemy melee attacks; side effect: gain 20% vulnerability to all damage during the phasing period.
  - Venom Hyper-Gland  | Toggle   | Toggle on: mutate venom glands into your hands; melee and close-range attacks deal bonus poison damage; toggle off to revert; while active, you cannot hold certain items and take 2% max HP per second.
  - Overdose Protocol  | Active   | Chug three potions simultaneously for massive stacked effects — triple the potency of three chosen buffs, but also triple all side effects; gain the Overdose Immunity passive (cannot die from side effects alone for 5 seconds).

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Perfect Mutation   | Transform| Carefully brew and consume an engineered mutation for 15 seconds: gain +50% to all stats, +30% damage, flight capability, and a unique aura that poisons nearby enemies passively; side effect: the mutation begins degrading at 10 seconds, causing random stat fluctuations in the final 5 seconds.
  - Chimera Serum      | Transform| Drink a cocktail of three random mutagens simultaneously; receive three random strong mutations from the pool — the combination is unpredictable but explosively powerful; side effects are combined and equally unpredictable; lasts 12 seconds.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Philosopher's Flesh| Transform| Consume the ultimate self-mutagen — a formula so advanced it rewrites your biology for 20 seconds: all your physical attributes become superhuman (+100% to all combat stats), you regenerate 5% max HP per second, attacks chain to adjacent targets, and you develop an aura that accelerates all nearby allies' healing by 50%; the mutation is too perfect for side effects — but when it ends, you revert completely to baseline stats for 10 seconds with no buffs or passives active as your body re-establishes homeostasis.

################################################################################
CLASS 36: Duelist (Fencing/Precision Melee)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A master of the blade's dialogue, the Duelist treats every fight as a conversation —
reading the opponent's intent, answering each advance with elegant precision, and deciding the
duel not with brute force but with superior timing, deception, and ironclad technique.

ARCHETYPE A: Riposte Master (Counter-Attack, Parry, Punish)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A reactive melee specialist who wins fights by enduring the enemy's aggression and
delivering devastating punitive counterstrikes at precisely the right moment.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - En Parade          | Reactive | Parry an incoming melee attack (0.5s window); the attack deals no damage and grants you 1 Riposte charge.
  - Repoussez          | Attack   | Spend 1 Riposte charge to deliver a precise counter-thrust dealing 200% weapon damage with bonus armor penetration (25%).
  - Bladework Rhythm   | Passive/Defense  | Successive parries within 6 seconds of each other cost 10% less reaction time (wider parry window each time, max 3 stacks).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Parry and Punish   | Reactive | After a successful parry, your follow-up attack within 2 seconds deals an additional 50% bonus damage and briefly stumbles the attacker (0.5-second delay to their next action).
  - Guard Break        | Attack   | Spend 1 Riposte charge on a sharp smashing bind that destroys an enemy's defensive stance, reducing their parry/block effectiveness by 50% for 5 seconds.
  - Contretemps        | Passive  | If you successfully parry a skill or special ability (not just basic attacks), you gain 2 Riposte charges instead of 1 and immediately reduce the skill's cooldown for the enemy by 0 (they cannot use it again for 4 seconds).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Perfect Riposte    | Attack   | Spend 2 Riposte charges for an absolute counter-attack: deals 350% weapon damage, ignores all armor, and cannot be interrupted, dodged, or blocked; only usable within 1.5 seconds of a parry.
  - Iron Wrist         | Passive/Defense  | You can parry magical and ranged attacks: parrying a ranged projectile deflects it back at the attacker; parrying a magical strike absorbs it for 1 Riposte charge and negates all damage.
  - Counter Momentum   | Passive  | Each consecutive parry without being struck yourself increases your next attack's speed by 15% (max 5 stacks); taking damage resets the counter.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Maestro's Answer   | Reactive | When an enemy uses a skill or charged attack, you may enter a perfect counter-window (1-second anticipation window visible as a shimmer); if you attack during this window, you deal 500% weapon damage and negate the entire incoming skill — effectively countering a powerful ability with a single strike.
  - Unbroken Guard     | Passive/Immunity  | For the first 10 seconds of combat, you cannot be staggered, knocked down, or interrupted; all attacks against you during this window automatically proc En Parade without requiring manual activation.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Final Lesson   | Channel  | Enter a 1-second anticipation stance, then for 8 seconds become completely untouchable by countered mechanics — every melee and ranged attack aimed at you is automatically parried (no input required) and each parry automatically triggers a free Perfect Riposte without consuming charges; at the end, release all stored parry energy in a single annihilating thrust that deals 100% weapon damage per parry accumulated.

>> Your notes on Riposte Master:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Feint Artist (Misdirection, Crit Setup, Vulnerability Windows)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A deceptive striker who creates false openings, baits enemy defenses out of position,
and exploits the inevitable moment of confusion to land devastating critical hits.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Feint              | Active   | Fake a strike to the left or right; if the enemy flinches (their defensive AI reacts or they manually dodge), your next attack within 1.5 seconds deals 150% damage and has +30% crit chance.
  - Tell-Read          | Passive/Utility  | You can read enemy attack telegraphs; enemies with long windup attacks are marked with a glow visible to only you, giving you a 0.3-second early warning.
  - Quick Draw         | Attack   | A deceptively fast draw-and-strike that deals moderate damage with +20% crit chance; enemies expecting a slow heavy attack are caught off guard.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Double Feint       | Active   | Fake two directions in rapid succession; if the enemy dodges both phantom strikes, your true third strike deals 250% damage and crits automatically.
  - Exposed Weakness   | Debuff   | After landing a crit, your reading of the enemy's form reveals a structural weakness; the target takes +20% damage from your next 3 attacks.
  - Blade Flash        | Stealth  | Flash your blade to briefly blind the enemy for 0.8 seconds; during their blindness, all your attacks hit automatically and crit if you attack from behind.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Grand Illusion     | Active   | Create an afterimage that walks forward while you sidestep; the afterimage absorbs the enemy's next attack, and your real position gets a guaranteed backstab crit (300% damage) when they commit to hitting the image.
  - Coup de Grace Setup| Passive  | Any enemy you feinted in the last 4 seconds takes +35% crit damage from you; this bonus is visible to you (the weakness glow deepens).
  - Phantom Step       | Movement | A feinted movement step — you appear to dash left but actually teleport right; useful for repositioning behind enemies or escaping AoEs while baiting enemy responses.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Misdirection | Active   | For 6 seconds enter a constant feinting rhythm: every 1.5 seconds automatically create a feint that baits enemy AI or forces defensive reactions; each successful bait increases your next actual attack's damage by 25% (stacks); at the end, all stacks release in a single overwhelming strike.
  - Thousand Cuts Setup| Passive  | Each time you crit, apply a stacking Vulnerability marker (max 10 stacks); at 10 stacks, trigger a Pressure Burst that deals 50% of all accumulated damage dealt in the last 10 seconds as a single simultaneous hit.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Perfect Feint  | Active   | Perform the ultimate feint — your entire body vanishes from enemy perception for 2 seconds while you reposition freely (full Stealth); at the moment of your choosing, materialize from an impossible angle and deliver a strike that deals 600% weapon damage as a guaranteed critical hit; this attack ignores all damage reduction, cannot be blocked or dodged, and applies Exposed Weakness at double stacks.

>> Your notes on Feint Artist:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: En Garde (Stance-Switching, Adaptive Style, Combat Tempo)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A dynamic combatant who constantly cycles through four fencing stances, each with
different strengths, and generates powerful combo bonuses for fluid stance transitions.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Offensive Form     | Stance   | Enter Offensive Stance: +20% attack speed, +15% damage; lose 10% damage reduction.
  - Defensive Form     | Stance   | Enter Defensive Stance: +25% damage reduction, +30% parry window; lose 15% attack speed.
  - Form Flow          | Passive/Utility  | Switching stances generates 1 Form Token; Form Tokens enhance subsequent attacks (max 5).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Mobile Form        | Stance   | Enter Mobile Stance: +30% movement speed, all attacks have a 20% chance to proc a free second strike; lose 10% base damage.
  - Bind Form          | Stance   | Enter Bind Stance: slow and powerful attacks deal +40% damage but have a 30% chance to lock your weapon against a blocking enemy's guard (neither can move for 0.5 seconds before resolution).
  - Transition Mastery | Passive/Utility  | Each stance transition within 3 seconds of the last restores 1 Form Token and reduces all current skill cooldowns by 0.5 seconds; combos of 3+ transitions in 6 seconds grant a free attack.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Form Synergy       | Passive  | Certain stance pairs unlock combination bonuses: Offensive + Mobile = Flurry Mode (+40% attack speed for 4 seconds); Defensive + Bind = Immovable Wall (cannot be moved or knocked back for 5 seconds); Offensive + Bind = Executioner (next attack deals 250% damage); Mobile + Defensive = Ghost Step (free Phantom Step, no cooldown).
  - Read the Room      | Active   | Rapidly assess the battlefield and automatically switch to the ideal stance for the current situation (game selects optimal stance based on enemy types and your HP); grants 3 Form Tokens for the perfect read.
  - Tempo Control      | Passive  | Your stance switches themselves become attacks — each stance transition within 2m of an enemy causes a blade flash (light, guaranteed-hit slash) dealing 50% weapon damage.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Cadence            | Active   | Enter a 6-second optimal dueling cadence: automatically cycle through all four stances every 1.5 seconds; during each stance window, attacks have perfect bonus stacking; Form Tokens generate at 2x rate; all Tempo Control blade flashes deal double damage.
  - Master's Sequence  | Active   | Execute a perfect pre-designed 4-hit combo: Offensive (heavy strike) -> Mobile (flanking slash) -> Bind (crushing lock) -> Defensive (parry-riposte); each hit deals 200% weapon damage in the sequence's context, and completing the full sequence grants a massive damage buff for 8 seconds.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - L'Art de l'Escrime | Transform| For 15 seconds, transcend individual stances and exist in all four simultaneously — you adapt your fighting style on a per-attack basis with no transition delay; each attack is perfectly optimized (maximum damage, parry windows active, mobility unrestricted, and binding power available simultaneously); Form Tokens are irrelevant as all bonuses are permanently at max; you fight as the perfect expression of the dueling art, and every 3rd attack during this form triggers a perfect automatic Riposte as if a parry had just been performed.

>> Your notes on En Garde:
>>
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON DUELIST]
>
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 37: Berserker (Rage/Primal Fury)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A warrior who has torn away civilization's restraints and embraced the screaming void
beneath — the Berserker transforms pain into power, movement into devastation, and their own
voice into a weapon that bends the will of lesser creatures to breaking.

ARCHETYPE A: Blood Fury (Damage Scales With Lost HP, Desperation Power)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A high-risk predator whose effectiveness inversely mirrors their health — the more
punishment they absorb, the more catastrophic their retaliation becomes.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Bloodletting        | Passive  | For every 10% of max HP you are missing, gain 5% bonus physical damage (max 50% at 10% HP remaining).
  - Pain Conversion     | Passive  | 20% of all damage you take is converted to Fury; Fury regenerates Rage resource and increases attack power by 1% per 10 Fury gained this combat (max 20%).
  - Wound Pride         | Attack   | Slash yourself for 5% of max HP to enter a frenzied state for 3 seconds where all your attacks deal bonus bleed damage equal to 50% of your self-inflicted wound.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Berserker's Bargain | Active   | Reduce your own max HP by 15% permanently for this combat; in exchange, immediately gain 30% damage increase and 20% attack speed that also scales with Bloodletting.
  - Crimson Surge       | Attack   | A massive strike fueled by desperation; deals 150% weapon damage plus 3% of your MISSING HP as bonus damage; most powerful at 10-20% HP.
  - Vital Hemorrhage    | Passive/Heal  | Your bleeds no longer tick away harmlessly — they now feed you; 25% of all bleed damage you inflict is returned to you as Fury, and at 50+ Fury you gain 1% HP regeneration per second.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Last Breath Surge   | Reactive | Triggered automatically when you drop below 15% HP: immediately deal a retaliatory strike to every enemy in 5m for 200% weapon damage and enter a 5-second enrage where Bloodletting is doubled; 60-second cooldown.
  - Martyr's Blade      | Attack   | Spend 10% of current HP to charge this strike for 1 second; the attack deals damage equal to 500% of the HP spent plus normal weapon damage — at low HP this is brutally efficient.
  - Hemorrhagic Frenzy  | Toggle   | Toggle on: your attacks stack Hemorrhage on enemies (5 stacks = spontaneous rupture dealing massive damage) but you take 1% max HP per second from sympathetic bleeding; the rupture damage scales with your missing HP.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Death's Door Mastery| Passive/Immunity  | While below 20% HP, you are immune to fear, CC effects, and knockback; your movement speed increases by 30%; and all Blood Fury damage bonuses are treated as if you are at 5% HP regardless of actual HP.
  - Undying Rage        | Reactive | Once per combat when you would be killed, survive with 1 HP and enter Undying Rage for 8 seconds: during Undying Rage you cannot die (damage reduces you to 1 HP only), all Bloodletting bonuses are at maximum, and every kill you make heals you for 15% max HP.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Crimson God         | Transform| For 12 seconds enter the ultimate Blood Fury form: your HP is frozen at its current value (cannot drop further), all missing HP bonuses are calculated as if you have 1 HP remaining (maximum Bloodletting), and your attacks deal additional damage equal to 1% of your MAX HP per hit; each kill during this form triggers a blood nova dealing 150% weapon damage to all enemies in 5m; when the form ends, take damage equal to all the HP that would have been lost during the form.

>> Your notes on Blood Fury:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Warpath (Charge, Cleave, Unstoppable Forward Momentum)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: An unstoppable offensive freight train who builds momentum through forward movement and
chain cleaving, rewarding aggression and punishing anything that dares stand in the way.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Bull Rush           | Movement | Charge forward in a straight line up to 8m; deal moderate damage to the first enemy hit and knock them back 3m.
  - Cleave              | Attack   | A wide horizontal sweep hitting all enemies in a 180-degree arc in front of you for 80% weapon damage each.
  - Momentum            | Passive  | Each time you move at full sprint speed for 1+ seconds before attacking, the attack deals 25% bonus damage; standing still for more than 2 seconds resets the bonus.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Titan Charge        | Movement | Charge up to 12m with unstoppable momentum; cannot be stopped or redirected mid-charge; deal 150% weapon damage to the primary target and 75% to all enemies within 2m of the impact point.
  - Whirlwind           | Attack   | Spin in place dealing 60% weapon damage to all enemies within 4m repeatedly for 2 seconds (approximately 4 hits); you can move slowly while spinning.
  - Battering Ram       | Passive  | Your charges destroy destructible cover, push back all enemies in your path (not just the primary target), and deal bonus damage proportional to the distance charged (5% per meter, max 50%).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Warpath Stride      | Stance   | Enter Warpath Stance: you cannot be stopped, slowed, or knocked back while moving; all movement-based attacks deal 40% bonus damage; attacks you make while moving gain cleave range (hit 2m wider arc); lasts until you stop moving for 2 seconds.
  - Chain Charge        | Movement | Charge at one target; on hit immediately charge the next nearest enemy within 8m for 75% damage; continue chaining up to 3 times with diminishing range but stacking damage (+25% per chain); cannot hit the same target twice.
  - Seismic Slam        | Attack   | Leap into the air and crash down with both fists; creates a shockwave in a 6m radius dealing 200% weapon damage and knocking enemies prone for 1.5 seconds; generates massive Momentum for the next attack.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Unstoppable Force   | Passive  | While in motion, you generate a physical force aura — enemies within 2m of your path while you move take moderate impact damage per second just from proximity; walls, doors, and thin barriers are smashed through automatically.
  - Rampage             | Active   | Enter a 10-second rampage state where Bull Rush, Chain Charge, and Titan Charge all reset their cooldowns every 2.5 seconds; during Rampage you must keep charging — if you stand still for more than 1 second, the rampage ends prematurely.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Living Avalanche| Transform| For 15 seconds, become a force of nature — your size increases by 30%, you gain complete immunity to all CC and physics effects, and every step you take deals AoE damage in a 3m radius; charge cooldowns become 1 second; each charge deals 300% weapon damage and travels through obstacles; enemies killed by charges explode dealing 100% of their max HP as physical damage to nearby survivors; the battlefield itself trembles — terrain features crack and collapse under your passage.

>> Your notes on Warpath:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Primal Howl (Fear, Intimidation, Presence Aura)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A psychological warfare specialist who weaponizes primal terror — routing weak enemies
outright, degrading the resolve of stronger ones, and sustaining an aura of intimidation that
makes every enemy on the battlefield fight at a permanent disadvantage.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Battle Cry         | Active   | Unleash a terrifying war cry in a 6m radius; enemies with low morale flee for 2 seconds (fear), all others suffer -15% attack damage for 5 seconds from intimidation.
  - Primal Presence    | Aura     | Passive aura: all enemies within 5m permanently suffer -10% damage and -10% accuracy from your overwhelming physical dominance.
  - Taunt              | CC       | Single-target intimidation that forces one enemy to attack only you for 4 seconds; while taunted, the enemy attacks with -20% effectiveness.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Howl of Terror     | Active   | A piercing primal howl that travels 15m; all enemies in range must make a morale check — weaker enemies flee for 4 seconds, mid-tier enemies are stunned for 1.5 seconds, strong enemies suffer -20% to all stats for 6 seconds.
  - Pack Breaker       | Passive/Utility  | When you fear at least 2 enemies simultaneously, the fear spreads to adjacent non-feared enemies within 4m of the fleeing ones (mob psychology); the secondary fear lasts 1.5 seconds.
  - Imposing Stature   | Passive/Utility  | Your Primal Presence aura radius increases to 8m; enemies who enter the aura for the first time in combat are briefly stunned for 0.5 seconds from shock.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Crushing Dominance | Debuff   | Target one enemy and psychologically crush them for 10 seconds; they cannot use morale-based or bravery-requiring skills (charges, big cooldowns), attack with -25% effectiveness, and suffer 30% increased damage from you.
  - Mass Rout          | Active   | Charge forward roaring; every enemy within 5m of your path has a 60% chance to immediately flee for 3 seconds; any that stand firm take 150% weapon damage from the charge; morale recovered enemies re-enter with 20% reduced stats.
  - Berserker's Legend | Passive/Utility  | Your reputation precedes you — at the start of combat, all enemies begin with reduced morale (fear thresholds lowered by 20%), making all your fear effects trigger more easily.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Alpha Presence     | Aura     | Upgrade your Primal Presence permanently for this combat: the aura now extends to 12m; enemies inside suffer -20% damage, -15% speed, and have a 10% chance to hesitate (delay their next action by 0.5 seconds) per attack they attempt.
  - War God's Roar     | Active   | Release a thunderous roar that simultaneously fears ALL enemies on the battlefield for 2 seconds and buffs ALL allies with +25% damage and immunity to fear for 8 seconds; the roar is so powerful it briefly shakes the terrain, knocking flying or jumping enemies out of the air.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Apex Predator      | Transform| For 15 seconds become the embodiment of primal terror — your Primal Presence aura fills the entire battlefield; all enemies permanently suffer -30% to all stats, cannot use morale-requiring abilities, and have a 25% chance to flinch (interrupt their own attacks) per hit they make; enemies who die while under your Apex Predator aura cannot be resurrected or rallied; your own allies gain +50% damage from witnessing your supremacy; when the form ends, release a final apocalyptic howl that resets all fear cooldowns and fears every surviving enemy for 3 seconds.

>> Your notes on Primal Howl:
>>
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON BERSERKER]
>
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 38: WARDEN OF CHAINS (Binding/Control)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A grim enforcer who weaponizes magical chains to lock down, punish, and isolate enemies, turning the battlefield into a web of inescapable restraints. The Warden does not kill — they make sure their targets wish they could.

ARCHETYPE A: SHACKLEMASTER (Single-Target Lockdown, Escalating Restraint)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Applies stacking restraint conditions to a single target, escalating from slowed to rooted to fully incapacitated.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Ankle Grab         | CC       | Hurl a chain at an enemy's feet, Slowing their movement speed by 40% for 4s.
  - Iron Cuff          | CC       | Bind an enemy's wrists, Silencing their ability use for 2s.
  - Weighted Links     | Passive/Utility  | Each chain effect applied to the same target within 8s stacks, increasing duration of the next effect by 0.5s per stack (max 3 stacks).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Pinning Strike     | CC       | Slam a chain spike into the ground beneath a target, Rooting them for 3s. Rooted targets cannot benefit from movement abilities.
  - Constriction       | Debuff   | Wrap chains around a target's torso, reducing their damage output by 25% and slowing their attack speed for 5s.
  - Chain Mastery      | Passive/Utility  | Slow, Silence, and Root applied to the same target within 10s automatically trigger Constriction with no action cost.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Spine Lock         | CC       | Coil a barbed chain around a target's spine. They are Stunned for 2s, then Rooted for 3s afterward — they cannot be cleansed of this effect more than once per combat.
  - Chokehold Shackle  | Channel  | Channel for up to 4s: a gripping chain keeps a target Silenced and Slowed. If the full channel completes, the target is also Stunned for 2s. Interrupted early still deals partial CC duration.
  - Break No Bonds     | Reactive | When an enemy attempts to cleanse or break a chain effect you applied, reflect the cleanse — they take 80% of the cleansed effect's remaining duration as direct damage instead.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Restraint    | CC       | Apply Slow, Silence, Root, and Stun simultaneously to one target for 2s each, staggered by 0.5s so they chain together seamlessly into 8s of continuous CC.
  - Warden's Mark      | Debuff   | Brand a target: for 12s, any time they attempt to use an ability or movement skill, they are Slowed for an additional 1.5s. If they are already Slowed, they are Rooted instead.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Eternal Sentence   | CC       | Encases one target in an absolute chain prison for 6s. They cannot move, act, use items, or be affected by ally abilities. Upon release, all chain stacks on the target explode, dealing damage equal to 40% of all damage they would have dealt during imprisonment.

>> Your notes on SHACKLEMASTER:
>> The escalating restraint stack from Weighted Links and Chain Mastery creates a satisfying ramp-up loop.
>> Break No Bonds is a strong anti-cleanse tool and rewards opponents who choose carefully about when to escape.
>> Eternal Sentence is a true kill-window tool — the delayed damage burst rewards coordinated team play.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: CHAIN LINK (Connect Enemies, Shared Damage)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Tethers multiple enemies together so that damage dealt to one propagates to all linked targets, punishing enemies for grouping or for separating too far.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Binding Tether     | CC       | Connect two nearby enemies with a magical chain. If they move more than 12m apart, both are Pulled toward each other and Stunned for 1s.
  - Pain Conduit       | Passive  | While two or more enemies are Linked, 20% of all damage dealt to one Linked enemy is also dealt to all others in the chain.
  - Taut Wire          | Debuff   | After placing a Binding Tether, both linked targets suffer -15% movement speed as long as the tether persists.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Resonant Strike    | Attack   | Strike one Linked enemy. The blow resonates through all chains, dealing 60% of the strike's damage to every other Linked enemy.
  - Triple Bind        | CC       | Extend a chain link to a third enemy, connecting all three. Any of the three being CC'd applies a 1s Slow to the other two.
  - Link Amplifier     | Passive  | Each enemy in a chain link beyond the first increases Pain Conduit's shared damage percentage by 5% (max +15% at 4 linked).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Sympathetic Agony  | Debuff   | Apply a debuff to one Linked enemy: for 8s, any debuff they receive is copied at 50% effectiveness to all Linked targets.
  - Chain Reaction     | Reactive | When a Linked enemy dies, the chain snaps violently — all remaining Linked enemies take 100% of the dead enemy's remaining HP as damage and are Stunned for 1.5s.
  - Tandem Torment     | Active   | Force all Linked enemies to take a synchronized action: they each perform a basic attack against themselves, dealing their own attack damage to themselves.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Grand Chain        | CC       | Link up to 5 enemies in a single sweeping chain. The chain persists for 10s and cannot be broken by movement. Linked enemies cannot use movement abilities while linked.
  - Collapse the Line  | Active   | Violently retract all active chains simultaneously, Pulling all Linked enemies to a central point and dealing 120% weapon damage to each. The collision stuns all of them for 2s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - One Suffers, All Suffer | Aura | For 12s, create an absolute pain network: 100% of all damage taken by any Linked enemy is replicated to every other Linked enemy (non-stacking). Any CC applied to one Linked enemy is simultaneously applied to all others at 75% duration. The chain cannot be broken during this window.

>> Your notes on CHAIN LINK:
>> Pain Conduit and Link Amplifier create a scaling AoE damage engine that rewards getting many targets linked.
>> Chain Reaction creates a snowball effect — killing one linked enemy can wipe the whole group.
>> One Suffers, All Suffer is a 12s window of absolute mayhem in group fights — devastating with a coordinated team.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: IMPRISONER (Cage/Contain/Isolate)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Creates physical chain constructs — cages, walls, and sealed zones — to trap enemies in place, isolate them from allies, and deny entire areas of the battlefield.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Chain Fence        | Utility  | Erect a wall of chains 6m wide that blocks movement and projectiles for 6s. Enemies can attack the wall to destroy it (200 HP).
  - Isolation Ring     | CC       | Surround a single enemy with a small chain barrier for 3s. They cannot be targeted by allies and cannot leave the ring.
  - Warden's Eye       | Passive  | Enemies inside any of your chain constructs (walls, rings, cages) take 10% increased damage from all sources.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Cage Drop          | CC       | Drop a prebuilt cage on a target location after a 0.5s delay. Any enemy caught inside is Imprisoned for 4s. The cage has 300 HP and can be destroyed to free the target.
  - Quarantine Zone    | Active   | Designate a 10m radius area. Chains erupt from the ground at the perimeter, creating an impassable barrier for enemies for 6s. Allies can freely pass through.
  - Chain Grid         | Passive  | Cages and Isolation Rings you place deal 20 damage per second to enemies inside them as the chain links tighten.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Solitary           | CC       | Bind a target in a full-body chain wrap for 5s. They are Silenced, Rooted, and cannot be targeted or assisted by enemy allies (including heals and buffs) for the duration.
  - Fortress of Chains | Active   | Instantly erect four Chain Fences in a square formation around your current position, creating an enclosed area. Lasts 10s.
  - The Black Cell     | Active   | Create an indestructible cage at a target location for 4s. The cage cannot be broken by any means — it simply expires. Anything caught inside is immune to outside effects (good or bad) for the duration.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Mass Incarceration | Active   | Launch chains outward in all directions from your position. Every enemy within 12m is pulled into a Cage Drop effect simultaneously. Each cage is individual and must be broken separately.
  - Lockdown Protocol  | Toggle   | Toggle on: all chain constructs you place persist twice as long, have double HP, and Imprisoned enemies inside them suffer a stacking -5% damage resistance per second (max -25%).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Grand Prison   | Active   | Erect a massive chain fortress (30m diameter) at a target location. For 15s, any enemy inside cannot leave (chains bar all exits), cannot receive outside heals or buffs, and takes 50 damage per second. Allies may enter and exit freely. At the end of the duration, the prison implodes — dealing 300% weapon damage to all enemies still inside.

>> Your notes on IMPRISONER:
>> Chain Grid and Warden's Eye synergize to turn cages into active damage zones, not just CC tools.
>> The Black Cell is a unique "untouchable" design — denies both damage AND healing, useful situationally.
>> The Grand Prison is a raid boss-tier capstone — it fundamentally controls a huge chunk of the battlefield for 15s.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON WARDEN OF CHAINS]
> The class plays as a pure CC/denial specialist and should be balanced around long cooldowns — every chain ability should feel like a major investment.
> The three archetypes cover three different scales: single-target lock (A), multi-target link (B), and area denial (C). Each has a distinct feel.
> Chain Link (B) has high group-fight value and should probably be restricted in PvP or have tuned-down sharing ratios for balance.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 39: MIMIC (Copy/Adaptation)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A fluid, unsettling combatant with no fixed form, the Mimic absorbs what it fights — stealing abilities from enemies, growing resistant to whatever hurts it, and permanently assimilating the essence of those it defeats.

ARCHETYPE A: COPYCAT (Steal Enemy Abilities, Temporary Mimicry)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Reads, steals, and weaponizes enemy abilities mid-combat, turning an opponent's own toolkit against them.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Mirror Scan        | Passive/Utility  | At the start of combat, passively scan all enemies within 20m — reveal one random ability from each enemy's kit as a "Learnable."
  - Quick Copy         | Active   | Steal one Learnable ability from a target enemy. Use it once before it expires. The enemy retains their ability but suffers a 3s cooldown on it.
  - Imitation Aura     | Aura     | While using a copied ability, gain +15% effectiveness for that ability (damage, healing, or CC duration) as your mimicry perfects the technique.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Deep Read          | Active   | Channel 1.5s on an enemy to expose all of their abilities as Learnables. The enemy is alerted when this happens.
  - Double Take        | Passive/Utility  | You may hold 2 copied abilities simultaneously instead of 1. Copied abilities last 30% longer.
  - Mockery            | Debuff   | When you use a copied ability against its original owner, they take 20% increased damage from that ability and suffer a 2s Silence.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Perfect Replica    | Active   | Copy an enemy's ability with full fidelity — gain all of its passive bonuses, enhancements, and augments the enemy has on it, not just the base effect. Lasts 20s.
  - Skill Drain        | Active   | Steal an ability outright for 10s — the enemy loses access to it entirely during that window, while you gain full use of it.
  - Mimic's Instinct   | Reactive | When struck by an ability, automatically Copy it as a Learnable (no action required) with a 10s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Repertoire         | Passive/Utility  | Increase your held copied abilities to 4 simultaneously. Each time you use a copied ability, reset 20% of its cooldown.
  - Turncoat Strike    | Attack   | Use any held copied ability, but it fires back at the original owner at 150% effectiveness, appearing to originate from them (confuses allied AI).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Complete Mimicry   | Transform| For 20s, become a perfect copy of a target enemy — gain all of their abilities, stats, and passives as if you were that character. You appear identical to them visually. Upon expiration, all abilities return to normal and the target is Stunned for 3s (having just fought alongside a copy of themselves).

>> Your notes on COPYCAT:
>> Skill Drain is the PvP standout — removing an ability from a boss or high-value enemy creates huge swings.
>> Mimic's Instinct makes the class reactive and rewards taking hits early to build a library of copies.
>> Complete Mimicry is a true shape-shifting capstone — best used to copy a tank and absorb punishment, or copy a healer to flip a fight.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: EVOLUTIONIST (Adapt Resistance to Damage Taken)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Learns from punishment — each time a damage type injures the Evolutionist, they develop increasing resistance to it, becoming harder to kill the longer a fight goes on.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Adaptive Skin      | Passive/Resistance  | The first time you take damage from any damage type in combat (fire, cold, lightning, physical, etc.), gain 5% resistance to that type permanently for the fight.
  - Pain Data          | Passive/Resistance  | Track a "Damage Log" — the last 3 damage types to hit you are displayed. Resistances to logged types increase by an additional 3%.
  - Reactive Tissue    | Passive/Resistance  | When you take more than 15% of your max HP in a single hit, automatically gain 8% resistance to that damage type (stacks with Adaptive Skin).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Stress Response    | Buff/Resistance     | Activate to force your body to process its current adaptations — double all current resistances for 6s, then reset them to base (no stacking bonus is lost, just temporarily doubled).
  - Immunological      | Passive/Immunity  | Status effects you have already been affected by (poison, burn, freeze, etc.) apply at 30% reduced duration the second time and beyond.
  - Hardened           | Passive/Defense  | After taking 3 hits of the same type, convert 5% of that resistance into a flat damage reduction (affects even "resistance-piercing" attacks).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Metabolize         | Active   | Consume an active debuff on yourself: remove it and gain 15% resistance to the damage type associated with it. (Burn = fire, Frost = cold, etc.)
  - Evolutionary Leap  | Active   | Instantly gain 20% resistance to a damage type of your choice, as if you had already been hit by it 4 times. 45s cooldown.
  - The Body Learns    | Passive/Resistance  | Resistances accumulated during combat persist after the fight ends, decaying by 2% per minute out of combat. Up to 25% of combat resistances can be permanently retained between encounters.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Overclock Adaptation | Buff/Resistance   | For 10s, every hit you take immediately triggers Adaptive Skin (normally once per type per combat) — you rapidly stack multiple resistances in a short window. Intentionally expose yourself.
  - Total Immunity     | Passive/Immunity  | After accumulating 40% or more resistance to a damage type, become completely immune to secondary effects of that type (no burns from fire, no slows from ice, etc.).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Apex Evolution     | Transform| Compress all resistances accumulated in this combat into your base form. For 15s, you become immune to every damage type you have taken at least one hit from this fight. Additionally, you gain an aura that reflects 25% of incoming damage of those types back to the attacker. After 15s, resistances reset to their pre-Apex values.

>> Your notes on EVOLUTIONIST:
>> The Body Learns is a cross-encounter progression tool — it rewards fighting the same enemy types repeatedly, fitting for dungeon runs.
>> Overclock Adaptation is a high-risk, high-reward ability — it works best when you have a healer.
>> Apex Evolution plays best mid-to-late combat. It's useless on the first hit but devastating after 30s of being punished.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: ASSIMILATOR (Absorb Defeated Enemy Traits Permanently)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Grows stronger with each enemy killed, permanently absorbing one trait, stat, or ability from every enemy it defeats — becoming a composite of everything it has slain.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Harvest            | Passive  | Upon killing an enemy, gain a "Trait Sample" from them — a minor version of one of their passive stats (e.g., +2 STR from a strong enemy, +5% crit from an assassin).
  - Sample Slot        | Passive/Utility  | You begin with 3 permanent Trait Sample slots. Samples collected beyond the slot limit replace the weakest held sample.
  - Trophy Hunt        | Passive  | Killing an enemy type for the first time ever grants a bonus Trait Sample with +50% potency compared to killing repeat enemy types.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Forced Assimilation | Active  | Instantly kill an enemy below 15% HP and immediately Assimilate their full passive trait set (as if Trophy Hunt triggered, regardless of whether you've killed that type before). 60s cooldown.
  - Sample Synthesis   | Active   | Merge two held Trait Samples into one combined trait — the new sample has the primary effect of both at 70% potency each, freeing up a slot.
  - Biomass            | Passive/Utility  | Increase Trait Sample slots to 5. Samples from Elite or Boss enemies count as 2 trait samples (occupying 2 slots, granting proportionally greater benefits).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Perfect Specimen   | Passive/Utility  | When you kill an enemy that has abilities (not just stats), you may absorb one of their active abilities as a permanent skill, replacing one of your own skill slots. You may only hold 1 absorbed active ability at a time.
  - Adaptation Chain   | Passive  | Each unique enemy type you have absorbed traits from grants +1% to all stats. (Rewards diversity of kills over grinding one type.)
  - Recessive Gene     | Active   | Reactivate a trait from any enemy you have ever assimilated, even ones no longer in your Sample slots, for 10s.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Apex Predator      | Passive  | Killing an enemy with more than 50% HP remaining grants double trait samples from them. Rewards aggressive, swift kills over attrition.
  - Living Archive     | Passive/Utility  | Expand your permanent Trait Slots to 10. Additionally, passively display your current absorbed trait list to your party — allies can request you activate specific traits to support the group.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Final Assimilation | Transform | Trigger on any kill: absorb the target's entire identity for 30s — temporarily gain their complete stat profile, all active abilities, all passive traits, and their HP as a temporary HP shield. When the duration ends, all their traits are permanently added at 30% potency to your sample pool (filling slots or replacing weaker entries). Can only trigger once per combat.

>> Your notes on ASSIMILATOR:
>> The permanent trait system requires careful tracking — ideally visualized in a dedicated UI panel for the player.
>> Perfect Specimen is the most powerful T3 in the archetype — absorbing an active ability is huge and may need a limited roster cap.
>> The Final Assimilation's temporary HP shield makes it viable as a lifesave in dire situations, not just a power-gain tool.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON MIMIC]
> The class lives and dies by combat variety — it becomes exponentially more powerful fighting diverse enemies rather than grinding one type.
> Copycat (A) is the PvP archetype. Evolutionist (B) is the solo/sustained dungeon archetype. Assimilator (C) is the long-campaign/roguelite archetype.
> Trophy Hunt and Adaptation Chain both explicitly reward fighting new enemy types — the game should present diverse encounters to make this class sing.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 40: STAR NAVIGATOR (Space/Stellar Sci-fi)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A traveler of the deep cosmos who harnesses the power of stellar physics — channeling plasma cores, reading constellations as tactical formations, and wielding the annihilating force of dark matter to unmake what exists.

ARCHETYPE A: STELLAR CORE (Star Fire/Plasma Combat)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Wields superheated plasma and solar fire as an offensive force, building pressure through heat stacks and releasing devastating stellar detonations.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Solar Flare        | Attack   | Launch a burst of superheated plasma that deals fire damage and applies 1 stack of Heat to the target (max 10 stacks).
  - Plasma Coat        | Buff     | Ignite your weapon or hands with plasma — melee attacks deal bonus fire damage and apply Heat stacks for 8s.
  - Thermal Pressure   | Passive  | Each Heat stack on a target reduces their armor by 2% and increases damage they take from your fire abilities by 1%.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Coronal Loop       | Attack   | Fire an arcing plasma beam between two targets — both take damage, and any target caught in the arc between them takes 60% of the beam's damage.
  - Stellar Wind       | Active   | Emit a shockwave of charged solar particles — pushes enemies back 8m and applies 3 Heat stacks to all within 10m.
  - Ignition Point     | Passive  | When a target reaches 5+ Heat stacks, they begin burning passively for 15 damage per second until stacks fall below 5.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Sunspot Bomb       | Attack   | Hurl a compressed ball of solar mass. On impact, it expands into a 6m radius zone of plasma for 5s, applying 2 Heat stacks per second to anything inside.
  - Chromosphere       | Aura     | Shroud yourself in a solar corona for 8s — melee attackers take plasma damage and gain 2 Heat stacks. You are immune to fire damage during this window.
  - Stellar Collapse   | Active   | Detonate all Heat stacks on a target simultaneously — each stack detonates for 30 damage. A target at max stacks (10) takes 300 instant damage and is Stunned for 2s.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - White Dwarf        | Channel  | Channel for 3s, compressing energy, then release a beam of pure stellar matter in a 20m line. Deals 400% weapon damage and strips all Heat stacks, converting them to bonus damage (+30 per stack removed).
  - Nova Pulse         | Active   | Trigger a miniature supernova centered on yourself — all enemies within 15m take 250 fire damage, gain 8 Heat stacks, and are knocked down for 1s. Allies within range receive a 20% damage buff for 5s (caught in the shockwave's wake).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Stellar Detonation | Active   | Select one enemy with 8+ Heat stacks and detonate them as a stellar core: they become the epicenter of a controlled stellar explosion. The target takes 600 damage. All enemies within 20m of the target take 300 damage and gain 5 Heat stacks from the plasma shockwave. The explosion leaves a 10m plasma field for 8s.

>> Your notes on STELLAR CORE:
>> Heat stack management is the core loop — building efficiently and detonating at the right moment via Stellar Collapse or White Dwarf.
>> Chromosphere is both offense (punishes melee) and defense (fire immunity), making it a smart panic button.
>> Stellar Detonation turns a single target into a bomb — maximized with a target that has 10 stacks before detonation for a true chain-reaction moment.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: CONSTELLATION (Pattern-Based Buff System)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Maps star patterns onto the battlefield and activates their mythological powers — buffs, formations, and cosmic resonances that reward positioning and preparation.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Chart the Stars     | Utility  | Place a Stellar Node (glowing star marker) at your location or a target point (up to 3 active at a time). Nodes persist for 30s.
  - Connect the Dots    | Active   | Draw a line between two Stellar Nodes, creating a "Constellation Link." Allies standing on or near a Link gain +10% move speed.
  - The Wandering Star  | Passive/Utility  | Gain a free roaming Stellar Node that orbits you at 4m range, counting as an active node for all constellation effects.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Triangle Formation  | Active   | If 3 Nodes form a triangle, activate the formation — allies inside the triangle gain +15% damage and +10% armor for 8s.
  - The Hunter Pattern  | Summon   | Arrange 4 Nodes in the Orion pattern (specific arrangement) to summon a spectral constellation hunter that attacks enemies for 10s dealing 80 damage per strike.
  - Star Path           | Passive  | Allies who walk along a Constellation Link gain a stacking "Stellar Charge" buff (+3% damage per charge, max 5) that lasts until combat ends.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - The Twins           | Active   | Place 2 Nodes within 5m of each other to activate the Gemini pattern — you split into 2 spectral selves for 6s, each able to perform actions. Cooldown 60s.
  - Celestial Roof      | Active/Shield   | Activate 4+ Nodes in a ring formation — a dome of starlight covers the interior for 10s. Enemies cannot enter; allies inside regenerate 2% max HP per second.
  - Deep Map            | Passive/Utility  | Increase max active Stellar Nodes to 5. Constellation patterns activate faster (no manual trigger needed — auto-detect valid formations).

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Zodiac Authority    | Active   | Choose a Zodiac constellation pattern (Aries = power, Aquarius = healing, Scorpio = poison, Leo = speed). Place nodes matching that pattern for 10s — the full map activates, applying the chosen buff to all allies on the field at 30% potency.
  - Binary System       | Passive  | When two Constellation Links overlap, the intersection point becomes a "Pulsar" — enemies passing through it take 80 damage and are Slowed. Allies passing through gain +20% crit chance for 5s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Grand Orrery        | Active   | Instantly place 7 Stellar Nodes in the configuration of a full star chart (covering the battlefield). All constellation patterns simultaneously activate for 15s — every formation effect applies at once (triangle buff, ring dome, hunter, star path). At the end of 15s, the Orrery supernovas, dealing 250 damage to all enemies on the field.

>> Your notes on CONSTELLATION:
>> This archetype rewards pre-battle setup and map awareness — it's the "chess player" option for deliberate, tactical players.
>> The Twins is the most disruptive skill — two simultaneous action players for 6s creates wild combo potential.
>> Grand Orrery should have a long cooldown (3+ minutes) — it's an entire fight transformation, not a rotation tool.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: VOID WALKER (Dark Matter/Antimatter)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Commands the annihilating properties of antimatter and dark matter — erasing projectiles, inverting forces, creating gravity wells, and building toward an antimatter detonation that destroys existence itself.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Dark Matter Bolt   | Attack   | Fire a bolt of condensed dark matter that phases through shields and armor, dealing unblockable damage.
  - Event Horizon      | Active   | Create a small gravity well at target location for 4s — pulls all enemies within 8m toward the center by 3m per second.
  - Null Field         | Passive/Evasion  | Passively emit a low-level antimatter field — all projectiles aimed at you from more than 10m away have a 15% chance to be annihilated before reaching you.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Antimatter Charge  | Buff     | Build up an Antimatter Charge (passive accumulation over 20s, or accelerated by taking hits). Releasing the charge triggers all T1 abilities at no cost in rapid sequence.
  - Gravity Flip       | Active   | Invert gravity in a 10m radius for 3s — enemies are lifted 5m into the air and take fall damage when dropped. Allies are unaffected.
  - Dark Resonance     | Passive  | Enemies affected by your gravity effects (pulled, lifted, or inside a well) take 20% increased damage from dark matter abilities.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Void Rupture       | Attack   | Tear a rift in spacetime at a target location — after a 1s delay, the rift implodes, pulling all nearby enemies into it for 180% weapon damage. The rift leaves a gravity field for 3s.
  - Phase Walk         | Movement | Step through the void — teleport up to 20m, phasing through all obstacles. You are untargetable during transit. Arrive dealing 60 damage in a 5m burst.
  - Antimatter Shell   | Reactive | When you would take lethal damage, automatically trigger: consume all built Antimatter Charge to create a blast that deals 300% of the damage you would have taken to all nearby enemies. You survive with 1 HP.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Singularity        | Active   | Create a true black hole at target location for 6s — a 15m radius pull that grows stronger each second, deals 80 damage per second to trapped enemies, and cannot be escaped without a teleport. After 6s, it collapses and explodes for 400 damage.
  - Dark Matter Siphon | Channel/Heal  | Channel for 4s, draining the mass of all enemies within 12m — they lose 20% of their current HP over the channel, and you gain that HP as a temporary shield.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Annihilation Event | Active   | Release a full antimatter detonation centered on yourself. All matter within 25m is subjected to annihilation: enemies take 800 damage (reduced to 400 if outside 12m). All buffs, shields, armor enhancements, and terrain effects within range are erased. You are briefly phased out of existence during the blast — untargetable for 2s. Cooldown: 3 minutes.

>> Your notes on VOID WALKER:
>> Null Field and Antimatter Shell create a passive safety net that rewards staying in the fight and building charge.
>> Singularity is a 6s black hole — it should feel genuinely terrifying to be caught in one.
>> Annihilation Event's "erase buffs/terrain" component makes it uniquely effective against defensive/support compositions.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON STAR NAVIGATOR]
> Three aesthetically and mechanically distinct archetypes: hot/aggressive (A), tactical/prep (B), and cold/annihilating (C).
> The class works best in a sci-fi or hybrid sci-fi-fantasy setting — visuals should feel cosmically grand, not just "fire mage with space colors."
> Constellation (B) requires the most game system support (node placement, pattern detection UI) — it's the most design-expensive archetype here.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 41: ELECTROMANCER (Pure Electricity)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A living conductor of raw electrical force, the Electromancer channels voltage through their body and the battlefield — arcing between enemies, overloading systems in suicidal bursts, and stockpiling charge to unleash catastrophic releases.

ARCHETYPE A: ARC WELDER (Chain Lightning/Bouncing Electricity)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Specializes in electricity that bounces between targets, rewarding enemy density and building chain reactions that span entire groups.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Fork Lightning     | Attack   | Strike one target with lightning. It arcs to 1 additional nearby enemy (within 6m) at 70% damage.
  - Conductive Ground  | Active   | Electrify a 10m patch of ground for 6s — any enemy standing on it takes 15 damage per second and counts as a valid arc target at 0m range.
  - Live Wire          | Passive  | Each time your arc lightning bounces to a new target, damage increases by 5% per bounce (max +25% at 5 bounces).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Bounce Weave       | Attack   | Launch a bolt that bounces up to 4 times between different enemies. Each bounce deals 80% of the previous hit's damage.
  - Magnetic Field     | Debuff   | Apply a magnetic charge to up to 3 enemies — for 8s, any lightning that arcs near them is pulled to them first (guaranteed arc target, even at 12m range).
  - Chain Reaction     | Passive  | When the same enemy is struck by arcing lightning 3 times in a single cast, they are Stunned for 1.5s and emit a secondary arc that hits one additional random target.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Arc Net            | Active   | Release a net of electricity — arcs simultaneously connect all enemies within 15m to each other and to you. Each connection deals 60 damage. The more enemies present, the more connections, the more total damage.
  - Feedback Loop      | Passive  | Lightning that bounces back to an already-struck target in the same chain deals 150% damage to that target.
  - Ground Fault       | Attack   | Slam the ground, sending a forking electrical current along the earth in a cone — any enemy it hits has arc lightning jump from them to every enemy within 5m, as if they were a Magnetic Field target.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Unlimited Arc      | Passive  | Remove the bounce cap from all arc abilities. Lightning continues bouncing until it runs out of valid targets within 8m. Each bounce beyond the normal cap deals 50% of the previous hit's damage.
  - Tesla Coil         | Summon   | Plant a Tesla Coil at a location for 15s. It automatically fires arc lightning at the nearest enemy every 2s, bouncing up to 3 times with Live Wire bonus. Enemies adjacent to the coil take 20 damage per second.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Lightning Storm | Active  | For 10s, become the epicenter of an uncontrolled arc storm. Every 0.5s, a bolt of arc lightning fires from your body to a random enemy within 20m and bounces up to 6 times. Each bolt applies Chain Reaction tracking. The storm also applies Conductive Ground to the entire battlefield for the duration.

>> Your notes on ARC WELDER:
>> The class reward density — bring it into large fights, not duels. Arc Net's total damage should be calculated per connection, making large groups devastatingly punishing.
>> Tesla Coil adds sustained passive arc damage during cooldowns, keeping the chain-lightning feel alive between casts.
>> The Lightning Storm is an absolute chaos engine — 20 bolts per 10s, each bouncing 6 times, is designed to be visually overwhelming.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: SURGE (Overcharge/Mana-Burn Burst)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Sacrifices resources, HP, and safety margins to overcharge abilities, pushing electricity past safe limits for catastrophic single-cast power spikes.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Overclock          | Buff     | Spend 20% of your current Mana to boost your next ability's damage by 40% and remove its casting time.
  - High Voltage       | Attack   | A raw, unrefined electrical blast dealing 150% weapon damage. Costs 15% more Mana than standard abilities. No frills, all power.
  - Burnout            | Passive  | When you spend more than 30% of your max Mana on a single ability (via Overclock or cost), gain a "Burnout" charge. At 3 Burnout charges, your next ability is free and deals an additional 50% damage.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Overload           | Active   | Immediately double your current Mana cost on the next ability you cast. In return, that ability triggers twice (two separate casts, one input).
  - Redline            | Toggle   | Toggle on: all abilities cost 50% more Mana but deal 60% more damage. Your HP drains at 2% per second while active. Toggle off to stop drain.
  - Capacitative Skin  | Passive/Defense  | While at below 30% Mana, gain a 20% damage reduction as your body enters emergency conservation mode. (Rewards riding the mana floor aggressively.)

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Surge Protocol     | Active   | Instantly restore 40% of your Mana by converting 25% of your max HP into raw electrical energy. This energy boosts your next 3 abilities by +30% damage each.
  - Breaker Box        | Active   | Target one ability in your kit and "break" its limiter for 10s — it costs 3x normal Mana but deals 3x damage, has no cooldown, and can be cast continuously until Mana depletes.
  - Voltage Spike      | Reactive | When you are struck while Redline is active, automatically release a voltage spike that stuns the attacker for 1.5s and deals 120 damage. No Mana cost (the hit charges it).

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Overcharge   | Active   | Spend your entire current Mana pool in one release — deal damage equal to 150% of all Mana spent as a raw lightning blast to a single target. Cooldown 45s.
  - Surge Immunity     | Passive/Utility  | Once per minute, when your Mana would hit 0% and an ability would fail to cast due to insufficient Mana, cast it anyway as a free cast that drains 10% of your max HP instead. You're never truly out of power.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Critical Overload  | Active   | Burn ALL remaining Mana and 50% of current HP simultaneously. Release it as an electrical superstrike on a single target: damage equals 200% of Mana spent + 300% of HP spent as lightning damage. This attack cannot be blocked, evaded, or reduced by resistances. Cooldown: 90s.

>> Your notes on SURGE:
>> Redline is the class identity skill — it enables a high-risk, high-reward playstyle where you stay in the danger zone as long as possible.
>> Breaker Box is extremely powerful in burst windows but is self-limiting via Mana drain — it's an "I have 10s and a full Mana bar" button.
>> Critical Overload is the most self-destructive capstone in the game. It requires setup (high Mana, high HP) but can one-shot almost anything.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: CAPACITOR (Store Damage, Release Later)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Absorbs incoming damage and environmental electricity as stored charge, then releases it in controlled or massive bursts — becoming more dangerous the longer they survive punishment.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Charge Absorption  | Passive/Utility  | Whenever you take damage, absorb 10% of it as stored Electrical Charge (displayed as a Charge bar, max 500).
  - Lightning Rod      | Toggle   | Toggle on: double Charge Absorption rate (20%), but take 10% more damage while active. Toggle off to stop the extra vulnerability.
  - Discharge          | Attack   | Release all stored Charge as a direct lightning bolt — damage equals 1 damage per 2 Charge points. Resets the Charge bar.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Partial Release    | Attack   | Release 40% of stored Charge as a targeted bolt. More precise than Discharge — use it to maintain a minimum charge floor while still dealing damage.
  - Overcharge Shell   | Buff/Shield     | Convert 100 Charge into a damage-absorbing shield (1 Charge = 2 shield HP). The shield crackles — enemies that strike it take 20 damage per hit.
  - Charge Siphon      | Active   | Drain electrical energy from nearby powered objects, enemy lightning effects, or other Electromancers — gain 80 Charge instantly. 20s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Capacitor Field    | Aura     | Emit a low-level electrical field while Charge > 200: all allies within 10m gain +10% attack speed as your charge hums through them. The field scales — at 400+ Charge, it also applies a 1s Slow aura to all enemies within 10m.
  - Controlled Burst   | Attack   | A precision discharge: release exactly 200 Charge for a guaranteed 200 damage + Stun (1.5s). Unlike Discharge, this is not affected by resistance — the stun always triggers.
  - Charge Spike       | Reactive | When struck by any lightning or electrical ability (friendly or enemy), gain 150 Charge instead of taking damage from it. You are immune to your own electrical abilities.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Maximum Charge     | Passive  | Raise the Charge bar maximum to 1000. When at max Charge, your body visibly arcs with electricity and you passively emit a 5m radius shock aura that deals 40 damage per second to enemies. Discharge at max Charge applies a 3s Stun.
  - Trickle Feed       | Passive/Utility  | Gain 5 Charge per second passively, regardless of incoming damage. Additionally, each ability you cast costs 50 Charge instead of Mana (Charge becomes a secondary resource alongside Mana). If Charge drops below 100, revert to Mana costs.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Supercapacitor     | Active   | Enter a charging stance for 5s — you cannot attack but gain triple Charge Absorption and absorb environmental electricity (rain, enemy arcs, nearby lightning effects) for bonus Charge. At the end of 5s, release ALL stored Charge in a cataclysmic burst: a 20m radius lightning explosion dealing 1 damage per 1 Charge stored. At max (1000 Charge), this deals 1000 area damage. Cooldown: 60s.

>> Your notes on CAPACITOR:
>> The charge bar is a third resource — visually prominent and communicated clearly so the player always knows where they stand.
>> Trickle Feed elegantly converts Charge into a Mana substitute at high levels, letting the player ignore Mana management entirely in the late game.
>> Supercapacitor rewards deliberately tanking hits during its charge phase — it's the ultimate payoff for the "absorb everything, release it all" fantasy.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON ELECTROMANCER]
> Three mechanically distinct resource models: Arc Welder uses enemy density, Surge uses Mana as fuel, Capacitor uses incoming damage as fuel.
> The class has strong visual identity at each tier — early game sparks and small arcs, late game full storm effects.
> Balance concern: Surge's Critical Overload and Capacitor's Supercapacitor should be on long cooldowns to prevent back-to-back nukes.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 42: SMOKE DANCER (Smoke/Vapor/Gas)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A mercurial fighter who dissolves boundaries between solid and intangible, fighting from within clouds of smoke — becoming the gas itself, choking enemies with toxic vapors, and leaving phantom images of themselves to mislead and destroy.

ARCHETYPE A: CLOUDFORM (Become Smoke/Intangibility)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Transforms parts or all of themselves into a gaseous state — phasing through attacks, flowing through barriers, and fighting while in an intangible form that physical force cannot stop.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Disperse           | Active   | Briefly convert your body to smoke for 1.5s — you phase through all physical attacks and projectiles during this window. Cooldown 8s.
  - Smoke Step         | Movement | Dash forward 10m as a puff of smoke, passing through enemies and thin barriers. Leaves a small cloud at departure and arrival points for 3s.
  - Vapor Body         | Passive/Immunity  | While in smoke form (Disperse or any smoke state), gain +20% move speed and take 0 damage from physical attacks.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Extended Form      | Passive/Utility  | Increase Disperse duration to 3s. Enemies who strike you during Disperse are Blinded for 2s (their hand passed through smoke).
  - Flowing Through    | Active   | Flow through a target enemy as smoke, dealing 80 damage and emerging on their far side. This movement cannot be blocked, dodged, or redirected.
  - Smoke Veil         | Buff/Evasion     | Shroud yourself in a permanent thin smoke layer — you gain 15% evasion against ranged attacks as long as this buff is active. Stacks with Disperse.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Gaseous Form       | Transform| Become fully intangible for 6s — you cannot deal physical damage but all attacks pass through you entirely. You can move through walls and closed doors during this state. Cooldown 45s.
  - Smoke Ambush       | Stealth  | While in Gaseous Form, you are invisible. When you return to solid form, you deal 200% weapon damage on the first strike and Stun the target for 2s.
  - Condensation       | Reactive | When you revert from a smoke state, condense rapidly — gain a 15% max HP shield for 5s from the rapid solidification.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Sustained Dissolution | Toggle | Toggle on: remain in a partial smoke state indefinitely. You take 40% less damage from all sources but deal 30% less damage. Abilities cost 20% more Mana. Toggle off to fully solidify.
  - Smoke Merger       | Active   | Merge with any allied smoke cloud or allied Smoke Dancer on the field — instantly teleport to their location as a puff of smoke, dealing 100 damage at arrival to nearby enemies.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Total Dissolution  | Transform| For 12s, become an enormous cloud of sentient smoke covering a 15m radius. You cannot be targeted as an individual. Enemies inside the cloud take 40 damage per second and are Blinded. You can reform anywhere within the cloud's radius as a free action, dealing a 300 damage Smoke Strike on reform. At the end of 12s, violently condense — everything caught in the cloud radius is compressed outward for 250 damage and Knocked Down.

>> Your notes on CLOUDFORM:
>> Gaseous Form + Smoke Ambush is the intended T3 combo — go invisible, stalk, emerge for a devastating strike.
>> Sustained Dissolution creates a sustained tank-ish playstyle, unusual for the class — a deliberate off-identity option at T4.
>> Total Dissolution is uniquely threatening because you cannot be targeted — enemies must attack the cloud area rather than "you."
>>


ARCHETYPE B: CHOKER (Poison Gas/Suffocation)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Deploys toxic, suffocating gases that debilitate, poison, and deny breathing to enemies — specializing in denying actions and draining enemies' capacity to fight over time.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Toxic Mist         | Attack   | Breathe a cone of noxious gas 8m long — enemies hit take poison damage over 5s (30 damage total) and have their Mana regeneration reduced by 30%.
  - Choke Point        | Active   | Deploy a canister of heavy gas at a location — creates a 6m radius cloud for 8s. Enemies inside are Slowed 25% and lose 5% Mana per second.
  - Asphyxiate         | Passive/Utility  | Enemies with 3+ stacks of your gas effects cannot use channel abilities — they cannot breathe steadily enough to maintain concentration.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Neurotoxin Burst   | Attack   | Fire a dart that releases neurotoxin in a 4m radius — affected enemies suffer -30% damage output and lose coordination (10% chance to miss each attack) for 8s.
  - Mustard Cloud      | Active   | Drop a persistent gas bomb that lingers for 12s in a 8m radius, dealing 15 damage per second and applying 1 gas stack per second. Stacks slow movement.
  - Respiratory Failure | Passive/Utility | Enemies at 5+ gas stacks lose the ability to use vocal abilities (shouts, war cries, bardic spells) and take an additional 10% damage from all sources while suffocating.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Smother            | CC       | Focus all nearby gas into one target — they are Silenced, have their vision obscured, and take 20 damage per second for 6s. Cannot be cleansed without first leaving the gas zone.
  - Dead Air           | Active   | Create a 20m radius zone of total oxygen deprivation for 6s — no burning effects, no fire abilities, and no shouted commands function inside. Enemies take 8 damage per second from oxygen loss.
  - Choking Grip       | Active   | Grab an enemy by the throat (melee range) and infuse gas directly into their airways — they are Stunned for 3s and take 200 poison damage. Other gas stacks on them double during the stun.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Lockdown Gas | Active   | Release a slow-spreading gas cloud that covers the entire visible area over 6s. All enemies in range gain gas stacks rapidly — reaching 5 stacks silences, reaching 10 causes them to choke (Stunned for 2s), reaching 15 deals 300 damage.
  - Feedback Toxin     | Debuff   | Apply a toxin that reacts to enemy healing — whenever the target receives healing while this debuff is active, they take 50% of the heal as poison damage instead of recovering it. Lasts 20s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Final Breath   | Active   | Fill the lungs of all enemies within 15m with a lethal gas mixture over 3s (channel). At the end of the channel, every affected enemy is simultaneously Silenced, Stunned, poisoned for 500 damage over 10s, and has their movement halved for 15s. Any enemy killed by the subsequent poison damage explodes in a toxic burst, applying half the effect to nearby enemies.

>> Your notes on CHOKER:
>> Asphyxiate and Respiratory Failure create a layered debuff system — gas stacks serve as both a damage resource and a control resource.
>> Dead Air is uniquely effective against fire-based enemies and in PvP against fire mages — it turns off entire ability kits.
>> Feedback Toxin is a standout anti-healer tool — it punishes the act of healing, not just the healer.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: PHANTOM (Smoke Clones/Misdirection)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Deploys illusory smoke copies of themselves to disorient enemies, bait attacks, and trigger traps — fighting through misdirection and punishing enemies for targeting the wrong figure.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Smoke Double       | Summon   | Spawn a stationary smoke clone at your location. The clone mimics your last idle animation and draws enemy AI targeting for 5s before dissipating.
  - Bait and Vanish    | Active   | Dash 8m while leaving a Smoke Double at your departure point. Any enemy who attacks the Double is revealed and Slowed for 2s.
  - Mirror Mind        | Passive/Defense  | Your Smoke Doubles inherit 15% of your current armor value — they take a few hits before dissipating, making them more convincing.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Reactive Double    | Reactive | When you take a hit that would reduce your HP by more than 15%, automatically spawn a Smoke Double at your current position and teleport 5m in a random direction. The Double absorbs the rest of the attack if it connects.
  - Phantom Step       | Movement | Teleport up to 15m and leave a moving Smoke Double that walks in the direction you were previously moving for 4s before dissipating.
  - Herd the Fools     | Active   | Command all active Smoke Doubles to converge on a single point — enemies tracking any Double are pulled into following all of them toward the destination (AI misdirection).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Kill the Copy      | Reactive | When a Smoke Double is destroyed by an enemy, it explodes — dealing 120 damage to all enemies within 5m and applying a 2s Blind.
  - The Three          | Summon   | Spawn 3 Smoke Doubles simultaneously around a target enemy — they orbit the enemy, making it impossible to determine which is real. You are hidden in smoke nearby. First strike from you is a guaranteed backstab (150% damage + Stun 2s).
  - Perfect Replica    | Passive  | Smoke Doubles now use basic attacks against nearby enemies at 30% of your damage. They appear completely real to enemy AI systems until struck.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Army of Phantoms   | Summon   | Spawn 5 Smoke Doubles simultaneously, spread across a 20m radius. Each Double lasts 10s and performs random movement patterns. Players/enemies cannot distinguish real you without direct ability use. All Doubles use Kill the Copy on destruction.
  - Phantom Ambush     | Stealth  | Go invisible for up to 15s while all active Doubles act as decoys. Your first attack from invisibility fires from the position of whichever Double is closest to the target, further obscuring your real location.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Infinite Smoke | Active   | For 20s, you and up to 7 Doubles exist simultaneously on the battlefield, all visually identical. All Doubles move and attack with full independence using your stats at 40% effectiveness. Enemies cannot target "you" specifically — they must damage all Doubles to find the real one. When the real you is struck during this window, instantly swap positions with the nearest Double (once every 3s). At the end of 20s, all Doubles detonate as Kill the Copy simultaneously.

>> Your notes on PHANTOM:
>> Phantom requires solid AI targeting behavior from enemies to function as intended — if enemy AI can easily detect the real player, the archetype collapses.
>> In PvP, Phantom is one of the most disorienting archetypes in the game — human opponents can't rely on hit registration giveaways the way AI can.
>> The Infinite Smoke is essentially an 8-player DPS check — forcing opponents to kill 8 identical targets to guarantee the kill.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON SMOKE DANCER]
> The class has three distinct fantasy executions of the same element: becoming it (A), weaponizing it as a poison (B), and using it as illusion (C).
> Cloudform plays closest to an assassin/escapist. Choker plays as a debuffer/controller. Phantom plays as a trickster/duelist.
> All three archetypes should have strong synergy with Stealth mechanics if the game has a stealth system.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 43: SEISMOLOGIST (Earth/Quake)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A warrior who reads the earth as a living weapon — splitting fault lines beneath enemy feet, bleeding magma through volcanic veins, and encasing themselves in geological mass to become an unstoppable force of natural catastrophe.

ARCHETYPE A: TECTONIC (Fault Line Attacks/Terrain Destruction)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Fractures and reshapes terrain in real time, creating fault lines, sinkholes, and crumbling ground that punishes enemy movement and opens devastating strike zones.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Fault Line         | Attack   | Slam the ground to create a cracking fault line 12m long in a chosen direction — enemies standing on the crack take 80 damage and are knocked sideways 3m.
  - Tremor             | Active   | Generate a ground tremor in a 10m radius — all enemies within range are Staggered (interrupted in their current action) and Slowed 20% for 3s.
