  - Rough Ground       | Passive/Utility  | Any terrain you have fractured with abilities becomes "rough ground" — enemies moving across it are Slowed 15% and have a 20% chance to Stumble (Staggered for 0.5s).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Tectonic Shove     | Attack   | Drive an underground rock shelf forward — a 3m wide, 15m long rock ram erupts from the earth and pushes everything above it forward. Enemies hit take 120 damage and are thrown 6m.
  - Sinkhole           | Active   | Open a 6m wide sinkhole at target location after a 1s delay. Enemies inside fall 3m (taking fall damage) and are trapped in difficult terrain. The hole persists for 10s.
  - Crumble            | Debuff   | Target a structure or terrain feature — the Seismologist weakens its geological foundation. After 5s, it collapses, dealing 200 damage to anyone underneath and creating rough ground.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Continental Drift  | Active   | Split a 20m long fracture through the earth — the two sides of the fracture drift apart 4m over 3s. Any enemy caught straddling the rift takes 200 damage and falls in (Trapped for 4s).
  - Aftershock         | Reactive | When you deal damage with any earth ability, trigger a 3s delayed aftershock — a free Tremor fires at the same location. No cost.
  - Subsidence         | Active   | Lower the terrain in a 12m radius by 2m — enemies within are knocked down, take 150 damage, and the lowered area becomes a bowl that channels all subsequent earth attacks inward for +20% damage.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Megaquake          | Active   | Trigger a massive seismic event: all rough ground tiles within 30m simultaneously explode upward, dealing 150 damage and Launching all enemies in those zones. The eruption creates a 10s period of unstable ground across the entire area.
  - Tectonic Authority | Passive/Immunity  | You are immune to your own terrain effects. Additionally, you can walk across Sinkholes, fault lines, and rough ground without penalty — they exist for your benefit alone.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Great Rift     | Active   | After a 2s stomp, split the entire visible battlefield with a 40m long, 3m wide chasm. Any enemy on the line of the split falls in (Trapped, taking 400 damage from the initial fall). The chasm walls crumble for 10s, dealing 50 damage per second to those inside. The chasm permanently divides the battlefield, blocking movement across it until the combat ends.

>> Your notes on TECTONIC:
>> Rough Ground is a passive battlefield state that accumulates through the fight — the longer it goes on, the more impassable the terrain becomes.
>> Tectonic Authority's immunity to own terrain is mandatory — without it, the Tectonic player would be trapped in their own sinkholes constantly.
>> The Great Rift is a true split-the-battlefield ability — it changes the physical map for the rest of the fight, which is uniquely powerful.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: MAGMA VEIN (Lava/Fire-Earth Hybrid)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Opens volcanic channels in the earth, filling terrain with liquid magma and igniting the battlefield floor — a hybrid offense that combines geological eruption with sustained fire damage zones.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Magma Jet          | Attack   | Crack the earth and fire a jet of magma 10m in a line — deals 70 fire damage and applies "Scorched" debuff (15 fire damage per second for 5s).
  - Lava Pool          | Active   | Open a volcanic vent beneath a target point — a 4m radius pool of magma forms over 1s and persists for 8s, dealing 25 fire damage per second to anyone standing in it.
  - Porous Earth       | Passive  | Any fault line or fracture you create also opens minor lava veins — enemies standing on cracked earth take 10 fire damage per second in addition to physical damage.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Eruption Pillar    | Attack   | Select a point — after 1.5s, a column of magma erupts 4m high. Enemies directly above take 180 fire damage and are Launched. Enemies adjacent take 80 fire damage.
  - Lava Flow          | Active   | Open a volcanic channel in a direction — magma flows 15m forward over 3s, leaving a 3m wide lava trail that persists for 12s. Anything in the path takes continuous fire damage.
  - Molten Core        | Passive/Utility  | When a Lava Pool or lava flow ages to more than 6s, it thickens — movement speed of enemies inside is reduced by an additional 30% as the magma cools to a viscous crust.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Volcanic Armor     | Buff/Defense     | Encase yourself in cooling magma for 10s — gain 25% damage reduction. Melee attackers take 60 fire damage per hit. When the armor expires, it shatters outward, dealing 100 fire damage in a 6m radius.
  - Pyroclastic Wave   | Attack   | Fire a rolling wave of superheated magma and ash outward in a 15m cone — deals 120 fire damage, applies Scorched, and creates a lava field covering the cone area for 6s.
  - Caldera            | Active   | Designate a 10m area as a caldera — the entire zone ignites and fills with shallow lava over 3s. Enemies in range when it fully ignites take 200 fire damage. The caldera persists for 10s, dealing 30 fire damage per second.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Mantle Breach      | Active   | Tear a hole through the earth's mantle at a target point — an uncontrolled eruption fires every 2s for 10s: each eruption is an Eruption Pillar at a random point within 10m of the breach. Unpredictable and devastating in dense groups.
  - Superheated        | Passive  | All of your fire-earth abilities now apply "Superheated" instead of Scorched — Superheated deals 30 fire damage per second (double Scorched) and causes the target to explode for 80 fire damage if they are healed while it's active.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Volcanic Awakening | Active   | For 15s, convert the entire battlefield floor into active volcanic terrain — lava pools connect, flow channels open, and the ground itself burns. All enemies take 20 fire damage per second from floor contact. Every 3s, a random Eruption Pillar fires somewhere on the field. Your Lava Pools deal triple damage during this window. At the end of 15s, the volcanic activity ceases — but every lava field turns to obsidian rough ground permanently for the fight.

>> Your notes on MAGMA VEIN:
>> The hybrid fire-earth design means the archetype deals two damage types — good against enemies resistant to one but not both.
>> Molten Core's slowing thickened lava creates a natural zone-denial that worsens over time, rewarding patience.
>> Volcanic Awakening's obsidian aftermath is a clever lasting change — the battlefield ends in rough difficult terrain even after the fire stops.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: FOSSIL KNIGHT (Petrify/Stone Armor)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Channels the patience of stone — hardening themselves into geological fortresses, slowly turning enemies to stone, and wielding petrification as both defense and devastation.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Stone Skin         | Buff/Defense     | Harden your skin to granite for 6s — gain 20% damage reduction and immunity to knockback effects. Your movement speed is reduced by 15% during this time.
  - Rock Hurl          | Attack   | Tear a chunk of earth from the ground and hurl it — deals 100 physical damage and applies 1 stack of "Petrification" to the target (max 10 stacks).
  - Ancient Patience   | Passive/Heal  | While Stone Skin is active, regenerate 1% max HP per second. Stone rewards those who endure.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Petrifying Gaze    | Debuff   | Lock eyes with one target — apply 2 Petrification stacks per second for 3s (requires line of sight). The target slows progressively with each stack.
  - Stone Sentinel     | Passive/Utility  | At 3+ Petrification stacks, a target's movement speed is reduced by 10% per stack. At 5+ stacks, their ability cast speed is reduced by 20%.
  - Fossil Shield      | Buff/Shield     | Grow a stone shield from your forearm — block the next 3 incoming attacks entirely (absorbs full damage), then the shield shatters. The shards deal 80 damage to nearby enemies.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Calcification      | CC   | Rapidly force 5 Petrification stacks onto a target instantly. If they were already at 5+, this instead triggers partial petrification — they are Rooted for 4s and take 50% reduced healing.
  - Stone Prison       | CC       | Encase a target at 8+ Petrification stacks in stone for 4s — they are fully Stunned, immune to outside damage (good or bad), and take 80 damage per second from internal calcification.
  - Living Monolith    | Passive  | You may maintain Stone Skin indefinitely at the cost of 3 Mana per second. While in permanent Stone Skin, Rock Hurl deals 50% bonus damage and applies 2 Petrification stacks per hit instead of 1.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Full Petrification | CC       | If a target reaches 10 Petrification stacks, they turn to stone completely — Stunned for 8s, immune to all damage (suspended in stone), and their stats are visible to the entire party (the stone reveals their internal data). When the stone breaks, they take 400 damage from re-animation.
  - Geomantic Plate    | Toggle   | Toggle on: your entire body is encased in living stone armor — gain 40% damage reduction, immunity to physical CC, and passively apply 1 Petrification stack per second to melee attackers. Toggle off to restore normal speed.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Age of Stone       | Aura     | For 20s, emit a petrification field — all enemies within 20m accumulate 1 Petrification stack per second passively, regardless of what they do. At 5 stacks, enemies begin visibly calcifying (slowed). At 10 stacks, they trigger Full Petrification automatically. You become immune to all damage that doesn't exceed 50 in a single hit (small hits shatter against stone). When the aura ends, all Petrification stacks on all enemies freeze in place — they don't decay for another 30s.

>> Your notes on FOSSIL KNIGHT:
>> The Petrification stack system is satisfying because it's visible — enemies can see and feel themselves slowing down as stacks accumulate.
>> Full Petrification is unique in that it also makes the enemy immune — it's a 8s "out of the fight" button but not a "you die now" button.
>> Age of Stone is a passive engine capstone — it doesn't require any targeting, just proximity, which rewards positioning.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON SEISMOLOGIST]
> Three archetypes from one element: terrain control (A), area fire damage (B), and escalating CC/fortification (C).
> Tectonic (A) is the most strategically complex — it requires the player to reshape the battlefield intentionally, not just attack into it.
> Fossil Knight (C) is the most defensive archetype of the three — it inverts the usual earth = aggressive stereotype into a patient fortress playstyle.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 44: ORACLE (Divination/Fate)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A seer whose power lies not in what they do, but in what they know is coming — dodging attacks before they land, buffing allies for events that haven't happened yet, and pulling the strings of probability to make fortune a weapon.

ARCHETYPE A: FORESIGHT (Precognition/Dodge/Prepare)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Perceives attacks a half-second before they happen — building an ever-updating picture of the immediate future to dodge, parry, and interrupt with uncanny precision.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Sixth Sense        | Passive/Utility  | Gain a brief visual cue (shimmer or outline) 0.5s before an enemy commits to an attack — telegraphed attacks targeting you are highlighted. Increases reaction window effectively.
  - Step Aside         | Reactive | When Sixth Sense triggers, you may spend a small Mana cost to automatically sidestep the attack by 2m in a direction you choose.
  - Read the Room      | Active   | Scan all enemies within 20m — reveal what ability each one will use next (displayed above their head for 4s). 20s cooldown.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Preemptive Parry   | Reactive | 0.3s before an attack lands, if you have not moved, automatically enter a perfect parry — reduce damage to 0 and deal 80 reflect damage. Can only trigger once every 8s.
  - Future Memory      | Passive/Defense  | Keep a running log of enemy attack patterns in combat. After seeing an ability used 2+ times, permanently reduce your damage taken from that ability by 15%.
  - Foreseen Path      | Movement | Choose a movement path up to 15m — the Oracle "pre-walks" it (seeing themselves do so) and then executes it without interruption. All obstacles and attacks in the path are avoided for the duration of the movement (they were already calculated).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Prediction         | Buff/Evasion     | Declare one enemy ability by name or target an enemy — for 10s, every time that specific ability is used (by that enemy or any enemy), you automatically dodge it and gain 50 Mana back. (Requires Read the Room to have been used.)
  - Counter-Foresight  | Reactive | When an enemy telegraphs an attack (any ability with a cast time), interrupt their cast with a counterstrike — dash to them and deal 120 damage, canceling their ability. 12s cooldown.
  - The Next 5 Seconds | Active   | Enter a brief oracle trance for 1s — see exactly what will happen in the next 5s of combat (communicated via visual overlay of ghost images). Gain perfect foreknowledge of one incoming attack per ally in the party during this window.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Perfect Evasion    | Passive/Evasion  | Sixth Sense now triggers for abilities targeting any ally within 10m of you, not just yourself. You can extend Step Aside to push that ally out of harm's way as well.
  - Temporal Interrupt | Active   | Target one enemy — for 8s, every time they attempt to cast an ability, you see it 1 full second before it completes and may spend a Reactive to cancel it (max 3 cancels per use). Cooldown 45s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - I've Seen This Before | Passive/Active | Passive: after 30s of combat, you have "seen enough" — your hit chance, dodge chance, and parry chance against all enemies you have fought this combat are each raised by 20%. Active: once per combat, declare "I've seen this before" — for 6s, you are completely invulnerable (you have already lived through this moment and know exactly how to survive it). Cooldown resets if this combat was ended without using it.

>> Your notes on FORESIGHT:
>> Read the Room is mandatory for Prediction — this intentional dependency means the archetype has a clear rotation: scan first, then exploit.
>> The Next 5 Seconds is a uniquely narrative skill — it's a flash-forward mechanic that should have a cinematic visual style when activated.
>> "I've Seen This Before" is elegantly self-referential — it rewards players who actually study enemy patterns over 30s of combat.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: PROPHET (Buff Allies Based on Predicted Events)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Speaks foreseen outcomes into being — buffing allies for attacks and events that haven't happened yet, pre-healing damage before it lands, and turning prophecy into a support toolkit.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Foretell Wound     | Buff/Defense     | Touch an ally — predict they will take damage in the next 6s. They gain a 15% damage reduction shield preemptively. If the prediction triggers (they take damage), the shield doubles to 30% for the hit.
  - Prophetic Strike   | Buff     | Predict that an ally will land a critical hit — for 5s, their next attack is guaranteed critical. (Only one Prophetic Strike may be active at a time.)
  - Reading Signs      | Passive  | Once per minute, automatically identify which ally is most at risk (lowest predicted HP ratio in 10s) and apply Foretell Wound to them for free.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Pre-Heal           | Heal     | Channel 0.5s and target an ally — a pre-heal is deposited that triggers 3s later, restoring 15% max HP. The heal fires even if you have been interrupted after placing it.
  - Vision of Triumph  | Buff     | Grant one ally a "Vision of Triumph" — for 8s, they believe they will win this engagement. They gain +20% damage, +10% move speed, and immunity to Fear and Panic.
  - Fateful Moment     | Passive  | If any of your predictions (Foretell Wound, Prophetic Strike, Pre-Heal) triggers exactly as timed (the event occurs within 0.5s of expiry), the effect is amplified by 50%.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - See the Opening    | Buff     | Apply to one ally — for 6s, the next time an enemy they are fighting drops below 50% HP or uses an ability with a cast time, the ally automatically receives a burst of +40% damage for their next attack (the Oracle spotted the window for them).
  - Mass Prophecy      | Buff/Defense     | Broadcast a vague prophecy to all allies simultaneously — for 8s, all allies gain +10% damage, +10% damage resistance, and +10% healing received (the prophecy is comfortingly vague but galvanizing).
  - The Inevitable     | Buff     | Choose one ally and declare their next kill as "inevitable" — for 30s, if that ally kills an enemy, they immediately reset all their ability cooldowns. If they don't get a kill in 30s, nothing happens.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Rewrite the Vision | Active   | Target one ally who is about to die (below 15% HP) — spend 40% of your Mana to rewrite their fate. They cannot die for 4s. At the end of 4s, they are restored to 30% HP regardless of damage taken during the window. Cooldown 90s.
  - Parallel Futures   | Buff     | Apply all your active T1-T2 buff predictions to every ally simultaneously at 60% potency. For 10s, the entire party operates under your foreseen version of events.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Prophecy Fulfilled | Active | Declare one prophecy for each ally: "You will survive," "You will deal the killing blow," "You will be unharmed," or "You will be healed to full." For 20s, work toward making each prophecy true — your abilities automatically prioritize fulfilling each ally's prophecy. At the end of 20s, every ally whose prophecy was fulfilled gains a permanent +5% stat boost in the relevant category for the rest of the session.

>> Your notes on PROPHET:
>> Fateful Moment rewards skillful timing — players who internalize prediction windows will get consistent 50% bonuses.
>> Rewrite the Vision is the most powerful emergency button in the archetype — it should have a very long cooldown to reflect its impact.
>> The Prophecy Fulfilled is uniquely structured as both reactive and active — the Oracle is managing party fulfillment in real time, which is a distinct support loop.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: FATE WEAVER (Manipulate Probability/Luck)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Bends the laws of chance itself — tilting critical hit probability, causing enemies to miss, making impossible things possible, and finally warping fate so catastrophically that causality itself becomes your weapon.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Weight the Odds    | Debuff   | Apply to one enemy — for 8s, their critical hit chance is reduced to 0% and their hit chance is reduced by 15%.
  - Lucky Break        | Buff     | Apply to one ally — for 6s, their critical hit chance is increased by 25% and one of their next 3 attacks will automatically strike even if it would otherwise miss.
  - Probability Field  | Passive/Evasion  | Passively maintain a subtle probability adjustment: all enemies within 12m have a 5% chance per attack to simply miss at random (fate redirects the blow).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Jinx               | Debuff   | Curse one enemy with catastrophically bad luck for 10s — every ability they use has a 20% chance to target themselves instead of the intended target.
  - Fortune's Favor    | Buff     | Grant an ally supernaturally good luck for 10s — their next 5 attacks each have a 15% chance to deal triple damage (rolled independently per attack).
  - Serendipity        | Passive/Immunity  | Once per fight, when an ally would take lethal damage, fate intervenes — the attack misses entirely (the attacker trips, fumbles, is distracted). Invisible effect, no indicator.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Twist of Fate      | Active   | Retroactively alter the outcome of the last attack made against you or an ally — if it was a hit, it becomes a miss. If it was a miss, it becomes a hit. The universe adjusts. 30s cooldown.
  - Inevitable Doom    | Debuff   | Mark one enemy with Inevitable Doom — for 20s, every 5s, one of their active buffs is randomly removed by "bad luck." They cannot critically hit and their next 3 abilities have their damage halved.
  - The Long Odds      | Passive  | Any ability with less than 20% chance to trigger (proc chances, low-probability effects) now triggers at 50% chance instead when cast by you or applied to your allies.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Critical Mass      | Active   | For 8s, guarantee that every attack made by every ally within 15m is a critical hit. No RNG — 100% crit rate for the duration. Cooldown 60s.
  - Curse of Misfortune | Debuff  | Apply to one enemy: for 15s, they attract bad luck. Projectiles fired by allies that would miss them have a 50% chance to curve back and hit them anyway. Any missed ability within 10m re-rolls against this target.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Unravel Fate       | Active   | Collapse the probability field around all enemies for 10s. Every enemy simultaneously: has their crit chance zeroed, misses their next 3 attacks regardless of accuracy, takes double damage from any source that would normally be a "glancing blow," and has a 25% chance per action to fumble completely (wasted action). Additionally, all allies gain guaranteed crits for the same 10s. The Oracle has unwoven the tapestry of battle.

>> Your notes on FATE WEAVER:
>> Jinx and Twist of Fate are the most "feels bad" mechanics in the entire game for opponents — Jinx in particular can cause friendly fire.
>> Serendipity's invisible lethal save is a design choice to make it feel like genuine luck rather than a visible shield — communicating it to the player post-fight in a combat log is important.
>> Unravel Fate is the ultimate expression of the class: not dealing damage, but making the enemy completely unable to function for 10s.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON ORACLE]
> The class is uniquely future-oriented — it deals with what will happen, not what is happening. This requires strong communication of predicted events to the player.
> Foresight (A) is a solo/self-reliant archetype. Prophet (B) is a pure support archetype. Fate Weaver (C) is a debuff/control archetype.
> Serendipity and Inevitable Doom are hidden effects — they should be communicated subtly (special sound, small particle, combat log) without explicit on-screen callouts.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 45: BONE KNIGHT (Skeletal Warrior)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A grim warrior who has weaponized death itself, growing living bone armor from the remains of the fallen, honing their own skeleton into blades and lances, and commanding armies of skeletal warriors summoned from the battlefield's dead.

ARCHETYPE A: OSSUARY ARMOR (Bone Plate Defense/Grow Armor from Kills)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Builds and thickens their bone armor from the calcium of the slain — every kill adds to their defenses, turning them into a walking fortress of fused skeletal mass.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Bone Plate         | Passive/Shield  | Begin combat with a base bone plating that absorbs 50 damage before breaking. It regenerates between encounters.
  - Harvest Bone       | Passive/Shield  | Upon any nearby kill (ally or enemy), absorb calcium from the corpse — restore 20 armor to your Bone Plate or, if full, add 20 to your current max Bone Plate value (max +200).
  - Calcify            | Buff/Defense     | Actively harden your bone plating for 5s — gain +30% damage resistance and immunity to knockback. Bone Plate does not drain during Calcify.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Bone Spur Eruption | Reactive | When your Bone Plate absorbs a hit, erupted bone spurs deal 40 damage back to the attacker. (Scales — larger hits trigger larger eruptions.)
  - Ossified           | Passive/Resistance  | For every 100 points your max Bone Plate has grown above base (via Harvest Bone), gain +5% to all resistances permanently for this combat. Caps at +25%.
  - Living Armor       | Active/Shield   | Command your bone plating to grow aggressively — spend 10% max HP to immediately add 100 to your current Bone Plate. Excess beyond the max is added as max.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Bone Cascade       | Active   | Release a burst of bone shards from your armor — deal 100 damage to all enemies within 8m and reduce your current Bone Plate by 100 (the armor sacrificed itself). The shards leave "bone field" rough terrain for 5s.
  - Regenerate Plate   | Heal     | Trigger a rapid bone regeneration cycle — restore 150 Bone Plate and heal 8% max HP. Any currently missing bone layers grow back instantly. 30s cooldown.
  - Fortress Form      | Transform| Enter Fortress Form for 10s — your bone plating becomes absolute. Bone Plate can no longer be depleted (all damage is absorbed), but you cannot move. You may still attack and use abilities. Cooldown 60s.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - The Bone Crown     | Passive/Utility  | At maximum Bone Plate growth (+200), bone spines erupt from your shoulders and crown — gain a passive 20m aura that causes enemies to flinch when they strike you (Stagger on every melee hit against you). You become visually imposing enough that enemies require a morale check to voluntarily attack you.
  - Marrow Vaults      | Passive/Shield  | Your Bone Plate now stores a secondary pool: when Bone Plate is fully depleted (broken), it immediately triggers a second layer of 50% of your max Bone Plate before incoming damage reaches your HP. The second layer does not regenerate in combat. (One-time emergency reserve.)

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Ossuary Ascension  | Transform| For 20s, the Bone Knight's armor grows to cover them completely in a cathedral of fused bone — they become a 3m tall Bone Colossus. Gain triple max Bone Plate, immunity to all CC, and +50% damage on all attacks (bone-augmented strikes). Every enemy killed during Ascension adds 1s to the duration. When the duration ends, the outer armor shatters — dealing 200 damage to all enemies within 10m from the bone shrapnel.

>> Your notes on OSSUARY ARMOR:
>> Harvest Bone creates a natural power-scaling loop — the longer the fight goes and the more allies and enemies die, the stronger the Bone Knight's defense becomes.
>> Fortress Form is a unique stationary tank mode — it's a genuine defensive option in real boss encounters, essentially a 10s invulnerability for the patient player.
>> Ossuary Ascension's duration extension on kills creates a feedback loop: kills give more time, more time allows more kills.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: MARROW BLADE (Bone Weapon Attacks/Sharpen/Extend)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Grows, reshapes, and weaponizes their own bones into living blades, lances, and whips — a melee-focused archetype that reshapes their skeleton mid-combat to gain reach, sharpness, and devastating cutting power.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Bone Blade         | Attack   | Extrude a blade from your forearm — a free melee weapon that deals 90 physical damage. Persists until you choose to retract it (no cooldown to extrude).
  - Hone               | Buff     | Sharpen the Bone Blade by drawing it across your own bones — +25% damage and +15% armor penetration for your next 3 melee attacks.
  - Splinter           | Passive  | When you score a critical hit with the Bone Blade, the blade splinters and sends bone fragments at one nearby additional enemy within 5m for 50% damage.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Lance Extension    | Attack   | Rapidly extend your arm bone into a 3m lance — thrust it forward in a line, dealing 130 damage and piercing through all enemies in the line.
  - Knuckle Burst      | Attack   | Erupt bone knuckles outward from your fist — a powerful short-range punch that deals 160 damage and Staggers the target. The bone retracts automatically.
  - Rapid Reshape      | Passive  | Switch between Bone Blade, Lance, and Knuckle forms with no delay — each reshape in quick succession (within 2s) grants +10% damage for the next attack in that form (max +30%).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Bone Whip          | Attack   | Extend your spine into a flexible bone whip — lash up to 10m, dealing 110 damage and Pulling the target 5m toward you.
  - Serrated Edge      | Passive  | Apply "Lacerate" to the Bone Blade — attacks cause bleeding (20 damage per second for 5s). Each subsequent hit on the same target before the bleed expires refreshes and adds 10 damage per second (max 50/s).
  - Twin Blade Form    | Buff     | Extrude a Bone Blade from both forearms simultaneously — gain two simultaneous attack options. Attacks in this form alternate arms and deal 75% of normal damage each but attack at double speed.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Skeleton Loom      | Active   | Temporarily extrude your entire rib cage outward as a cage — enemies within 4m are trapped inside your ribcage for 3s (cannot escape). You take 30% more damage during this time (your torso is exposed) but can attack anything trapped inside freely.
  - Infinite Sharpness | Passive  | Your Bone Blade's sharpness grows with use: each kill with the Bone Blade permanently increases its base damage by 15 (for this combat). It can also now penetrate 100% of armor — bone finds the gaps.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Full Skeleton  | Transform| For 15s, externalize and weaponize your entire skeleton — bone blades, spines, and lances protrude from every joint and surface. You gain: +80% melee damage, a 3m reach on all attacks, a passive 5m bone spike aura that deals 60 damage per second to nearby enemies, and every attack applies Lacerate. You cannot use ranged abilities during this form. On expiration, retract all extensions with a burst that deals 200 damage to any enemy within 4m.

>> Your notes on MARROW BLADE:
>> Rapid Reshape rewards fluid combat — switching between forms rapidly should feel like a martial art, not a mechanical quirk.
>> Skeleton Loom is the most unique skill — trapping enemies inside your ribcage is viscerally horrifying and creates a "you're committed now" melee moment.
>> The Full Skeleton is a walking blender — the 3m reach on every attack combined with the spike aura means enemies can't safely get near you.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: SKELETON LORD (Skeletal Minion Army)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Raises and commands an army of skeletal warriors from battlefield corpses, growing more powerful as more enemies fall — a strategic summoner who fights through coordinated undead forces.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Raise Dead         | Summon   | Target a fresh corpse within 15m — raise it as a basic Skeletal Warrior. It has 25% of the original creature's stats and lasts until destroyed or combat ends. Max 2 active skeletons.
  - March of Bones     | Active   | Command all active skeletons to advance on a target enemy — they each perform a basic attack simultaneously. Simple, reliable activation.
  - Bone Link          | Passive/Utility  | You and your skeletons share a bone network — each active skeleton reduces the Mana cost of Raise Dead by 10% (max 30%).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Upgrade Skeleton   | Active   | Spend 50 Mana to upgrade one skeleton — it gains 50% more HP, deals 50% more damage, and gains the ability to use one of its original body's abilities at 40% effectiveness.
  - Skeleton Archer    | Summon   | Raise a corpse as a Skeletal Archer instead — it fires bone arrows from range (15m), dealing 60 damage per shot, and has 40% of the original creature's HP.
  - Undying Loyalty    | Passive/Utility  | When a skeleton is destroyed, its bones scatter across the battlefield. Within 10s, you can re-raise it at the same location for free (once per skeleton per combat).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Bone Titan         | Summon   | Spend 150 Mana to fuse 3 active skeletons into a single Bone Titan — a large (2m tall) construct with the combined HP of all 3, triple the attack damage of a standard skeleton, and the ability to use a bone slam AoE attack every 8s.
  - Raise the Fallen   | Summon   | Raise all corpses on the field simultaneously (up to 6) — regardless of your current skeleton cap. This temporary army lasts 15s before the hastily-raised dead collapse. 90s cooldown.
  - Skeletal Commander | Passive  | Increase your permanent skeleton cap by 2 (total 4). Upgraded skeletons now use 2 of the original creature's abilities instead of 1. You can issue formation commands (surround, focus, defend) with one button.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Death March        | Active   | Send all skeletons on an unstoppable charge — they each become invulnerable for 4s and deal 150% damage during the charge. Any skeleton killed during Death March instantly re-raises at its destruction point.
  - The Bone General   | Passive  | You permanently raise to 6 active skeletons. Your presence gives all skeletons: +25% damage, +25% HP, and the ability to equip dropped weapons from the battlefield (skeletons pick up weapons from fallen enemies automatically).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Legion of the Dead | Summon   | Raise every corpse that has been killed in this entire combat (not just current battlefield corpses — those long since cleared). Up to 20 skeletons rise simultaneously from the earth. These battlefield veterans retain memories of the combat — they use their full ability set at 60% effectiveness. They last for 25s. At the end of 25s, they collapse and their bones fuse into a single Bone Colossus with HP equal to 50% of all the raised skeletons' combined HP.

>> Your notes on SKELETON LORD:
>> Raise the Fallen vs. Legion of the Dead are two different mass-raise tools: one is mid-combat desperation, one is a final-act power fantasy.
>> Bone Titan is satisfying because it rewards efficient skeleton management — three basic skeletons become something dramatically more powerful.
>> The Bone General's auto-weapon-pickup is a genuinely fun systemic interaction — skeletons equipping loot from dead enemies requires careful tracking by the game engine.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON BONE KNIGHT]
> Three archetypes from one theme: survival through death (A), offense through anatomy (B), and power through undead numbers (C).
> Ossuary Armor pairs naturally with Skeleton Lord in a multi-spec context — kills fuel both armor growth and available corpses to raise.
> Marrow Blade is the most solo-focused and mechanically technical of the three — it rewards players who practice the reshape rotation.
> All three archetypes benefit from fights with high body counts — the class should be placed in combat environments with many combatants.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 46: Hive Keeper (Insect/Swarm)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A keeper of living colonies who commands insects as extensions of their own will, flooding battlefields with countless bodies that sting, bite, and overwhelm through sheer biological multiplicity.

ARCHETYPE A: Drone Swarm (Cloud, Numbers, Attrition)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Maintain a living storm of insects around you, dealing constant chip damage to everything nearby and scaling power through colony size.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Swarm Mantle       | Aura     | Surround yourself with a buzzing cloud; enemies entering melee range take 5 insect damage per second.
  - Locust Bolt        | Attack   | Hurl a concentrated mass of biting locusts at a target, dealing damage and reducing their armor by 5% for 4s.
  - Propagate          | Passive  | Each kill by your swarm produces 1 Drone Token (max 10); tokens passively increase swarm damage by 2% each.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Blot Out the Sun   | Active   | Launch a wide swarm volley that blankets a targeted area (8m radius), dealing damage over 3s and obscuring enemy vision.
  - Hive Split         | Active   | Divide your Swarm Mantle into two smaller satellite clouds that orbit independently, each dealing 60% normal aura damage.
  - Frenzy Pheromone   | Buff     | Spend 5 Drone Tokens to excite the swarm; swarm damage increases 40% and attack speed of cloud pulses doubles for 6s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Devouring Column   | Channel  | Channel a pillar-shaped swarm into one enemy for 4s, dealing escalating damage each second; interrupted if you move.
  - Colony Surge       | Active   | Instantly maximize Drone Tokens to 10 and release a burst wave of insects dealing burst AoE damage in 12m.
  - Metamorphic Swarm  | Toggle   | Toggle to evolve your swarm into a specialized form: Wasps (armor shred), Fireflies (DoT burn), or Beetles (slow).

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Endless Host       | Passive/Utility  | Your swarm can no longer be fully destroyed; if reduced to 0 tokens, automatically regenerate 3 Drone Tokens every 5s.
  - Living Tempest     | Active   | Merge all satellite clouds and Swarm Mantle into one catastrophic storm (20m radius) for 10s; deals massive damage but consumes all Drone Tokens on expiry.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Swarm IS You   | Transform | You dissolve partially into your colony for 15s: become immune to targeted attacks, move freely through terrain, and deal rapid multi-hit damage to everything within 15m; Drone Tokens regenerate instantly during this state.

>> Your notes on DRONE_SWARM:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Queen's Guard (Beetle Pets, Tanking, Bodyguard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Raise enormous armored insects as loyal bodyguards that intercept attacks, hold the line, and crush enemies with heavy chitin and mandible strikes.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Summon Scarab      | Summon   | Call forth a Scarab Beetle companion (moderate HP, 80 armor) that attacks nearby enemies and taunts them off you.
  - Shell Polish       | Passive/Defense  | Your summoned beetles gain 10% additional armor rating; each beetle alive also reduces your incoming damage by 3%.
  - Mandible Strike    | Active   | Command your active beetle to perform a powerful bite on a target, dealing heavy damage and applying Stagger for 1.5s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Titan Grub         | Summon   | Summon a larval Titan Grub: it has very high HP and taunts all nearby enemies, but cannot attack; matures into a Titan Beetle after 20s.
  - Chitin Barrier     | Reactive | When you take damage above 15% of your max HP in one hit, the nearest beetle rushes to interpose itself, absorbing 50% of the overflow damage.
  - Royal Scent        | Buff     | Emit pheromones that bind all your beetles to you tightly; beetles gain +20% damage and move 30% faster for 8s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Rhinoceros Spawn   | Summon   | Summon a Rhino Beetle with a horn charge ability; every 12s it charges in a line, dealing heavy knockback damage to all enemies hit.
  - Molt and Harden    | Buff/Defense   | Command a target beetle to shed its shell: it loses 50% armor for 5s but at the end regrows a reinforced shell granting 200% normal armor and healing it to full.
  - Swarm Throne       | Passive/Defense  | While 3 or more beetles are alive simultaneously, you gain a personal damage reduction aura of 15% and beetles deal 25% bonus damage.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Brood Mother's Edict | Active  | Sacrifice one beetle to permanently empower all remaining beetles: they gain +30% HP, +30% damage, and their abilities gain 1 additional charge.
  - Chrysalis Surge     | Active  | Force all your beetles to instantly molt and re-emerge as Elite forms with unique visual designs and a 40% stat increase for 12s, after which they return to normal.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Queen Rises    | Summon   | Summon the Queen Beetle, a colossal creature that dominates the battlefield: it taunts all enemies within 25m, deals massive cleave damage, and periodically spawns Scarab reinforcements every 8s for 30s.

>> Your notes on QUEENS_GUARD:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Parasite (Infest, Body Control, Internal Sabotage)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Infiltrate enemies with burrowing insects that eat them from within, disrupting their abilities and eventually seizing control of their bodies entirely.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Infest             | Debuff   | Burrow a parasite into a target; it deals low DoT damage for 12s and stacks up to 5 times on the same target.
  - Larval Tap         | Passive  | Each Infest stack on an enemy generates 1 Larva resource per 3s (max 20 Larva); Larva fuels higher parasite abilities.
  - Nerve Bite         | Attack   | A parasitic larva bites a nerve cluster, dealing moderate damage and reducing the target's movement speed by 20% for 4s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Ability Worm       | Debuff   | Insert a worm that feeds on magic; target has a 30% chance to fail ability activation for 6s. Stacks with Infest.
  - Burst Larvae       | Active   | Detonate all Infest stacks on one target simultaneously, dealing burst damage per stack and spreading 1 Infest to nearby enemies.
  - Consume Resources  | Active   | Spend 10 Larva to drain a target's stamina/mana (20% of their max) and add that amount to your own resource pool.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Puppet Thread      | CC       | Spend 15 Larva to implant a controlling parasite; target is Rooted for 3s as you briefly force them to walk toward your position.
  - Internal Colony    | Debuff   | A parasitic colony takes hold inside a target with 5 Infest stacks; they take bonus damage from all your abilities and their healing is reduced by 40%.
  - Symbiotic Harvest  | Passive/Utility  | Whenever an Infested target dies, you recover 10 Larva and gain a short burst of movement speed.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Hijack             | CC       | Spend 20 Larva to fully seize a non-boss enemy's body for 5s, controlling them directly as an avatar; your real body becomes invulnerable but stationary during this time.
  - Gestating Horror   | Channel  | Channel for 3s on a target with Internal Colony; at the end, a massive parasite erupts from them, dealing massive burst damage and applying Infest x3 to all adjacent enemies.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Infestation Cascade | Active  | Release an intelligent meta-parasite: it autonomously jumps from enemy to enemy every 3s, applying 3 Infest stacks and spending up to 8 hops; any target that dies while hosting the meta-parasite explodes, spreading Infest to all enemies within 6m.

>> Your notes on PARASITE:
>>
>>

################################################################################
CLASS 47: Venom Fang (Poison Specialist)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A master of biological toxins who studies the chemistry of death, crafting venom cocktails that seize nerves, shred blood, and dissolve the very cells that bind flesh together.

ARCHETYPE A: Neurotoxin (CC, Slow, Paralyze)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Deploy nerve-targeting poisons that progressively rob enemies of motor control, turning fast threats into helpless targets shaking on the ground.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Numbing Strike     | Attack   | Inject numbing venom on hit; target's movement speed is reduced by 15% for 6s and stacks up to 3 times.
  - Tremor Coat        | Passive  | Your attacks have a 15% chance to apply Nerve Tremble: the target has a 20% chance to miss their next attack.
  - Slow Drip          | Debuff   | Toss a venom capsule onto a target; it releases neurotoxin slowly over 8s, increasing their cast times by 30%.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Palsy Burst        | Active   | Detonate the neurotoxin in a target already under Numbing Strike, causing a convulsive spasm: they are Stunned for 1.5s and drop any held item temporarily.
  - Creeping Numbness  | Passive  | Every 3s a target remains slowed by your neurotoxins, the slow intensity increases by 5% (caps at 60%).
  - Synapse Blocker    | Debuff   | Apply a toxin that prevents the target from using any ability with a cast time longer than 1s for 5s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Grand Mal          | CC       | After a target has 3 stacks of Numbing Strike, trigger Grand Mal: they seize completely for 3s (full incapacitate) then emerge slowed by 30% for 4s.
  - Nerve Web          | Active   | Spray a web of neurotoxin mist (cone 10m); all enemies hit begin accumulating Numbing Strike stacks at 1 per 2s for 6s.
  - Antidote Lock      | Debuff   | Poison a target so that any cleanse or antidote they use is itself poisoned; the cleanse still fires but immediately reapplies 2 stacks of Numbing Strike.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Paralysis    | CC       | Spend all Numbing Strike stacks on a target (min 3) to trigger complete motor shutdown for 4s; they cannot move, attack, or use abilities, but are also immune to damage during paralysis.
  - Neural Override    | Active   | Apply a toxin that briefly hijacks the target's motor neurons: they involuntarily move 10m in a direction you choose before being Stunned for 2s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Neuro Collapse     | Active   | Trigger a catastrophic neurotoxin cascade in one target: they convulse for 8s, attacking random adjacent allies and enemies each second, before collapsing into a 3s Stun; any target killed during the convulsion spreads 2 Numbing Strike stacks to nearby enemies.

>> Your notes on NEUROTOXIN:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Hemotoxin (Bleed, Anticoagulant, DoT)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Corrupt blood and tissue with anticoagulant venoms, making every cut bleed catastrophically and turning healing into a liability.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Hemorrhage Fang    | Attack   | Bite or inject venom that causes Bleed for 8s, dealing damage per second; stacks up to 6 times.
  - Thinning Agent     | Passive  | Your Bleed effects deal 10% bonus damage per stack currently on the target (compounding).
  - Open Wound         | Debuff   | Apply anticoagulant to an existing Bleed stack; healing received by the target is reduced by 25% while any Bleed is active.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Cascade Rupture    | Active   | Force all Bleed stacks to proc simultaneously in 1s rather than over their duration; deals burst damage but consumes stacks.
  - Sanguine Feast     | Passive/Heal  | Each tick of Bleed damage on any enemy you've poisoned restores a small amount of your HP (0.5% max HP per tick).
  - Venom Splash       | Attack   | Slash in an arc, applying 2 Bleed stacks to all enemies in front of you and reducing their physical armor by 10% for 6s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Clot Bomb          | Active   | Hurl a coagulated venom grenade; on impact it applies 4 Bleed stacks to all enemies within 5m and Roots them for 1s as thickened blood slows their joints.
  - Hemophiliac's Mark | Debuff   | Mark a target: any source of damage (not just yours) applies 1 Bleed stack to them; lasts 10s or until 6 stacks are applied.
  - Exsanguinate        | Channel  | Channel for 4s on a target with max Bleed stacks; drain their blood energy, dealing massive damage while restoring your HP equal to 50% of damage dealt.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Rupture Point      | Passive  | When a target reaches 6 Bleed stacks, their next instance of damage taken triggers a Rupture: they take 300% of one Bleed tick as instant damage and the wound cannot be healed for 4s.
  - Crimson Mist       | Active   | A target at max Bleed stacks explodes in a spray of venom-blood; all enemies within 6m gain 2 Bleed stacks and are briefly Blinded (0.5s).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Bloodtide Venom    | Active   | Inject an irreversible hemotoxin into one target: their Bleed stacks no longer expire naturally for 20s; every 2s the stacks count doubles (capped at 12); at 12 stacks they enter a Hemorrhagic Shock state, losing 5% of max HP per second and being unable to use active abilities until healed above 50%.

>> Your notes on HEMOTOXIN:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Cytotoxin (Armor Shred, Cell Dissolution, Decay)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Apply corrosive cell-destroying venoms that eat through armor and flesh alike, making enemies increasingly fragile with each passing second.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Corrode            | Debuff   | Apply cytotoxin to a target; reduces their physical armor by 8% for 8s, stacking up to 5 times.
  - Acid Spit          | Attack   | Spit a glob of corrosive venom at a target, dealing immediate damage and applying 2 stacks of Corrode.
  - Enzymatic Touch    | Passive  | Your melee attacks have a 20% chance to apply 1 Corrode stack on hit without consuming a skill slot.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Dissolving Agent   | Active   | Accelerate cytotoxin breakdown on one target: each Corrode stack now also reduces their magic resistance by 4% per stack.
  - Flesh Rot          | Debuff   | Apply a rotting agent to a target with 3+ Corrode stacks; their HP recovery from any source is halved and their armor regeneration is disabled for 10s.
  - Caustic Aura       | Aura     | Toggle: emit trace cytotoxins around yourself; enemies within 4m accumulate 1 Corrode stack every 4s. Costs a small amount of resource per second.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Structural Failure | Active   | Detonate all Corrode stacks on a target in a shockwave; each stack deals damage and the target's armor is reduced to zero for 3s regardless of remaining stacks.
  - Necrotic Splash    | Active   | Burst a cytotoxin capsule in a 6m radius; all enemies hit take moderate damage and receive 2 Corrode stacks; targets already at 5 stacks take bonus burst damage instead.
  - Adaptive Venom     | Passive  | Your cytotoxin learns from each target's resistance; the 3rd application of Corrode on the same enemy in a fight ignores 50% of their innate resistance to poison.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Dissolution  | Active   | A target with 5 Corrode stacks enters full cytolytic breakdown: they take 15% of their current HP as damage per second for 5s; this damage cannot be mitigated by armor.
  - Venom Lattice      | Debuff   | Weave a cytotoxin web onto a target: any time they are healed, 30% of the heal is converted into Necrotic damage dealt back to them over 2s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Primordial Dissolve | Active  | Inject the ultimate cytotoxin into one target: it methodically dismantles their defenses over 15s — every 3s one layer of protection is permanently stripped (armor, magic resist, damage reduction, evasion, then invincibility frames), leaving them completely naked to all damage; boss targets suffer a weaker version losing 40% of each stat instead.

>> Your notes on CYTOTOXIN:
>>
>>

>

################################################################################
CLASS 48: Soul Weaver (Soul Energy)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A practitioner of soul science who harvests the departing essence of the slain, forging it into weapons, weaving it between living targets, and ultimately commanding the threads of fate that bind every consciousness to the mortal coil.

ARCHETYPE A: Soul Reaper (Harvest, Kill Resource, Accumulation)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Turn every kill into fuel; bank soul essence from fallen enemies and spend it as a powerful second resource that amplifies all your abilities.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Soul Siphon        | Passive  | On killing any enemy, harvest their soul essence, gaining 5 Soul Charge (max 100); weaker enemies grant less, elites grant bonus.
  - Reaper's Scythe    | Attack   | Slash with a spectral scythe, dealing damage; if this kills the target, immediately harvest their soul for double Soul Charge.
  - Hungry Void        | Passive  | Enemies below 20% HP radiate weakened soul energy; your abilities deal 10% bonus damage against them and killing them restores 5 extra Soul Charge.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Soul Surge         | Active   | Spend 20 Soul Charge to release a burst of compressed soul energy in a 10m radius, dealing moderate damage to all enemies hit.
  - Mass Harvest       | Passive  | When 3 or more enemies die within 3s of each other, you gain a Soul Windfall: +30 Soul Charge and a brief speed boost.
  - Spectral Claws     | Attack   | Spend 10 Soul Charge to slash with phantom hands that pierce armor; deals bonus damage equal to 1% of target's max HP per 10 Soul Charge you currently hold.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Soul Overload      | Active   | While at 80+ Soul Charge, release all charge in a single catastrophic burst (20m range), dealing damage per 10 charge spent; resets Soul Charge to 0.
  - Grave Robber       | Active   | Target a corpse within 10m to extract residual soul energy: gain 15 Soul Charge and briefly reanimate the corpse as a weak ghost ally for 8s.
  - Death Affinity     | Passive  | Your maximum Soul Charge increases by 25, and you passively generate 2 Soul Charge every 5s even without kills.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Soul Economy       | Passive  | All Soul Charge costs are reduced by 20%; additionally, any ability that spends Soul Charge has a 15% chance to refund the cost fully.
  - Reaper's Apex      | Stance   | Enter a Reaper Stance: attacks gain spectral range (+3m), and each hit on a living enemy drains 3 Soul Charge from them (if they have any from previous debuffs) directly into your pool.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Great Harvest  | Active   | Unleash a soul-tearing vortex centered on yourself (30m radius) for 6s; all enemies within are continuously drained of life, dealing damage and generating 5 Soul Charge per enemy per second; any enemy that dies during the vortex generates triple Soul Charge and permanently increases your maximum Soul Charge cap by 2.

>> Your notes on SOUL_REAPER:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Soul Forge (Crafting, Buff Weapons, Temporary Enchantment)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Transmute harvested souls into powerful temporary enhancements — forging soul-imbued weapons, armor, and consumables mid-combat.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Soul Temper        | Buff     | Spend 15 Soul Charge to imbue your weapon with soul energy for 10s; your attacks deal bonus spirit damage and ignore 10% armor.
  - Ghost Iron Coat    | Buff/Shield     | Spend 15 Soul Charge to coat yourself in spectral armor for 8s, absorbing up to 200 damage before shattering.
  - Essence Smelt      | Passive  | Forged items and buffs from Soul Forge abilities last 20% longer; their power is also based on current Soul Charge at time of casting.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Soul Shotgun       | Active   | Spend 20 Soul Charge to forge a one-use spectral crossbow bolt; immediately fire it for high single-target spirit damage that ignores all resistances.
  - Veil Weave         | Buff/Defense     | Spend 25 Soul Charge to weave a soul veil around an ally; they gain 15% damage reduction and their next 3 attacks deal bonus spirit damage.
  - Forge Burst        | Active   | Overload a previous Soul Forge buff currently active on yourself or an ally; the buff instantly expires, dealing AoE spirit damage equal to 50% of the buff's remaining value.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Philosopher's Soul | Active   | Spend 40 Soul Charge to forge a Soul Catalyst: a floating orb that empowers the next 5 abilities cast (yours or an ally's) with 25% bonus spirit damage.
  - Weapon Ascension   | Transform | Spend 50 Soul Charge to temporarily ascend your weapon into a legendary spirit form for 12s; it gains massive damage and a unique effect based on your class (scythe, blade, staff, etc.).
  - Soulstone Cache    | Utility  | Store up to 30 Soul Charge in a pocket soulstone; release it later as a burst heal to yourself or an ally equal to 2 HP per stored charge.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Living Relic       | Summon   | Spend 60 Soul Charge to forge a Soul Totem; it stands for 20s, continuously granting nearby allies +15% damage and 10 Charge regeneration per 5s.
  - Grand Imbue        | Buff/Defense     | Spend 50 Soul Charge to forge the ultimate temporary enchantment: target ally (or self) gains +30% to all damage, 20% damage reduction, and spirit afterimages on every attack for 15s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Soul Apotheosis    | Transform | Spend 80 Soul Charge to enter an Apotheosis state for 20s: your body becomes partially soul-matter, all your Soul Forge buffs cost zero Soul Charge and can be applied simultaneously to yourself and all allies within 15m; at the end of Apotheosis each active buff detonates dealing AoE spirit damage equal to its original cost x10.

>> Your notes on SOUL_FORGE:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Soulbind (Tether, Shared Fate, Link Mechanics)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Connect souls between targets with invisible threads so that what happens to one ripples through all — sharing damage, healing, and death across an entire linked network.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Soulthread         | Debuff   | Tether two enemies together with a soul thread; 25% of damage dealt to one is also dealt to the other. Lasts 8s.
  - Lifeline           | Buff/Heal     | Bind your soul to an ally; 20% of healing you receive is also applied to them for 10s. Costs a small amount of Soul Charge.
  - Resonance          | Passive/Utility  | Each active Soulbind tether you maintain reduces your ability cooldowns by 0.3s per tether when you deal damage.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Pain Conduit       | Active   | Redirect a portion of incoming damage you would take (30%) and distribute it equally across all enemies with active tethers.
  - Soul Network       | Active   | Extend Soulthread to connect up to 4 enemies in a chain; all damage dealt to any node propagates at 15% efficiency to all others.
  - Bind Destiny       | Debuff   | Tether an enemy to a nearby ally simultaneously; the enemy takes damage whenever the ally is healed (equal to 50% of the heal amount).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Thread Snap        | Active   | Violently sever a Soulthread, dealing burst spirit damage to both ends of the tether equal to the total damage that passed through it.
  - Web of Fate        | Active   | Spend 30 Soul Charge to bind all enemies within 12m into a single Soul Web; any damage dealt to the web's strongest node deals 30% to all others for 10s.
  - Shared Mortality   | Passive  | If any enemy tethered to another dies, the surviving tethered enemy loses 15% of their max HP instantly as sympathetic death shock.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Soul Mirror        | Reactive | When you would take lethal damage, a Soulthread snaps and redirects that killing blow to the strongest tethered enemy instead; 60s cooldown.
  - Inverse Bind       | Debuff   | Bind an enemy to themselves: 40% of any healing or damage they self-apply is reflected back as damage; lasts 12s or until the thread absorbs 3 healing events.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Grand Weave    | Active   | Weave every living entity in a 25m radius — allies and enemies — into a single grand soul tapestry for 12s: all damage is equalized across all nodes every 2s (redistributed to the mean); on expiry the tapestry collapses, dealing spirit damage to every enemy equal to the total damage they caused during the weave and healing every ally for the same amount.

>> Your notes on SOULBIND:
>>
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON SOUL WEAVER]
>
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 49: Technomancer (Magic + Technology)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A rogue engineer-wizard who has discovered that magic is simply code the universe executes, and has learned to compile their own instructions — running scripts against reality, overclocking arcane circuits, and deploying autonomous construct agents with machine intelligence.

ARCHETYPE A: Overwrite (Hack Reality, Code-Spells, Exploit Glitches)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Treat the world as a system with exploitable vulnerabilities — inject malicious instructions into enemy routines, debug the battlefield, and execute admin-level commands no ordinary mage can access.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Syntax Strike      | Attack   | Fire a compact hex-packet at an enemy; deals moderate arcane damage and tags them with a Debug Marker for 8s (enables exploit abilities).
  - Null Pointer       | Debuff   | Inject a null command into a target's action queue; their next active ability fails to execute and goes on cooldown anyway.
  - Cache Flush        | Cleanse  | Purge corrupted instructions from yourself or an ally, removing 2 debuffs and briefly boosting their action speed by 20% for 4s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Buffer Overflow    | Attack   | Overload a Debug Marked target with excess data; they take burst arcane damage and are Stunned for 1.5s as their systems reboot.
  - Exploit Routine    | Active   | Run a scan on all enemies within 15m; for 6s you can see their cooldown states, HP percentages, and buffs, and your next attack against each gains 20% bonus damage.
  - Patch Denial       | Debuff   | Apply a firewall-blocker to a Debug Marked enemy: any cleanse or buff applied to them has a 50% chance to fail for the next 8s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Root Access        | Active   | Gain temporary admin-level override for 10s: your abilities ignore resistances, deal 30% extra damage, and one random environmental element (doors, traps, platforms) activates in your favor.
  - Fork Process       | Active   | Duplicate the last ability cast, executing it again for free at 70% effectiveness. If cast on a Debug Marked target, it executes at full effectiveness.
  - System Crash       | CC       | Trigger a forced shutdown in a target with 3+ active debuffs; they are fully incapacitated for 3s then are Slowed by 40% for 4s as they reboot.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Recursive Loop     | Channel  | Channel for 2s on a Debug Marked target; at the end, lock them in a recursive behavior loop for 5s: they repeat their last action (attack, movement, or skill) uncontrollably each second.
  - Zero Day           | Active   | Deploy a previously unknown exploit; this ability does something different each combat encounter (drawn from a pool: AoE silence, mass slow, invert healing, reveal stealth, or gravity reversal in a zone).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - God Mode           | Transform | Execute a root-level command against reality for 12s: your abilities cost no resource, all Debug Markers refresh instantly, cooldowns are halved, and any target you kill has a 50% chance to be converted into a temporary Ghost Process ally that fights for you for 8s before deleting itself.

>> Your notes on OVERWRITE:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Mana Circuit (Tech-Enhanced Spells, Amplify, Overclock)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Weave technomagic circuits into your spellcasting to amplify output far beyond normal limits, at the risk of dangerous overloads.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Circuit Tap        | Buff     | Install a mana circuit into your spellcasting for 10s; your next 3 spells cost 15% less resource and deal 10% bonus damage.
  - Overclock          | Buff/Risk     | Overcharge your mana processor: for 6s your cast times are halved but each spell drains 10% extra mana.
  - Efficiency Matrix  | Passive  | Passively reduce all ability costs by 5%; for every 3 abilities cast in a row without stopping, gain a Circuit Stack (max 5) that further reduces cost by 2% per stack.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Signal Amplifier   | Active   | Place a relay amplifier on the battlefield for 15s; all your spells cast within 12m of it gain +25% area or range and +15% damage.
  - Thermal Bleed      | Passive  | When you Overclock, excess heat becomes a weapon: enemies within 4m take minor fire-arcane damage per second during the Overclock period.
  - Parallel Process   | Active   | Cast two different abilities simultaneously; one fires normally, the second fires at 50% effect. Cannot use the same ability twice. 20s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Feedback Loop      | Reactive | When an enemy ability hits you, your mana circuits absorb a portion of the energy; gain Circuit Stacks equal to the damage taken / 50 (max 5 per hit).
  - Overclock Burst    | Active   | Spend all current Circuit Stacks to unleash a supercharged spell: your next ability deals bonus damage equal to 20% per stack consumed and fires twice.
  - Mana Condenser     | Passive/Utility  | Your resource regenerates 20% faster when below 30% mana; when you reach zero mana, your circuits shock you for minor damage but immediately restore 25% mana (once per 30s).

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Arc Cascade        | Active   | Chain arcane lightning through all enemies within 12m sequentially; each hop deals 10% less damage than the last but applies a Circuit Disruption debuff reducing their damage by 10% for 8s.
  - Mana Singularity   | Channel  | Channel for 3s, drawing in ambient mana from the environment; at full channel, immediately restore 40% of max mana and release a shockwave dealing damage equal to mana restored.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Total Overclock    | Transform | Enter Total Overclock for 15s: all ability costs drop to zero, all Circuit Stack bonuses are maxed, and each ability you cast automatically fires a second free Parallel Process echo at 80% power; when the state ends your circuits burn out, disabling non-passive abilities for 4s.

>>

ARCHETYPE C: Golem OS (Construct AI, Autonomous Units, Upgrade Systems)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Design and deploy semi-intelligent construct units, continuously upgrading their operating systems mid-combat so they adapt to the enemy and fight with increasing sophistication.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Deploy Scout       | Summon   | Deploy a Scout Drone that patrols an area (15m radius), revealing stealth and marking enemies for 6s after detection; fragile but fast.
  - Deploy Chassis     | Summon   | Assemble a basic Combat Chassis that attacks nearby enemies; starts with low stats but accepts OS upgrades to improve.
  - Uplink             | Passive  | Your active constructs share sensor data; you always know their health, position, and target — and they gain 10% accuracy from the coordinated network.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Install: Combat OS | Buff     | Upload a Combat OS to one Chassis; it gains +30% damage, learns to prioritize dangerous enemies, and can now use a single basic ability.
  - Install: Shield OS | Buff/Shield     | Upload a Shield OS to one Chassis; it gains a reflective shield absorbing 300 damage and learns to position itself between you and enemies.
  - Overclock Construct | Active  | Temporarily overclock a construct's processor for 8s; it acts 50% faster and deals 20% bonus damage, but takes 10% of its max HP in heat damage per second.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Deploy Siege Unit  | Summon   | Deploy a slow-moving Siege Construct that launches explosive arcane shells every 4s in a targeted direction, dealing AoE damage (15m range, 4m blast).
  - Adaptive Learning  | Passive  | Each time a construct kills an enemy, it permanently gains 5% damage (max +25%); if a construct dies, 50% of its accumulated learning is transferred to the next construct you summon.
  - Install: Medic OS  | Buff/Heal     | Upload a Medic OS to one Chassis; it stops attacking and instead repairs nearby constructs for 50 HP per second and can apply a 3s repair burst to allies.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Neural Splice      | Active   | Splice the operating systems of two constructs together; the resulting merged unit has the combined abilities of both with 50% stat bonus, but returns to a single construct if one's OS is overwritten.
  - Emergency Protocol | Reactive | When any construct drops below 20% HP, it automatically installs a Survival OS: it becomes immune to damage for 3s and either retreats or self-destructs for an AoE explosion (your choice, set on ability slot activation).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Golem Singularity  | Active   | Initiate the Golem Singularity Protocol: all your constructs merge into a single massive Apex Golem with HP equal to the sum of all merged units, damage equal to 200% of the best unit, and a unique ability from each OS installed; the Apex Golem fights autonomously and intelligently for 30s, then disassembles back into individual units with 50% HP.

################################################################################
CLASS 50: Wind Runner (Air/Speed)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A fighter who has tamed the wind itself — striking with the force of gales, vanishing into currents of air, and sculpting tornadoes that drag the battlefield into beautiful, spinning chaos.

ARCHETYPE A: Gale Blade (Wind Melee, Extended Reach, Pressure Cuts)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Channel wind pressure through your weapon strikes to extend their range and force far beyond what any physical blade should achieve.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Wind Slash         | Attack   | Release a crescent of compressed air with a melee swing; deals damage at 6m range as if in melee, striking targets you cannot physically reach.
  - Pressure Edge      | Passive  | Your melee attacks generate 1 Wind Pressure stack per hit (max 5); each stack increases your next Wind Slash's damage by 8%.
  - Updraft Step       | Movement | Leap 8m forward on a compressed air burst, immediately following up with an automatic Wind Slash on landing.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Gale Cross         | Attack   | Swing twice in rapid succession releasing two crossing air blades; each deals moderate damage and together they briefly pin a target in a crosswind for 0.8s.
  - Wind Drive         | Active   | Spend all Wind Pressure stacks to charge a single devastating air-strike dealing massive damage; range extends by 1m per stack spent.
  - Razor Gale         | Passive  | Your Wind Slashes now cause minor Bleed (1 stack) when they hit; the wind's edge is keen enough to cut even at range.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Storm Step Combo   | Attack   | Chain 3 rapid teleport-dashes (3m each) in different directions, releasing a Wind Slash at each endpoint simultaneously; all three slashes hit within 0.5s.
  - Pressure Burst     | Active   | Collapse all wind pressure in a 10m cone in front of you: deals damage based on stacks, knocks enemies back 4m, and strips 1 buff per stack spent.
  - Vortex Blade       | Stance   | Toggle: your weapon is wreathed in spinning wind; melee hits deal 20% bonus wind damage and pull enemies 1m closer on each strike.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Infinite Edge      | Passive  | Your Wind Slashes have no effective maximum range (extend to 15m) and pierce through all enemies in their path, hitting everyone in a line.
  - Hurricane Slash    | Attack   | Wind up for 1s then release an enormous 180-degree arc of compressed air; deals massive damage to all enemies within 12m and launches them into the air for 1.5s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Pressure  | Active   | For 12s your attacks generate a catastrophic pressure field: every swing releases a Wind Slash automatically in all directions simultaneously (360 degree), Wind Pressure stacks generate at double rate, and each expended stack creates an additional pressure echo that strikes a random enemy within 15m.

>>

ARCHETYPE B: Zephyr (Evasion, Mobility, Untouchable)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Become so attuned to air currents that attacks pass harmlessly through you as you slip between gusts, making you nearly impossible to pin down or land a clean hit on.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Wind Step          | Movement | Dash 5m in any direction on a cushion of air; brief invincibility frame (0.3s) during travel.
  - Air Sense          | Passive/Evasion  | Passively detect incoming attacks via air pressure disturbance; gain 10% evasion and a visual cue 0.5s before a heavy attack lands.
  - Slip Current       | Reactive | When struck by a melee attack, automatically dart 3m backward on reflex; 6s cooldown.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Feather Form       | Buff/Evasion     | Reduce your weight to near-zero for 6s: movement speed +40%, evasion +20%, and all knockback or crowd control effects are negated.
  - Whirlwind Dodge    | Movement | Spin 360 degrees while dashing in a circle (4m radius) at high speed; evades all attacks during the spin and knocks enemies outward on completion.
  - Counter Gust       | Reactive | When you successfully evade an attack, immediately retaliate with a Wind Slash counterattack at no cost; 4s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Zephyr Veil        | Stealth  | Wrap yourself in barely-visible air currents; enter a semi-stealth state where you cannot be targeted by abilities (but can still take splash damage) for 5s.
  - Speed of Air       | Passive  | When you have evaded 3 attacks in the last 6s, your movement speed doubles and your next attack deals 50% bonus damage (Zephyr Burst).
  - Drift              | Toggle   | Toggle: you float 1m above the ground, becoming immune to ground-based effects (traps, slow fields, earthquakes); your movement speed increases by 15% but you cannot stop moving.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Phase Wind         | Active   | For 4s become almost entirely intangible: all physical attacks have an 80% chance to pass through you; you deal reduced damage (50%) but cannot be Stunned or Rooted.
  - Eternal Current    | Passive/Evasion  | Your evasion stat now also contributes 50% of its value to your damage reduction against magic attacks; you are never fully stationary — even standing still you exhibit micro-movements that count as evasion checks.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Eye of the Storm   | Transform | For 15s become the literal eye of a personal storm: you are completely immune to all damage, move at triple speed, and any attack directed at you auto-redirects to a random nearby enemy at full original damage; when the state ends the storm collapses outward dealing AoE damage to all enemies within 20m.

ARCHETYPE C: Cyclone (Tornado, AoE Pull, Vortex Control)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Conjure and steer tornadoes across the battlefield, pulling enemies into grinding vortex cores and controlling the entire fight through superior air dominance.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Summon Twister     | Summon   | Conjure a small tornado (3m radius) at a target location; it remains stationary for 6s, dealing damage per second and Slowing enemies inside by 25%.
  - Updraft            | Active   | Fire a focused updraft under a target, launching them 6m into the air for 1.5s (airborne state, disabling ground abilities).
  - Wind Tunnel        | Debuff   | Create a directional wind channel between two points (up to 12m apart); all enemies in the tunnel are pushed toward the far end at moderate speed for 4s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Steer Cyclone      | Active   | Take control of a summoned tornado and slowly move it in a direction of your choice for up to 6s; enemies flee but are often caught by its edge.
  - Vortex Pull        | Active   | Activate a reverse updraft in a 10m radius: all enemies are pulled 6m toward the center point simultaneously.
  - Chain Twisters     | Summon   | Summon 3 small tornados simultaneously in a triangle formation (10m apart); they orbit each other slowly and share damage to anything between them.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - F3 Tornado         | Summon   | Summon a large (8m radius) tornado that slowly tracks the nearest enemy cluster; deals heavy continuous damage and applies Airborne to enemies it engulfs.
  - Eye Wall           | Active   | Create a ring of condensed wind (15m radius) around yourself; enemies outside the ring cannot enter and enemies inside cannot leave for 6s.
  - Cyclone Merge      | Active   | Merge two active tornados together: the result is a single massive tornado (combined radius + 50%) with doubled damage output and double remaining duration.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Supercell          | Channel  | Channel for 4s to build a supercell storm system; release to drop an F5-level tornado (15m radius) at a target location for 10s; too large for enemies to escape without significant movement.
  - Aerial Supremacy   | Passive  | While any tornado is active on the battlefield, you gain 25% movement speed and your Airborne-inducing abilities have their duration extended by 1s; enemies that are Airborne take 30% bonus damage from all your attacks.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Great Cyclone  | Summon   | Merge all your active tornados and summon additional ones to form a single colossal tornado (30m radius) that fills the entire battlefield for 20s; it continuously pulls all enemies toward the center (2m per second), deals massive ongoing damage, and enemies carried to the center are launched upward in an explosion of compressed air dealing burst damage on landing.
