################################################################################
CLASS 51: Radiant (Light Magic)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A wielder of pure concentrated light who can collapse photons into searing beams, scatter them into kaleidoscopic networks of simultaneous strikes, or weave them into luminous shields and regenerative auras that sustain allies through the darkest battles.

ARCHETYPE A: Beam Focus (Laser Damage, Concentration, Sustained Fire)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Concentrate light into a devastating single-point weapon — a sustained beam that intensifies the longer it holds on a target, capable of cutting through even the heaviest defenses.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Radiant Bolt       | Attack   | Fire a focused photon bolt at a target, dealing moderate light damage and briefly Blinding them (0.5s vision reduction).
  - Concentration      | Passive  | Each consecutive second you deal light damage to the same target, your light damage against them increases by 5% (stacks to 40%).
  - Bright Line        | Attack   | Draw a thin laser line across a 12m path; deals light damage to every enemy it crosses and marks them (ignoring 5% of their light resistance).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Sustained Beam     | Channel  | Channel a continuous laser at a target; deals steady damage per second, increasing by 10% per second channeled (max 5s); requires maintaining line of sight.
  - Focal Point        | Active   | Plant a lens crystal on the battlefield for 12s; Sustained Beam routed through it splits into two parallel beams, both dealing full damage.
  - Burn Through       | Passive  | After 3 consecutive seconds of beam damage on a single target, your beam ignores 25% of their armor and resistance for the remainder of that channel.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Supernova Pulse    | Active   | Release all accumulated Concentration stacks in a single burst; deals damage equal to 50% per stack in a concentrated 5m cone at point-blank range.
  - Orbital Strike     | Active   | Call down a focused beam of pure sunlight from above onto a 4m radius area; deals massive light damage and leaves a Scorched Zone (half damage, movement penalty) for 5s.
  - Phase Coherence    | Passive  | Your Sustained Beam is now coherent-phase light; it is not blocked by non-magical barriers or shields and passes through thin cover (walls < 0.5m, shields, most summons).

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Death Ray          | Channel  | A slow-sweeping 180-degree channel (3s): the beam cuts across all enemies in the arc, dealing very high damage on initial pass and 50% on a return sweep automatically.
  - Lens Array         | Active   | Deploy 3 lens crystals simultaneously (placed by you in a triangle); any beam ability you fire bounces between all lenses indefinitely for 8s, hitting enemies caught between them repeatedly.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Solar Pinnacle     | Transform | Become a living star for 12s: your body radiates unbounded light dealing AoE damage per second to all enemies within 20m; Sustained Beam becomes a tri-beam (three simultaneous channels to different targets); all Concentration stacks are shared across all targets simultaneously; and any enemy reduced to 20% HP is incinerated instantly by a final focused burst.

>> Your notes on BEAM_FOCUS:
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Prism Array (Split Light, Multi-Target, Refraction)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Exploit light's refractive nature to strike many targets at once, building a lattice of splitting beams and ricocheting photons that cover the entire battlefield.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Prismatic Shot     | Attack   | Fire a light bolt that splits into 3 smaller bolts on impact; each smaller bolt deals 40% of original damage and seeks the nearest unique enemy.
  - Refraction         | Passive  | Your light abilities that split or ricochet deal 10% bonus damage per additional target they've already hit in the same chain.
  - Crystal Shard      | Active   | Throw a prism crystal to a location; the next light ability you fire that passes through it splits into 5 beams radiating outward in a star pattern.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Arc Light          | Attack   | Chain a bolt of light between up to 5 enemies (10m max hop distance); each hop deals 85% of the previous hit's damage.
  - Mirror Trap        | Active   | Place a floating mirror at a point; the next enemy ability or your own beam ability that hits the mirror is reflected at a different target at full damage.
  - Rainbow Barrage    | Active   | Fire 7 bolts in quick succession; each is a different wavelength (color) that applies a unique minor effect (red: burn, orange: slow, yellow: blind, green: poison, blue: chill, indigo: silence, violet: weaken).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Prism Field        | Active   | Fill a 15m zone with floating prism shards for 8s; every light ability fired within or through this field automatically splits and ricochets between all enemies in the zone.
  - Light Cascade      | Active   | Trigger a cascade: the next light hit on any enemy bounces to 3 others, each of those bounces to 2 others, until chain reaches 12 total hits or no new targets are available.
  - Kaleidoscope       | Channel  | Channel for 2s while spinning prisms around yourself; at release, fire a dense multi-directional explosion of 20 prismatic bolts in all directions.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Spectral Divide    | Passive  | Any single-target light ability you use automatically fires a 30% power phantom copy at a second random enemy within 15m, with no additional cost.
  - Full Spectrum      | Active   | For 8s each hit of a chained or split light ability now applies one of the Rainbow Barrage status effects in sequence; enemies hit by all 7 suffer an 8th effect: Exposed (take 30% bonus damage from all sources for 5s).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Refraction | Active  | Detonate a light singularity at a central point: it refracts outward in a perfect sphere, automatically chaining between every enemy in a 25m radius 3 times each, applying all Rainbow Barrage effects simultaneously; any enemy that dies from the refraction explodes in a burst of light that triggers one additional Arc Light chain from their position.

ARCHETYPE C: Aurora Veil (Light Shields, Healing, Protective Auras)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Weave light into living armor and regenerative halos, sustaining allies through attrition by converting photon energy into protective and restorative force.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Photon Ward        | Buff/Shield     | Wrap an ally (or yourself) in a light shield absorbing 150 damage for 8s; while active the shielded target emits light that slightly Blinds attackers.
  - Solar Mend         | Heal     | Channel sunlight into a target, healing them for a moderate amount over 3s; healing is increased by 20% in open areas with natural light.
  - Lux Aura           | Aura     | Toggle: radiate a gentle healing aura within 8m; allies inside recover a small amount of HP per second. Slightly drains your resource continuously.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Luminous Shell     | Buff/Shield     | Create a durable light barrier around an ally for 10s; the first ability that would deal damage above 20% of their max HP is completely negated and releases a Blind flash on the attacker.
  - Aurora Band        | Buff/Defense     | Link yourself to an ally with an aurora thread; 30% of damage they take is redirected to your light shield instead (must have Photon Ward active on yourself).
  - Daybreak           | Heal     | Release a pulse of dawn-light in 10m radius; heals all allies for moderate HP and cleanses 1 negative effect from each.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Beacon of Hope     | Summon   | Plant a Beacon of pure light at a location for 15s; allies within 8m of the beacon gain +15% healing received and regenerate 2% max HP per second.
  - Prismatic Fortress | Buff/Shield     | Encase an ally in a full prismatic shell for 6s: they are immune to all damage, but also cannot act; on expiry the shell shatters, dealing light damage to all enemies within 5m.
  - Dawn's Renewal     | Heal     | Invoke a powerful single-target heal: restore 40% of target's max HP instantly, then grant them Regeneration (2% HP/s) for 8s; if the target was below 20% HP, also cleanse all debuffs.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Solar Plexus       | Aura     | Upgrade your Lux Aura to Solar Plexus: healing output doubles, range extends to 15m, and allies at full HP within the aura gain a stacking 5% damage bonus (max 20%) instead of healing.
  - Light Transfer     | Active   | Absorb 50% of all active Photon Wards and Luminous Shells on your allies and redirect them as a single concentrated shield onto the most injured ally; that shield is worth 120% of the total absorbed.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Eternal Aurora     | Active   | Call down an aurora curtain across the entire battlefield for 20s: all allies are continuously healed for 3% max HP per second, all incoming damage to allies is reduced by 20%, any ally that dies during the aurora is immediately resurrected to 30% HP with a full Luminous Shell (this resurrection can only trigger once per ally per aurora); when the aurora ends it pulses outward dealing light damage equal to all healing done during its duration split across all enemies.

################################################################################
CLASS 52: Void Knight (Anti-Magic Tank)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A warrior who has stared into the absence of all arcane force and returned armored in nothingness — a walking dead zone who silences casters, consumes spells as food, and reflects magic through plates of compressed void-matter.

ARCHETYPE A: Null Field (Silence Zone, Magic Absorption, Dead Zone)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Project an expanding anti-magic field that strips enemies of their spellcasting, turning even the most dangerous mages into helpless mundane fighters.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Null Pulse         | Active   | Release a brief anti-magic pulse in 8m radius; all enemy spellcasting within range is interrupted and the interrupted abilities go on full cooldown.
  - Silence Blade      | Attack   | Strike a target with void energy; deals moderate damage and Silences them (cannot use magic abilities) for 3s.
  - Dampening Field    | Aura     | Toggle: maintain a persistent anti-magic aura within 5m of yourself; enemy spells cast within this range deal 25% less damage and cost 20% more resource.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Dead Zone          | Active   | Project a 10m radius Null Field at a location for 8s; no magic abilities can be cast or activated inside the zone (mundane attacks still function).
  - Void Aegis         | Passive/Immunity  | You are immune to magical Silence, Interrupt, and Mana Drain effects; additionally you take 10% less magic damage for each second you remain inside a Null Field.
  - Frequency Jam      | Debuff   | Apply a specific anti-resonance to an enemy caster; they cannot use abilities of a chosen school (fire, ice, lightning, etc.) for 8s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Expanding Null     | Active   | Deploy a Null Field that grows over 6s from 5m to 20m radius, sweeping outward to catch fleeing casters; deals minor void damage to enemies as it passes through them.
  - Spell Void         | Passive  | While inside your own Null Field or Dead Zone, your melee attacks deal +20% damage and you gain 1 Void Stack per enemy ability that fails within your field (max 10).
  - Gravity of Nothing | CC       | Collapse a Dead Zone inward instead of expiring naturally; all enemies within are pulled to the center point and Stunned for 2s as the null gravity implodes.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Silence      | Active   | Project a global Null Field across the entire battlefield for 6s; all magic abilities are suppressed for all enemies regardless of position; your own abilities function normally.
  - Null Resonance     | Passive  | Each Void Stack increases your magic resistance by 4% and your next melee attack by 6% damage; spending all 10 stacks at once (with Void Release) detonates a Null Shockwave.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Zero Field | Active  | Become the epicenter of total magical nullification for 12s: a 25m radius Dead Zone follows you, your melee attacks Silence on each hit (2s per hit), all Void Stacks are maxed immediately, and any spell that attempts to enter the field is absorbed and converted to 5% HP recovery for you; when the ability expires, the absorbed spell energy detonates as a void explosion dealing damage equal to all spells absorbed x200%.

>> Your notes on NULL_FIELD:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Spell Devour (Eat Spells, Power Absorption, Counter-Cast)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Rather than deflecting magic, consume it — let spells hit you, absorb their energy, and channel that power back as devastating counter-abilities.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Hungry Void        | Passive/Defense  | When you take magic damage, 15% of that damage is absorbed as Spell Essence instead of damaging you; Essence fuels Devour abilities.
  - Void Stomach       | Passive/Defense  | Increase your maximum Spell Essence capacity by 50 (base 100); also increases your magic damage reduction by 5%.
  - Essence Spit       | Attack   | Spend 20 Spell Essence to spit a bolt of consumed spell energy at a target; deals damage equal to the type of spell most recently absorbed.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Open Maw           | Reactive | Stance for 2s: your next incoming spell is completely absorbed (taking zero damage) and fully converted to Spell Essence; 12s cooldown.
  - Regurgitate        | Active   | Spend 30 Spell Essence to vomit a cone of raw mixed spell energy (10m); deals moderate magic damage of a random type and applies a random debuff from recently absorbed schools.
  - Sympathetic Hunger | Passive  | Nearby ally magic users within 8m who take magic damage also funnel 10% of that damage as Spell Essence to you (your absorption applies, not theirs).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Copy Digest        | Active   | After absorbing a spell with Open Maw, spend 40 Essence to fire an identical copy of that spell at any target with your own targeting; lasts until the next absorption.
  - Void Metabolism    | Passive/Shield  | Your Spell Essence regenerates 5 per second passively; additionally, when your Essence pool is full, excess absorption converts to temporary HP shields (2:1 ratio).
  - Feral Consumption  | Stance   | Enter a Devouring Stance: all your magic resistance drops to zero (you take full magic damage) but ALL incoming magic damage converts to Spell Essence at 100% efficiency; lasts 6s.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Spell Mimicry      | Active   | Spend 50 Spell Essence to perfectly recreate the last spell absorbed (including all effects, range, and power) at 120% effectiveness; usable once per absorption event.
  - Bottomless Gullet  | Passive  | You can now absorb multiple different spell schools simultaneously; each unique school absorbed in the last 10s grants you a 5% bonus to that school's damage when you fire absorbed energy.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Devouring      | Transform | For 15s enter a state of absolute spell consumption: all magic aimed at you (and within 10m of you) is automatically absorbed at 100% efficiency regardless of source; you cannot be damaged by magic; your Essence capacity doubles; and every 3s you automatically release a burst of absorbed energy dealing AoE void damage in 15m equal to all Essence gained in those 3s; when the state ends, all remaining Essence detonates in one final cataclysmic blast.

>> Your notes on SPELL_DEVOUR:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Aegis of Nothing (Void Armor, Reflect, Inversion)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Clad yourself in armor forged of compressed void that doesn't just block magic — it inverts and reflects spells back at their casters with amplified force.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Void Plate         | Buff/Shield     | Activate a layer of void armor for 8s, absorbing up to 200 magic damage; when it shatters, release a small void burst (3m) dealing 50% of absorbed damage.
  - Invert Shield      | Reactive | When struck by a magic attack for more than 10% of your max HP, automatically reduce that damage by 30% and reflect 15% back at the caster; 8s cooldown.
  - Nothing Between    | Passive/Resistance  | Your physical armor also contributes 30% of its value toward magic damage reduction as void-hardened metal.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Mirror Nothing     | Active   | For 4s, become a perfect magic mirror: the next spell that targets you is completely negated and reflected at the caster for full damage; 15s cooldown.
  - Void Carapace      | Buff/Defense     | Encase yourself in a thicker void shell for 10s; take 20% less all damage and your melee attacks deal bonus void damage equal to 10% of current remaining shell HP.
  - Null Return        | Passive  | After successfully reflecting a spell (via any means), your next melee attack deals bonus void damage equal to 50% of that spell's damage.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Absorption Lattice | Active   | Weave a void lattice across yourself for 8s: all magic damage is reduced by 40% and one-third of all damage absorbed is reflected as void bolts at random enemies.
  - Void Form          | Buff/Evasion     | Briefly compress your body into a smaller void-matter state for 6s: become immune to all targeted spells (AoE still affects you), gain 30% evasion, and your reflect damage is doubled.
  - Consecrated Nothing | Aura    | Toggle: project a void aura (8m radius); allies within take 15% less magic damage; enemies within have all their spell reflections that hit them doubled in severity.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Inversion    | Active   | For 6s, any magic ability that targets you is fully inverted: heals become damage, buffs become debuffs, and damage becomes healing — all effects applied to their original caster; 40s cooldown.
  - Void Sentinel      | Passive/Shield  | While Void Plate or Void Carapace is active, automatically generate 1 Null Charge per 2s (max 5); spend all charges to instantly reconstruct a depleted void armor to full.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Aegis of Absolute Nothing | Stance | Enter the Aegis of Absolute Nothing permanently toggled for 15s: all magic damage you receive is reflected at 200% back to its source; physical damage you receive charges a void counter; at the end of the stance all charged physical damage is released as a directed void beam at the enemy who dealt the most physical damage to you during that period; you cannot die during this stance (excess lethal damage converts to void counter charge instead).

################################################################################
CLASS 53: Frostfire Adept (Ice + Fire Hybrid)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A paradox-wielder who has mastered both absolute cold and absolute heat simultaneously, using rapid temperature extremes to shatter enemies, build explosive steam engines, and forge weapons from the impossible boundary where ice and fire coexist.

ARCHETYPE A: Thermal Shock (Rapid Hot/Cold Cycling, Burst Damage)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Alternate between freezing and igniting a target in rapid cycles, exploiting the catastrophic physical stress of extreme temperature change to deal burst damage that scales with how quickly you switch.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Sear               | Attack   | Apply a Burning stack to a target (deals fire DoT for 4s); can stack up to 3 times.
  - Chill              | Attack   | Apply a Frozen stack to a target (reduces movement by 15% per stack for 4s); can stack up to 3 times.
  - Thermal Cycling    | Passive  | Alternating between a fire and ice ability on the same target generates 1 Thermal Stress stack (max 10); each stack adds 8% to the next Thermal Shock detonation.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Thermal Shock      | Active   | Detonate all Thermal Stress stacks on a target in an explosive temperature reversal; deals burst damage per stack and Stuns the target for 1s per 2 stacks.
  - Hot-Cold Combo     | Active   | Fire and ice hit the same target simultaneously; applies 1 Burning and 1 Frozen stack and immediately generates 3 Thermal Stress stacks.
  - Brittle Point      | Passive  | A target with both Burning and Frozen stacks active simultaneously has all incoming damage increased by 15% (the conflicting temperatures weaken their form).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Flash Point        | Attack   | Instantly apply 3 Burning stacks then immediately detonate them as a fire burst, all in one action; generates 3 Thermal Stress stacks as if you had cycled normally.
  - Ice Quench         | Attack   | Instantly apply 3 Frozen stacks then shatter them as an ice burst; generates 3 Thermal Stress stacks and briefly encases the target in ice (0.5s hard CC).
  - Stress Fracture    | Passive  | At 10 Thermal Stress stacks, a target begins passively cracking; they take 5% of their current HP as damage per second until Thermal Shock is detonated or they drop below 5 stacks.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Hypercycle         | Buff     | For 8s your fire and ice abilities no longer deplete when cast — applying to the same target automatically before any cooldowns; Thermal Stress generates at double rate.
  - Catastrophic Shock | Active   | Spend 10 Thermal Stress stacks to trigger a Catastrophic Shock: deals massive burst damage, Freezes the target for 2s, then immediately ignites them for 3s of fire DoT — the ignition can generate new stacks.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Thermal Collapse | Active | Trigger a total temperature singularity on one target: they are simultaneously flash-frozen and flash-ignited for 10s — every second they alternate between Frozen (x3) and Burning (x3) automatically, each alternation generating 3 Thermal Stress stacks; at the end of 10s all accumulated stacks detonate in a single Catastrophic Shock that also deals AoE splash damage (50% power) to all enemies within 8m.

>> Your notes on THERMAL_SHOCK:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Steam Engine (Vapor, Pressure, Environmental Control)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Combine fire and ice to produce superheated steam, building pressure in enemies and the environment to create explosive release mechanics and battlefield zone control.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Steam Vent         | Active   | Rapidly melt ice water and superheat it; fire a burst of scalding steam (6m cone), dealing moderate damage and applying Scalded (5% damage vulnerability) for 5s.
  - Pressure Build     | Passive  | Each Steam ability adds 1 Pressure Stack to all enemies it hits (max 8); stacks increase the damage of the next Pressure Release ability by 10% each.
  - Cold Injection     | Debuff   | Inject ice mana into a target's water content; they become partially Frozen on the inside — their movement speed is reduced by 20% and they take bonus steam damage.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Geyser             | Active   | Trigger a pressurized geyser from the ground at a target location (6m tall, 3m wide); enemies hit are launched upward and Scalded; the geyser persists for 4s.
  - Steam Cloud        | Active   | Fill a 10m radius area with dense steam for 8s; enemies inside are Blinded, take minor damage per second, and are Scalded; the cloud blocks projectiles.
  - Pressure Cooker    | Debuff   | Lock a target's Pressure Stacks in place for 6s; they cannot be reduced by any means and the next Pressure Release on them deals 50% bonus damage.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Boiler Burst       | Active   | Detonate all Pressure Stacks on a target in a pressurized explosion; deals burst damage per stack and fires steam shrapnel hitting enemies within 5m for 30% splash.
  - Engine Room        | Active   | Create a Steam Engine zone (12m radius) for 12s: fire and ice abilities inside the zone automatically generate steam clouds at no extra cost, and all Pressure Stacks build 50% faster.
  - Industrial Scale   | Passive  | Your Steam abilities can now apply Pressure Stacks to all enemies hit simultaneously (previously single-target); Boiler Burst also becomes an AoE centered on the primary target (6m radius).

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Turbine Form       | Stance   | Activate your Steam Engine internalized for 10s: steam vents from your body continuously, damaging nearby enemies (4m AoE per second), and each melee attack you land builds 2 Pressure Stacks; melee hits also Scald targets.
  - Zero to Boil       | Active   | Instantly bring a target with 0 Pressure Stacks to maximum (8 stacks) in 0.5s through a rapid heat-freeze-steam cycle; immediately follow with a Boiler Burst at no cooldown.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Grand Turbine  | Transform | Become a living industrial engine for 15s: your body radiates scorching steam in 20m (continuous AoE damage), all Pressure Stacks on all enemies build to maximum within 3s, Boiler Burst can be triggered simultaneously on all pressurized enemies at once, and a massive Geyser erupts under each enemy that dies during this state launching nearby survivors into the air.

>> Your notes on STEAM_ENGINE:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Flash Forge (Freeze then Shatter with Fire, Material Creation)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Use ice to instantly harden targets and the environment, then use fire to shatter that frozen state into catastrophic crystalline explosions — also forging physical ice-fire constructs mid-combat.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Flash Freeze       | Active   | Instantly encase a target in a thin layer of ice (1s hard CC); the ice layer is fragile — any damage breaks it.
  - Shard Strike       | Attack   | Shatter a frozen target with a fire punch or strike; deals bonus damage based on how quickly the shatter follows the freeze (within 1s: +50%, within 2s: +25%).
  - Forge Shard        | Utility  | Rapidly freeze and fire-harden a handful of shards mid-air; throw them as a spread shot (5 shards, 8m range), each dealing minor damage and applying 1 Thermal Stress.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Pillar Forge       | Active   | Flash-freeze a column of ground water and fire-harden it into a permanent ice-fire pillar at a location; it blocks movement, provides cover, and shatters into a 5m explosion if destroyed.
  - Cryo Burst         | Attack   | Freeze a target solid for 1.5s then trigger a timed detonation; on the second beat, internal fire ignites causing the ice to explode outward in shards (8m splash).
  - Sculpture          | Active   | Freeze and fire-sculpt a moving construct from the environment: a temporary ice-fire wolf, spike barrier, or bridge (your choice); each lasts 10s before melting.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Glass Cannon Forge | Active   | Forge a crystalline fire-ice cannon barrel; fire one devastating fused beam (both fire and ice simultaneously) in a line up to 20m, Freezing and Burning every target hit at once.
  - Brittle Crafting   | Passive  | All ice constructs you create (pillars, sculptures, etc.) are also loaded with pressurized fire; when they shatter they deal bonus fire-splash damage equal to 30% of the construct's remaining HP.
  - Mass Flash Freeze  | Active   | Flash-freeze all enemies in a 10m radius for 1s; immediately follow with a heat detonation that shatters all of them simultaneously in a chain of cascading ice-fire explosions.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Forge Armor        | Buff/Shield     | Flash-forge a suit of frostfire crystal armor onto yourself or an ally for 10s: absorbs 400 damage; when destroyed it explodes in a 6m burst dealing fire-ice damage.
  - The Shattering     | Active   | Force-freeze one target into a massive ice statue (3s stasis, immune to damage during); at end of duration or on command, detonate them for catastrophic AoE damage (15m) — the larger the enemy the more damage.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Flash Forge Apocalypse | Active | Trigger an instantaneous battlefield-wide Flash Forge event: all enemies are simultaneously frozen for 1s, then immediately shattered by a cascading internal fire detonation — the chain of shatters creates a recursive explosion loop where each shattered enemy's explosion freezes nearby enemies who then shatter as well; the cascade continues for 8s or until no new freezes can occur; environmental ice-fire constructs are also detonated, joining the chain.

################################################################################
CLASS 54: Illusionist (Trickery/Deception)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A mind-bending trickster who weaponizes perception itself — making phantom injuries real through belief, drowning enemies in an army of doppelgangers, and seizing control of enemy minds through hypnosis and charm that unravels from within.

ARCHETYPE A: Phantasm (Fake Damage Made Real, Belief Mechanic)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Project phantom wounds onto enemies; the damage is not real at first — but the longer an enemy believes the illusion, the more their conviction converts it into genuine lethal injury.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Phantom Strike     | Attack   | Appear to strike a target; they take no actual damage but accumulate 10 Belief (max 100); at 100 Belief, phantom damage becomes real.
  - Illusory Wound     | Debuff   | Manifest a convincing visual wound on a target; they lose 5% of their shown max HP visually (not actually) and gain 15 Belief as they react to the perceived injury.
  - Doubt Seed         | Passive  | Each second a target has any Belief above 0, they accumulate 2 additional Belief from self-doubt; weaker-minded enemies (minions) accumulate at double rate.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Cruel Illusion     | Active   | Show a target a fabricated vision of a devastating hit; they flinch (0.5s interrupt) and gain 25 Belief instantly; if already above 50 Belief, they also take minor real psychic damage.
  - Phantom Bleed      | Debuff   | Apply an illusory Bleed to a target; they believe they are bleeding (visible effect to them only) and gain 20 Belief; at 80+ Belief the phantom bleed deals real damage.
  - Mass Delusion      | Active   | Project phantom strikes to all enemies in a 12m radius; each gains 15 Belief and is briefly distracted (-10% accuracy) for 3s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Belief Cascade     | Passive  | When one target's Belief reaches 100 and phantom damage becomes real, adjacent enemies within 6m who witness it gain 20 Belief from the shock of seeing "real" damage occur.
  - Phantom Execution  | Active   | Show a target a perfect illusion of their own death; they gain 50 Belief instantly and are Feared for 2s (flee away from you uncontrollably).
  - Convincing Agony   | Debuff   | Apply a sustained pain illusion for 8s; target gains 8 Belief per second and their damage output drops by 15% as they react to phantom pain.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Full Conversion    | Active   | Instantly convert all Belief stacks on a target into real damage (1% of max HP per Belief point, maximum 100% of HP converted); this ability resets their Belief to 0.
  - Unshakeable Phantom | Debuff | Apply an unremovable phantom wound; cleanse abilities on the target do not remove their Belief stacks — the illusion is so deep it persists through magical intervention.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Last Illusion  | Active   | Project a perfect illusion of absolute death on all enemies within 20m: those below 50% HP gain 100 Belief instantly (and take full HP-based conversion damage); those above 50% HP gain 60 Belief and are Feared for 3s; any enemy that dies from Full Conversion triggered by this ability explodes in a psychic burst, transferring 40 Belief to nearby survivors — the cascade continues until no enemies have Belief stacks or all are dead.

>> Your notes on PHANTASM:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Mirror Hall (Clone Army, Misdirection, Deception)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Flood the battlefield with convincing duplicates of yourself and allies, forcing enemies to waste attacks on fakes while the real you strikes from unexpected angles.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Mirror Image       | Summon   | Create 2 illusory copies of yourself that mimic your movement and positioning; each absorbs 1 hit before shattering; copies cannot deal damage.
  - Decoy Dash         | Movement | Dash 8m in a chosen direction while leaving a stationary copy at your origin; enemies target the copy for up to 3s before it fades.
  - Trick Step         | Passive/Evasion  | After any of your copies is destroyed, gain 10% movement speed and 10% evasion for 3s (the enemy's wasted attack becomes your advantage).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Clone Army         | Summon   | Summon 4 mirror copies; they slowly walk toward nearby enemies (appearing to attack); copies shatter on any damage but each detonates into a brief Blind flash on destruction.
  - Perfect Double     | Active   | Create one high-fidelity clone that perfectly mirrors your attacks (deals 30% of your damage); it has 200 HP and lasts 12s or until destroyed.
  - Hall of Mirrors    | Active   | Fill a 15m zone with reflections for 8s; all enemies inside have a 40% chance to target a mirror reflection instead of a real target.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Identity Confusion | Active   | Swap your position with a Perfect Double instantly; enemies targeting you continue targeting your previous position for 1s as they fail to process the swap.
  - Echo Strike        | Passive  | When you attack while a Perfect Double is alive, your copy simultaneously attacks the same target at 50% damage; if both hits land within 0.1s, they trigger a brief Stun (0.5s).
  - Infinite Regress   | Summon   | Summon a clone that summons its own smaller clone every 4s (max 3 sub-clones); sub-clones deal 15% of your damage and have 50 HP each.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Mass Substitution  | Active   | Simultaneously swap all your clones' positions with random enemies in 15m range; enemies suddenly in clone positions are disoriented (Confused for 2s), attacking random targets.
  - Untraceable        | Stealth  | Briefly become actually invisible for 3s while 3+ copies are active; enemies continue targeting the copies; your next attack from stealth deals 100% bonus damage.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Infinite Hall  | Transform | For 15s saturate the entire battlefield with clones: the enemy cannot determine which target is real; 10 high-fidelity copies are active at all times (instantly replaced on destruction), all dealing 40% of your damage; every attack any clone receives reflects 20% back to the attacker; you gain Untraceable continuously during this state; at the end of the state all surviving copies converge on your position and detonate simultaneously in an implosion of deceptive energy.

>> Your notes on MIRROR_HALL:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Mesmerist (Hypnosis, Charm, Confusion)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Reach directly into enemy minds with irresistible hypnotic commands — charming them to fight for you, confusing them into attacking allies, and unraveling their perception of reality.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Hypnotic Gaze      | CC       | Lock eyes with a target within 6m; they are Rooted for 2s as long as you maintain line of sight (broken by looking away or taking damage).
  - Suggestion         | Debuff   | Whisper a mental command to a target; they obey one simple behavioral instruction for 3s (move here, drop weapon, etc.); complex targets resist sooner.
  - Shimmer Mind       | Passive  | Your charm and confusion effects last 20% longer; additionally, targets under any of your mental effects take 10% bonus psychic damage.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Bewilderment       | CC       | Plunge a target into complete confusion for 4s; they attack the nearest entity to them regardless of ally or enemy status, with no preference.
  - Fascinate          | CC       | Lock a target in fascination for 5s (stand still, watch you); they are not incapacitated — if harmed by anyone other than you they snap out immediately.
  - Mass Suggestion    | Debuff   | Broadcast a mental command to all enemies within 10m: for 3s they all hesitate, moving at 50% speed and losing 20% accuracy as conflicting commands create indecision.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Deep Charm         | Active   | Fully charm a non-boss enemy for 8s; they join your side completely, fighting for you at full effectiveness; when charm expires they are Stunned for 1s from the dissonance.
  - Mind Fracture      | Debuff   | Shatter a target's mental coherence; for 6s they have a 30% chance to cancel any ability they attempt to cast and a 20% chance to target themselves instead of you.
  - Group Hypnosis     | CC       | Project a hypnotic wave in a 12m cone; all enemies hit are Rooted for 1.5s and their ability cooldowns are paused (not extended) during the root.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Puppet Master      | Active   | Maintain a deep hypnosis on up to 2 charmed targets simultaneously (via Deep Charm); both fight for you, and any ability one uses also has a 30% chance to trigger on the other.
  - Psychic Collapse   | Active   | Overwhelm a target's mind: they experience every negative mental effect simultaneously for 4s (confusion, fear, fascination, suggestion to flee) — effectively removing them from combat completely.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Grand Mesmerism    | Active   | Broadcast a total psychic override to all enemies within 30m: for 10s every enemy is simultaneously charmed and confused — charmed enemies believe you are their ally and attack each other, while confused enemies cannot determine friend from foe; any enemy that kills another enemy under Grand Mesmerism has their charm duration extended by 3s as the successful kill reinforces the illusion; when Grand Mesmerism expires, surviving enemies are Stunned for 2s from the mental whiplash.

>> Your notes on MESMERIST:
>>
>>

################################################################################
CLASS 55: Battlechef (Cooking Combat)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A culinary warrior who treats the battlefield like a kitchen, combining volatile ingredients mid-fight to produce explosive buffs, punishing flavor debuffs, and restorative feasts that keep their party fed and fighting long after lesser warriors have fallen.

ARCHETYPE A: Recipe Master (Combination, Crafting, Custom Effects)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Combines collected ingredients during combat to produce unique compound effects, rewarding preparation and improvisation with powerful customized outcomes.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Gather Ingredient    | Active   | Harvest a random ingredient from the environment (Spice, Fat, Acid, Sweet, Umami); stock up to 6 at once.
  - Quick Rub            | Buff     | Apply a single ingredient to an ally's weapon; each ingredient type grants a different 8s bonus (Spice: +fire dmg, Fat: +durability, Acid: +armor pen, Sweet: +lifesteal, Umami: +stagger).
  - Taste Test           | Passive  | At the start of combat, begin with 2 random ingredients already in stock.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Compound Marinade    | Buff     | Combine any 2 ingredients to apply a stacking dual-effect buff to an ally for 15s; combinations are tracked in your Recipe Book.
  - Recipe Book          | Passive  | Unlock 3 starter recipes; each discovered combination is permanently logged and can be repeated instantly without re-combining.
  - Mise en Place        | Active   | Prep 3 ingredients simultaneously at combat start; cooldown resets every 45s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Master Blend         | Buff     | Combine 3 ingredients to produce a high-tier compound potion: effects scale combinatorially (Acid+Spice+Umami = Corrosive Brine: burns armor and staggers per hit).
  - Secret Sauce         | Active   | Spend all current ingredients to create a random legendary condiment with a powerful unknown effect; higher ingredient count improves outcome quality.
  - Kitchen Instinct     | Passive  | Whenever you discover a new combination, instantly gain a 10s damage and haste buff — curiosity has its rewards.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Signature Dish       | Active   | Select one recipe from your Recipe Book and auto-apply its full compound effect to your entire party for 20s; 90s cooldown.
  - Reactive Reduction   | Reactive/Defense  | When an ally is critically hit, instantly consume 1 ingredient to coat them with a protective glaze, absorbing 30% of the next hit's damage.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Chef's Tasting Menu  | Channel  | Spend 4s plating a 7-course tasting menu; each course applies a stacked compound buff to each ally (one ingredient combination per course), resolving in sequence. The final course detonates all active ingredient buffs simultaneously into a massive explosion centered on all enemies within 15m, dealing damage equal to 80% of every active buff's bonus combined.

>> Your notes on Recipe Master:
>> The Recipe Book is the key design challenge — need a matrix of at least 10 combination outputs to make the system feel rich.
>> Secret Sauce is intentionally chaotic and should have a loot-table of ~15 outcomes, some bad (Burnt = DoT on self), most good, a few legendary.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Seasoning (Flavor Debuffs, Status Application, Utility)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Applies culinary flavor-state debuffs mid-combat that create layered weaknesses, exploiting taste interactions to compound enemy suffering.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Shake of Salt        | Debuff   | Apply Salty to a target: reduces their healing received by 20% and increases thirst (stamina drain +15%) for 10s.
  - Pepper Fling         | Debuff   | Hurl ground pepper into a target's face: inflict Spiced (Burning eyes: -20% accuracy, -10% crit chance) for 8s.
  - Vinegar Splash       | Debuff   | Douse a target in acid vinegar: apply Sour (-15% physical armor) for 10s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Bittersweet Glaze    | Debuff   | Apply both Bitter (reduces will to fight: -10% damage dealt) and Sweet (makes them a magnet for nearby enemies: aggro radius +50%) simultaneously for 12s.
  - Palate Clash         | Passive  | Targets afflicted with two or more flavor states take 15% increased damage from all sources.
  - Umami Overload       | Debuff   | Flood a target's senses with savory excess: apply Saturated (slowed, confused — 20% chance to misfire abilities) for 10s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Full Seasoning       | Debuff   | Apply all five flavor states (Salty, Spiced, Sour, Bitter, Sweet) to a single target at once for 8s; Palate Clash activates at maximum potency.
  - Flavor Transfer      | Active   | Strip all flavor debuffs from one target and instantly reapply them to a different target within 12m.
  - Acquired Taste       | Passive  | Each second an enemy maintains 3+ flavor states, they take escalating psychic damage from sensory overload (2% max HP per second, stacking up to 10%).

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Blanching Strike     | Attack   | Deal physical damage and apply Blanched (stripped defenses: -25% all resistances for 15s); Blanched enemies cannot resist flavor applications.
  - Chef's Critique      | Debuff   | Mark an enemy as Poorly Seasoned; all your flavor applications on them last 50% longer and have their stat penalties increased by 25%.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Overpowering Course | Active | Unleash a 10s assault of pure sensory warfare on all enemies within 12m: every 2s a new flavor state is applied to all targets, cycling through all five in sequence, each one amplified by Palate Clash, Acquired Taste, and any active passives — by the fifth cycle, enemies are staggered, burning, slowed, losing HP, and bleeding stamina simultaneously. Enemies who die under Full Seasoning explode in a flavor burst that reapplies one random flavor state to nearby foes.

>> Your notes on Seasoning:
>> The five flavor states need clear iconography — suggest color-coding (Salty=blue, Spiced=red, Sour=yellow, Bitter=purple, Sweet=pink).
>> Flavor Transfer is the standout skill for skilled play — burst one target to full seasoning then pivot it to a high-value target instantly.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Feast (AoE Party Sustain, Group Buffing, Cooking Ritual)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Transforms the battlefield into a dining hall, cooking large restorative feasts and communal dishes that sustain and empower the entire party over extended engagements.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Trail Ration         | Heal     | Toss a wrapped ration to an ally; restore moderate HP over 8s.
  - Camp Fire            | Heal   | Conjure a small campfire at a location; allies standing near it regenerate 2% max HP per second (radius 5m, lasts 20s).
  - Taste of Victory     | Passive  | Whenever an enemy dies within 10m of you, all nearby allies receive a minor morale snack buff: +5% damage for 5s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Broth of Fortitude   | Buff/Defense     | Ladle hearty broth to up to 3 allies; each gains +15% max HP and +10% physical resistance for 20s.
  - Spiced Stew          | Active   | Place a cauldron of spiced stew on the ground; allies in range gain +20% attack speed and minor HP regen for 15s.
  - Prep Kitchen         | Passive  | Reduce all feast and buff cooking skill cooldowns by 20%; stacks with other cooldown reductions.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Grand Feast          | Channel  | Channel 3s to lay out a full spread; all allies in 15m gain a custom 5-buff feast blessing lasting 30s (rotating from a pool of 10 possible boons).
  - Communal Pot         | Summon   | Deploy a persistent communal pot for 25s; allies can eat from it once each, healing 30% max HP and removing one debuff.
  - Banquet Aura         | Aura     | Emanate a warm food-smell aura in 8m; allies inside gain +10% healing received and their HP regen is doubled.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Last Supper          | Heal     | Restore a downed ally to 50% HP and remove all debuffs from them; they gain Satiated for 15s (+20% to all stats).
  - Chef's Table         | Active   | Upgrade the next feast or stew placed to legendary quality: double all effects and duration, and add a damage boost component not normally present.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Eternal Banquet  | Summon   | Conjure a Legendary Feast Table for 30s in a 20m radius; every ally is constantly fed throughout, receiving 3% max HP per second regeneration, immunity to hunger/stamina effects, a rotating feast buff that changes every 5s (cycling through Strength, Speed, Armor, Lifesteal, and Crit Rate boosts of 30% each), and on enemy kills within the feast zone, a fragment of the meal explodes outward dealing moderate AoE damage to all nearby foes.

>> Your notes on Feast:
>> Feast archetype favors longer fights and party compositions — position it as the premier support option for sustained dungeon runs vs. spike fights.
>> Grand Feast's 10-boon pool needs full design pass: suggest including unusual utility boons (Immunity to Fear, free movement through difficult terrain, etc.) to make it feel like a true feast spread.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON BATTLECHEF]
> Three distinct play modes: Recipe Master is the active crafter who rewards build knowledge; Seasoning is the aggressive debuffer who stacks flavor states for Palate Clash explosions; Feast is the dedicated party support sustainer.
> Cross-archetype synergy: Seasoning's Blanched status could reduce resistance to Feast buffs being applied (allowing Chef's Table to affect even resistant bosses). Recipe Master's Compound Marinade could use cooking ingredients that the Feast archetype generates passively.
> The Battlechef should feel distinctly chaotic and flavorful — lean into the absurdity while keeping the mechanics tight. Recommend a dedicated ingredient inventory UI rather than relying on hotbar slots.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 56: Warp Dancer (Teleportation Specialist)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A fighter who treats space itself as a weapon, flickering through reality in the blink of an eye to deliver precise strikes, weave networks of persistent portals across the battlefield, and scramble enemy formations by swapping positions with lethal precision.

ARCHETYPE A: Blink Striker (Teleport Melee, In-and-Out, Burst Combos)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Uses rapid short-range teleports as integral components of melee attack chains, making each engagement a vanishing act of incoming strikes from impossible angles.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Blink Strike         | Attack   | Teleport instantly to a target within 8m and strike them for moderate damage; 3s cooldown.
  - Afterimage           | Passive/Evasion  | Enemies who attack you immediately after a Blink Strike have a 25% chance to hit your lingering afterimage instead.
  - Phase Dash           | Movement | Teleport 6m in any direction without triggering attacks of opportunity; no target required.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Double Blink         | Attack   | Perform two rapid sequential Blink Strikes against the same target within 1.5s; second strike deals 130% damage.
  - Phantom Flurry       | Active   | Teleport around a single target 5 times in 3s, striking once from each angle for light damage; each hit applies a Disoriented stack (-5% accuracy per stack).
  - Slipstream           | Passive/Movement  | For 2s after any teleport, your movement speed is increased by 40% and your next melee attack deals +20% damage.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Vanishing Strike     | Stealth  | Teleport behind a target, immediately enter stealth for 1s, then launch a backstab dealing 250% damage; can chain into another Blink Strike.
  - Spatial Combo        | Passive  | Each consecutive Blink Strike on the same target within 5s increases damage by 15% (max 5 stacks); stacks reset if you strike a different target.
  - Fold Space           | Active   | Compress space around yourself and a target: pull them into melee range and simultaneously reposition yourself behind them, dealing light damage.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Phase Execution      | Attack   | Teleport through a target dealing heavy damage; if this kills them, immediately Blink Strike to the nearest enemy for free with full damage.
  - Warp Mastery         | Passive  | Blink Strike's cooldown is reduced by 0.5s for every enemy you have struck in the last 6s (max -2.5s reduction).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Dimensional Rampage  | Transform | Enter a 12s state of spatial fury: Blink Strike has no cooldown and each use generates a fresh Slipstream, Spatial Combo stacks never reset, and after each teleport you leave a spatial shockwave behind that deals AoE damage equal to 30% of the triggering strike — during this state you are effectively unhittable, flickering too fast for most tracking systems to register.

>> Your notes on Blink Striker:
>> The Spatial Combo stacking system is the core risk/reward loop — rewarding target focus vs. the instinct to spread damage.
>> Phase Execution's chain-kill condition will feel incredible; ensure the visual transition between kills is fast and readable.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Portal Keeper (Persistent Portals, Movement Control, Trap Network)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Places persistent linked portal pairs across the battlefield to control flow, create ambush lanes, and route both allies and projectiles through unexpected vectors.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Set Portal Alpha     | Active   | Place a Portal Alpha at your feet or a targeted location within 10m.
  - Set Portal Beta      | Active   | Place a Portal Beta, linking it to the most recent Alpha; any entity passing through Alpha exits Beta and vice versa. Portals last 30s.
  - Portal Sight         | Passive  | You can see and hear through your active portals as though standing at their location; radius 3m around each portal.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Extended Network     | Passive  | Increase maximum simultaneous portal pairs from 1 to 2; each pair operates independently.
  - Guided Projectile    | Active   | Fire a projectile into an Alpha portal; it exits from the Beta portal at full speed and original trajectory — can be used with any ranged attack or skill.
  - Reinforced Gate      | Active   | Fortify a portal for 15s: it blocks enemy passage while still allowing allies through; the barrier deals moderate damage to enemies who attempt to cross.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Tripwire Portal      | Active   | Set a concealed portal pair; the first enemy to step through Alpha is forcibly teleported and arrives stunned for 2s at Beta's location (which you choose).
  - Cascade Link         | Passive  | Portals now chain: exit from Beta can be re-routed through a second Alpha if one is within 3m of the Beta exit point, creating portal sequences.
  - Rapid Deployment     | Active   | Instantly deploy a portal pair at two targeted locations within 20m simultaneously; 45s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Killbox             | Active   | Designate two portals as a Killbox pair; any projectiles (enemy or ally) entering one portal loop between the pair, accelerating each loop (+20% damage per loop, max 5 loops) before exiting at the second portal as a hyper-velocity strike.
  - Permanent Waypoint   | Passive  | One portal pair per encounter can be designated Permanent; it does not expire until you replace it or the encounter ends.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Spatial Web          | Active   | Spend 4s weaving a 10-portal network across the battlefield (5 pairs auto-placed at tactically optimal positions within 30m); for the next 40s every ally can use any portal freely, all projectiles route through the web automatically for +40% damage bonus, enemies who enter any portal are randomly redirected to another portal's exit point, and the web pulses a spatial distortion every 8s that confuses enemy pathfinding in a 20m radius.

>> Your notes on Portal Keeper:
>> Cascade Link is the most technically demanding skill — requires robust portal chaining logic; cap loops at 3 without Killbox active.
>> Killbox has major potential for cheese builds; cap projectile loops at 5 and give enemies a chance to resist on higher difficulties.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Displacement (Position Swapping, Attack Redirection, Spatial Manipulation)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Scrambles positioning by swapping entity positions mid-combat, redirecting attacks through dimensional shortcuts, and weaponizing enemy momentum against them.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Body Swap            | Active   | Instantly exchange positions with a target ally within 15m; neither party takes damage from the swap.
  - Redirect             | Reactive | When targeted by a single-target projectile, teleport it mid-flight to a new target within 10m; 8s cooldown.
  - Dimensional Shove    | Active   | Displace an enemy 5m in any direction, ignoring terrain (can throw them off ledges or into walls for bonus impact damage).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Enemy Swap           | CC       | Swap the positions of two enemies within 15m of each other; both are briefly disoriented for 1.5s upon arrival.
  - Decoy Placement      | Active/Defense   | Swap your position with a conjured spatial decoy; the decoy absorbs the next 2 attacks before disappearing.
  - Spatial Judo         | Passive  | Whenever you displace an enemy, they take damage equal to 10% of their max HP from spatial collision forces.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Mass Displacement    | Active   | Swap the positions of all enemies within 12m with random others within that radius simultaneously; triggers Spatial Judo on each swap.
  - Intercept Swap       | Reactive | When an ally within 15m is targeted by a powerful attack, swap their position with the nearest enemy; the enemy takes the hit instead.
  - Phase Through        | Active   | For 4s, all attacks targeting you pass through you and continue in their original direction, potentially hitting enemies behind you.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Axis Flip            | Active   | Invert all enemy positions within 20m along a chosen axis (X or Y); enemies who were in the back suddenly find themselves at the front line and vice versa.
  - Displacement Cascade | Passive  | Each successful position swap within 6s of the last triggers a secondary 3m displacement on both swapped entities, chaining spatial impacts.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Reality Shuffle      | Active   | For 10s, activate total positional chaos across the battlefield within 25m: every 2s all entities (enemies only) are randomly repositioned to a new location within the radius, attacks they were mid-swing continue to their original trajectory (now likely hitting allies or other enemies), and each displacement triggers Spatial Judo at 200% potency — the battlefield becomes an absolute positional nightmare for any enemy relying on formation or positioning.

>> Your notes on Displacement:
>> Intercept Swap is the standout defensive support skill — a clutch save mechanic that also deals damage. Design should highlight this in tutorials.
>> Axis Flip needs a clear visual indicator of the axis before confirmation to avoid confusion; consider a ghost preview line.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON WARP DANCER]
> Three teleportation philosophies: Blink Striker uses teleportation offensively as attack delivery; Portal Keeper uses it architecturally to reshape the battlefield permanently; Displacement uses it tactically to manipulate entity positioning.
> Cross-archetype potential: Portal Keeper portals could amplify Blink Striker's Phase Dash (using portal exits as blink destinations mid-combo). Displacement's Mass Displacement could funnel enemies through Portal Keeper's Tripwire Portal for guaranteed stuns.
> Warp Dancer is mechanically intensive — recommend a dedicated teleport cooldown tracker UI element separate from standard ability bars.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 57: Astromancer (Star/Celestial Magic)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A celestial scholar who has decoded the language of stars and bends the light, fire, and gravity of distant suns to their will, harnessing stellar plasma for explosive destruction, wields the dual nature of eclipses to shift between radiant and void states, and channels the power of ancient zodiac constellations for deep combinatorial bonuses.

ARCHETYPE A: Starfire (Stellar Damage, Plasma, Cosmic Explosions)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Channels raw stellar energy as plasma and superheated light, building toward catastrophic stellar detonations that scorch everything in range.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Solar Bolt           | Attack   | Fire a concentrated bolt of stellar plasma dealing moderate fire/radiant damage to a single target.
  - Corona Burn          | Debuff   | Wrap a target in a coronal discharge: deal light damage per second and reduce their fire resistance by 15% for 10s.
  - Stellar Charge       | Passive  | Each attack builds 1 Stellar Charge (max 10); Charges increase the damage of the next plasma skill by 8% per stack consumed.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Plasma Lance         | Attack   | Spend 3 Stellar Charges to fire a piercing lance of plasma through all enemies in a 15m line for heavy damage.
  - Sunspot              | Active   | Place a burning sunspot at a location; enemies standing on it take escalating radiant damage per second for 10s (doubles every 2s it ticks).
  - Flare Burst          | Attack   | Detonate a burst of solar flares around yourself: deal moderate AoE radiant damage in 8m and blind all hit enemies for 2s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Stellar Collapse     | Active   | Spend 8 Stellar Charges to collapse a star at a target location: a 3s delay then catastrophic explosion dealing massive radiant damage in a 10m radius, leaving a burning stellar field for 8s.
  - Nova Remnant         | Passive  | Enemies killed in Stellar Collapse leave a Stellar Remnant for 12s; touching the remnant detonates it for moderate AoE damage and grants 5 Stellar Charges.
  - Prominence           | Active   | Launch a massive arcing solar prominence: hits up to 3 targets in a chain for heavy radiant damage, each bounce gaining 20% more damage.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Supernova Protocol   | Passive  | When Stellar Charges reach maximum (10), your next ability automatically triggers an additional free Flare Burst around the target at no cost.
  - Pulsar Barrage       | Channel  | Channel for 4s, firing rapid pulsar bolts at 5 per second at a target or target area, each dealing light radiant damage that builds Stellar Charges on hit.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Stellar Apotheosis   | Transform | For 15s you become a living star: Stellar Charges generate passively at 2 per second, Stellar Collapse's cooldown is eliminated, each cast of Stellar Collapse expands by 3m radius, and the Stellar Field left behind becomes self-sustaining — dealing heavy damage per second and generating Charges for you each second enemies stand in it. At the end of the transformation, release all accumulated Charges in one final supernova that deals 10x the normal Stellar Collapse damage.

>> Your notes on Starfire:
>> Stellar Charge is the resource management core — tension between spending for Plasma Lance or holding for Stellar Collapse creates the archetype's central decision.
>> Stellar Apotheosis's final supernova should scale with Charges accumulated during the transform window, rewarding players who manage Charges efficiently even during the buff.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Eclipse (Light/Dark Toggle, Dual-Mode, Phase Switching)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Exists in one of two states — Radiance (solar power, healing and light damage) or Umbra (void power, shadow damage and draining) — and generates power from switching between them at the right moment.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Radiant Strike       | Attack   | (Radiance only) Fire a bolt of pure light dealing moderate radiant damage; heals you for 15% of damage dealt.
  - Shadow Strike        | Attack   | (Umbra only) Fire a bolt of void energy dealing moderate shadow damage; applies Darkened (-10% damage output) for 6s.
  - Phase Shift          | Active   | Toggle between Radiance and Umbra; each shift generates 1 Eclipse Charge (max 5) and briefly empowers the next ability cast in the new mode by 30%.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Annularity           | Passive  | Entering Umbra immediately after dealing radiant damage (within 2s) extends Shadow Strike's Darkened debuff duration by 100%. Vice versa: entering Radiance after shadow damage boosts Radiant Strike's heal by 100%.
  - Penumbra             | Buff/Immunity     | (Either mode) Surround an ally in the opposite phase's energy; they are immune to the element they are coated in (Radiance ally = shadow immune, Umbra ally = radiant immune) for 10s.
  - Totality             | Active   | Spend 3 Eclipse Charges to trigger a momentary total eclipse: deal moderate radiant and shadow damage simultaneously to all enemies in 10m.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Solar Flare Reversal | Reactive | (Umbra) When you would take radiant damage, absorb it into an Eclipse Charge and convert 50% of the damage to shadow damage returned to the attacker.
  - Darklight Fusion     | Attack   | Spend 5 Eclipse Charges to unleash a combined radiant-shadow explosion dealing massive hybrid damage to a single target; ignores resistances of either damage type.
  - Horizon Pulse        | Active   | Emit a pulse wave at a Phase Shift: the wave applies Radiance to allies (minor HoT) and Umbra to enemies (minor DoT) simultaneously in 12m.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Perfect Eclipse      | Passive/Utility  | At exactly 5 Eclipse Charges, your next Phase Shift triggers a Perfect Eclipse: all enemies in 15m are simultaneously Blinded (by light) and Silenced (by dark) for 3s.
  - Tidal Resonance      | Passive  | Each Phase Shift increases your total damage by 3% for 10s, stacking independently per shift (max 6 stacks / 18% bonus at maximum shift speed).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Eternal Eclipse      | Toggle   | Lock yourself in Eternal Eclipse state for 20s — you exist in both Radiance and Umbra simultaneously: all abilities hit for both radiant and shadow damage, Eclipse Charges generate at 1 per second passively, Phase Shift can be cast freely without consuming a charge (but still triggers Annularity and Horizon Pulse bonuses), and Perfect Eclipse triggers every 5 Eclipse Charges rather than at maximum. During Eternal Eclipse you are immune to both radiant and shadow damage entirely.

>> Your notes on Eclipse:
>> The toggle design requires the UI to very clearly display current mode — consider a split circle indicator that fills with light/dark based on Eclipse Charges.
>> Annularity's timing window (2s) rewards skillful sequencing; could add a visual on the character body showing the window is active.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Zodiac (Constellation Buffs, Sign-Based Bonuses, Astral Alignment)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Channels the power of the twelve zodiac constellations, activating their sigils to grant persistent positional and tactical bonuses that compound across active signs.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Invoke Aries         | Buff     | Activate the Ram's sign for 20s: gain +15% melee and close-range damage; charges reset on kill.
  - Invoke Taurus        | Buff/Defense     | Activate the Bull's sign for 20s: gain +20% physical resistance and +10% max HP temporarily.
  - Read the Stars       | Passive  | At encounter start, one random zodiac sign is pre-active at no cost; you learn which sign 3s before combat begins.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Invoke Gemini        | Buff     | Activate the Twin's sign for 20s: every skill you cast fires a 50%-power echo that targets a secondary enemy within 8m.
  - Invoke Scorpio       | Debuff   | Activate the Scorpion's sign for 20s: all your attacks apply a venom DoT stack; enemies with 3+ stacks are Poisoned (-20% healing received).
  - Celestial Alignment  | Passive  | For each active zodiac sign beyond the first, gain +5% to all damage and +5% to all resistances (max 4 signs active simultaneously).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Invoke Sagittarius   | Buff     | Activate the Archer's sign for 20s: all ranged attacks pierce through the first target and hit a second target behind them for 70% damage.
  - Invoke Aquarius      | Active   | Activate the Water Bearer's sign for 20s: project an astral water field in 10m that reduces enemy speed by 25% and removes burning effects from allies.
  - Sign Rotation        | Active   | Instantly cycle all active zodiac signs to the next sign in sequence; refreshes their duration and changes their granted bonuses — useful for adapting mid-fight.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Grand Conjunction    | Active   | Spend 40s of cooldown to align all 12 zodiac signs simultaneously for 8s; all their effects combine and Celestial Alignment reaches maximum (+55% all damage, +55% all resistances).
  - Invoke Capricorn     | Passive  | Activate the Goat's sign permanently (does not count toward active sign limit): all zodiac sign durations are extended by 50%.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Celestial Apex       | Active   | Spend 3s in astral meditation to align with the Celestial Apex: for 25s all zodiac signs are permanently active, Celestial Alignment operates beyond its normal cap (+8% per active sign, all 12 active = +96% total), Sign Rotation triggers automatically every 5s cycling through empowered combinations, and at the end of the Apex your constellation explodes across the battlefield in a map-wide astral strike that deals damage based on the number of enemies present multiplied by your active sign bonuses.

>> Your notes on Zodiac:
>> Twelve sign system is ambitious — suggest launching with 6-8 signs at release and expanding via content patches; each sign should have 3-4 sentence mechanical descriptions in an internal doc.
>> Grand Conjunction is a design risk if 8s of max buffs breaks encounter tuning; cap Celestial Alignment during Grand Conjunction at +40% and only unlock full 55% during Celestial Apex.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON ASTROMANCER]
> Three stellar philosophies: Starfire is pure nova-damage caster with a Charge economy; Eclipse is a high-skill dual-mode switcher who rewards rhythm; Zodiac is a slow-burn buff accumulator whose power scales with fight duration.
> Cross-archetype synergy: Eclipse's Darklight Fusion ignoring resistances could pair with Zodiac's Scorpio venom stacks since many enemies are poison-resistant but not shadow-resistant. Starfire's Stellar Collapse could benefit enormously from Zodiac's Aries sign stacking at close range.
> The Astromancer should feel cosmically large in scope — recommend ambient celestial visuals (star trails, constellation outlines) appearing on the character model when signs are active.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 58: Netrunner (Cyberpunk Hacker)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A ghost in the machine who fights not with muscle but with mastery of information systems, slipping past digital firewalls to disable enemy augmentations mid-fight, crashing through hardened defenses with brute-force intrusion spikes, and deploying autonomous daemon programs that execute their own agendas long after the Netrunner has already pivoted elsewhere.

ARCHETYPE A: Intrusion (Hack Abilities, System Disable, Exploitation)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Targets enemy digital systems and augmentations, selectively disabling abilities, reversing their functions, and draining resources to leave opponents fighting blind and powerless.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Exploit Scan         | Active   | Scan a target to reveal all active augmentations/abilities and identify one Vulnerability (a specific skill that is now hackable); 10s cooldown.
  - Ability Jam          | Debuff   | Exploit a target's identified Vulnerability to jam one of their active abilities, disabling it for 8s.
  - Root Access          | Passive  | After a successful hack, gain Root Access to the target for 5s — all subsequent hacks on them resolve 50% faster and bypass 1 layer of ICE.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - System Drain         | Debuff   | Drain a target's energy/mana/stamina pool at 5% per second for 8s; drained resources are added to your own resource pool at 50% efficiency.
  - Reroute Attack       | Reactive | When an enemy activates a targeted ability at you, hack mid-cast to reroute it to a random nearby enemy instead; 12s cooldown.
  - Deep Exploit         | Passive  | Each additional Vulnerability you expose on the same target (via repeated scans) stacks — up to 3 Vulnerabilities active simultaneously, each increasing hack effectiveness by 25%.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Full System Override | Active   | With 3 active Vulnerabilities on a target, trigger Full System Override: take complete control of their next 2 ability activations, redirecting them as if you cast them yourself.
  - Logic Bomb           | Debuff   | Embed a hidden hack in a target's system; when they next use an ability, the Logic Bomb triggers — dealing damage equal to 300% of that ability's power cost and silencing them for 4s.
  - Cascade Failure      | Active   | Force a chain reaction through a target's augmentation network — disable all their active abilities simultaneously for 5s; longer cooldown per ability disabled (base 40s, +5s per ability).

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Mirror Hack          | Active   | Copy a target's most recently used ability into your own hotbar for 30s; you can cast it once with full potency before it expires.
  - System Collapse      | Active   | Push a Full System Override target into total system collapse: they are stunned for 4s while all their abilities are disabled, and upon recovery they reboot with all abilities on a 10s global cooldown.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - God Mode Intrusion   | Channel  | Spend 3s breaching a target's core architecture: for the next 20s you have unrestricted control of that target — they fight as your ally using their own skills, and any damage they deal counts as your damage for scoring. At the end of the hack, the target's systems reboot with a full ability disable lasting 6s. Works on elite enemies; bosses resist after 10s but still suffer the disable.

>> Your notes on Intrusion:
>> Full System Override and Mirror Hack are the marquee skills — they should have a dramatic visual effect (enemy briefly flickers with your color palette) to communicate the hack clearly.
>> God Mode Intrusion is the most powerful crowd control in any class — requires strict enemy tagging to prevent exploiting in PvP; consider making it PvE only or capping controllable duration to 10s in PvP.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: ICE Breaker (Defense Bypass, Firewall Shattering, Burst Damage)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Specializes in tearing down defensive systems through escalating intrusion pressure, building to devastating burst windows after firewalls collapse.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Port Scan            | Active   | Probe a target for active ICE (defensive layers); each layer found is marked (max 4 layers visible). Reveals defensive cooldowns.
  - Packet Flood         | Attack   | Bombard a target with junk data: deal light damage 5 times rapidly; each hit degrades 1 ICE layer by 10%.
  - Hardline             | Passive  | Your intrusion attacks bypass the first ICE layer on any target automatically; this does not consume the layer.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - ICE Pick             | Attack   | Precision intrusion spike: deal moderate damage and forcibly remove 1 complete ICE layer from the target regardless of its remaining integrity.
  - Surge Overload       | Active   | Overload your hack signal; for 6s your attacks deal +30% damage to targets with at least 1 ICE layer remaining, but deal -10% to fully stripped targets.
  - Static Discharge     | Passive  | When you destroy the last ICE layer on a target, discharge stored static: deal moderate damage to all enemies within 5m of the target.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Breach Protocol      | Active   | Spend 2s executing a full breach: remove all ICE layers from a target simultaneously and deal damage for each layer destroyed (80% of Packet Flood's damage per layer).
  - Exposed System       | Passive  | Targets with no active ICE layers take 25% increased damage from all sources (not just your attacks); stacks with other debuffs.
  - Redundant Firewall   | Debuff   | Reverse-hack a target's backup ICE: force them to reinstall all ICE layers but each layer is corrupted — ICE layers now deal 5% of blocked damage back to the target.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Blackout Strike      | Attack   | After Breach Protocol, execute a Blackout Strike within 6s: deal massive burst damage (scales with number of ICE layers destroyed in the breach, 150% per layer).
  - Zero Day             | Active   | Discover a zero-day exploit: the next ICE Pick, Breach Protocol, or Packet Flood ignores all ICE entirely and deals its full damage directly (no degradation needed); 60s cooldown.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Total Blackout       | Active   | Initiate a full blackout sequence across all enemy systems within 20m: all ICE on all targets is simultaneously shattered, Static Discharge triggers on every target at once (chain AoE), all shattered-ICE targets gain Exposed System, and you enter a 10s window where all your attacks are counted as Blackout Strikes — dealing devastatingly amplified damage against every now-defenseless target in the zone.

>> Your notes on ICE Breaker:
>> Redundant Firewall is a high-risk punish skill — the concept of corrupted ICE that damages the host is mechanically unique and should be explored for boss mechanics where enemies have self-healing ICE.
>> Blackout Strike's scaling with breach layer count needs a visible counter during the 6s window to help players maximize its damage.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Daemon (Persistent Programs, Autonomous Hack Bots, Automation)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Deploys autonomous hack daemons that execute their own routines independently, building a persistent digital infrastructure that compounds in power the longer a fight continues.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Deploy Watcher       | Summon   | Deploy a Watcher daemon near a target: it passively scans and reveals enemy cooldowns to you in real time; lasts until destroyed.
  - Deploy Crawler       | Summon   | Deploy a Crawler daemon that autonomously attacks a designated target's ICE for 20s, degrading 1 layer per 5s.
  - Daemon Uplink        | Passive  | Daemons within 15m of you gain +25% effectiveness; daemons beyond 15m operate at base efficiency.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Deploy Spoofer       | Summon   | Deploy a Spoofer daemon that intercepts enemy ability signals; 20% chance to cause any targeted ability aimed at allies to re-target to the nearest enemy instead for 25s.
  - Daemon Mesh          | Passive  | When 2 or more daemons are active simultaneously, they link into a mesh network: each daemon's effectiveness increases by 15% per additional daemon in the mesh.
  - Remote Trigger       | Active   | Issue a simultaneous command to all active daemons to execute their special burst action at once (Watcher blinds target, Crawler executes a full ICE strip, Spoofer guarantees its next interception).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Deploy Bomber        | Summon   | Deploy a Bomber daemon that attaches to a target and detonates after 8s for heavy AoE damage based on how many ICE layers the target had when the daemon attached (+100% per layer).
  - Daemon Factory       | Summon   | For 15s, summon daemons at twice the normal rate and at no resource cost; existing daemons are immediately refreshed to full duration.
  - Persistence Layer    | Passive  | When a daemon is destroyed, it leaves a ghost process for 10s that continues to apply its effect at 50% potency.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Deploy Replicant     | Summon   | Deploy a Replicant daemon that copies the behavior of one other active daemon; if that daemon uses its burst, the Replicant mirrors it at 70% potency.
  - Distributed Load     | Passive  | Increase maximum simultaneous daemon count from 3 to 5; Daemon Mesh bonuses are recalculated per-daemon rather than per-type.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
