  - Daemon Singularity   | Active   | Fuse all active daemons into a single Singularity Daemon for 20s; the Singularity simultaneously executes all merged daemons' functions at 150% effectiveness every 3s, is immune to destruction, and at the end of its duration explodes in a data cascade — dealing damage equal to the total damage all merged daemons dealt during the Singularity window, distributed evenly across all enemies in 20m.

>> Your notes on Daemon:
>> The daemon mesh scaling requires a visible network indicator showing which daemons are linked — recommend a thin glowing line between active daemon units.
>> Daemon Singularity is the ultimate support-centric capstone; consider allowing it to persist past 20s if no enemies remain in range (transitions to exploration buff mode).
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON NETRUNNER]
> Three hacking disciplines: Intrusion surgically dismantles individual targets; ICE Breaker tears down defensive layers for burst windows; Daemon builds autonomous infrastructure for passive pressure.
> Cross-archetype synergy: Intrusion's Full System Override can be combined with Daemon's Remote Trigger for a double-punch where you force the enemy to act while your daemons explode simultaneously. ICE Breaker's Exposed System passive amplifies all damage including daemon auto-attacks for massive sustained DPS.
> Netrunner should feel like you are fighting on two layers simultaneously — physical space and digital space — consider a secondary HUD overlay showing the "network view" of active hacks/daemons.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 59: Biomancer (Flesh/Organic Shaping)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A practitioner of the ancient and unsettling art of biological manipulation who stitches surplus organs onto willing allies for grotesque power, reshapes their own body mid-combat into whatever form the fight demands, and drives regeneration so extreme that severed limbs regrow in seconds and fatal wounds close themselves before the attacker can follow up.

ARCHETYPE A: Graftmaster (Organ Grafts, Ally Augmentation, Body Modification Buffing)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Attaches harvested or conjured biological grafts onto allies, granting them powerful organic augmentations that change their combat capabilities — for better and occasionally worse.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Graft Sinew          | Buff     | Attach reinforced muscle tissue to an ally; increase their physical damage by 15% and movement speed by 10% for 20s.
  - Graft Dermis         | Buff/Defense     | Layer thickened skin over an ally; increase their physical damage reduction by 20% for 20s.
  - Harvest Organ        | Passive  | Upon an enemy death within 10m, automatically harvest 1 random organ (Heart, Lung, Eye, Spine, Gland) for use in future grafts.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Graft Heart          | Buff/Shield     | Attach an additional heart to an ally; they gain +25% max HP and generate a 10% HP shield every 15s automatically.
  - Graft Eye            | Buff     | Attach an additional sensory organ to an ally; they gain true-sight (see hidden/invisible enemies), +20% crit chance, and +15% ranged accuracy for 25s.
  - Rejection Risk       | Passive/Utility  | Grafts applied have a 10% chance to be empowered (Augmented: +50% bonus) and a 5% chance to Reject (the ally takes moderate damage and the graft is removed early, but gains immunity to future Rejection for 30s).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Graft Spine          | Buff/Immunity     | Fuse a reinforced spine column to an ally; they become immune to knockback, gain +30% CC resistance, and their melee strikes stagger enemies.
  - Graft Gland          | Buff     | Attach a potent toxin gland; the ally excretes venom passively — melee attacks poison targets for 8s (stacking DoT), and they are immune to poison.
  - Composite Graft      | Active   | Simultaneously apply 2 different grafts from your organ inventory to one ally; both take effect and Rejection chance is halved for composite applications.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Legendary Graft      | Buff     | Construct and apply a unique Chimera Graft to an ally: fuse Sinew, Heart, and Eye effects into one permanent graft that lasts the entire encounter (cannot be dispelled, only destroyed by the ally's death).
  - Graft Cascade        | Active   | For 10s, graft applications have no cooldown and Harvest Organ triggers off any kill anywhere on the map; rapidly augment the entire party.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Living Arsenal       | Transform | Spend 4s in a grotesque ritual to fully graft your entire organ inventory onto all party members simultaneously: every ally receives a Chimera Graft appropriate to their role (auto-selected based on their primary damage type), all grafts are Augmented (no Rejection possible), and for 30s all grafts generate one additional effect — each graft causes the host to visually mutate and project a minor AoE aura of their graft type (Spine = stagger aura, Eye = blind aura, Gland = poison cloud, etc.).

>> Your notes on Graftmaster:
>> The Harvest Organ system needs a compact organ inventory UI — suggest max 6 slots, with visual icons for each organ type.
>> Legendary Graft's permanence within an encounter makes it the biggest single buff in any support archetype — balance by requiring 2 of each organ component to craft.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Fleshweaver (Body Reshaping, Self-Transformation, Melee Combat)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Constantly reshapes their own body mid-combat, extending limbs for surprising reach, hardening flesh into armor, and reconfiguring their form to create biological weapons on demand.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Elongate Limb        | Attack   | Extend an arm 4m beyond normal reach for one rapid strike; deals moderate damage with +4m unexpected range.
  - Harden Flesh         | Buff/Defense     | Crystallize subcutaneous tissue; reduce incoming physical damage by 25% for 6s.
  - Reshape Strike       | Attack   | Reform a limb into a blunt club or spike mid-swing: blunt deals +40% damage to armored targets, spike pierces armor (ignores 20% physical armor).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Bone Blade           | Active   | Extrude a bone blade from your forearm for 15s; all melee attacks with it deal +20% damage and apply Bleed (light DoT); the blade shatters if blocked 3 times (absorbs the block).
  - Mass Shift           | Buff/Evasion     | Rapidly redistribute body mass for 8s: gain +30% movement speed and +15% dodge chance as you flow between denser and lighter forms.
  - Pain Threshold       | Passive  | Damage dealt while below 50% HP increases your Reshape abilities' damage by 20%; your body's stress response amplifies your biology.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Tentacle Lash        | Attack   | Extrude a 6m flexible limb; sweep it in an arc dealing moderate damage and knocking down all hit enemies for 1.5s.
  - Carapace            | Buff/Defense     | Grow a chitinous carapace across your torso for 15s; gain +35% physical resistance and reflect 15% of melee damage back to attackers.
  - Metabolic Surge      | Buff/Risk   | Temporarily overdrive your biology: gain +50% attack speed and +20% all damage for 8s, but at end of surge take 8% max HP as exhaustion damage.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Apex Form            | Transform | Spend 2s reshaping into a combat-optimized form for 20s: gain +25% size, +40% melee damage, +20% HP, all Reshape abilities cost no resources, and attacks can reach 2m further than normal.
  - Biological Weapon    | Active   | Reform your dominant hand into a unique biological weapon of your choice each use (rotating between: Acid Spray 6m cone, Impact Fist knockback slam, Bone Saw bleeder, or Venom Fangs grab+inject); 8s duration per weapon, 15s cooldown.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Chimera Ascension    | Transform | Spend 3s in full-body transformation to become a Chimera form for 25s: all four Biological Weapon forms are available simultaneously (one per limb), Apex Form is automatically active, Tentacle Lash gains 4 additional tentacles that each independently target nearby enemies, and your body becomes self-reconstructing — reducing all incoming damage by 15% and immediately reconstructing any lost body part (negating CC that relies on limb targeting). At the end of Chimera Ascension, you release a biological shockwave dealing heavy AoE damage.

>> Your notes on Fleshweaver:
>> Biological Weapon's rotating choices should have clear visual differentiation — the form swap should be dramatic and readable from a distance.
>> Chimera Ascension is visually demanding — multiple independent tentacle animation systems targeting separate enemies simultaneously; budget accordingly in development.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Regenerator (Extreme Healing, Regrowth, Biological Resilience)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Drives regenerative processes to superhuman extremes — healing wounds that would kill mortals, regrowing lost tissue instantly, and sustaining both self and allies through biological restoration far beyond normal recovery.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Rapid Regrowth       | Heal     | Accelerate cellular regeneration in a target: restore moderate HP over 5s (ramps up each second — slow start, fast finish).
  - Clot Response        | Reactive | When you or an ally takes a hit exceeding 20% of max HP, automatically trigger a Clot: absorb 30% of the overage as a temporary barrier; 8s cooldown.
  - Biological Fortitude | Passive/Resistance  | Reduce all DoT (bleed, burn, poison, venom) durations on yourself by 35%; your biology processes toxins and damage faster.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Cell Storm           | Heal     | Trigger a rapid healing storm in a target: restore 5% max HP per second for 6s; removing any debuff from the target extends duration by 1s.
  - Regrowth Pulse       | Heal     | Emit a regenerative pulse in 10m; all allies in range receive a minor HoT for 8s (2% max HP/s).
  - Overheal            | Passive/Shield  | Healing that exceeds a target's max HP converts to a temporary HP shield (max 20% max HP as shield); shield decays at 5% per second when not in combat.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Limb Restore         | Heal     | Fully regrow a severed or severely damaged body part on a target: remove all penalties from limb-targeting CC (cripple, arm break, blinding wound) and heal 25% max HP instantly.
  - Regenerative Aura    | Aura     | Emanate a continuous regenerative field in 8m: all allies gain 3% max HP regeneration per second while in range; enemies in range heal at 50% efficiency from their own healing (biological rejection).
  - Viral Recovery       | Heal   | Inject a biological agent into an ally that causes their next 3 incoming hits to trigger an immediate 15% max HP heal and a full status-effect purge.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Death Reversal       | Heal     | Bring a recently downed ally (within 8s of incapacitation) back to 40% HP, removing all debuffs; they also gain Regeneration Overcharge for 12s (+50% healing received).
  - Adaptive Biology     | Passive/Resistance  | The first time you take each damage type in an encounter (fire, frost, shadow, etc.), develop a resistance to it (+8% per new type, max 6 types = +48% mixed resistance).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Biological Immortality | Channel | Channel for 3s to trigger a full party-wide Regeneration Cascade for 20s: all allies regenerate 5% max HP per second, all DoTs are immediately purged and cannot reapply for the cascade duration, Overheal shields are always active at maximum capacity, and each heal tick has a 10% chance to trigger a biological surge on the target — doubling their healing received and granting them a 5s immunity to the next lethal blow. At cascade end, each fully-healed ally gains a 15% max HP shield.

>> Your notes on Regenerator:
>> Death Reversal needs an 8s window that is clearly visible to the player — countdown indicator over downed allies is essential UX.
>> Adaptive Biology is a long-fight reward passive that becomes extremely powerful in encounters with diverse damage types; may need a per-resistance cap in specific boss tuning contexts.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON BIOMANCER]
> Three biological philosophies: Graftmaster transforms allies through external augmentation; Fleshweaver transforms themselves through internal reshaping; Regenerator preserves the biological integrity of the entire group through extreme resilience.
> Cross-archetype synergy: Graftmaster's Harvest Organ and Fleshweaver's Biological Weapon can share resource pools (harvested organs could fuel biological weapon upgrades in a hybrid build). Regenerator's Adaptive Biology could apply to allies receiving Graftmaster's grafts — the graft material teaches their immune system new resistances.
> Biomancer should feel distinctly unsettling aesthetically — recommend wet, organic sound design for all abilities and a visual aesthetic that emphasizes growth, mutation, and biological excess rather than clean magic.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 60: Luck Gambler (Chance/Fortune)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A reckless fortune-seeker who has made a deity of probability itself, betting health and resources on coin-flip combats, drawing power from a spectral deck of fate cards, and bending the laws of chance so far that what looks like luck is actually a precise science of calculated risk.

ARCHETYPE A: High Roller (Risk/Reward, HP Betting, Volatile Damage)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Wagers health, resources, and even death itself on high-stakes gambles that pay out in devastating damage — the lower you fall, the higher you rise.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Reckless Bet         | Attack   | Bet 5% of your current HP: flip a coin. Heads — deal 250% weapon damage. Tails — deal 50% weapon damage and take the 5% HP cost as additional damage.
  - Lady Luck's Eye      | Passive/Utility  | Crits have a 20% chance to refund their resource cost entirely; you can feel when the dice are hot.
  - All In Stance        | Stance   | Enter All In Stance: all Reckless Bet outcomes have their damage multiplied by 1.5x but the HP wager increases to 10% per cast.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Double Down          | Active   | After any successful coin-flip, immediately gamble again at double the HP cost for double the payout — the window is 3s; miss the window and the moment is gone.
  - Bankroll             | Passive/Utility  | When you fall below 30% HP, Reckless Bet costs no HP but still wagers as if at 30%; your survival instinct found a financial loophole.
  - Push Your Luck       | Active   | Roll a die (1-6): deal weapon damage x the result; on a 1, become Stunned for 2s. On a 6, the roll immediately resets for a free second roll.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - House Edge           | Passive  | Winning coin flips 3 times in a row grants Momentum; while Momentous, all bets pay out at 1.5x the advertised rate for the next 15s.
  - Death Bet            | Active   | Wager your life: deal damage equal to 150% of your missing HP to a target. If this kills them, restore all wagered HP. If it does not kill them, you fall to 1 HP. 90s cooldown.
  - Jackpot Trigger      | Reactive | On any attack that deals a critical hit, 15% chance to trigger Jackpot: deal triple damage and gain 10s of Momentum.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Snake Eyes           | Active   | Force a situation of pure chance: both you and the target roll a d20 simultaneously. Higher roll wins — the winner deals 500% weapon damage to the loser. On a tie, both take moderate damage.
  - Fortune Favors       | Passive  | Your maximum HP functions as a resource — each % of max HP you lose today (across the entire session, not just combat) increases your damage permanently by 0.5% until rest. Cap at +30%.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Ultimate Gamble  | Active   | Flip a single coin to decide the fate of the entire encounter: Heads — all enemies in 20m instantly lose 50% of their current HP, you gain Momentum and full resource restore, and Reckless Bet is free for 20s. Tails — you are reduced to 1 HP, all your cooldowns are reset to full, and your next Reckless Bet automatically crits for 500% regardless of the flip result (the house always collects eventually). 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on High Roller:
>> Fortune Favors is the most unusual passive in the game — tracking session-wide HP loss creates a persistent risk incentive across all content. Cap needs hard testing; could break endgame if encounters are long.
>> Death Bet is the ultimate risk skill — the 1 HP failure state needs a visual/audio moment of dramatic tension (slowed music, held breath SFX) to make both outcomes feel earned.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Card Shark (Draw Mechanics, Poker Hands, Card Combos)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Draws from a spectral deck mid-combat and plays cards individually or in poker hand combinations for cascading effects — the better your hand, the more devastating the outcome.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Draw Card            | Active   | Draw 1 card from your spectral deck (52-card standard deck); hold up to 5 cards in your hand at once. Card suit and value affect skill interactions.
  - Play Single          | Active   | Play one held card: numbered cards deal damage equal to (card value x 5%) weapon damage; face cards trigger special effects (Jack = Haste, Queen = Heal, King = Shield, Ace = wildcard).
  - Fast Hands           | Passive  | Reduce Draw Card cooldown by 1s; you can draw one additional card per 10s of combat.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Play Pair            | Active   | Play 2 matching-value cards (a Pair): deal moderate damage to a target and apply a debuff matching the suit (Hearts = Bleed, Diamonds = Armor Break, Clubs = Stagger, Spades = Slow).
  - Play Flush           | Active   | Play 4 cards of the same suit (a Flush): trigger a powerful suit effect (Hearts Flush = full party heal 20% HP; Diamonds Flush = shatter all armor; Clubs Flush = AoE stun 2s; Spades Flush = mass slow 50% for 10s).
  - Reshuffle            | Active   | Discard your entire hand and redraw 5 cards; 30s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Play Full House      | Active   | Play 3 matching-value cards (a Three-of-a-Kind, upgraded to Full House with a held Pair): deal heavy AoE damage in 8m and apply all four suit debuffs to the primary target.
  - Play Straight        | Active   | Play 5 consecutively valued cards (a Straight): deal heavy damage to a single target and apply a permanent stat reduction (-15% to a random stat) that lasts until the encounter ends.
  - Card Counter         | Passive/Utility  | Track which cards have been played; after 26 cards played this encounter, your probability intuition peaks: for 20s, all draws pull only face cards.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Play Royal Flush     | Active   | Play Ace, King, Queen, Jack, and Ten of the same suit (Royal Flush): trigger a legendary suit explosion — the most powerful combo in your deck; each suit has a unique catastrophic AoE effect (e.g., Spades Royal Flush = Shadow Coffin: bury all enemies in 15m for 3s, dealing 50% HP on emergence).
  - Dead Man's Hand      | Active   | Intentionally play a losing hand (any 5 non-matching cards): instead of the usual weak effect, the dead hand triggers reverse luck — you gain +40% damage for 15s and all enemies in 10m are Cursed (they have disadvantage on their next 3 attacks/checks).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - House of Cards       | Active   | Build a House of Cards for 5s by playing all 5 held cards in sequence — the final card placed determines the outcome based on the composite hand value: any 5-card hand below a Full House triggers a double draw (draw 10 cards instantly, resolvable as two separate 5-card plays); a Full House or better triggers a Cascade Effect where every card value played independently detonates its suit effect simultaneously across all enemies within 20m, and if the hand was a Royal Flush, all effects are doubled and the suit explosion hits twice.

>> Your notes on Card Shark:
>> The card draw system requires a visible hand display UI — suggest a fanned card display in the lower right with suit/value clearly readable.
>> Royal Flush should be genuinely rare during play but achievable through skilled hand management; consider a Reshuffle-targeting mechanic where players can "burn" cards they don't need.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Fortune Teller (Probability Manipulation, Outcome Guarantee, Fate Control)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Operates above luck by controlling probability directly — guaranteeing specific outcomes, forcing re-rolls, and cursing enemies with strings of unavoidable bad fortune.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Foresee              | Active   | Reveal the next 3 outcomes of random chance effects (your own and enemies') in text form for 5s before they resolve; acts as spoilers for the dice.
  - Minor Hex            | Debuff   | Curse a target with Minor Bad Luck: their next 2 attacks have a 40% chance to critically miss (dealing 0 damage and wasting the skill).
  - Favorable Reading    | Buff/Utility     | Read an ally's fortune for 15s: their next 2 random outcomes are weighted toward success (+30% to favorable result).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Reroll               | Active   | Force a reroll on any random event that occurred in the last 2s (your crit miss, an enemy's crit hit, a failed ability check); 15s cooldown.
  - Doom Reading         | Debuff   | Read a target's fortune and reveal their fate: apply Major Bad Luck — for 20s, any ability that has a chance of failure or randomness always chooses the worst outcome.
  - Statistical Anomaly  | Passive  | Once per minute, a random ability of yours automatically performs at its maximum possible value (guaranteed maximum roll result, no crit ceiling).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Probability Lock     | Active   | Lock a specific probability outcome for the next 8s: choose one event type (e.g., "enemy crits" or "my dodge") — that event type always resolves in your favor for the duration.
  - Fate Swap            | Active   | Exchange probability states between yourself and a target: if you are on a losing streak, swap your recent outcome history with theirs (mechanically: transfer your active debuff from bad luck rolls to them and gain their current streak bonuses).
  - Cascade of Misfortune | Debuff  | Apply a chain curse to one target: each time they fail a check or trigger bad luck, the curse spreads to an adjacent enemy; chain can hit up to 5 targets.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Absolute Certainty   | Active   | For 6s, remove all randomness from your abilities: every attack crits, every heal is maximum, every dodge succeeds, every random effect takes its best possible outcome. No chance — only inevitability. 75s cooldown.
  - Ill Omen             | Debuff   | Curse an enemy with Ill Omen for the entire encounter: once per minute, one of their ability activations is automatically converted to a critical failure (the ability fires but at 0 effectiveness and wastes cooldown).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Grand Reckoning  | Active   | Spend 3s calculating the mathematical fate of all enemies in 25m: impose a Predetermined Doom on each target — each enemy is secretly assigned a specific fate (death trigger, crippling debuff, or ability wipe) that resolves the next time they meet a triggering condition (e.g., "next time this enemy uses a heal, they heal an ally for zero and take the healing as damage instead"). The conditions can be read in the target's debuff tooltip. The Reckoning persists until each fate is triggered or 90s elapse.

>> Your notes on Fortune Teller:
>> Absolute Certainty is the most deterministic skill in a randomness-themed class — intentionally creates a 6s window of pure reliability as counterpoint to the chaos, which may feel more satisfying than players expect.
>> The Grand Reckoning's predetermined fate system is complex to implement — requires a fate-assignment engine that reads enemy ability pools and assigns plausible trigger conditions. Consider starting with 5 preset fate templates and expanding.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON LUCK GAMBLER]
> Three probability philosophies: High Roller embraces chaos and thrives on volatility; Card Shark imposes structure on randomness through known deck composition; Fortune Teller transcends randomness by controlling it directly.
> Cross-archetype synergy: Foresee (Fortune Teller) revealing the outcomes of Reckless Bet (High Roller) before they resolve creates a powerful hybrid — knowing the coin flip result in advance removes the risk. Consider whether this interaction should be intentional or restricted.
> The Luck Gambler class should have a strong audio identity — dice rolling, card shuffling, and coin flipping sounds should punctuate every ability activation to reinforce the gambling fantasy.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 61: Mindsculptor (Memory/Psychic)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A psychic artisan who works not with clay or stone but with the fabric of consciousness itself — stealing memories to rob enemies of learned abilities, building inviolable mental architecture in an inner palace that projects psychic constructs into physical space, and unraveling the fundamental sense of self until an enemy cannot remember why they are fighting or who they are.

ARCHETYPE A: Memory Thief (Steal Abilities, Erase Knowledge, Learned Power)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Reaches into enemy minds to extract their skills and knowledge, using stolen abilities against their former owners while leaving them diminished and confused.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Memory Probe         | Active   | Reach into a target's mind to identify one stored ability or passive skill; reveals its function and marks it as Accessible (stealable).
  - Skim Surface         | Debuff   | Rapidly skim a target's recent memories: cause Disorientation (-15% accuracy, -10% ability response speed) for 8s.
  - Retain               | Passive  | You can hold up to 2 stolen memories simultaneously; additional steals overwrite the oldest.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Memory Snatch        | Active   | Extract one Accessible ability from a target: they lose access to it for 15s, and you gain a one-time-use copy at 80% potency.
  - False Memory         | Debuff   | Plant a fabricated memory in a target: once in the next 20s, they automatically use a chosen ability at a moment of your selection (trigger it mentally as a reaction).
  - Deep Archive         | Passive  | Increase retained stolen memory slots to 3; memories stored for more than 20s degrade to 60% potency but persist for up to 60s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Selective Erasure    | Debuff   | Permanently erase one ability from a target for the entire encounter (not just 15s); the ability is completely inaccessible — affects both active skills and passive bonuses.
  - Perfect Copy         | Passive  | Stolen memories no longer degrade; copies retain 100% potency for their full duration and can be used twice before expiring.
  - Mass Skim            | Debuff   | Skim the surface memories of all enemies within 10m simultaneously: apply Disorientation to all hit and randomly reveal one Accessible ability on each.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Identity Theft       | Active   | Steal a target's primary identity memory: they forget their role for 10s (healer forgets heal priority, tank forgets aggro management, DPS forgets target selection) — they still act but make suboptimal decisions. You gain a copy of their identity's core bonus (+20% to whatever stat defines their role).
  - Archive Overload     | Active   | Release all stored stolen memories simultaneously as a psychic detonation: deal psychic damage for each memory released (100% of each ability's power as direct damage) in a 6m radius burst.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Great Theft      | Channel  | Spend 4s in deep psychic immersion against a single target: extract their entire memory archive — stripping all their active abilities, passives, and identity bonuses for 20s. You gain access to all extracted abilities simultaneously (at 75% potency) and can cast any of them freely during the window. The target spends the 20s in a confused Amnesia state — still mobile and reactive but unable to initiate any ability use. When memories return, one ability is permanently Accessible to you as a persistent passive steal.

>> Your notes on Memory Thief:
>> False Memory's triggered activation mechanic is unique — it essentially gives you a free "puppet action" at a moment of your choosing; needs a clear signal to the target player in PvP that their ability was hijacked.
>> Selective Erasure against bosses should target weaker abilities; design boss ability pools with 1-2 "stealable" non-critical skills to prevent trivializing fights.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Architect (Psychic Constructs, Mind Palace Defense, Mental Infrastructure)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Builds and maintains a complex mental architecture that projects psychic constructs into physical space as walls, weapons, and autonomous defenders.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Conjure Mindstone    | Summon   | Project a psychic anchor stone at a location within 10m; acts as a focal point for construct placement and increases construct effectiveness within 5m.
  - Thought Barrier      | Active   | Erect a 3m wide psychic wall at a target location; blocks projectiles and costs 2 actions to breach physically. Lasts 15s.
  - Mental Blueprint     | Passive  | Reduce construct placement cooldowns by 20%; your mental architecture plans are always ready.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Construct Sentinel   | Summon   | Project a psychic sentinel (autonomous humanoid construct) near a Mindstone; it patrols and attacks enemies within 8m for moderate psychic damage. Lasts 25s.
  - Thought Spike        | Active   | Extend a razor-sharp psychic spike 4m from a Thought Barrier's surface: impales the first enemy to touch the barrier for heavy damage and a 2s pin.
  - Reinforced Mind      | Passive/Utility  | Constructs and barriers have +50% HP; they cannot be disrupted by non-magical attacks.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Mind Palace          | Active   | Designate a 15m radius as your Mind Palace for 30s: all constructs within gain +30% effectiveness, Mindstones provide double the focal bonus, and you can see through any construct within as if it were your own eye.
  - Psychic Turret       | Summon   | Place a stationary psychic turret near a Mindstone: fires moderate psychic bolts at 1 per second at the nearest enemy within 12m; prioritizes targets struck by recent Memory Thief abilities.
  - Labyrinth            | Active   | Project 4 interconnected Thought Barriers in a square formation at a target area; enemies inside cannot exit without destroying a wall; the interior is psychically charged (enemies inside take +20% damage from all sources).

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Grand Architect      | Passive  | Increase maximum simultaneous constructs from 3 to 6; Mindstones can now link to each other (chain their focal bonuses across their 5m radii, creating a network).
  - Ego Construct        | Summon   | Project a full copy of your own psychic form as an autonomous combatant for 20s; it mirrors your current ability loadout at 60% potency and is immune to Memory Thief effects.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Eternal Palace   | Active   | Spend 5s erecting your Eternal Mind Palace across 25m: deploy 3 Mindstones, 6 Thought Barriers (arranged optimally by the system), 2 Sentinels, 2 Turrets, and 1 Labyrinth simultaneously — all linked in the Grand Architect network and operating within the Mind Palace bonus. The Eternal Palace persists for 45s and cannot be collapsed by fewer than 3 simultaneous attacks on the same construct. For its duration you are immune to Silence and Stun, drawing power from the palace's stability.

>> Your notes on Architect:
>> The Mind Palace's visual design is a major art challenge — recommend a distinctive color palette (crystalline blue-violet) that clearly differentiates constructs from the environment.
>> Labyrinth + Psychic Turrets creates a genuine arena-control scenario that could be used defensively by support builds or offensively by trapping enemies inside.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Ego Death (Identity Destruction, Will Erasure, Sense of Self)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Targets the fundamental sense of self in a target, dissolving their identity, purpose, and will until they cannot maintain coherent action or even basic self-preservation.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Question Existence   | Debuff   | Plant an existential doubt in a target: apply Uncertain (-10% damage output, -10% ability activation speed) for 10s as they question every action.
  - Identity Crisis      | Debuff   | Introduce a conflicting personality fragment: 20% chance per action for the target to hesitate (0.5s pause before acting) for 12s.
  - Psychic Pressure     | Passive  | Each consecutive Ego Death debuff applied to the same target increases all subsequent debuff penalties by 5% (max +25%).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Shatter Confidence   | Debuff   | Destroy a target's belief in their own abilities: reduce their damage by 20% and cause them to fail ability activations at 15% chance for 15s.
  - Mirror Self          | Active   | Force a target to see a reflection of their true self for 3s: they become briefly non-hostile (stunned by existential paralysis), neither attacking nor moving.
  - Cognitive Dissonance | Debuff   | Implant two contradictory beliefs simultaneously: the target attacks then immediately retreats, advances then stops — all movement and attack commands have 25% chance to reverse direction for 12s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Ego Fragmentation    | Debuff   | Shatter a target's identity into 3 fragments: each fragment penalizes a different stat (-25% damage, -25% defenses, -25% speed) and the fragments can only be reassembled by the target spending 3s doing nothing.
  - Will Drain           | Channel  | Channel for 4s targeting one enemy: continuously drain their will, reducing their next ability's damage by 10% per second channeled (max -40%); the drained will restores 2% of your own max HP per second.
  - Nihilism Field       | Aura     | Emanate an aura of purposelessness in 8m: enemies in range lose 2% damage and 2% move speed per second they remain inside (stacking, max -20%); effect fades 5s after leaving the aura.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Dissolution    | Active   | Attempt to dissolve a target's ego completely: if they are below 40% HP, they immediately surrender — becoming passive and non-hostile for 10s (they do not fight back; they simply exist). Above 40% HP, apply maximum Ego Fragmentation and Cognitive Dissonance simultaneously.
  - Inherited Trauma     | Passive  | When a target dies under 2+ Ego Death debuffs, their death releases a psychic echo — the Ego Death debuffs transfer to the nearest enemy at 75% duration.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Void Within      | Active   | Spend 3s in deep psychic contact with a target to reach their core identity and annihilate it: for 15s the target experiences Ego Death — they cannot initiate any ability, cannot choose attack targets (they flail randomly), cannot move purposefully (wander aimlessly), and their identity resonates outward as a 10m psychic grief wave every 5s that applies Shattered Confidence to all enemies nearby. When the Ego Death ends, the target reboots with every stat reduced by 15% for the remainder of the encounter, a permanent scar of psychic trauma.

>> Your notes on Ego Death:
>> Total Dissolution's surrender mechanic is the most unusual CC in the game — it is not a stun or a freeze but a complete motivation collapse; pairs well with stealth classes who can reset threat during the surrender window.
>> The Void Within's "wander aimlessly" behavior requires a specialized AI behavior state that overrides all existing behavior trees; budget AI development time accordingly.
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON MINDSCULPTOR]
> Three psychic disciplines: Memory Thief weaponizes the past (stolen knowledge); Architect weaponizes the present (physical psychic constructs); Ego Death weaponizes the future (destroying capacity for purposeful action).
> Cross-archetype synergy: Constructs (Architect) can serve as focal points for Memory Thief's Mass Skim — imbue a Sentinel with the last stolen ability so it uses it autonomously. Ego Death's Nihilism Field overlapping with a Labyrinth (Architect) creates an inescapable combination debuff zone.
> The Mindsculptor should feel intellectually threatening rather than physically powerful — recommend understated visual effects (subtle mental distortion, wireframe construct aesthetics) that convey surgical precision rather than brute psychic force.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 62: Oath Breaker (Anti-Paladin/Corruption)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A fallen champion who discovered that every sacred oath shattered fuels a darker power, wielding corruption like a scalpel to invert holy blessings into festering curses, drawing strength from every promise they have broken and every vow they have defiled, and commanding lesser beings through absolute dread — a sovereign of fear whose authority is enforced not by love but by the certainty that disobedience means annihilation.

ARCHETYPE A: Desecrator (Corruption, Buff Inversion, Holy Defiling)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Hunts positive effects to corrupt them — turning enemy blessings into curses, flipping ally buffs on enemies, and making the act of self-improvement a dangerous gambit against the Desecrator.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Corrupt Touch        | Debuff   | Defile one active buff on a target: the buff remains but its effect is inverted (Haste becomes Slow, Armor becomes Vulnerability, Regen becomes DoT) for the buff's remaining duration.
  - Desecration Wave     | Active   | Emit a corruption pulse in 8m: apply Defiled (-15% effectiveness to all buffs currently active on enemies in range) for 10s.
  - Profane Senses       | Passive  | You can detect all active buffs on enemies within 15m; corrupted buffs are highlighted in your vision and deal +10% more damage when exploited.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Taint the Holy       | Debuff   | Target a healing effect before it resolves; corrupt it mid-cast so it deals damage equal to its heal value instead (+15s cooldown compared to Corrupt Touch).
  - Corruption Bloom     | Passive  | When a Corrupt Touch inverted buff expires, it releases a Corruption Bloom — dealing moderate dark damage to all enemies within 5m of the target.
  - Desecrate Ground     | Active   | Corrupt a 10m radius of terrain for 20s: any healing or buff cast inside the zone has a 50% chance to be automatically corrupted upon application.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Mirror Curse         | Debuff   | Reflect a positive ability back at an ally-buffed enemy: copy a buff from an ally's body and deliver a corrupted version to their nearest enemy (inverted, full potency).
  - Cascade Corruption   | Passive  | When you corrupt a buff, there is a 30% chance corruption chains to one other buff on the same target automatically; if that triggers, chain again at 15% chance (max 3 in a chain).
  - Anti-Blessing        | Active   | Place an invisible Anti-Blessing on a target: the next buff they receive is immediately corrupted upon application with no action from you; lasts 20s or until triggered.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Desecration    | Active   | Corrupt all active buffs on a single target simultaneously; each corrupted buff triggers Corruption Bloom, and the combined bloom explosion radius scales with the number of buffs corrupted (3m per buff, max 15m).
  - Holy Harvest         | Passive/Heal  | Each successfully corrupted buff on an enemy restores 3% of your max HP and grants 1 Oath Shard (see Dark Vow cross-reference); corrupting holy or divine buffs grants double.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Unholy Conversion | Channel | Channel for 3s and then unleash a Desecration Field across 20m for 20s: all buffs on all enemies in range are simultaneously and irreversibly corrupted, Desecrate Ground permanently occupies the zone for the duration, any ally who casts a buff on an enemy inside (through mind control, compelled casting, etc.) has that buff auto-converted to its worst possible inversion, and every 5s the field pulses, re-corrupting any buff that has been re-applied since the last pulse. Enemies who die in the field have their death-buffs (martyr abilities, death-triggered bonuses) corrupted into death debuffs that affect their allies instead.

>> Your notes on Desecrator:
>> Taint the Holy is specifically designed as an anti-healer tool — corrupting a heal mid-cast is a dramatic, readable event that should have a distinctive sound cue and visual.
>> The Unholy Conversion interacting with death-triggered bonuses is a niche but crucial mechanic for boss encounters where elite enemies have martyrdom effects; corruption of those effects is a strategic consideration, not just damage.
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Dark Vow (Promise Breaking, Stacking Power, Oath Accumulation)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Draws power from every broken promise, cataloguing oaths both made and shattered into Oath Shards that fuel escalating abilities — the more they betray, the stronger they become.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Swear Oath           | Active   | Make a false promise to gain immediate power: declare an Oath (Oath of Strength, Oath of Speed, Oath of Defense) and gain the bonus for 10s; at 10s, the Oath shatters, generating 3 Oath Shards and removing the bonus.
  - Oath Shard           | Passive  | Passively track Oath Shards (max 30); Shards are spent by Dark Vow abilities and decay at 1 per minute when out of combat.
  - Vow of Blood         | Debuff   | Swear a blood vow against a target: for 15s, deal +15% damage to them; when the vow expires unbroken (the target is not dead), gain 2 Oath Shards from the unfulfilled vengeance.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Shatter Oath         | Active   | Spend 5 Oath Shards to instantly break any active Oath or Vow: receive the full power burst of breaking (deal dark damage equal to 150% of the broken bonus for each second it had remaining) and apply the Broken Oath debuff to nearest enemy (-20% resistances for 15s).
  - Chain of Oaths       | Passive  | Each consecutive Oath sworn within 30s of the previous grants an additional Oath Shard on breaking; up to +3 bonus Shards per chain (chain of 4 Oaths = base 3 + 3 bonus = 6 Shards per break).
  - Profane Covenant     | Active   | Enter a pact with your own darkness: for 20s, all Oaths generate Shards twice as fast (Oath of Strength broken after 5s yields 6 Shards instead of 3).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Oath Storm           | Active   | Spend 15 Oath Shards to release a storm of broken promises as dark energy: deal heavy AoE dark damage to all enemies in 12m (damage scales with Shards spent beyond 15, up to 30 Shards = 200% damage).
  - Legacy of Treachery  | Passive  | Each enemy you have broken a Vow of Blood against (without killing them) permanently grants +2% all stats (max 5 enemies, +10% total); their unresolved grievance fuels your power.
  - Covenant Blade       | Active   | Spend 10 Oath Shards to conjure a dark blade for 15s; each strike applies Shattered (enemy's buffs reduced to 50% effectiveness) and restores 1 Oath Shard per hit.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Endless Betrayal     | Passive  | Oath Shards no longer decay in combat; you can hold up to 50 Shards (increased from 30). Legacy of Treachery cap increases to 10 enemies (+20% total).
  - Grand Betrayal       | Active   | Spend 25 Oath Shards to trigger the Grand Betrayal: simultaneously shatter all active Oaths and Vows, triggering each one's breaking effect at 200% potency, and apply Broken Oath to all enemies within 15m. Enemies marked by Vow of Blood take triple the damage.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Oath That Cannot Be Kept | Active | Swear an impossible Oath: for 20s you pledge to be invincible — gain complete immunity to all damage during this window. At the end of 20s, the Oath shatters: generate 30 Oath Shards instantly, deal dark damage equal to 100% of all damage blocked during the immunity window to all enemies in 20m, and for the next 30s all Oath-spending abilities cost half their Shard price while your betrayal of your own invincibility resonates through the battlefield. Every enemy present gains the Broken Faith debuff: -30% damage and -30% resistances until the encounter ends.

>> Your notes on Dark Vow:
>> The impossibility of "The Oath That Cannot Be Kept" is a narrative design element — the Oath Breaker swears to be invincible knowing they will break it to fuel their power, completing the identity arc.
>> Legacy of Treachery's persistent session bonuses from non-killed Vow targets encourages intentionally not finishing certain enemies — a unique kill-avoidance incentive that no other class has.
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Tyrant (Fear Commands, Domination, Absolute Authority)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Rules through dread, issuing commands that enemies cannot disobey under the crushing weight of supernatural terror, and growing stronger the more fear they generate.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Terrifying Presence  | Aura     | Radiate an aura of absolute authority in 8m; enemies in range suffer -10% damage and -10% move speed from ambient dread (passive, always active).
  - Issue Command        | Active   | Issue a direct psychic command to a target: force them to take one specific action (kneel, stop, retreat 5m, drop current target); they resist if above 60% HP.
  - Fearmonger          | Passive  | Enemies who fail to resist Issue Command gain 1 Terror stack; at 3 Terror stacks, they become Terrified (-25% all stats, won't approach you without CC).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Execution Order      | Active   | Designate one Terrified target as Marked for Execution: all allies and controlled enemies gain +30% damage against the Marked target; the Mark creates visible despair in the target (-15% additional stats).
  - Kneel                | CC       | Crush a target with psychic authority: force them to kneel for 2s (unable to act), usable on targets up to 80% HP; Terrified targets can be Knelt at any HP.
  - Dread Commander      | Passive  | Enemies who witness another enemy die under your control gain 1 Terror stack automatically; death proves your power.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Rule Through Fear    | Active   | Unleash a terrifying proclamation in 15m: all enemies gain 2 Terror stacks simultaneously, and enemies already Terrified must flee in panic for 3s (uncontrolled movement away from you).
  - Forced Allegiance    | Active   | Spend 10s of commanding a Terrified target: force them into temporary servitude for 20s — they fight for you but at 70% combat effectiveness (they resent it and it shows).
  - Fear Cascade         | Passive  | Enemies who witness Forced Allegiance applied to one of their allies immediately gain 2 Terror stacks; the subjugation of a companion breaks morale.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Absolute Dominion    | Active   | For 10s, all enemies within 15m cannot willingly target you (they are psychically commanded away from direct conflict with you); they still fight but redirect attacks to your allies. 60s cooldown.
  - Reign of Terror      | Passive  | The more Terror stacks active across all enemies simultaneously, the more powerful your Terrifying Presence becomes: +3% Aura effectiveness per Terror stack on any enemy (max +30% at 10 total stacks across enemies).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Iron Throne      | Active   | Spend 3s establishing absolute dominion across 25m: all enemies in range immediately gain maximum Terror stacks (instantly Terrified regardless of HP), Issue Command works freely on all targets with no resistance, Forced Allegiance can be applied to all Terrified enemies simultaneously (creates an entire army of resentful servants for 15s), and during the 15s dominion window enemies who die under your control drop their Terror as a psychic pressure explosion — applying 2 Terror stacks to all enemies within 8m of the corpse. When the dominion ends, surviving enemies who were conscripted suffer a morale collapse: they flee combat for 5s and return with Broken Will (-20% all stats, permanent for the encounter).

>> Your notes on Tyrant:
>> Forced Allegiance at Tier 5 creating multiple resentful servants simultaneously is the ultimate power fantasy payoff — the servants' "resentment" should be visible in their animations (slumped posture, reluctant attack animations) for maximum flavor.
>> Absolute Dominion is a defensive power fantasy skill — being unkillable by enemy choice is unusual; ensure it has clear communication to both the Tyrant player and the enemies (enemies should have a clear "redirected" visual cue).
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON OATH BREAKER]
> Three corruption disciplines: Desecrator is reactive and punishing — it answers enemy buffs with inverse punishment; Dark Vow is accumulative and explosive — it rewards patience and deliberate oath-cycling; Tyrant is commanding and presence-driven — it controls the battlefield through terror rather than direct damage.
> Cross-archetype synergy: Dark Vow's Oath Shards can be generated by Desecrator's Holy Harvest passive, creating a resource pipeline between the two trees. Tyrant's Terrified status makes enemies highly susceptible to Desecrator's Corrupt Touch (Terrified enemies have reduced resistance to corruption effects as a design hook).
> The Oath Breaker is the darkest class in the roster — recommend a visual identity centered on cracked holy iconography (shattered halos, corrupted paladins' crests, chains of broken oaths visualized as links snapping in slow motion on each Shard generation).
>


################################################################################
CLASS 63: WORLDSINGER (Creation/Genesis)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A mystic who wields the primordial language of creation, capable of
singing matter into existence, naming truths into reality, or unmaking the very
fabric of what is. They do not cast spells — they author reality.

ARCHETYPE A: SHAPER (Construct, Genesis, Terrain)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Sculpts raw matter from nothing to reshape the battlefield, erect
barriers, and bury or elevate foes using created terrain.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Raise Stone         | Active    | Conjure a stone pillar from bare ground, blocking movement and line of sight for 4s. Can be used as cover or to launch nearby enemies upward.
  - Clay Fist           | Attack    | Hurl a dense clump of conjured clay at a target, dealing blunt damage and slowing their movement speed by 20% for 3s.
  - Terraform           | Utility   | Flatten rough terrain or fill gaps in a small area, removing hazardous ground effects and allowing free movement across the zone.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Conjured Rampart    | Active    | Erect a curved wall of solid stone up to 8m long. Enemies can destroy it (moderate HP), but it fully blocks projectiles.
  - Matter Seed         | Active    | Plant an invisible conjured node in the ground. After 3s or on command, it erupts into a mass of jagged rock, launching nearby enemies and dealing area damage.
  - Shaper's Lattice    | Passive   | Each time you create terrain, your next attack ignores 15% of the target's armor as conjured matter permeates their defenses.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Entomb              | CC        | Encase a single target in a rapidly formed stone shell, rendering them immobile and untargetable for 3s, then shattering it for moderate AoE damage around them.
  - Genesis Spire       | Active    | Raise a massive spire from the earth at a chosen point, dealing heavy impact damage on eruption and acting as an elevated platform afterward.
  - Sculptor's Eye      | Passive/Defense   | Your created terrain adapts to your intent — walls curve around allies, pillars angle to deflect projectiles. Conjured structures gain +40% durability.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Architect's Domain  | Active    | Over 2s of channeling, raise a full arena of walls, platforms, and chokepoints in a 20m radius. Reshapes the battlefield for 30s before crumbling.
  - Living Stone        | Transform | Encase yourself in conjured granite armor for 12s. Gain massive damage reduction, become immune to knockback, and your melee attacks send stone shockwaves forward.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - In the Beginning    | Channel   | Channel for 3s, then speak a location into being: summon a fully-formed fortress structure or canyon or chasm of your design anywhere in a 40m radius. Lasts 60s. Allies gain full cover; enemies caught in the summoned terrain take crushing damage.

>> Your notes on SHAPER:
>> - Shaper is a high-impact battlefield control archetype. Skills should feel weighty and geological.
>> - Entomb is the signature single-target CC and should feel satisfying to land.
>> - "In the Beginning" is intentionally world-altering — best used to flip the geometry of a fight.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: TRUE NAMER (Name, Decree, Unmake-Speak)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Speaks the hidden name of things to bend their nature — binding
enemies with their true names, amplifying allies, or rewriting properties of
objects and creatures mid-combat.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Name-Bind           | CC        | Speak a target's true name aloud, rooting them in place for 2s. They cannot act but also cannot be harmed during the bind — they are "fixed" in reality.
  - Decree of Weakness  | Debuff    | Declare an enemy "brittle" — they take 15% increased damage from all sources for 6s.
  - Rename              | Active      | Speak a new name for an ally, briefly redefining them. Grant one random positive status (haste, shield, regen, or accuracy boost) for 5s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Unname              | Debuff    | Strip a target of their name, causing confusion. They lose access to one random ability for 5s and have their threat generation reduced.
  - Decree of Ending    | Attack    | Declare that a summoned creature, construct, or non-player entity "ends." Instantly destroy summoned entities with fewer than 30% HP. Deal heavy damage to others.
  - Syllable of Truth   | Passive/Utility   | Enemies affected by your naming effects cannot become invisible or teleport. Your name-binds pierce evasion effects.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - True Name Revelation| Active    | Spend 2s studying a target to learn their true name. Grants 25% increased damage against that target for the fight and allows your naming CCs to affect them even if they are normally immune.
  - Decree of Motion    | Active    | Declare an ally "swift." They gain 40% movement speed and their next 3 attacks cannot miss, as reality itself has ordained their success.
  - Living Word         | Summon    | Speak a creature into existence from pure language. Summons a Word-Beast (medium strength) that obeys your commands for 20s. Each time it deals damage, it speaks a fragment of an enemy's name, applying a stacking vulnerability.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Edict              | Active    | Speak a single-word decree that affects all enemies in a 15m radius: choose "FREEZE," "FALL," or "SILENCE." Applies the chosen status for 3s. Cooldown: 30s.
  - Authorship         | Passive   | When you apply a naming effect to a target, you may immediately apply a second naming effect of lower tier to the same target for free. Your first naming skill each encounter costs no resources.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The First Word      | Active    | Speak the primordial word of creation. All enemies in a 25m radius have their current status (buffs, resistances, immunities) stripped and are reset to their baseline state. All allies in range have their cooldowns refreshed and their HP partially restored. Reality "reboots" around you for 5s, during which no new status effects can be applied to anyone.

>> Your notes on TRUE NAMER:
>> - True Namer rewards players who read enemy types — True Name Revelation is a payoff skill for scouts/planners.
>> - "The First Word" is a fight-resetting Legendary that punishes enemies who invest in buff stacks.
>> - Edict is intentionally simple to execute but requires choosing the right word at the right moment.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: UNMAKER (Entropy, Dissolution, Erasure)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Speaks existence into absence — erasing projectiles, dissolving
constructs, unwinding enemy enhancements, and eventually unwriting enemies from
the world entirely.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Dissolve            | Active/Defense    | Unwrite a physical object (arrow, thrown weapon, summoned projectile) from existence mid-flight. Nullifies one incoming projectile per cast.
  - Entropy Touch       | Attack    | A melee strike infused with dissolution energy. Deals moderate damage and reduces the target's armor or resistances by 10% for 5s (stacks up to 3x).
  - Erase Trace         | Utility   | Remove all environmental evidence of your party's passage in a small area — footprints, scent, magical signatures. Counters tracking and detection magic.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Unwrite Buff        | Debuff    | Target one active buff on an enemy and erase it. The buff cannot be reapplied for 8s. Does not require the buff to be identified first.
  - Void Seam           | Active    | Tear a small seam of non-existence in the ground. Functions as an invisible trap — the first enemy to step on it takes heavy falling/null damage and is stunned for 1.5s.
  - Fraying             | Passive   | Each consecutive hit you land on the same target increases the damage of your Unmaker skills against that target by 5%, up to 25%. Resets if you switch targets.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Unmake Construct    | Active    | Target a wall, barrier, summoned creature, or magical structure. Instantly erase it from existence — bypasses HP entirely for objects under a size threshold.
  - Dissolution Field   | Aura      | Toggle. Emit a 6m aura that degrades incoming projectiles (they deal 30% less damage) and causes summoned creatures in the area to lose HP each second.
  - Memory Wipe         | CC        | Erase the last 4 seconds from an enemy's perception. They forget what happened, return to their prior position, and lose their current action. Does not affect HP. Strong CC on cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Unraveling         | Channel   | Channel for up to 4s on a target. Each second of channeling strips one layer of their existence — armor, then resistances, then buffs, then 20% of max HP. Any portion not completed on interrupt still applies.
  - Null Zone          | Active    | Create a 10m zone where healing is reduced by 50%, summoned entities instantly die on entry, and all damage dealt ignores 20% of resistances. Lasts 15s. Cannot be dispelled.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Erasure            | Active    | Point at a single target and speak the word of ending. After a 2s delay (visible to all), that target is removed from existence — effectively a kill. If the target has more than 40% HP, the effect is downgraded to a 6s stun and a massive HP reduction instead. Cannot target player characters. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on UNMAKER:
>> - Unmaker is a high-risk, high-payoff archetype. Erasure is the most dramatic capstone in the Worldsinger class.
>> - Memory Wipe is intentionally unusual — it is CC that denies action without damage, useful for stopping a boss mid-cast.
>> - Null Zone should feel like "anti-magic zone" for summoner-heavy encounters.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON WORLDSINGER]
> Worldsinger is a prestige-feel class built around the fantasy of language as power. Each archetype expresses a different relationship to creation: Shaper imposes, True Namer defines, Unmaker negates. They are thematically opposed but mechanically complementary in a party.
> Shaper + Unmaker synergy: Shaper raises terrain, Unmaker erases enemy cover — both reshape the field but in opposite directions.
> True Namer's Legendary is deliberately non-damaging; it is a "reset" skill that rewards players who understand the tempo of a fight.
> Erasure (Unmaker Legendary) must be balanced carefully — the 40% HP threshold prevents it being an instant-kill button in most boss fights.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 64: CORSAIR (Pirate/Naval Combat)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A fearless outlaw of the open sea who turns every battle into a
plundering raid — blasting enemies apart with cannon fire, stripping them of
their advantages, and calling upon the terrifying creatures of the deep as
allies. They fight dirty, fight loud, and always profit.

ARCHETYPE A: BROADSIDE (Cannon, Explosive, Siege)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Transforms ranged combat into artillery warfare, lobbing explosive
ordinance, grapeshot, and incendiary rounds that devastate grouped enemies and
structures alike.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Cannonball          | Attack    | Fire a heavy iron ball in a line. Pierces through the first target, dealing heavy damage to both it and any enemy directly behind it.
  - Grapeshot           | Attack    | Fire a spread of metal fragments in a short cone. Low individual damage but hits all enemies in the arc; excellent at point-blank range.
  - Powder Keg          | Active    | Hurl a keg of black powder that lands at a target location. Detonates after 2s or when struck by any attack, dealing massive AoE damage in a 5m radius.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Chain Shot          | CC        | Fire two cannonballs linked by chain. Travels in a wide arc, dealing moderate damage and entangling the legs of all enemies it passes through, rooting them for 2.5s.
  - Incendiary Round    | Attack    | A flaming shot that deals moderate damage on impact and leaves a burning patch on the ground for 6s. Enemies in the patch take fire damage each second.
  - Rolling Broadside   | Passive   | Each time you fire an explosive attack, reduce its cooldown by 0.5s (max reduction 2.5s). Stacks reset if you use a non-explosive skill.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Barrage             | Channel   | Channel for up to 4s, firing a cannonball every 0.8s at a designated area. Each successive shot in the same area increases damage by 15%.
  - Siege Mode          | Stance    | Plant yourself in place. While stationary, your ranged attacks gain 30% damage, 50% range, and your Powder Kegs detonate instantly on landing. Ends if you move.
  - Dead Reckoning      | Passive   | Your explosive skills now lead moving targets automatically, predicting their position. Enemies cannot dodge-roll out of your Cannonball or Grapeshot while this is active.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Man-o-War Salvo     | Active    | Unleash a devastating volley of 6 cannonballs at chosen targets over 2s. Each ball hits a different target (or the same target if fewer than 6 exist), dealing heavy damage per hit. 25s cooldown.
  - Black Powder Heart  | Passive   | When you are reduced below 30% HP, your next explosive attack automatically detonates at double radius and triple damage. Recharges once per encounter.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Long Nine       | Channel   | Spend 3s aiming, then fire a single legendary shot. The projectile travels in a straight line of unlimited range, passing through every enemy, wall, and barrier in its path, dealing catastrophic damage to each. After it passes, the path it traveled ignites and smolders for 8s, dealing damage to anything that crosses the line.

>> Your notes on BROADSIDE:
>> - Broadside rewards patience and positioning. Siege Mode is the high-risk, high-reward stance for committed artillery players.
>> - Dead Reckoning is a skill-expression reducer — it removes the hardest part of landing shots but is gated behind Tier 3.
>> - The Long Nine should feel cinematic and rare. The 3s aim time makes it interruptable but worth protecting.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: PLUNDERER (Steal, Strip, Pillage)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Robs enemies of their greatest advantages mid-fight — stealing buffs,
draining resources, looting gold and items off their corpses, and turning their
own power against them.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Cutpurse Strike     | Attack    | A swift melee strike that deals light damage and steals a small amount of the target's resource (mana, stamina, or energy). You gain the stolen amount.
  - Plunder Buff        | Active    | Target an enemy with one active buff. Steal it entirely — removing it from them and applying it to yourself for half its remaining duration.
  - Appraise            | Utility   | Identify all buffs, resistances, and stored resources on a target enemy. Also marks which of their abilities is currently on the shortest cooldown.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Swipe               | Active    | Instantly steal a consumable item or held object from a target enemy (a potion, a thrown weapon, a held torch). You gain the item and can use it.
  - Pillage             | Passive   | Each enemy you kill drops a bonus loot token. These tokens grant temporary combat bonuses when picked up: random between +5% damage, +5% speed, or a small HP restore.
  - Debt Collector      | Debuff    | Mark a target as "indebted." For 10s, 20% of all healing or buffs they receive is redirected to you instead.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Grand Larceny       | Active    | Steal ALL active buffs from a single target at once. Gain each buff for 40% of its remaining duration. 30s cooldown.
  - No Mercy Tax        | Passive   | Every time an enemy attempts to use a healing skill while you are in melee range of them, they are interrupted and you gain a stack of Plunder (each stack = +3% damage, max 10 stacks). Stacks last 15s.
  - Contraband          | Active    | Reach into a target's pocket dimension, inventory, or magical reserve and extract one random ability charge or cooldown. Reduces their ability's cooldown by 0 and resets it — then grants you one free use of your own highest-cooldown ability.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Hostile Takeover   | Active    | Target a summoned creature controlled by an enemy. Steal control of it for 15s. After the duration, the creature returns to neutral rather than returning to the enemy.
  - Pirate's Fortune   | Toggle    | While toggled on, all damage you deal has a 15% chance to also drain 5% of the target's current resource pool. You recover HP equal to half the resource drained. Costs a small amount of your own resource per second.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Crown and Plunder   | Active    | Target one enemy and declare them "claimed." For 20s, every ability they use has its effect copied and applied to you as well — their heals also heal you, their damage buffs also buff you, their shields also protect you. When the duration ends or the target dies, you gain a massive resource and gold reward. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on PLUNDERER:
>> - Plunderer is extremely satisfying against heavily buffed boss enemies. Grand Larceny into a boss's own power fantasy is the core loop.
>> - Hostile Takeover gives Plunderer a summon-denial angle that differentiates it from the Spellthief class (which copies, not commands).
>> - Crown and Plunder is best used as an opener against enemies who self-buff heavily at the start of their turn.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: SEA DEVIL (Water, Kraken, Seamonster)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Channels the terrifying power of the deep ocean — drowning enemies in
summoned water, pulling them into the dark with tentacles, and becoming a
seamonster incarnate in the heat of battle.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Salt Lash           | Attack    | Crack a whip of conjured seawater at a target, dealing moderate damage and reducing their vision range by 30% for 4s (salt in the eyes).
  - Undertow            | CC        | Conjure a surging current beneath a target's feet, dragging them 6m toward you and slowing their movement by 25% for 3s.
  - Brine Coat          | Buff/Resistance      | Coat yourself in a layer of seawater brine. Gain 15% resistance to fire damage and leave a slippery patch when you move for 5s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Tentacle Strike     | Attack    | Summon a massive kraken tentacle that slams down at a target location, dealing heavy damage in a 4m radius and knocking enemies prone.
  - Drowning Grasp      | CC        | A spectral tentacle wraps around a target, restraining them for 3s and dealing water damage each second. Restrained targets cannot use movement abilities.
  - Depth Pressure      | Passive   | Each time you deal water damage, you apply a stack of Pressure on the target. At 5 stacks, the target is Crushed — they take 20% increased damage and move at half speed for 6s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Kraken's Reach      | Summon    | Summon 3 independent tentacles at chosen positions in a 15m area. Each tentacle strikes the nearest enemy every 2s for 10s before dissolving.
  - Maelstrom           | Active    | Create a spinning vortex of water 8m wide at a target location. Pulls all enemies toward its center, dealing continuous damage and preventing escape for 5s.
  - Deep Dread          | Aura      | Toggle. Emit an aura of abyssal terror. Enemies within 10m feel crushing pressure — they take 10% more damage from all sources and have a 20% chance to flee rather than attack on their turn.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Leviathan's Maw    | Active    | Open a colossal water maw beneath a target. They are sucked under for 3s (untargetable, unable to act), then expelled for massive damage. Functions as a brief banish.
  - Sea Devil's Mark   | Debuff    | Brand a target with the mark of the deep. For 20s, all your water and tentacle attacks prioritize and deal 25% bonus damage to the marked target. When they die, the mark transfers to the nearest enemy.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Rise of the Deep    | Transform | Transform into a partial kraken form for 15s. Gain massive HP, your attacks become multi-tentacle slams that hit all nearby enemies, you become immune to movement CC, and you gain a permanent aura that pulls enemies toward you each second. All water skills cost no resources during the transformation.

>> Your notes on SEA DEVIL:
>> - Sea Devil is a CC-heavy bruiser. The Depth Pressure stack mechanic encourages sustained focus on one target.
>> - Maelstrom pairs brilliantly with Broadside's explosive skills in a multi-archetype party.
>> - Rise of the Deep is a transformation capstone — it should feel terrifying and cinematic. The 15s window is intentionally short to avoid overpowering.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON CORSAIR]
> Corsair is a class built around spectacle and opportunism. Broadside is the loudest archetype in the game; Plunderer is the most psychologically disruptive; Sea Devil is the most visually dramatic.
> The class lacks passive healing, making positioning and resource management critical. A Plunderer stealing heals from enemies is their primary sustain mechanism.
> Thematic coherence: all three archetypes are about taking — territory (Broadside), power (Plunderer), and life (Sea Devil).
> Sea Devil's Maelstrom + Broadside's Barrage is an intended high-synergy two-archetype combo for players who can split investment.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 65: GEOMANCER (Earth/Stone)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A master of living stone who commands the earth itself as a weapon —
splitting the ground with seismic fury, sculpting walls and fortresses from
bedrock, and petrifying foes into immobile statues of crystal and mineral.
The earth answers only to those patient enough to hear it.

ARCHETYPE A: QUAKE CALLER (Seismic, AoE, Shockwave)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Unleashes the destructive power of tectonic forces — triggering
earthquakes, erupting geysers, and sending shockwaves across the ground that
devastate everything in their path.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Tremor               | Active    | Stomp the ground to send a low rumble through the earth. All enemies within 8m are briefly staggered (0.5s interrupt) and slowed by 15% for 3s.
  - Fissure Strike       | Attack    | Drive a weapon or fist into the ground, sending a crack along the earth for 10m in a line. Enemies standing on the crack take moderate damage and fall prone.
  - Earthen Slam         | Attack    | Gather stone around your fist or weapon and deliver a ground-shaking blow. Deals heavy single-target damage and creates a shockwave ripple that deals half damage to enemies within 3m of the target.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Rupture              | Active    | Cause the ground to crack and buckle in a 6m radius. All enemies in the zone are knocked off their feet (1.5s knockdown) and the terrain becomes difficult ground for 8s.
  - Seismic Pulse        | Active    | Pound the ground repeatedly for 2s, releasing three successive shockwaves outward. Each wave deals increasing damage (80%, 100%, 130%) and travels 12m.
  - Aftershock           | Passive   | For 5s after using any seismic skill, the ground underfoot remains unstable. Enemy movement in that area triggers micro-tremors that deal 20% of the initial skill's damage each time an enemy steps.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Richter's Wrath      | Active    | Strike the earth to trigger a 12m radius earthquake lasting 4s. Enemies continuously stumble, suffering 30% reduced movement speed and a 25% chance to be interrupted when casting or using abilities.
  - Geyser              | Active    | Select a ground location up to 15m away. After 1s, a scalding stone geyser erupts, launching a single target or cluster of enemies upward 6m and dealing heavy damage on landing.
  - Resonant Ground      | Passive   | Seismic skills now trigger resonance — each seismic skill you use within 10s of the last one deals 10% more damage and has 5% wider AoE, up to 40% bonus and double AoE.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Continental Divide   | Active    | Cleave the earth open along a 20m line, creating a 2m-wide impassable chasm that deals massive falling damage to any enemy standing on it when it opens. Chasm persists for 15s.
  - Avalanche Stomp      | Active    | Leap into the air and come crashing down at a chosen point within 10m. On landing, a massive boulder-shockwave erupts outward 10m in all directions, dealing catastrophic damage and launching enemies.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Great Rift       | Channel   | Channel for 2s while planting both hands in the ground, then trigger a catastrophic tectonic event in a 30m radius. The ground fractures into shifting plates — enemies are repeatedly knocked down, take massive damage per second for 5s, and have all movement abilities suppressed. The terrain remains shattered (difficult ground) for 60s afterward.

>> Your notes on QUAKE CALLER:
>> - Quake Caller is the most AoE-centric archetype of the Geomancer. Resonant Ground rewards players who chain skills efficiently.
>> - Geyser is intentionally a single-target launcher — it pairs well with Stoneshaper walls that enemies can slam into.
>> - Continental Divide permanently alters terrain for the fight duration, which creates unique tactical situations.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: STONESHAPER (Walls, Fortification, Terrain)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Sculpts the battlefield through stone architecture — raising barriers,
creating chokepoints, and manipulating terrain to force enemies into
disadvantageous positions while protecting allies.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Stone Wall           | Active/Defense    | Raise a flat wall of stone 4m wide and 3m tall at a target location. Moderate HP. Blocks movement and projectiles.
  - Boulder Toss         | Attack    | Rip a chunk of earth from the ground and hurl it at a target. Deals moderate damage and creates a small rubble patch (difficult terrain) where it lands.
  - Fortify              | Buff/Defense      | Coat an ally (or yourself) in a thin layer of hardened stone for 8s. They gain 20% physical damage reduction and cannot be staggered by attacks of moderate force.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Labyrinth            | Active/Defense    | Raise a series of low walls in a 10m area, creating a maze-like pattern of cover and chokepoints. Enemies must navigate around them; allies who know the layout pass freely.
  - Sinkhole             | Active    | Collapse a 3m section of ground at a target location into a pit. Enemies fall in (4m deep, takes fall damage) and must spend 2s climbing out or use a movement ability.
  - Living Architecture  | Passive/Defense   | Your conjured walls and terrain features slowly repair themselves — recovering 5% of max HP per second. Additionally, allies who stand adjacent to your walls gain 10% damage reduction.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Monolith             | Active    | Raise a 10m tall stone tower at a target location. Functions as a massive cover piece and can be climbed by allies (elevated vantage). Enemies who run into it take impact damage.
  - Shifting Earth       | Active    | Manipulate existing terrain in a 15m area — push walls, raise or lower ground sections, close sinkholes, or redirect the layout. Treat the battlefield as partially programmable for 8s.
  - Master Shaper        | Passive/Utility   | Your conjured stone structures now have 50% more HP, no longer crumble after their duration expires (they persist until destroyed), and you can maintain 2 additional terrain features simultaneously.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Siege Works         | Active    | Spend 3s constructing a full defensive emplacement — a curved wall with two flanking pillars and an elevated firing platform. Designed to shelter 3-5 allies. Lasts 40s.
  - Earth Golem         | Summon    | Pull stone and rubble together to form a Stone Golem companion (large, durable, moderate damage). The Golem automatically uses rubble from your terrain features as ammunition, gaining +10% damage for each of your active structures.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Eternal Citadel  | Channel   | Channel for 4s, then raise a complete stone fortress structure around your party's current position — walls, towers, a sealed gate, and arrow slits — in a 25m radius. The citadel has its own HP pool. Enemies must destroy sections to enter. Lasts 90s or until fully destroyed. While inside, all allies gain 15% damage reduction.

>> Your notes on STONESHAPER:
>> - Stoneshaper is the premier defensive terrain archetype. The Eternal Citadel is a siege-reversal capstone.
