>> - Earth Golem synergizes with your own terrain features — more structures means a stronger Golem.
>> - Labyrinth is a mind-game skill — it disadvantages enemies more if the player has laid it out thoughtfully.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: PETRIFIER (Stone-Curse, Crystallize, Slow)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Suffuses enemies with mineral essence, gradually transforming them
into helpless stone statues — slowing, crystallizing, and ultimately fully
petrifying targets before shattering them for massive damage.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Mineral Seep         | Debuff    | Infuse a target with slowly spreading stone energy. They take 8% reduced movement speed and 5% increased damage from earth skills. Stacks up to 3 times.
  - Stone Gaze           | Attack    | A focused beam of petrifying energy. Deals light damage and applies 1 stack of Calcification (target becomes progressively stiffer at 3 stacks).
  - Quartz Shell         | Buff/Resistance      | Crystallize the outer layer of an ally's skin, granting them 20% physical resistance for 8s. The crystals shatter when the buff expires, dealing minor AoE damage around the ally.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Calcification        | Passive   | At 3 stacks of Calcification, a target becomes Partially Petrified — halved movement speed, 20% reduced attack speed, and 15% increased damage taken. This state lasts 8s.
  - Basalt Burst         | Attack    | Detonate the stone energy built up in a Calcified target, dealing heavy damage and resetting Calcification stacks. More powerful the higher the stack count on detonation.
  - Crystal Grip         | CC        | Crystalline tendrils erupt from the ground and encase a target's legs, rooting them for 3s and adding 2 stacks of Calcification.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Full Petrification   | CC        | On a target at Partially Petrified or higher: instantly fully petrify them — they become a statue for 4s, completely immune to all effects but also unable to move, act, or be targeted by enemies. At the end of the duration, they take a hit of moderate stone-shatter damage.
  - Mineral Plague       | Debuff    | Spread Calcification stacks. A target with 3+ stacks infects all enemies within 5m with 1 stack of Calcification on each of your hits for 8s.
  - Stone Blood          | Passive   | Enemies affected by any Calcification effect bleed mineral dust on hit. Your critical strikes against Calcified targets deal 30% bonus damage and generate an additional stack of Calcification.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Statue Garden       | Active    | Simultaneously apply 2 stacks of Calcification to all enemies within 10m. Any already at max stacks are immediately Partially Petrified.
  - Obsidian Edge       | Stance    | Your attacks become laced with razor obsidian. You deal 15% increased damage to stone-affected targets, and each attack has a 25% chance to add a Calcification stack. Your own skin hardens — gain 10% all damage reduction while in this stance.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Medusa's Decree     | Active    | Unleash a wave of pure petrification energy in a 20m cone. All enemies hit are immediately Fully Petrified (4s statue state) regardless of current Calcification stacks. When the statues shatter at the end of the duration, each one explodes for massive AoE damage. The stone debris from shattered enemies grants Partially Petrified status to any enemy standing in the rubble. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on PETRIFIER:
>> - Petrifier has the most satisfying visual and mechanical buildup of the three archetypes — every enemy encounter becomes a slow ramp toward statuing them.
>> - Basalt Burst is the key payoff skill for stack management — detonate at max stacks for best results.
>> - Medusa's Decree ignores the stack requirement, making it a strong opener as well as a finisher in mass encounters.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON GEOMANCER]
> Geomancer excels at deliberate, methodical play. No archetype rewards reckless button-mashing — all three reward reading the terrain and managing timers.
> Quake Caller + Stoneshaper synergy: Stoneshaper raises walls, Quake Caller erupts geysers that slam enemies into them for bonus damage.
> Petrifier + Quake Caller synergy: Calcified (slowed) enemies are easier to hit with seismic AoEs; shattered statue explosions trigger Aftershock.
> The class lacks mobility skills — this is intentional. Geomancer is king of their ground but falls apart if displaced. Party composition should account for this.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 66: FROST WARDEN (Ice Tank)
################################################################################
Fantasy: An unyielding sentinel of arctic fury who transforms punishment into
power — the more enemies attack, the colder and more lethal the Frost Warden
becomes. They do not avoid damage; they absorb it, crystallize it, and send it
back as a glacier.

ARCHETYPE A: GLACIAL BULWARK (Ice Armor, Absorption, Reduction)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Layers ice armor upon ice armor to achieve extraordinary damage
reduction — each hit they survive makes the next defense even stronger, turning
sustained bombardment into a strategic mistake for attackers.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Ice Plate            | Buff/Shield      | Coat yourself in a layer of magical ice armor. Gain a shield worth 15% of your max HP. The shield regenerates 3% per second if not damaged for 3s.
  - Frost Shell          | Reactive/Defense  | When you take damage, automatically crystallize a thin layer of ice over the wound. Reduce the triggering hit's damage by 10% (retroactive reduction applied as healing). 4s internal cooldown.
  - Glacial Stance       | Stance    | Adopt a defensive posture. Gain 10% damage reduction and reduce incoming CC duration by 25% while in this stance. Movement speed reduced by 10%.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Permashield          | Passive/Shield   | Your Ice Plate armor now has two layers. The second layer activates automatically when the first is destroyed, at 50% of the original value. Total layer count increases with further investment.
  - Glacial Resolve      | Passive/Defense   | When you are struck, you generate 1 stack of Resolve (max 10). Each stack gives +2% damage reduction. Stacks decay at 1 per 3s when out of combat.
  - Cold Density         | Passive/Resistance   | Your ice armor absorbs a portion of elemental damage — gain 15% resistance to fire, lightning, and cold damage while any layer of ice armor is active.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Cryogenic Plate      | Buff/Defense      | Upgrade your active ice armor to Cryogenic tier. Any melee attacker who strikes your armor takes 15% of the damage they dealt back as cold damage. Lasts 12s.
  - Absolute Zero Shell  | Active    | Spend all current Resolve stacks (minimum 3 required). For each stack spent, reduce incoming damage by an additional 5% for 8s (up to 50% reduction at 10 stacks).
  - Permafrost Core      | Passive/Shield   | Your ice armor can now never be fully destroyed in a single hit — any hit that would destroy a layer instead reduces it to 1 HP. Prevents one-shot shield destruction.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Arctic Fortress      | Active/Defense    | Encase yourself in a full-body fortress of glacial ice for 6s. Become immune to all damage and CC, but cannot move or attack. All damage that would have been dealt to you during this time is stored, then dealt to nearby enemies when the shell shatters.
  - Diamond Ice          | Passive/Defense   | When your ice armor reaches max stacks of reinforcement, it crystallizes into diamond-hardness. Gain immunity to piercing damage and 30% reduction to all other physical damage. This state lasts until the armor is destroyed.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Glacier Incarnate    | Transform | Transform for 15s. Your ice armor becomes a living glacier — you triple your max shield HP, become immune to all knockback and movement CC, and emit a constant aura that damages and slows all attackers. When the transformation ends, the glacier shatters, dealing damage to all nearby enemies equal to 50% of all the damage you absorbed during the transformation.

>> Your notes on GLACIAL BULWARK:
>> - Glacial Bulwark is the classic "reactive tank" archetype. The more you get hit, the more dangerous you become.
>> - Arctic Fortress is a powerful but risky ability — being immobile is a real cost in most encounters.
>> - Glacier Incarnate's damage return mechanic should be calculated clearly in the UI so players can know when it's worth triggering.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: PERMAFROST AURA (Zone, Slow, Freeze Field)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Radiates an ever-intensifying aura of arctic cold that freezes the
ground, slows all nearby enemies, and eventually locks them in place — turning
the Frost Warden into a living weather event.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Frost Aura           | Aura      | Toggle. Emit a chilling aura in a 6m radius. Enemies in range suffer -15% movement speed. Costs a small amount of resource per second.
  - Glacial Ground       | Active    | Coat the ground in a 6m radius with ice. The area becomes difficult terrain for enemies (slipped footing) and persists for 10s. Allies are unaffected.
  - Biting Cold          | Passive   | Each second an enemy spends inside your aura adds 1 stack of Chill (max 5). At 5 stacks, they become Frozen Solid for 2s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Expanding Frost      | Passive   | Your Frost Aura radius increases by 1m for every 3 stacks of Chill present on enemies within it (max +4m). A fully chilled battlefield expands the aura to 10m.
  - Cold Snap            | Active    | Release a pulse of cold energy. All enemies in aura range immediately gain 2 stacks of Chill and are briefly slowed by 30% for 2s, regardless of their current stack count.
  - Windchill            | Debuff    | Target a specific enemy within aura range. Their Chill stacks now build twice as fast and the Freeze threshold triggers at 4 stacks instead of 5 against them.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Permafrost Pulse     | Active    | Flash-freeze the ground in a 12m radius. All enemies are instantly Frozen Solid (2s). The ground remains coated in deep ice for 20s, refreshing Chill stacks on any enemy who enters or stands in the area.
  - Arctic Presence      | Passive   | Enemies who are Frozen Solid near you shatter rather than thaw — on breaking free from freeze, they take damage equal to 8% of their max HP and are slowed for 3s.
  - Deep Winter          | Aura      | Toggle. Upgrade your Frost Aura to Deep Winter tier. Range expands to 10m, enemies take cold damage each second they remain inside, and the slow effect increases to 30%.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Blizzard Step        | Movement  | Move up to 8m instantly, leaving a trail of deep frost behind you. The trail persists for 8s, applying Chill stacks to enemies who walk across it.
  - Eternal Winter       | Toggle    | While active, your aura never diminishes. Chill stacks on enemies decay 75% slower, and any enemy who breaks free of Freeze immediately begins building Chill stacks again from the moment they unfreeze.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Tundra      | Active    | Expand your aura to a 25m radius for 12s. All enemies caught in the expansion are immediately Frozen Solid. For the duration, all enemies within the zone take cold damage each second, cannot receive movement-speed buffs, and accumulate Chill stacks 3x faster. When the duration ends, all remaining Frozen enemies shatter simultaneously for massive AoE damage.

>> Your notes on PERMAFROST AURA:
>> - Permafrost Aura is uniquely powerful in crowded encounters — the more enemies, the more rewarding the stack-building loop.
>> - Blizzard Step gives a rare mobility tool to the otherwise stationary Frost Warden, creating interesting positioning decisions.
>> - Absolute Tundra is the best crowd-freezing capstone in the game for large-scale encounters. Pair with AoE damage dealers.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: AVALANCHE (Offensive Ice, Counterattack, Brawler)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Weaponizes the cold as raw offensive force — retaliating against
attackers with ice shards, charging through enemies like a glacier, and
delivering crushing counterattacks that punish aggression.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Iceberg Punch        | Attack    | An armored fist strike that encrusts the target in frost on impact. Deals moderate damage and applies a 2s slow. Each time you are hit before using this skill, the damage increases by 10% (max 40%).
  - Frost Retaliation    | Reactive  | When you take a melee hit, automatically fire an ice shard back at the attacker dealing light damage and slowing them. No cooldown.
  - Glacial Momentum     | Passive   | When you move toward an enemy before attacking, the attack deals 15% bonus damage. The longer the approach distance, the higher the bonus (up to 30% for 6+ meters).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Avalanche Charge     | Movement  | Charge forward up to 10m in a straight line. Deals damage to the first enemy struck and sends ice debris flying outward in a cone, slowing all enemies in a 4m cone behind the primary target.
  - Shard Storm          | Attack    | Release a volley of ice shards in a wide cone, dealing moderate damage to all enemies in the arc. Enemies already slowed take 25% bonus damage from this skill.
  - Cold Fury            | Passive   | Track how many times you've been hit since last dealing damage. Each hit taken builds Cold Fury stacks. Your next attack consumes all stacks and deals bonus damage (5% per stack, max 50%).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Frozen Haymaker      | Attack    | Wind up for 0.5s and deliver a devastating ice-encrusted blow. Deals 200% damage and fully Freezes the target for 3s on hit. The windup is telegraphed but the freeze reward is significant.
  - Glacier Slam         | Active    | Leap up to 6m and come crashing down with ice-enhanced force. Deal heavy damage in a 5m radius on landing and create a large ice patch that persists for 8s.
  - Ice Tide             | Passive   | Each time a frozen or slowed enemy attacks you and you survive the hit, gain 5% attack speed and 5% damage for 8s (stacks up to 4 times). Represents you feeding on their wasted efforts.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Glacial Rampage     | Active    | Enter a berserker ice-fury for 8s. All your attacks deal bonus cold damage, each hit applies a Chill stack, and your counterattack shards fire automatically twice as fast. 30s cooldown.
  - Permafrost Skin     | Passive/Immunity   | When you would be killed, instead trigger a full-body ice shell — survive at 1 HP, become immune to damage for 3s, then shatter the shell to deal massive damage around you. Recharges once per encounter.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Avalanche       | Active    | Channel for 1.5s, encasing yourself in a rolling boulder of ice and snow, then hurl yourself forward up to 20m. Anything in your path takes catastrophic damage and is knocked aside. At the end of the roll, explode in a burst of ice shards that hit everything within 8m. If you collide with a wall or terrain feature, the explosion is doubled. 60s cooldown.

>> Your notes on AVALANCHE:
>> - Avalanche is the aggressive counterpart to the defensive Glacial Bulwark. Both take hits, but Avalanche takes them as fuel for offense.
>> - Permafrost Skin is the tank's emergency button — a once-per-encounter cheat death that rewards staying in the fray.
>> - The Avalanche capstone is designed to feel like a freight train. Collision with terrain doubling the explosion rewards environmental awareness.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON FROST WARDEN]
> Frost Warden is the most distinctive tank in the roster because all three archetypes engage with hits differently: Glacial Bulwark absorbs them into armor, Permafrost Aura ignores them in favor of field control, and Avalanche converts them into offensive output.
> The class has almost no healing — sustainability comes from damage reduction and reactive tools. Pair with a healer or Ballad Bard for long encounters.
> Cross-archetype synergy: Permafrost Aura slows enemies, Avalanche deals bonus damage to slowed targets, and Glacial Bulwark's Cryogenic Plate punishes the slowed enemies for daring to attack at all.
> The Frost Warden rewards patience and positioning — players who panic and run will lose their aura stacks and cold fury buffs simultaneously.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 67: STAR KNIGHT (Cosmic Paladin / Sci-Fi)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A warrior-saint empowered by the light of dying stars and the gravity
of collapsed suns — equal parts holy knight and cosmic force. They judge the
wicked with stellar fury, defend the worthy with constellations made manifest,
and bend the very physics of combat to serve their divine purpose.

ARCHETYPE A: NOVA GUARD (Stellar Energy, Shield, Burst Counter)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Stores the energy of incoming attacks in a stellar capacitor and
releases it as blinding nova bursts — the harder enemies hit, the brighter and
more destructive the retaliation.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Solar Ward           | Buff/Shield      | Conjure a shield of compressed stellar energy worth 20% of your max HP. When the shield is destroyed, it releases a small flash of light that briefly blinds nearby enemies (1s).
  - Star Pulse           | Attack    | Release a concentrated pulse of stellar energy forward. Deals moderate damage and pushes the target back 3m.
  - Photon Catch         | Reactive  | When struck by a projectile or energy attack, absorb a portion of its energy into your stellar capacitor. Reduce the damage by 15% and gain 1 charge of Stellar Energy (max 5).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Nova Burst           | Active    | Expend 3 Stellar Energy charges to release a nova explosion in a 6m radius. Deals moderate damage, blinds enemies for 1.5s, and knocks them back 2m.
  - Corona Shield        | Buff/Defense      | Upgrade your active shield with a corona layer. While the corona layer exists, enemies who hit you in melee take 10% of their damage back as radiant heat.
  - Solar Accumulator    | Passive   | Each Stellar Energy charge now adds +5% to the next Nova Burst's damage. Charges gained from absorbing enemy hits generate 1.5x as many charges as normal.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Flare Retaliation    | Reactive  | The first time you are critically hit in an encounter, instantly release all stored Stellar Energy charges as a blinding flare — dealing 15% of your max HP as damage to all nearby enemies and applying Blinded (2s) to the attacker.
  - Supernova Mantle     | Buff/Defense      | Encase yourself in a mantle of supernova light for 10s. All incoming damage is reduced by 20% and converted into Stellar Energy charges (up to 1 per hit). The mantle does not consume the shield but adds on top of it.
  - Event Horizon Fist   | Attack    | A punch charged with collapsed-star density. Deals heavy damage and briefly creates a micro-gravity field on impact — all enemies within 4m of the target are pulled 3m toward the impact point.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Star Fortress        | Active    | Manifest a full stellar energy barrier around yourself and one adjacent ally for 8s. Absorbs up to 60% of your combined max HP in damage. When destroyed early by enemy fire, explodes for massive radiant damage.
  - Luminous Apotheosis  | Passive   | When your Stellar Energy hits max charges (5), you automatically emit a radiant pulse every 3s that deals light damage in a 5m radius. You can still manually trigger Nova Burst — but now it costs only 2 charges and deals an additional 25% damage.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Dying Star           | Active    | Detonate your stellar core. For 3s, you become an unkillable radiant star — immune to all damage and CC. At the end of 3s, explode in a catastrophic stellar burst dealing massive damage in a 15m radius. The explosion scales with how much damage you absorbed during the 3s immunity window, adding 5% bonus damage per hit you took. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on NOVA GUARD:
>> - Nova Guard is a "suffer to retaliate" archetype. Dying Star is the ultimate payoff for standing in the fire.
>> - Photon Catch is interesting because it makes ranged enemy encounters actively more dangerous for the ranged enemies.
>> - The Stellar Energy economy (charges, burst, accumulator) creates a resource loop that feels organic and responsive.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: CONSTELLATION ARMOR (Pattern, Evolving Defense, Formations)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Manifests defensive constellations that adapt and evolve mid-battle —
locking in star-patterns that grant different bonuses, shifting configurations
as the fight progresses, and eventually achieving a final perfect constellation
form.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Star Map             | Active    | Manifest your first constellation node. Gain one of three patterns (chosen at use): Orion (melee damage +10%), Cassiopeia (healing received +15%), or Ursa (damage reduction +8%).
  - Celestial Link       | Passive/Movement   | Each constellation node you have active increases your movement speed by 3% (max 4 nodes, +12% total). The nodes trail behind you as orbiting light.
  - Astral Light         | Buff      | Emit a soft glow of constellation light that reveals invisible or hidden enemies within 8m and grants you 10% bonus accuracy for 8s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Twin Stars           | Active    | Add a second constellation node, choosing a second pattern independent of the first. Having 2 active nodes unlocks the first Dual-Pattern bonus.
  - Pattern Lock         | Passive   | When you have 2 nodes of the same pattern type active, that pattern's bonus doubles. You cannot be forcibly dispelled from constellation effects.
  - Shifting Heavens     | Active    | Rearrange your active constellation nodes in any order without losing them. Use to swap patterns mid-fight or trigger a different Dual-Pattern bonus. 8s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Third Star           | Active    | Add a third constellation node. Unlocks Triple-Pattern bonuses: three Orion nodes grant 25% attack speed; three Cassiopeia nodes grant regeneration; three Ursa nodes grant 30% damage reduction.
  - Astral Armor         | Passive/Evasion   | Your constellation nodes orbit defensively. Incoming projectiles have a 15% chance per active node to be deflected by a passing star (max 45% deflection chance at 3 nodes).
  - Zenith               | Active    | Instantly shift all your constellation nodes to a chosen single pattern for 6s, triggering the Triple-Pattern bonus regardless of your actual node distribution. Costs moderate resource.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Fourth Node         | Passive   | Unlock a fourth constellation node slot. Adds new Quadrant bonuses when all four nodes match: Orion x4 = fury (double attack speed), Cassiopeia x4 = undying (continuous healing), Ursa x4 = bastion (50% damage reduction, immune to knockback).
  - Falling Star        | Attack    | Mark a single constellation node and send it crashing into an enemy target as a meteor. Deals heavy damage, removes the node (reducing your active count by 1), and creates a 3m blast zone. The node regenerates after 12s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Celestial Alignment  | Transform | For 15s, achieve perfect stellar alignment. All four constellation nodes activate simultaneously at maximum pattern bonuses (you choose: all Orion, all Cassiopeia, or all Ursa), your armor becomes visible as a glowing suit of constellation-mapped stars, and you gain immunity to status effects. When the alignment ends, the constellations supernova — dealing damage scaled to how many node-activations occurred during the duration. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on CONSTELLATION ARMOR:
>> - Constellation Armor is the most strategic of the Star Knight archetypes — it rewards players who read the fight and adapt their pattern.
>> - Falling Star is a gamble: you sacrifice a node for a big damage hit. Timing matters.
>> - Triple and Quadrant bonuses should be clearly explained in the UI — the pattern system needs to be legible to be fun.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: COSMIC JUDGE (Gravity, Smite, Judgment)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Channels gravitational force and stellar judgment to deliver massive
single-target condemnations — slamming enemies with the weight of dying stars,
marking the guilty, and passing sentence that cannot be appealed.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Gravity Strike       | Attack    | A weapon strike laced with gravitational force. Deals moderate damage and increases the target's effective weight — they move 15% slower for 4s and fall faster when launched.
  - Mark of Judgment     | Debuff    | Brand a target with a stellar sigil. They deal 10% less damage and take 10% more damage from your attacks for 8s.
  - Cosmic Weight        | Passive   | Your melee attacks carry gravitational momentum. Enemies hit by your attacks are pushed down (grounded) — they cannot jump or fly for 2s after each hit.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Orbital Drop         | Attack    | Call a mass of stellar debris to fall on a target location after 0.8s. Deals moderate-heavy damage and creates a gravity field (6m) that slows all enemies inside by 25% for 5s.
  - Sentence             | Debuff    | Announce judgment on a marked target. For 12s, the first ability they use after being Sentenced costs double their resources. If they cannot pay, the ability is cancelled.
  - Gravity Well         | Active    | Create a dense gravity field at a target location for 6s. Pulls all enemies within 8m toward the center each second. Allies are unaffected.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Starfall Smite       | Attack    | Channel the power of a falling star into your weapon and deliver a devastating blow. Deals 150% damage, ignores 25% of armor, and stuns the target for 1.5s.
  - Verdict              | Active    | Pass judgment on an enemy who has used more than 3 abilities in this encounter. Deal damage equal to 5% of the total damage they have dealt to any party member in this fight. 20s cooldown.
  - Crushing Gravity     | Passive   | Enemies affected by Mark of Judgment have gravitational debuffs compounded — each gravity-based skill applied to them deals 15% bonus damage and lasts 25% longer.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Event Horizon        | Active    | Create a massive gravity anomaly at a target location for 8s. All enemies in a 12m radius are pulled inexorably toward the center (cannot resist), taking damage each second. Those who reach the center are suppressed (unable to act) until the field collapses.
  - Cosmic Condemnation  | Active    | Declare a target condemned. They cannot flee, use teleportation, or become invisible for 20s. If they die while Condemned, the condemnation leaps to the nearest enemy and resets to 10s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Gravity of Judgment  | Channel   | Channel for 2s while pointing at a single target, then collapse the local gravity around them. The target is suspended in place for 5s, taking massive damage each second as gravity crushes them from all directions. For each second they survive, they also gain stacks of a permanent -5% max HP reduction (reset at encounter end). When the 5s expires, they are slammed into the ground for damage equal to 10% of their max HP. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on COSMIC JUDGE:
>> - Cosmic Judge is the anti-boss archetype. Verdict is uniquely powerful in long fights where bosses spam many abilities.
>> - Gravity of Judgment is a slow but devastating capstone. The 5s window should feel agonizing for the enemy and triumphant for the player.
>> - Event Horizon pairs excellently with allies who have burst AoE — gather everything together, then detonate.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON STAR KNIGHT]
> Star Knight is the sci-fi paladin fantasy at its most fully realized. Each archetype represents a different axis of the "judge and protector" identity: Nova Guard retaliates against aggression, Constellation Armor adapts to survive, Cosmic Judge condemns and executes.
> The class is intentionally high-investment for high reward. Understanding when to trigger Nova Burst vs when to hold charges, which constellation pattern to lock, and when to spend Verdict are all skill-expression points.
> The aesthetic should be distinct from typical fantasy paladins — glowing constellation lines on armor, gravitational distortion effects, and cosmic light rather than gold and scripture.
> Cross-archetype synergy: Mark of Judgment (Cosmic Judge) amplifies all damage taken — Nova Guard's nova burst becomes even more punishing against a Judged target.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 68: RIFTWALKER (Dimensional Traveler)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A traveler between the folds of reality who treats the fabric of space
as a personal tool — attacking from impossible angles, storing and retrieving
objects from a private dimension, and rewriting the physical laws of localized
zones. To fight a Riftwalker is to fight on their terms, in their reality.

ARCHETYPE A: RIFT SLASH (Dimensional Strike, Reach, Ambush)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Opens micro-rifts to attack from any direction simultaneously —
striking through walls, emerging from unexpected angles, and repositioning
through the between-space faster than the eye can track.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Rift Cut             | Attack    | Open a small dimensional rift to extend your melee strike through space. Attack any target within 12m as if they were adjacent to you. Cannot be blocked by walls.
  - Phase Step           | Movement  | Instantly teleport up to 6m through dimensional space. Does not trigger attacks of opportunity. Leaves a brief afterimage at your departure point.
  - Rift Mark            | Debuff    | Mark a target with a dimensional scar. Your next Rift Cut against them deals 25% bonus damage and arrives from a random direction (bypasses directional defenses).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Dimensional Barrage  | Attack    | Open three sequential rifts and deliver three rapid strikes through them against the same or different targets within 10m. Each strike deals 70% of normal damage.
  - Flicker              | Movement  | Teleport to a target enemy's location, deal a quick strike on arrival, then teleport back to where you started. Total displacement: 0 — but the strike lands.
  - Between Spaces       | Passive/Evasion   | While you remain stationary for 1s, you partially phase into dimensional space. Gain 20% dodge chance, and your next teleport costs no resource.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Rift Storm           | Active    | For 5s, open rifts automatically every 0.8s, each delivering a strike at a random enemy in a 15m radius. These auto-strikes deal 60% damage but benefit from all your weapon buffs.
  - Spatial Puncture     | Attack    | Drive your weapon through dimensional space into an enemy's position from the opposite side. Deals heavy damage and applies a spatial bleed — they take minor damage each time they move for 5s, as the rift in their space reopens.
  - Rift Sense           | Passive   | You perceive space in 4 dimensions. Gain 360-degree vision (no backstab vulnerability), see through walls within 10m, and detect invisible entities within 6m.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Dimensional Echo     | Active    | For 8s, every melee strike you land spawns a delayed dimensional echo of that strike that hits 1.5s later from a different angle, dealing 50% of the original hit's damage. Cannot be blocked or dodged.
  - Rift Clone           | Active    | Open two rifts simultaneously — one you step into, one you step out of. At the entry rift, leave a dimensional copy of yourself that draws enemy attention and deals 30% of your damage for 8s before dissolving.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Omnidirectional Rift | Active    | For 6s, you exist at every point within a 15m radius simultaneously. All enemies in range take continuous damage as you strike from infinite angles. You cannot be targeted (enemies cannot select you as a target), cannot be AoE damaged, and your attacks cannot miss. When the effect ends, return to your original position and any enemies in the area are briefly disoriented (1.5s stun). 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on RIFT SLASH:
>> - Rift Slash is an assassin archetype that removes the normal positioning requirements of melee. It should feel slippery and disorienting to fight against.
>> - Rift Storm is a channeling DPS cooldown — the auto-strikes randomize targeting, which requires the player to accept controlled chaos.
>> - Omnidirectional Rift is the most visually impressive capstone — the player briefly becomes a dimensional hazard zone.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: POCKET DIMENSION (Storage, Retrieval, Safe Zone)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Maintains a personal pocket dimension that can store items, enemies,
allies, and threats — pulling things in and out of reality to control the tempo
and resources of a fight.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Banish Object        | Utility   | Send any non-living object (a projectile in flight, a weapon, a thrown item) into your pocket dimension. Retrieve it later or let it disappear. Interrupts certain environmental hazards.
  - Cache                | Utility   | Store a consumable item in your pocket dimension for later. Retrieve it instantly as a free action even during combat. Can store up to 3 items.
  - Dimensional Pocket   | Passive   | Your pocket dimension is always slightly open. Gain +2 inventory slots that are inaccessible to pickpockets, thieves, or Plunderer-class steal effects.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Pull Through         | Active    | Reach into your pocket dimension and retrieve a stored item or apply a stored buff/effect to yourself or an ally. Frees up Cache slot on use.
  - Fold Space           | Active    | Step into your pocket dimension for 1.5s — becoming completely untargetable and invisible before emerging at a location within 10m of where you entered.
  - Dimensional Anchor   | Active    | Throw an anchor into your pocket dimension and attach the other end to an ally. For 8s, that ally cannot be displaced by knockback, pulls, or movement CC (the dimension absorbs the force).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Swallow              | CC        | Grab a small enemy (below a size threshold) and shove them into your pocket dimension. They are stored for 4s — completely removed from the fight — then expelled back at a chosen location, taking fall damage and being briefly stunned (1s).
  - Armory Pull          | Active    | Pull a weapon, potion, or piece of equipment from your pocket dimension that you specifically stored before the encounter. Functions as a mid-fight weapon swap or supply grab.
  - Dimensional Overflow | Passive   | Your pocket dimension has expanded. Swallow can now target medium-sized enemies. Cache can store 5 items. Banished projectiles deal 30% bonus damage when retrieved and thrown back.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Safe Room            | Active    | Open a door into your pocket dimension for 4s. One ally can step inside — becoming completely untargetable and regenerating 20% of their max HP before stepping back out. The ally exits at a location of your choice within 10m.
  - Dimensional Arsenal  | Active    | Expel the contents of your pocket dimension all at once — all stored objects fly out as projectiles, all stored effects activate simultaneously, and any Swallowed enemies are expelled directly into each other for collision damage.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Inside Out           | Active    | Invert your pocket dimension, overlaying it on the battlefield. For 10s, you and your allies exist in the real world while enemies are pulled partially into pocket space — they take 30% increased damage from all sources, their abilities have a 30% chance to fail as dimensional interference disrupts them, and you can move freely through them. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on POCKET DIMENSION:
>> - Pocket Dimension is a utility/control archetype with enormous creative flexibility. Players who think laterally will find wild applications.
>> - Swallow is the signature CC — brief but absolute. Timing the 4s window is the key skill.
>> - Inside Out is a fight-flipping Legendary that is less about damage and more about creating a 10s window of absolute advantage.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: PLANAR SHIFT (Zone Rewrite, Physics Alteration, Reality Zones)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Creates localized zones where the rules of physics are rewritten —
reversing gravity, nullifying friction, altering projectile trajectories, and
layering environmental effects that make certain areas uniquely dangerous or
advantageous.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Low Gravity Zone     | Active    | Create a 6m zone at a target location for 8s. Gravity in the zone is reduced by 60%. All entities in the zone jump higher, fall slower, and have reduced impact from knockdowns.
  - Friction Null        | Active    | Remove friction in a 5m patch. Enemies moving through the zone slide uncontrollably, unable to stop or change direction. Lasts 6s.
  - Resonance Field      | Active    | Create a zone where sound and vibration are amplified. Enemies in the zone take 15% bonus damage from physical attacks for 8s (concussive resonance).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Gravity Spike        | Active    | Reverse gravity in a 4m area for 3s. Enemies are launched upward (dealing falling damage when the effect ends). Heavy enemies or enemies with grounding effects resist the launch but are still disoriented.
  - Refraction Layer     | Active    | Create a zone that bends light. Enemies targeting anyone inside the zone have 25% reduced accuracy. Allies inside gain 20% evasion. Lasts 10s.
  - Planar Overlay       | Passive   | You can maintain 2 Planar Shift zones simultaneously instead of 1. Each additional zone you create beyond 1 costs 20% more resource.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Time Dilation Field  | Active    | Create a zone of slowed time — 6m radius, 6s duration. Enemies inside move and attack at 60% speed. Allies inside move at normal speed (you are the source and exempt from the slow). Projectiles passing through are also slowed.
  - Null Physics         | Active    | Designate a 8m zone where conventional physics cease to apply entirely for 5s — projectiles go in random directions on entering the zone, enemies cannot predict their footing, and all movement in the zone is halved. Chaos zone.
  - Field Architect      | Passive   | Your Planar zones are now anchored — they do not drift when the environment shakes or when you are displaced. Zone durations increase by 3s each. You can stack two different zone effects in the same area.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Phase Cage          | Active    | Create a zone that functions as an inescapable spatial cage — 8m radius. Enemies inside cannot use teleportation or movement abilities out of the zone. Any enemy who attempts to leave the edge is repelled back. Allies pass freely. Lasts 10s.
  - Entropy Zone        | Active    | A 10m zone where entropy accelerates — all status effects on enemies expire 50% faster (beneficial to you) and all durations for buffs on allies extend by 50%. Active effects on enemies tick twice as fast. Lasts 12s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Reality Override    | Active    | Choose one of three laws of physics and rewrite it in a 20m zone for 15s: GRAVITY (enemies continuously take falling damage and cannot jump); VELOCITY (all movement speed is halved, attack speed is halved, and projectile speed is halved); MASS (all enemies become massively heavy — 50% movement penalty, instantly grounded, knockback-immune but also 40% more vulnerable to crushing and fall damage). Allies are exempt. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on PLANAR SHIFT:
>> - Planar Shift is the most creative and open-ended archetype in the Riftwalker class. Players will find unexpected applications.
>> - Phase Cage is the engagement-control tool — it prevents escape and should be used on priority targets the party wants to kill.
>> - Reality Override gives three very different encounter tools — GRAVITY is best in open-air fights, VELOCITY counters boss mobility, MASS punishes heavy melee enemies.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON RIFTWALKER]
> Riftwalker is the class that most rewards spatial intelligence and creative thinking. All three archetypes manipulate space in different ways: Rift Slash personalizes attacks through space, Pocket Dimension stores and retrieves the contents of space, Planar Shift rewrites what space does.
> The class is intentionally complex and rewards experience. New players will find Pocket Dimension most accessible; veterans will abuse Planar Shift.
> Cross-archetype combos: Phase Cage (Planar Shift) traps enemies while Dimensional Barrage (Rift Slash) hits them from all angles — a two-archetype prison setup.
> Pocket Dimension's Swallow + Planar Shift's Gravity Spike: swallow an enemy, set up Gravity Spike, expel the swallowed enemy directly into the spike zone. Intended combo.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 69: SPELLTHIEF (Magic-Stealing Rogue)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A cunning arcane predator who treats enemy magic as a personal
resource — draining mana, stealing spells mid-cast, and turning the enemy's
own magical arsenal against them. The more powerful the opponent's magic, the
more dangerous the Spellthief becomes.

ARCHETYPE A: SIPHON (Drain, Leech, Resource Theft)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Drains mana, stamina, and magical energy from enemies to fuel their
own abilities and deny enemies the resources to fight back.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Mana Drain           | Attack    | A quick melee or ranged touch attack. Deals light damage and steals 8% of the target's current mana (or equivalent resource), transferring it to yourself.
  - Arcane Leech         | Passive   | Each time you deal damage, recover 3% of the damage dealt as mana. Doubles to 6% against targets currently affected by any magical debuff.
  - Spell Sap            | Debuff    | Apply a draining hex to a target. Each ability they activate over the next 8s costs 15% more resource than normal, and you receive the excess as mana.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Hollow Touch         | Attack    | A strike that empties magical reserves. Deals moderate damage and drains 20% of the target's max mana pool. The drained mana is transferred to you (capped at your own max).
  - Siphon Chain         | Active    | A chain of draining energy leaps between up to 3 enemies. Each enemy loses 10% of their current mana, which accumulates and goes to you. Prioritizes highest-mana targets.
  - Hungry Blade         | Passive   | Your weapon becomes attuned to magical energy. Each kill you make that was draining mana at time of death refunds all mana spent in that engagement plus 10% bonus.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Mana Void            | Debuff    | Place a draining void on a target. For 12s, all mana regeneration they receive is nullified and redirected to you at 50% efficiency. Stacks with Spell Sap.
  - Arcane Hemorrhage    | Attack    | A deep strike that causes magical hemorrhage — the target leaks mana continuously for 8s (5% per second). You absorb the leaking mana if within 10m of them.
  - Spell Parasite       | Passive   | When an enemy uses a spell while they are below 40% mana, there is a 30% chance the spell fails to cast and you gain the mana they spent trying to cast it.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Soul Siphon          | Channel   | Lock eyes with a target and channel for 3s. Drain 40% of their total mana pool and 10% of their max HP, transferring both to yourself. Cannot be used on the same target twice in one encounter.
  - Famine              | Aura      | Toggle. All enemies within 8m regenerate mana 60% slower. Enemies in the aura who attempt to cast a spell while below 30% mana take psychic damage equal to 10% of their max HP.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Void        | Active    | Target one enemy. Instantly erase their entire mana pool — reduce them to 0 mana. You gain mana equal to half what they lost (capped at your max). The target cannot regenerate mana for 15s. Any ability they attempt to use during this time has a 60% chance to fail outright. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on SIPHON:
>> - Siphon is the resource denial archetype. Against spell-heavy enemies, it is devastating; against physical fighters with no mana, it is weaker — intentionally.
>> - Absolute Void is a hard counter to mana-dependent bosses and should be treated as a "silver bullet" design.
>> - Mana Void + Arcane Hemorrhage stacking creates a strong sustained drain combo at Tier 3.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: COUNTERSPELL (Interrupt, Deny, Punish Casting)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Watches for the precise moment an enemy commits to a magical cast and
punishes them for it — interrupting spells, storing their energy, and making
enemies afraid to cast at all.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Read the Weave       | Passive   | Passively detect when any enemy within 12m begins casting a spell (visual/audio cue + UI indicator). You gain 0.5s of advance warning before the spell fires.
  - Quick Intercept      | Reactive  | When an enemy begins a channeled or charged spell, dash up to 8m and deal a strike that interrupts the cast. 12s cooldown. The interrupted spell deals no effect.
  - Spell Silence        | Debuff    | Clap a magical silence over a single target. For 5s they cannot cast spells (physical abilities unaffected). 10s cooldown.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Arcane Punish        | Passive   | Whenever you successfully interrupt a spell, deal bonus damage equal to 20% of the interrupted spell's mana cost as a free strike. Also gain 5% increased damage for 8s.
  - Null Ward            | Active    | Create a ward in a 6m zone for 8s. Any spell entering or leaving the zone is interrupted and fizzles. Costs no mana while active but has a moderate cooldown.
  - Anticipation         | Passive   | After interrupting a spell, your next attack is guaranteed to critically strike. Stacks once — the crit is consumed on next attack regardless.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Void Interrupt       | Active    | Interrupt ANY enemy ability — not just spells. Interrupt a melee special, a buff, or a channel. The interrupted ability goes on full cooldown for the enemy. 20s cooldown.
  - Caster's Bane        | Debuff    | Apply a hex to a target. For 15s, any spell they cast triggers a 10% chance to backfire — dealing the spell's damage or effect back at themselves instead of at the intended target.
  - Perfect Counter      | Passive   | Your interrupts have no cast time — all are instant. Additionally, you take 15% less magical damage from sources whose abilities you have interrupted at least once this encounter.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Lockdown            | Active    | Apply a comprehensive magical lockout to a target. For 8s, all their spells are silenced AND their physical special abilities have a 40% chance to fail. A total combat suppression. 30s cooldown.
  - Spell Scar          | Passive   | Each time you interrupt a spell, leave a Spell Scar on the caster that persists for the encounter. Each scar increases your damage against that target by 3% (max 10 scars = +30% damage).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Last Word        | Active    | Point at a target and declare their magic ended. For 10s, the target cannot cast any spell, ability, or special move — they are reduced to basic attacks only. Simultaneously, you gain a copy of their most recently interrupted spell as a one-time-use ability that you can fire back immediately. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on COUNTERSPELL:
>> - Counterspell is the most reactive of the three archetypes. Players must be attentive and responsive rather than proactive.
>> - Spell Scar creates a compounding damage advantage in long fights — each interrupt builds toward a massive damage bonus.
>> - The Last Word is both a denial tool and a payoff — the stolen ability copy makes it feel like justice rather than just suppression.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: SPELL MIRROR (Copy, Reflect, Redirect)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Catches enemy spells and transforms them — reflecting them back at
their source, redirecting them at new targets, or copying them into your own
arsenal for repeated use.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Arcane Catch         | Reactive  | When struck by a spell, absorb a small portion of its magic into a mirror shard. Reduce the incoming damage by 10% and store 1 Mirror Charge. Max 3 charges.
  - Flashback            | Active    | Release all stored Mirror Charges as a single magical burst in a 6m cone, dealing damage equal to 40% of the absorbed spells' combined power.
  - Mirror Skin          | Passive/Resistance   | Your skin faintly reflects magical energy. Gain 10% innate magic resistance. Additionally, projectile spells that graze you (but don't directly hit) have a 20% chance to reflect.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Redirection          | Reactive  | When a projectile spell is aimed at you, deflect it to a new target within 8m. The spell deals its full effect to the new target instead. 8s cooldown.
  - Copy Spell           | Active    | Observe an enemy casting a spell and swipe a duplicate of it from the weave. Copy their currently-casting spell and add it to your active abilities for one use. 15s cooldown.
  - Reflective Stance    | Stance    | Enter a reflective stance for 5s. Any spell that hits you in this window is reflected back at the caster at 75% power. Melee attacks pass through normally.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Grand Reflection     | Active    | Reflect the next spell that hits you back at double power regardless of its damage type. Single use per activation, then a 20s cooldown.
  - Spell Library        | Passive   | You can store up to 3 copied spells at a time instead of 1. Copied spells persist until used (not just for the encounter). Each copied spell used in combat refreshes your Mirror Charge generation.
  - Echo Trap            | Active    | Set an invisible mirror ward at a location. The first spell cast at or near that location (within 3m) is automatically caught and reflected at the original caster at full power. 12s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Perfect Mirror       | Active    | For 6s, all spells aimed at you are perfectly mirrored back to the caster at 100% power without damaging you. During this window, you are immune to magical damage. 35s cooldown.
  - Spell Cascade        | Passive   | When you reflect or redirect a spell that damages an enemy, it has a 30% chance to cast again at a random nearby enemy for 50% power. The cascaded copy cannot cascade again.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Mirror World         | Active    | For 8s, invert all magic in a 15m radius. All spells cast by enemies have their targets reversed (they hit the caster) at 80% power. Allies' spells are unaffected. Enemies who realize what is happening can choose not to cast — but their silence plays into your Counterspell partners' hands. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on SPELL MIRROR:
>> - Spell Mirror is the most satisfying archetype when it works — nothing compares to a boss killing themselves with their own ultimate.
>> - Mirror World is the capstone that requires the most coordination. Against enemies with scripted ability rotations, this can be fight-defining.
>> - Spell Cascade creates a snowball potential: reflected spells can chain to more enemies if lucky, which makes the archetype explosive in multi-target fights.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON SPELLTHIEF]
> Spellthief is the premier magic-counter class. All three archetypes engage differently with enemy magic: Siphon takes the fuel, Counterspell takes the moment, Spell Mirror takes the effect.
> In a world where powerful enemy casters are common encounters, Spellthief is invaluable. In a purely physical combat environment, their power decreases significantly — this is an intentional design asymmetry.
> The class should feel like judo — using the enemy's power against them. Skills should have satisfying audio/visual feedback (glowing stolen spells, mirrored beams, etc.).
> Cross-archetype synergy: Counterspell's interrupts feed Mirror Charges (Arcane Catch), and Siphon's Hollow Touch softens targets before Counterspell locks them down entirely.
> Compare and contrast with Corsair Plunderer: Plunderer steals buffs and resources; Spellthief steals magic mechanics. Plunderer is broader; Spellthief is deeper.
>


################################################################################
CLASS 70: BARD (Music/Performance)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A performer who treats combat as a stage — weaving melodies that mend
wounds and bolster allies over time, hammering out war rhythms that drive
companions into a killing fury, and singing dirges so mournful they break enemy
will and unravel their capabilities. The music is real, and it is lethal.

ARCHETYPE A: BALLAD (Sustained Healing, Buffs, Songs Over Time)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Maintains a healing atmosphere through continuous song — layering
regenerative melodies on allies, amplifying their stats over time, and building
toward powerful crescendo heals that reward sustained performance.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Song of Mending      | Heal      | Begin a gentle healing melody. Target ally regenerates 5% of max HP per second for 6s. Cancels if the Bard takes heavy damage (30%+ in one hit).
  - Verse of Valor       | Buff      | Sing a brief empowering verse to one ally, granting them +10% attack damage and +10% movement speed for 8s.
  - Lullaby of Focus     | Buff/Immunity      | Hum a focusing tune that clears minor mental debuffs (confusion, fear, charm) from a target ally and grants them +15% ability accuracy for 6s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Chorus Heal          | Heal      | Sing an uplifting chorus that heals all allies within 8m for a moderate amount simultaneously. More powerful if at least 3 allies are in range (bonus 20% effectiveness).
  - Ballad of Endurance  | Buff/Immunity      | A slow, steady ballad applied to a single ally. Increases their maximum HP by 15% for 12s and grants them immunity to the next crowd control effect they receive.
  - Resonant Verse       | Passive/Utility   | Each second you spend singing (any song skill active), build 1 stack of Resonance (max 10). Stacks amplify your next Heal or Buff by 5% per stack.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Epic Ballad          | Channel   | Channel a long-form ballad for up to 8s. Each second heals all allies in a 10m radius for 4% max HP. The ballad can be briefly interrupted by movement but resumes if you stop within 1.5s.
  - Song of Rising       | Heal      | Revive a downed ally mid-combat, restoring them to 30% HP with a 5s window of damage reduction (50%) so they can stabilize. Cooldown: 45s.
  - Harmonic Aura        | Aura      | Toggle. Emit a gentle harmonic aura. Allies within 8m regenerate 2% max HP per second passively. Drains a small amount of your resource per second.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Grand Ballad        | Channel   | Channel for 3s and unleash a powerful healing wave: restore 35% of max HP to all allies within 12m and apply 6s of immunity to the next damaging debuff each ally would receive.
  - Crescendo           | Active    | Consume all current Resonance stacks. For each stack spent: heal the lowest-HP ally for 4% of their max HP and apply one 6s buff stack (damage, speed, or protection — whichever that ally most lacks). One massive payoff at max stacks.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Eternal Refrain  | Toggle    | While toggled, perform an unending ballad. All allies within 12m receive continuous healing (3% max HP per second), a stacking 5% damage reduction (up to 25% over 5s), and immunity to instant-death effects. Costs high resource per second. When toggled off or interrupted, trigger a final healing pulse restoring 20% max HP to all allies in range.

>> Your notes on BALLAD:
>> - Ballad is a slow-burn support archetype. Resonance stacking rewards players who stay in the fight singing rather than breaking off to attack.
>> - Song of Rising (Tier 3) is one of the few mid-combat revives in the game — a defining party-utility moment.
>> - The Eternal Refrain is best in sustained long encounters. Short fights won't let it ramp up, which is an intentional limitation.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: WAR MARCH (Tempo, Attack Speed, Aggression Buffs)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Drives allies into a combat frenzy through escalating tempo — building
attack speed, amplifying damage, granting extra actions, and eventually
conducting a full berserker symphony that turns the entire party into a
relentless offensive machine.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Quick March          | Buff      | Beat out a rapid march tempo. Target ally gains +20% movement speed and +10% attack speed for 8s.
  - Battle Hymn          | Buff      | A rousing war cry-song applied to one ally. Their next 3 attacks deal +15% bonus damage.
  - Drumbeat             | Passive   | While any War March skill is active on an ally, they generate 10% more threat (enemies prefer to target them) — the Bard channels aggression outward, not inward.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Rallying Chorus      | Buff      | Apply a rallying war chorus to all allies within 8m. Grant +10% attack speed and +5% critical strike chance for 10s.
  - Ferocity Song        | Buff      | Target one ally and build their ferocity. Stack 1 charge of Ferocity on them per second for 6s. Each stack of Ferocity increases their damage by 4% and reduces their cooldowns by 0.5s (up to +24% damage and -3s cooldowns at 6 stacks).
  - Tempo Surge          | Passive   | When an ally under your buff scores a kill, reset the duration of all your active song buffs on them to full. Chain-kills extend the march indefinitely.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - War Cadence          | Aura      | Toggle. Emit a constant war cadence aura in 8m. All allies in range gain +15% attack speed and +10% damage. Costs moderate resource per second.
  - Berserker's Anthem   | Buff/Risk      | Unleash an intense, frenzied anthem on a single ally. For 8s they gain double attack speed, 25% bonus damage, but also take 15% more damage (battle rage removes defensive instincts). Exhilarating but risky.
  - The Extra Beat       | Passive   | Allies with your attack speed buffs have a 15% chance on each attack to gain an extra immediate free attack. This free attack inherits all current buffs but cannot itself trigger another extra beat.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - March of Doom       | Active    | A thunderous war march that affects the entire party. All allies gain +25% attack speed, +20% damage, and +15% movement speed for 10s. Additionally, the first hit each ally lands during this window cannot miss and is automatically a critical strike.
  - Conductor's Fury    | Active    | For 6s, take direct control of your party's tempo — synchronize all allies' attacks to fire simultaneously once every 1.5s in a unified strike. Each synchronized strike deals 80% of each ally's normal attack damage but hits all enemies in front of the party simultaneously. 35s cooldown.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Indomitable March    | Channel   | Channel for 1s to begin the legendary war march. For 15s, the entire party enters an unstoppable combat frenzy: +30% damage, +30% attack speed, +20% movement speed, and cannot be CC'd, slowed, or rooted. All kills during the march have a 50% chance to reset the cooldown of the killed enemy's most threatening ability from triggering again (cutting down their revival or power surge mechanics). When the march ends, all allies are briefly exhausted (10% reduced stats for 5s), but they are alive. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on WAR MARCH:
>> - War March is the premier offensive support archetype. Berserker's Anthem is high-variance and should be saved for DPS allies who can handle the tradeoff.
>> - Conductor's Fury is the highest-skill-expression skill in the War March tree — it requires the player to understand their party's composition and enemy clustering.
>> - Indomitable March is a fight-turning "never say die" Legendary, best used when the party is about to wipe.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: DIRGE (Debuff, Despair, Enemy Degradation)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Sings the songs of sorrow and ending — lacing melodies with hopeless
dread that unravels enemy morale, strips their combat effectiveness, and
ultimately drives them to surrender, flee, or destroy each other.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Lament               | Debuff    | Begin a mournful lament aimed at a target enemy. They suffer -15% damage output for 6s as despair saps their will to fight.
  - Discordant Note      | Attack    | A sharp, painful note launched at a target. Deals light damage and interrupts their current action (cancels casting or a charged attack).
  - Elegy of Exhaustion  | Debuff    | A draining melody that slows one target's attack and movement speed by 20% for 8s. Visually, they appear weighed down by grief.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Despair Chorus       | Debuff    | Broadcast a chorus of despair in a 10m radius. All enemies inside suffer -10% damage, -10% attack speed, and -10% movement speed for 6s. The debuffs stack with individual Dirge effects.
  - Cacophony            | CC        | A burst of dissonant sound that disorients one target. They suffer 2s of confusion — attacking a random ally or wasting an ability on an invalid target.
  - Sorrow's Weight      | Passive   | Each time you apply a Dirge debuff, it also applies a stacking Sorrow charge on the target (max 5). At 5 stacks, the target becomes Demoralized — taking 20% increased damage from all sources.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Wailing Wall         | Active    | Create a 10m wall of sonic despair at a target location. Enemies who attempt to cross the wall are hit with a burst of Dirge energy, suffering heavy damage and a 3s fear effect. The wall lasts 8s.
  - Song of Unraveling   | Debuff    | Begin unraveling a target's capabilities. Each second this song plays (up to 6s), remove 1 random buff from the target. 6s duration, affecting up to 6 buffs.
  - Siren's Call         | CC        | A mesmerizing melody compels one enemy to walk toward you for 3s. They take no action except movement. Cannot affect enemies immune to charm. 20s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Requiem             | Active    | Channel for 2s and perform a haunting requiem. All enemies within 12m who are below 50% HP immediately flee in terror for 4s, unable to fight. Enemies above 50% HP suffer a 3s fear instead (no flee). 30s cooldown.
  - Funeral March       | Aura      | Toggle. Play a funeral march that affects all enemies within 10m. Their resource regeneration is reduced by 40%, their ability recharge speeds are slowed by 20%, and any healing they receive is reduced by 25%. A constant grinding pressure. Moderate resource cost per second.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Last Lullaby     | Channel   | Channel for 3s, filling the battlefield with an ethereal lullaby. All enemies within 20m must save against the song or fall into a 6s combat-sleep (frozen in place, unable to act, reduced to 1 damage resistance). Enemies who save still suffer 3s of heavy despair debuffs (-30% to all stats). During the sleep, allies may freely act. When the lullaby ends or a sleeping enemy is struck, they wake with a 2s disorientation. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on DIRGE:
>> - Dirge is the debuff Bard — the dark mirror of Ballad. While Ballad heals and builds allies up, Dirge tears enemies down.
>> - Song of Unraveling is a slow dispel — less burst than Grand Larceny (Corsair) but more methodical and can strip a boss over multiple seconds.
>> - The Last Lullaby is one of the most powerful Legendaries in the game for mass encounters. Its 3s channel makes it interruptable — protecting the Bard during the channel is critical.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON BARD]
> Bard is the purest support class in the game and the most thematically cohesive. All three archetypes represent the same performance mechanic expressed in three emotional registers: Ballad is hope, War March is fury, Dirge is despair.
> Bard can function as a full healer (Ballad), a force multiplier (War March), or a debuff specialist (Dirge) — or a mixed performer. The class rewards players who read which role the party needs moment to moment.
> The "song cancels on heavy hit" mechanic on some Ballad skills creates interesting defensive micro-decisions. Consider letting the Bard invest in song endurance as a late upgrade.
> Dirge's Last Lullaby + War March's March of Doom is the ultimate dual-archetype combination: put enemies to sleep, then wake them up into a party that has been buffed to terrifying levels for the remaining 15s. Intended and very satisfying.
> The Bard closes the 63-70 roster as its most traditional class. Their presence anchors the group of eight classes, ensuring there is always an accessible, party-oriented option alongside the more exotic entries (Worldsinger, Riftwalker, Spellthief).
>


################################################################################
CLASS 71: Monk (Martial Arts/Ki)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A living weapon who has spent a lifetime refining their body into an instrument of pure martial precision, channeling ki energy through disciplined strikes, fluid counters, and explosive inner-fire techniques that make the unarmed body more dangerous than any blade.

ARCHETYPE A: Iron Fist (Striking, Combo, Raw Power)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Builds combo chains through disciplined striking sequences, spending accumulated hits to trigger devastating finishers.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Jab                 | Attack   | A fast light punch dealing low damage but generating 1 Combo Point; lowest cooldown of all strikes
  - Cross               | Attack   | A straight powerful punch dealing moderate damage and generating 2 Combo Points
  - Iron Stance         | Stance   | Enter Iron Stance; all strike damage is increased by 10% but movement speed is reduced by 10%

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Rising Uppercut     | Attack   | A rising strike that launches the target slightly airborne for 1s, dealing good damage and generating 3 Combo Points
  - Body Blow           | Attack   | Strike the torso, dealing moderate damage and applying a 15% damage reduction debuff to the target for 10s
  - Combo Mastery       | Passive  | At 5 Combo Points, your next strike automatically triggers Combo Finisher; Finisher damage scales with points spent

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Hundred Fists       | Attack   | Spend 4 Combo Points to deliver a 10-hit flurry in 2s, each hit dealing moderate damage
  - Armor Crack         | Attack   | Spend 3 Combo Points for a precise strike that reduces the target's physical armor by 25% for 15s
  - Steel Body          | Passive/Defense  | Increase physical damage reduction by 15% while in Iron Stance; blocking a hit refunds 1 Combo Point

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Dragon's Fist       | Attack   | Spend all Combo Points to deliver a single catastrophic blow dealing damage multiplied by Combo Points spent (max 10x)
  - Perfect Form        | Buff     | Enter perfect iron form for 12s; all attacks generate double Combo Points and Finisher damage is increased by 50%

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Thousand Strike Technique | Active | Channel your life's training into a 6s burst of absolute striking mastery; you execute 30 perfectly placed strikes in sequence against up to 3 targets, each hit generating Combo Points that are immediately spent on Finishers, creating a chain of Finishers that deals cascading damage, and culminating in a single Dragon's Fist using all accumulated points simultaneously

>> Your notes on Iron Fist:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Flowing Water (Evasion, Counter, Redirect)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Flows around attacks like water, using enemy force against them through precise redirections and counter-strikes that punish aggression.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Step Aside          | Movement | Dodge an incoming attack by stepping 2m to the side; the dodge generates no cooldown and costs only stamina
  - Redirect            | Reactive | Catch and redirect a melee strike, pushing the attacker off balance and dealing 50% of their attack back as damage
  - Water Stance        | Stance   | Enter Water Stance; gain +20% dodge chance and all successful dodges generate a Counter Charge

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Flow Counter        | Reactive | After a successful dodge in Water Stance, automatically counter-attack for 120% of normal damage
  - Fluid Step          | Movement | Chain up to 3 quick directional steps in rapid succession, evading multiple attacks in sequence
  - Like Water          | Passive/Defense  | In Water Stance, CC effects have 30% reduced duration; each successful counter reduces the next incoming attack's damage by 10%

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Whirlpool Counter   | Reactive | Absorb an attack and spin with it, redirecting the enemy's force into a sweeping strike that hits all enemies around you for damage based on the absorbed attack's power
  - Still Water         | Active   | Become perfectly still for 2s; all attacks aimed at you during this time generate maximum Counter Charges but deal only 30% damage
  - Current Strike      | Attack   | Spend 3 Counter Charges to release them in a rapid fluid combo that hits the target 5 times with perfect precision

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - River's Fury        | Active   | For 8s, every attack aimed at you automatically generates a Counter Charge and triggers an immediate counter-strike at 80% damage
  - Perfect Flow        | Passive/Immunity  | In Water Stance with 5 or more Counter Charges, your movement becomes frictionless — you cannot be slowed, knocked back, or rooted

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Ocean's Wrath       | Active   | Enter a 15s state of absolute fluidity; all enemy attacks are automatically dodged and converted into Counter Charges, all Counter Charges are spent as immediate counter-strikes that deal the original attack's full damage back to the attacker, and you flow freely between all enemies in range using their own force to chain between targets — the more enemies attack, the more devastation you redirect

>> Your notes on Flowing Water:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Inner Fire (Ki Projection, Energy, Range)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Focuses ki energy outward, projecting it as ranged blasts, auras, and explosive waves that make the Monk dangerous at any distance.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Ki Bolt             | Attack   | Project a focused bolt of ki energy at a target, dealing moderate damage at range
  - Ki Aura             | Aura     | Radiate ki energy in a 5m field; all enemies within take minor ki damage per second and have their movement slightly slowed
  - Focus               | Active   | Spend 2s in meditation to fully restore your Ki Gauge and gain a brief +20% ki damage bonus

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Ki Blast            | Attack   | Release a larger ki projectile dealing heavy damage to a single target at range
  - Inner Flame         | Buff     | Ignite your ki into a visible flame; for 15s all ki attacks deal bonus fire damage and your melee strikes burn the target
  - Wave of Force       | Attack   | Release a forward ki wave hitting all enemies in a 10m line for moderate damage and pushing them back 3m

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Ki Explosion        | Active   | Concentrate ki to a point on an enemy then detonate it for heavy AoE damage in a 6m radius
  - Focused Beam        | Channel  | Channel a continuous ki beam for up to 4s, dealing escalating damage each second it connects to the same target
  - Ki Resonance        | Passive  | Multiple ki hits on the same target within 5s create a resonance; the 4th hit triggers an automatic ki explosion on that target

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Spirit Bomb         | Channel  | Charge for 4s to gather ki from your surroundings then release a massive ki sphere dealing catastrophic damage in a 12m radius
  - Inner Supernova     | Active   | Release all ki energy in an omnidirectional burst from your body, dealing heavy AoE damage to all enemies within 15m and launching them outward

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Transcendent Ki     | Transform| Achieve ki transcendence for 20s; your body becomes a living ki reactor — all ki attacks are free-cast and automatically chain to nearby enemies, your Ki Aura expands to 15m and deals significant damage per second, your melee strikes each release a ki bolt as bonus damage, and every 5s your ki overflows in a Spirit Bomb release that costs nothing; the transcendence ends with a final ki nova that deals massive damage to all enemies on the field

>> Your notes on Inner Fire:
>>
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON MONK]
>
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 72: Summoner (Pure Summoning)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A commander of conjured beings who wages war through proxy, their true power measured not in personal combat ability but in the quality, variety, and coordination of the entities they bring into existence.

ARCHETYPE A: Eidolon (Single Summon, Customize, Powerful)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Maintains a single powerful Eidolon companion that can be customized between encounters, fighting as a bonded pair with deep mechanical synergy.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Summon Eidolon      | Summon   | Summon your Eidolon into battle; it fights independently with moderate base stats
  - Bond Strike         | Active   | Command your Eidolon to make an empowered attack, dealing 150% of its normal damage on the next hit
  - Shared Vitality     | Passive/Heal  | Your Eidolon and you share a portion of HP regeneration; 20% of HP healed on either transfers to the other

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Eidolon Evolution   | Active   | Between combats, add one Evolved Trait to your Eidolon (choose from: armored, swift, elemental, toxic, or vanguard)
  - Combined Arms       | Active   | Command the Eidolon to fight in perfect sync with your next attack; both strike simultaneously dealing combined damage
  - Eidolon Resilience  | Passive/Heal  | Your Eidolon has 50% of your maximum HP and regenerates 2% per second when not taking damage

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Full Evolution      | Active   | Apply 2 additional Evolved Traits simultaneously, creating a powerful hybrid Eidolon variant for this encounter
  - Soul Link           | Buff     | Forge a deep soul link; for 15s your Eidolon's damage is added to yours on every strike and vice versa
  - Recall and Reform   | Summon   | Recall the Eidolon and instantly resummon it at full HP at any location within 20m; 30s cooldown

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Apex Eidolon        | Active   | Trigger your Eidolon's apex transformation for 20s; all its stats are doubled and it gains a new ability based on its Evolved Traits
  - Shared Wrath        | Active   | For 10s, every attack you land is simultaneously mirrored by your Eidolon on the same target at 80% of your damage

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Eternal Bond        | Transform| Achieve perfect fusion with your Eidolon for 25s; you fight as one being with combined HP pools, all of the Eidolon's Evolved Trait abilities added to your own kit, double the attack speed of either alone, and a shared rage state that triggers at 50% HP to double all damage output — when the fusion ends the Eidolon re-separates at full HP and immediately uses Apex form for the remaining encounter

>> Your notes on Eidolon:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Elemental Circle (Rotation, Elements, Synergy)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Maintains a rotating circle of elemental summons — fire, water, earth, and wind — cycling through them to create elemental synergies between active combinations.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Summon Ember        | Summon   | Summon an Ember elemental that attacks with fire damage and applies burn stacks
  - Summon Ripple       | Summon   | Summon a Ripple elemental that attacks with water damage and applies slow stacks
  - Circle Awareness    | Passive  | Gain elemental awareness; see the current active elements and their synergy bonuses in real time

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Summon Shard        | Summon   | Summon a Shard elemental that attacks with earth damage and applies armor reduction stacks
  - Summon Gust         | Summon   | Summon a Gust elemental that attacks with wind damage and applies movement disruption stacks
  - Elemental Synergy   | Passive  | Two active opposing elementals (fire/water, earth/wind) create a synergy bonus that increases both their damage by 25%

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Steam Surge         | Active   | Trigger a fire/water synergy explosion; both elementals combine their next attack into a scalding steam burst dealing heavy AoE damage
  - Landslide           | Active   | Trigger an earth/wind synergy strike; both combine into a debris storm dealing AoE damage and obscuring the battlefield for 8s
  - Full Circle         | Active   | Maintain all 4 elementals simultaneously for 15s, triggering all possible synergies at once

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Elemental Rotation  | Summon   | Rapidly cycle through all 4 elementals in sequence, each one releasing a burst attack before the next is summoned, creating 4 rapid hits of different damage types
  - Resonant Circle     | Passive  | With all 4 elementals active, they resonate and automatically combine for a free group attack every 8s dealing all 4 damage types simultaneously

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Grand Elemental     | Active   | Fuse all 4 active elementals into a single Grand Elemental for 20s; it deals all 4 damage types per hit, hits 3 targets per attack, applies all elemental status effects simultaneously, and its destruction at duration's end releases a catastrophic elemental explosion applying maximum stacks of all 4 status effects to all enemies on the field

>> Your notes on Elemental Circle:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Horde Master (Mass Summons, Numbers, Swarm)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Maintains a large number of individually weak summons, overwhelmingly enemies through sheer numbers and clever direction of mass swarm attacks.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Summon Imp          | Summon   | Summon a single Imp with low stats that attacks the nearest enemy; can maintain up to 8 Imps simultaneously
  - Horde Command       | Active   | Direct all active summons to focus a single target for 5s, coordinating their attacks
