ARCHETYPE C: Event Horizon (Pull-In Tank Aura / Inescapable / Gravity Well)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: An immovable anchor who warps gravity around themselves to draw enemies
inward, punishing any attempt to flee and making the Gravity Knight the inescapable
center of the fight.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Gravity Well       | Aura      | Emit a persistent field pulling enemies within 8m toward you by 1m per second. Toggle on/off.
  - Anchor Point       | Active    | Plant yourself; become immovable for 4s and generate a stronger pull field (12m range, 2m/s).
  - Inertia Lock       | Passive/Defense   | Enemies that have been pulled toward you at least 3m suffer a 15% damage penalty on their next attack.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Horizon Pulse      | Active    | Release a wave of inverted gravity; briefly launches all nearby enemies up before the gravity snaps back, slamming them down.
  - Dense Mantle       | Passive/Defense   | Your own armor is effectively doubled while standing still; pulling enemies increases your armor rating by 3% per enemy within 5m.
  - Black Hole Taunt   | CC        | Force all enemies within 12m to target only you for 5s while the pull field intensifies, making evasion 30% harder.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Tidal Crush        | Active    | Reverse the gravity field to maximum pull for 3s; all enemies within 15m are dragged to your position and take increasing crush damage the closer they get.
  - Inescapable Orbit  | Passive   | Enemies that have been within your pull field for 4+ consecutive seconds are Tethered: they cannot move more than 8m from you.
  - Singularity Step   | Passive   | When you move, your gravity well moves with you at full strength; no cooldown on repositioning the field.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Event Horizon Flare| Active    | Spike your pull field to maximum intensity for 6s; all enemies within 20m are frozen in pulled trajectory (cannot move away), and you deal 10% of their HP as crush damage per second.
  - Collapsed Star     | Passive/Defense   | When 4+ enemies are simultaneously within 5m of you, your armor becomes absolute (90% damage reduction) and your attacks deal 25% more damage for 3s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Gravity   | Active    | For 12s, emit an inescapable event horizon: no enemy within 25m can move away from you, all ranged attacks fired away from you are curved back toward your position, and you generate a crushing DoT that deals 3% of each enemy's max HP per second. Ends with a collapse burst dealing 20% max HP to all in range. 120s cooldown.

################################################################################
CLASS 83: Soulbound Archer (Spectral Ranged)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A warrior whose spirit has partially crossed into the realm of the dead,
allowing them to fire arrows of pure ectoplasm that phase through walls, summon
guiding spirits to track quarry, and rain down the wrath of an entire army of
fallen archers from beyond.

ARCHETYPE A: Ghost Arrow (Phasing / Piercing / Wall Penetration)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A ranged striker who ignores terrain and conventional cover, threading
spectral shots through solid matter and phasing past shields to reach enemies who
believe themselves safe behind walls.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Ectoplasm Shot     | Attack    | Fire an arrow of condensed spirit energy; deals moderate damage and passes through one piece of thin terrain (doors, light walls).
  - Phase Nock         | Buff      | Your next shot becomes fully phased: it ignores all terrain and shields, striking the first living target in its path regardless of cover.
  - Soul Sight         | Passive   | See enemy outlines through walls within 10m; this sight persists for 3s after they leave your line of sight.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Spectral Pierce    | Attack    | Fire an arrow that passes through unlimited enemies in a line (up to 15m), dealing full damage to each.
  - Haunt Shot         | Debuff    | An arrow that embeds a haunting spirit in the target; for 6s, they are visible through terrain and take +10% damage from all your attacks.
  - Phase Window       | Utility   | Create a 2m spectral aperture in a wall; you can fire through it and see through it for 10s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Wall Walk Round    | Attack    | Fire an arrow that travels through walls and curves slightly to track the last known position of a target behind cover.
  - Veil Barrage       | Active    | Fire 4 rapid ghost arrows in sequence, each phasing through terrain; targets hit by 2+ arrows in one barrage are briefly exposed (cannot use cover for 3s).
  - Ethereal Quiver    | Passive   | All arrows deal bonus damage equal to 5% of your max HP as spirit damage that bypasses armor entirely.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Haunting Volley    | Attack    | Fire 6 phasing arrows simultaneously in a tight spread; they all converge on a single marked point on the other side of any intervening terrain.
  - Death Sight        | Passive   | You permanently see all living beings through walls within 20m; your phasing shots travel 30% faster and deal +15% damage.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Arrow of Finality  | Channel   | Spend 2s aiming through all terrain in a 40m line; fire a single massive spectral arrow that passes through every object and enemy in the path, dealing 400% weapon damage to each. The arrow leaves a trail of spectral energy that slows all who cross it for 5s. 60s cooldown.

ARCHETYPE B: Spirit Guide (Homing / Tracking / Self-Guiding Projectiles)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A spiritual sharpshooter whose arrows are possessed by guiding spirits
that track targets around corners, over obstacles, and through evasive movement —
making escape nearly impossible.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Seeking Soul       | Attack    | Fire an arrow inhabited by a guiding spirit; it curves slightly to track the last known position of a target, compensating for minor movement.
  - Soul Tag           | Active    | Mark a target with a spiritual tether for 8s; all your homing arrows during this window prefer marked targets by 50%.
  - Spirit Quiver      | Passive   | Your arrows' homing range increases by 3m; arrows that miss and pass within 2m of a target curve back for a second pass.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Guided Bend        | Attack    | Fire a shot that immediately curves 90 degrees on command (activated by a second button press within 0.5s of firing).
  - Relentless Seeker  | Passive   | Homing arrows that miss return to you and reload automatically; can recycle up to 3 arrows per 10s.
  - Twin Spirit        | Active    | Fire two simultaneously homing arrows that split to target two different enemies; both carry full guidance.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Spirit Lock        | Debuff    | Fired arrow embeds a spirit anchor in the target; for 4s, any homing arrows fired by allies also prefer this target.
  - Wraithfire Volley  | Attack    | Fire 3 arrows that each independently home in on different random enemies within 20m.
  - Predict and Strike | Passive   | Homing arrows track predicted movement, not current position; they lead targets by 0.5s, making dodging 50% less effective.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Inescapable Trail  | Active    | Fire a spirit arrow that orbits a target continuously for 6s, dealing small periodic damage; if target tries to flee, it detonates for massive damage.
  - Spirit Swarm       | Active    | Release 10 small guided spirit bolts that swarm independently toward the nearest 5 enemies (2 bolts each), dealing moderate damage per hit.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Inevitable Arrow   | Active    | Fire a single spirit-possessed arrow that cannot be blocked, phased, or dodged; it tracks its target globally (across any distance) for 15s before striking for 500% weapon damage. If the target dies before the arrow lands, the arrow redirects to the next nearest enemy. 120s cooldown.

  - Risen Archer       | Summon    | Summon one ghostly archer that fires independently at nearby enemies for 10s, then fades.
  - Mass Grave Sense   | Passive   | Each enemy killed in the area increases your next volley's arrow count by 1 (up to +5), as the fallen join your call.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)

Tier 4 (Epic)

  - Regimental Call    | Buff      | For 15s, every time you fire an arrow, 2 additional spirit arrows mirror your shot from adjacent positions, tripling your effective ranged output.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Eternal Barrage    | Channel   | Channel for 3s, summoning the memory of every archer who ever fell in this region; for 10s, a relentless rain of spectral arrows covers the entire visible battlefield (30m radius), dealing moderate damage per second to all enemies with no gaps in coverage. Enemies cannot use cover, shields, or barriers against this attack. 150s cooldown.

################################################################################
CLASS 84: Entropy Engineer (Sci-fi Decay)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A scientist-turned-weapon who harnesses the universe's natural tendency
toward disorder — irradiating targets with half-life decay, dissolving armor with
corrosive agents, and draining the thermal energy from entire zones until nothing
can move or function.

ARCHETYPE A: Half-Life (Radiation DoT / Falloff / Contamination)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A sustained damage specialist who doses enemies with radiation that
intensifies over time, contaminates adjacent targets through proximity, and
punishes enemies who cluster together.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Rad Burst          | Attack    | Fire a radioactive charge at a target; applies Irradiated (5 damage/s for 8s). Stacks up to 3 times.
  - Geiger Sense       | Passive   | Irradiated enemies glow on your HUD; you deal 5% extra damage to Irradiated targets.
  - Isotope Tag        | Debuff    | Mark an enemy with an unstable isotope; they emit a 3m contamination aura that applies 1 Rad stack to nearby enemies every 2s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Critical Exposure  | Passive   | If a target has 3 Irradiated stacks, each additional stack past 3 also reduces their healing received by 10%.
  - Fallout Zone       | Active    | Detonate an Irradiated target's stacks; they explode in a 4m radius applying 2 Rad stacks to all nearby enemies.
  - Half-Life Decay    | Passive   | Each second of Irradiation reduces target's armor by 1% (up to 15% reduction), as the radiation eats through their defenses.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Hot Zone           | Active    | Irradiate a 6m area for 10s; all enemies who enter take 1 Rad stack per second. Area glows visibly.
  - Meltdown           | Active    | Choose an Irradiated enemy; their Rad stacks count is immediately doubled, then begin rapidly ticking down over 4s.
  - Sympathetic Decay  | Passive   | When an Irradiated enemy dies, they explode with all remaining stacks converted to a burst of 15 damage per stack to all in 5m.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Chain Reaction     | Active    | Trigger a cascade: Irradiated enemies within 8m of each other simultaneously detonate their stacks, each explosion potentially igniting the next.
  - Leaking Core       | Aura      | Passively emit low-level radiation; all enemies within 6m accumulate 1 Rad stack per 3s without any action required from you.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Total Meltdown     | Channel   | Target a location and channel for 2s; initiate a nuclear cascade that spreads Irradiation at max stacks to all enemies in a 20m radius, then detonates every stack simultaneously. Irradiated terrain remains hazardous for 30s afterward. 120s cooldown.

ARCHETYPE B: Corrosion (Armor Dissolution / Structure Destruction / Defense Bypass)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: An aggressive offense-oriented engineer who deploys caustic agents to
strip away every layer of protection — armor, barriers, fortifications — leaving
enemies utterly exposed.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Acid Spray         | Attack    | Spray a burst of corrosive fluid; applies Corroded (−8% armor for 6s). Stacks up to 5 times.
  - Etching Round      | Attack    | Fire a corrosive slug that embeds on impact and continues dissolving armor for 4s after initial hit.
  - Weak Point Finder  | Passive   | Corroded enemies display structural weak points; attacking them deals +10% damage past their remaining armor.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Structural Solvent | Active    | Target a barrier, wall, or fortification; apply concentrated acid that reduces its HP by 30% and halves its repair rate for 10s.
  - Dissolution Pulse  | Active    | Emit a wave of corrosive gas in a 5m radius that applies 2 Corroded stacks to all enemies caught.
  - Catalytic Agent    | Passive   | Each Corroded stack on a target also reduces their resistance to all damage types by 2% (not just physical).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Total Strip        | Active    | If a target reaches 5 Corroded stacks, consume them all to temporarily set their armor to 0 for 4s.
  - Acid Rain          | Active    | Create a 8m corrosive rain zone for 6s; enemies inside accumulate 1 Corroded stack per second and suffer 20 damage/s.
  - Reactive Coat      | Reactive      | Apply a reactive corrosive layer to yourself; melee attackers who strike you receive 2 Corroded stacks per hit.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Fortress Bane      | Active    | Fire a concentrated corrosive missile that bypasses shield HP entirely and applies 5 Corroded stacks to all targets in a 6m radius simultaneously.
  - Entropy Amplifier  | Passive   | Each Corroded stack now also reduces target's energy shield regeneration by 5%; at 5 stacks, their regen halts entirely.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Complete Dissolution| Active   | Select a target; over 3s, apply a custom-designed solvent that strips all armor and shields to zero, prevents armor/shield regeneration for 15s, and causes any protective ability they activate to instead deal damage to them equal to 10% of its protection value. 90s cooldown.

ARCHETYPE C: Heat Death (Energy Drain / Entropy Slow / Thermal Freeze)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A battlefield controller who steadily drains thermal energy from the
environment, slowing enemies to a glacial crawl, shutting down energy systems, and
ultimately reducing entire zones to absolute stillness.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Thermal Siphon     | Active    | Drain heat from a target; they move 20% slower and deal 10% less fire/energy damage for 5s. You recover 5% of your energy.
  - Entropy Field      | Aura      | Toggle: emit a 5m aura that reduces all enemy movement speed in range by 15% and slows their attack animations.
  - Cold Snap          | Active    | Flash-freeze a small 3m area; enemies caught are slowed 50% for 2s (brief but sharp interrupt to momentum).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Power Drain        | Debuff    | Attach an entropy tap to a target; drain 5% of their energy/mana per second for 6s, adding it to your reserves.
  - Creeping Cold      | Passive   | For each second an enemy remains in your entropy aura, their slow effect increases by 3% (caps at 60% slow after 15s).
  - Heat Death Pulse   | Active    | Fire a pulse of entropy energy; all enemies in a 6m cone have their attack speed and cast speed reduced by 30% for 5s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Thermal Lock       | CC        | Freeze a single target in suspended low-energy stasis for 3s; they cannot move or act but are also invulnerable. Useful for removing dangerous enemies temporarily.
  - Absolute Zero Zone | Active    | Create a 6m area of extreme entropy for 8s; enemies inside cannot regenerate energy or HP and move at 30% speed.
  - Cascade Entropy    | Passive   | When an enemy reaches below 20% movement speed (heavily slowed), they radiate entropy to adjacent enemies in 4m, applying 20% slow to them.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Entropy Vortex     | Active    | Create a 10m vortex for 10s that continuously siphons energy from all enemies inside, slowing them further each second and converting drained energy into bonus damage for your next attack.
  - Heat Death Wave    | Active    | Send out a wave of pure entropy across 15m; all enemies hit have their speed, attack, and energy rates halved for 6s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Universal Entropy  | Channel   | Channel for 3s, then unleash thermodynamic collapse in a 25m radius: all enemies are reduced to 10% movement speed, their energy systems go offline (no abilities) for 8s, and they take increasing cold damage every second the effect persists. After 8s, the field collapses inward and detonates for massive damage to all still inside. 150s cooldown.

################################################################################
CLASS 85: Chrono-Assassin (Time + Stealth)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A killer who operates outside the normal flow of time — stopping moments
to deliver flawless setups, reading enemy futures to step aside from every blow,
and erasing their own history to undo detection and retry perfect executions.

ARCHETYPE A: Time Stop Strike (Freeze Time / Burst Setup / Suspended Moment)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A precision burst combatant who briefly halts time around a target to
land an elaborate combination of attacks before the world resumes — rewarding
mechanical skill with catastrophic opener damage.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Freeze Frame       | Active    | Stop time in a 4m radius for 1.5s; you move freely while enemies are suspended. Cooldown: 20s.
  - Clockwork Blade    | Attack    | A quick strike; if used during a time stop, deals +40% damage (enemy cannot react or dodge).
  - Temporal Momentum  | Passive   | Attacks landed during time stop generate Temporal Charge; each charge increases time stop duration by 0.1s next use (max 5 charges, +0.5s max).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Extended Freeze    | Passive   | Time stop duration increased by 0.5s baseline; each kill during a time stop adds 0.25s to the next time stop's duration.
  - Setup Strike       | Active    | During a time stop, plant a delayed explosive charge on the target; it detonates 0.5s after time resumes for 250% weapon damage.
  - Frozen Weak Point  | Passive   | While an enemy is frozen in time, they are automatically exposed to critical hits; all attacks against them during the stop have +30% critical chance.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Time Fracture      | Active    | Expand time stop to a 10m radius for 2.5s; all enemies in range are frozen. Cooldown: 30s.
  - Shatter Moment     | Active    | At the end of a time stop, release all accumulated kinetic energy at once; frozen enemies take 30% of all damage dealt during the stop as an additional burst.
  - Suspended Animation| Utility   | While time is stopped, enemies maintain their suspended animations (mid-dodge, mid-attack) when time resumes; this means you can predict their next action precisely.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Pocket Eternity    | Active    | Enter personal time stop for 3s; only you experience this time — no cooldowns tick, you can reposition, reload, and prepare. Does not affect enemies but gives full action freedom. 45s cooldown.
  - Cascade Freeze     | Passive   | When you kill an enemy during a time stop, time stop duration refreshes by 1s (can only trigger once per kill, up to 3 times per cast).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Zero Moment| Active  | Freeze all time globally in a 30m radius for 5s; you act with full speed and ability use. During this window, all attacks deal 200% damage and all stealth abilities are free. On time resume, a shockwave of restored momentum knocks all enemies back and deals 100% weapon damage. 90s cooldown.

ARCHETYPE B: Precognition (See Future / Perfect Dodge / Reactive Counter)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A defensive assassin who perceives incoming attacks before they land,
achieving perfect evasions and punishing opponents with counterstrikes timed
exactly to the moment their attack fails.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Future Sight       | Passive/Evasion   | Brief ghost silhouettes of enemy attacks appear 0.3s before they hit; increases dodge window by 0.3s on all attacks.
  - Prescient Step     | Reactive  | Automatically dodge the next attack that would deal more than 15% of your max HP. 12s cooldown.
  - Read Intent        | Active    | For 5s, all enemy ability animations are slowed visually for you (only you see slow-mo telegraphs), making reaction windows twice as large.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Counter Prophecy   | Reactive  | When you successfully dodge an attack, automatically riposte with a quick strike dealing 80% weapon damage. Requires a dodge, not a block.
  - Fate Diversion     | Active    | See and divert a single incoming projectile; it reverses direction and targets the shooter for full damage. 15s cooldown.
  - Precognitive Stance| Stance    | Toggle: reduce your movement speed by 10% but gain a 20% flat dodge chance bonus and Prescient Step refreshes 30% faster.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Event Prediction   | Active    | Predict the next 3s of a single target's actions; their next 3 abilities cast at reduced effectiveness (20% penalty each) as you've already countered them mentally.
  - Phantom Future     | Buff/Evasion      | Briefly become a step ahead of time; for 4s, you dodge all attacks automatically without any input required. 40s cooldown.
  - Reflex Loop        | Passive   | After a perfect dodge (within 0.1s of impact), your next attack deals +60% damage as your body acts before your mind — pure trained instinct.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Temporal Mirror    | Reactive  | When hit despite precognition for more than 25% HP damage, rewind 1s (only your position and HP; enemies retain their states) and dodge the attack properly. 30s cooldown.
  - Absolute Foresight | Buff/Evasion      | For 10s, you see all enemy attacks 1 full second before they occur; perfect dodge window opens for every attack in range. 60s cooldown.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Destiny Override   | Active    | For 8s, enter a state of complete temporal foresight: all incoming attacks auto-dodge (100% evasion), each dodge triggers a Counter Prophecy riposte, and any attack that somehow still lands is reduced to 1 damage. At the end of the duration, predict and execute the most lethal strike the current situation allows — dealing damage equal to all damage you would have received during the window. 120s cooldown.

>>

ARCHETYPE C: Rewind Kill (Undo Detection / Retry Stealth / Timeline Revision)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A methodical stealth operative who treats each engagement as a puzzle
they can restart — rewinding their own timeline to undo mistakes, erase detection,
and approach assassinations from a theoretically perfect position.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Memory Rewind      | Active    | Rewind your own position and HP to 2s ago; enemies lose your current position and resume searching where you last were. 18s cooldown.
  - Ghost Print        | Passive   | After rewinding, you leave no footsteps, body heat, or sound for 3s — perfect stealth slate.
  - Trial Run          | Utility   | Preview a 3s window of what happens if you move to a target location; no commitment, pure scouting of future state.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Undo Detection     | Active    | If detected within the last 2s, rewind just your detection status while keeping your current position; enemies resume patrol patterns. 25s cooldown.
  - Perfect Approach   | Passive   | After a successful rewind that eliminates detection, your next attack deals +50% damage (the ideal approach bonus).
  - Temporal Cache     | Utility   | Store a timestamp; activate within 30s to snap back to that exact position, HP, and stealth state. 45s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Assassination Retry| Active    | If a stealth kill attempt fails (target survives or you are detected), instantly rewind 3s; the target has no memory of the attempt. 30s cooldown.
  - Causal Erase       | Active    | Rewind a 6m radius 2s back; all enemies in the area lose any actions they took in those 2s (abilities reset, movement reversed, detection erased).
  - Split Timeline     | Passive   | Maintain two save-states simultaneously; choose which to rewind to. First is set automatically at combat start; second can be manually set once per fight.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Temporal Ambush    | Active    | Plant yourself at a location 4s in the future; when the timeline reaches that moment, you appear from stealth and your position is guaranteed to be optimal for a backstab attack. 50s cooldown.
  - Perfect Crime      | Passive   | Kills made from stealth leave no timeline signature; allies and enemies who witnessed the kill forget it occurred after 2s. Detection from kill animations is nullified.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Clean Slate        | Active    | Fully rewind the last 8s of the entire local encounter (30m radius): enemies return to prior positions and states, your HP is restored to its 8s-ago value, all cooldowns are reset, and you retain full memory of what just occurred. Effectively lets you replay any failed engagement with perfect foreknowledge. 180s cooldown.

################################################################################
CLASS 86: Architect (Building / Construction)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A master builder who shapes the battlefield through deliberate
construction — raising defensive fortifications, executing delayed tactical
blueprints, and detonating their own creations in devastating controlled
demolitions when the structures have served their purpose.

ARCHETYPE A: Foundation (Defensive Structures / Walls / Bunkers)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A positional defender who constructs durable battlefield fortifications
that protect allies, create choke points, and reward careful positioning before
the fight begins.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Stone Wall         | Utility   | Construct a 3m wide, 2m tall stone wall at target location in 1.5s; wall has 400 HP and blocks movement and projectiles.
  - Fortify            | Buff/Defense      | Reinforce a placed structure; increases its HP by 50% and adds a 2s construction immunity where it cannot be targeted.
  - Barricade Post     | Utility   | Place a low barrier that blocks movement but not projectiles; allies can fire over it. 200 HP.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Sandbag Line       | Utility   | Rapidly deploy a 6m long low fortification that provides half-cover to allies crouching behind it.
  - Reinforced Bunker  | Active/Defense   | Build a 2x2m bunker structure; allies inside gain 30% damage reduction and shooting ports allow attacks from within.
  - Seismic Foundation | Passive/Utility   | All structures you build have +25% HP and cannot be displaced by knockback or gravity effects.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Layered Defense    | Passive/Defense   | Each structure within 5m of another structure gains a linked armor bonus (+15% DR shared between linked structures).
  - Command Rampart    | Utility   | Erect a large 8m wall with a walkable parapet; allies can stand on top and fire from elevation, gaining high ground bonuses.
  - Repair Team        | Heal      | Summon two mechanical repair drones that continuously repair the nearest damaged structure at 20 HP/s for 15s.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Fortress Protocol  | Active    | Instantly construct a complete defensive perimeter: 4 walls in a square formation around a target point in 2s. Creates an instant stronghold.
  - Living Wall        | Utility   | Build a wall segment that slowly regenerates (10 HP/s) and periodically fires small bolts at nearby enemies (every 3s, 30 damage).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Grand Citadel      | Channel   | Channel for 4s; construct a massive multi-layer fortification at target location: outer wall (1000 HP), inner keep (600 HP), a central tower with 360-degree firing ports, and 4 auto-turret positions. Persists for 90s. Allies inside gain 25% damage reduction and +10% damage. 180s cooldown.

>>

ARCHETYPE B: Blueprint (Delayed Execution / Planned Abilities / Pre-set Combos)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A forward-thinking strategist who sets up elaborate sequences of delayed
abilities in advance, triggering them in precise order when the situation is right
— rewarding preparation over improvisation.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Schematic          | Utility   | Place a blueprint marker at a location; up to 3 markers can be active. Activating a marker instantly executes a stored basic ability at that point.
  - Timed Strike       | Active    | Designate a target; after a 3s delay, unleash a strike at that location for 150% weapon damage regardless of your current position.
  - Forethought        | Passive   | Abilities used while no enemies are in range reduce their cooldown by 20% (reward for pre-fight setup).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Delayed Detonation | Utility   | Plant an invisible charge at a location; activate within 30s to detonate for 200% AoE damage. Multiple charges can be pre-placed.
  - Contingency Plan   | Reactive  | Pre-set a defensive ability (barrier, heal, or movement) to trigger automatically when your HP drops below 25%. Configured during calm.
  - Parallel Processing| Passive   | You can have 2 abilities on pre-scheduled delays simultaneously; both execute when triggered without interfering with each other.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Blueprint Cascade  | Active    | Trigger all active blueprint markers simultaneously; each executes its stored ability in rapid sequence over 1s.
  - Preemptive Strike  | Utility   | Set a trigger condition (enemy enters zone); the linked ability fires automatically when the condition is met. 1 active trigger at a time.
  - Architect's Intuition| Passive | Delayed abilities deal 15% bonus damage; abilities that execute exactly on their listed delay (no early trigger) deal 30% bonus damage.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Grand Scheme       | Active    | Set up a 5-step ability sequence with delays between each step (each step timed 2-5s apart); once armed, execute the entire plan with one button press.
  - Redundant System   | Passive/Utility   | If a blueprint trigger is destroyed or blocked, it automatically re-arms at the nearest valid location within 5m after a 3s delay.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Masterwork         | Active    | Spend 5s preparing a complete tactical scenario: pre-place 6 schematic markers, configure 3 trigger conditions, and set a finale detonation. Arm and execute over 30s. The finale ability (a devastating targeted strike or AoE) deals 500% damage and cannot be interrupted once the sequence begins. 120s cooldown.

ARCHETYPE C: Demolisher (Destroy Structures / Burst AoE / Recycle)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: An aggressive disruptor who obliterates structures — both enemy and
friendly — in massive explosions, recycling the materials for power while dealing
devastating AoE burst damage through controlled collapse.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Controlled Blast   | Attack    | Detonate a structure (any faction) in a 4m radius for 150 AoE damage; reclaim 20% of the structure's material cost.
  - Breaching Charge   | Utility   | Place an explosive that destroys a wall section in 2s; creates a breach point that allies can move through.
  - Salvage            | Passive/Shield   | When a structure is destroyed (any cause) within 10m, gain 5% of your max HP as a temporary shield.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Implosion          | Active    | Target a structure; instead of exploding outward, it collapses inward, dealing damage only to enemies within 3m (no collateral) for 250% normal demolition damage.
  - Chain Demolition   | Passive   | When one structure is destroyed, all structures within 5m also explode in sequence (1s delay between each), triggering full demolition damage.
  - Reclaim            | Active    | Instantly dissolve one of your own structures; recover 75% of its cooldown and gain a 10s damage buff equal to 15% of its max HP value.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Wrecking Ball      | Active    | Launch a massive wrecking projectile that destroys the first structure it contacts and deals full demolition damage in an 8m radius from impact.
  - Rubble Avalanche   | Passive   | Destroyed structures leave impassable rubble for 10s; enemies in rubble zones move 40% slower and take 10 damage/s from debris.
  - Overcharge Blast   | Active    | Supercharge a structure with energy; it grants +30% bonus damage when detonated, and explodes in a 7m radius instead of normal.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Cascading Collapse | Active    | Trigger a chain demolition that guarantees every structure in a 20m radius detonates in sequence over 4s; each explosion is full-strength regardless of prior damage.
  - Phoenix Material   | Passive   | When you demolish a structure, instantly rebuild a smaller version (50% HP) at the same location from recycled material — the rubble becomes a new forward position.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Total Demolition   | Channel   | Channel for 2s; designate a 25m radius zone. Every structure in the zone — friendly, enemy, and neutral — implodes simultaneously for maximum demolition damage. The resulting material surge grants you a 30s hyper-empowered state: 30% damage bonus, free structure placement, and all demolition abilities have their cooldowns reset. 120s cooldown.

################################################################################
CLASS 87: Chaosmancer (Entropy / Wild Magic)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A caster who has surrendered control to the raw forces of magical
entropy — rolling the dice on spectacular random surges, chaining spells into
self-propagating cascade reactions, and rewriting the fundamental rules of the
battlefield itself to render enemy tactics obsolete.

ARCHETYPE A: Wild Surge (Random Effects / Chaotic Casting / High Variance)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A high-risk, high-reward spellcaster who embraces randomness, building
a resource called Chaos Flux that powers increasingly powerful (and unpredictable)
surges.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Unstable Cast      | Attack    | Fire a bolt of chaotic energy; roll 1-3 randomly: 1 = lightning (stun 0.5s), 2 = fire (DoT), 3 = arcane (amplified damage). Generates 10 Chaos Flux.
  - Flux Charge        | Buff      | Spend 20 Chaos Flux to empower next cast: it rolls twice and applies both effects.
  - Chaotic Aura       | Passive   | At 50+ Chaos Flux, your spells gain a 10% chance to fire twice automatically.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Wild Missile Storm | Active    | Release 6 missiles each with a random elemental type (fire, ice, lightning, arcane, void, force); each targets the nearest enemy independently.
  - Luck Amplifier     | Passive   | Whenever a Wild Surge produces a "best" result, gain 15 bonus Chaos Flux.
  - Controlled Chaos   | Toggle    | Toggle: reduce Chaos Flux gain by 30% but gain the ability to reroll any Wild Surge result once per surge.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Grand Surge        | Active    | Spend 80 Chaos Flux: roll on the Grand Surge table (d6): 1-2 = massive AoE blast, 3-4 = summon a random powerful creature for 15s, 5 = transform enemies in range into harmless animals for 6s, 6 = deal max-damage of every element simultaneously to all enemies in 10m.
  - Chaos Absorption   | Passive/Utility   | When an enemy ability fails against you (resisted or dodged), gain 20 Chaos Flux from the disrupted energy.
  - Surge Cascade      | Passive   | Wild Surge results that deal damage generate an additional 15 Chaos Flux on kill.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Maximum Entropy    | Active    | Spend all current Chaos Flux; the surge effect is proportional to the amount spent. At 100 Flux, maximum effect from the Grand Surge table is guaranteed. Excess Flux increases the effect's duration or damage by 1% per 5 Flux over 100.
  - Probability Warp   | Aura      | Emit a field within 10m that causes all enemy abilities to randomly fail 20% of the time (applying their effect to themselves instead).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Chaos     | Active    | Trigger an uncontrolled maximal surge; Chaos Flux resets to 0 and an entirely random catastrophic event occurs in a 30m radius. Possible outcomes (equal probability): meteor shower, temporal loop, random ally/enemy stat swap, spontaneous portal opening, mass polymorphing of all enemies, reality inversion (damage heals, heals damage) for 5s. Effect is unknown until it fires. 120s cooldown.

>> Your notes on WILD SURGE:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Cascade (Chain Reactions / Spell Combos / One Triggers Next)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A deliberate combo-builder who threads spells together into chain
reactions, where each cast triggers the next in sequence — rewarding knowledge of
spell interactions with compounding, unstoppable damage chains.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Primer             | Attack    | Apply a Primed status to a target; the next spell that hits them gains +20% damage and triggers Cascade (chains to next spell in sequence automatically).
  - Spark Chain        | Attack    | A small lightning bolt that, if Cascade is active, automatically leaps to a second target for 60% damage.
  - Chain Memory       | Passive   | Track your last 2 spell types; if you cast them in the same order again, the chain activates at +10% damage.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Detonation Link    | Active    | Link two targets; when one takes damage, 40% of that damage instantly chains to the linked target.
  - Spell Echo         | Passive   | After any Cascade trigger, there is a 25% chance the chain continues one additional step automatically.
  - Sequence Setter    | Utility   | Pre-set a 3-spell sequence; casting them in order (within 8s) triggers a bonus cascade effect at the end: all 3 spells fire again at 50% power.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Resonant Cascade   | Passive   | Each step in a cascade chain deals 10% more damage than the previous step (compounding multiplicatively, not additively).
  - Elemental Handoff  | Passive   | Cascade chains that switch between damage types (fire → ice → lightning) deal +25% bonus at each switch.
  - Infinite Loop      | Active    | Force a cascade chain to loop back to the first spell in sequence; 2 complete loops maximum. 20s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Critical Chain     | Passive   | If any spell in a cascade chain crits, all subsequent spells in that chain also crit automatically.
  - Reaction Web       | Active    | Link all enemies in a 12m radius into a cascade network for 8s; damage dealt to any one enemy chains to all others at 30% value.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Perpetual Cascade  | Active    | For 10s, every spell you cast automatically triggers a cascade chain of 4 additional spell echoes at 70% power each; chains spread between multiple enemies if possible. Resonant Cascade, Elemental Handoff, and Critical Chain all apply to every echo. 90s cooldown.

>> Your notes on CASCADE:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Anomaly (Warp Battlefield Rules / Change Physics / Reality Override)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A reality-bending disruptor who alters the fundamental rules of the
battlefield — reversing gravity, making damage heal, flipping ability logic — to
completely invalidate conventional enemy tactics.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Gravity Flip Zone  | Active    | Create a 5m area where gravity is reversed for 4s; enemies inside float and are unable to approach grounded targets.
  - Rule Bend          | Debuff    | Apply a rule inversion to one enemy: their next offensive ability instead applies its effect to themselves.
  - Phase Shift Zone   | Utility   | Create a 4m zone where physical terrain doesn't apply for 5s; all movement (including walls) is passable within the zone.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Damage Reversal    | Active    | For 4s, your damage type becomes reversed in a 6m aura: fire damage chills, ice damage burns, etc. Enemies with elemental resistances now take bonus damage from their resisted type.
  - Speed Inversion    | Debuff    | Apply to an enemy: for 6s their fast attacks become slow and their slow attacks become fast (animation speed inverted, not timing).
  - Mass Repulsion     | Active    | Create a zone where like-faction units repel each other; enemies cannot cluster within the zone.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Physics Override   | Active    | Designate a 10m zone for 8s; within it, choose one rule to invert: projectiles travel backward, melee attacks have doubled range, or healing deals damage.
  - Ability Echo       | Passive   | Once per 20s, when an enemy uses an ability in your presence, that ability is duplicated and fires back against them at 75% power.
  - Dimensional Pocket | Utility   | Create a 4m space where time moves twice as fast for 10s; allies inside act twice, enemies age and are slowed.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Reality Fracture   | Active    | Break one rule of combat for 6s in a 15m radius: choose — projectiles pass through all targets dealing double damage, all crits apply to whoever crit (self-hit for enemies), or melee damage is converted to ranged damage at twice the range.
  - Logic Bomb         | Debuff    | Embed a paradox in a target: for 8s, whenever they use an ability, a random different ability from their kit fires instead. They cannot control which.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Total Anomaly      | Active    | For 12s, rewrite all rules of combat in a 25m radius: damage heals, healing damages, movement is reversed (forward moves backward), and all ability effects are inverted. You are immune to all inversions. Enemies must navigate completely counterintuitive combat logic. On expiry, a reality snap deals damage to all enemies equal to 20% of their max HP for each ability they used during the anomaly. 150s cooldown.

>> Your notes on ANOMALY:
>>
>>

################################################################################
CLASS 88: Abyssal (Deep Sea / Eldritch Ocean)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A being touched by the incomprehensible depths of the ocean's darkest
trenches — weaponizing crushing pressure, calling forth leviathan horrors that
dwarf the battlefield, and wielding bioluminescent light as both lure and
blinding doom.

ARCHETYPE A: Pressure (Depth / Crush Mechanics / Pressure Damage)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A sustained damage tank who simulates the crushing weight of the deep
ocean, applying escalating Pressure stacks that eventually become lethal while
making enemies sluggish and brittle.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Deep Crush         | Attack    | Apply 1 Pressure stack on hit; each stack slows the target by 3% and deals 5 damage per second. Max 10 stacks.
  - Depth Charge       | Active    | Hurl an explosive pressure mine that detonates on contact, applying 3 Pressure stacks in a 4m radius.
  - Abyssal Density    | Passive/Resistance   | Your own body is acclimated to pressure; incoming slows and roots are 30% less effective against you.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Squeeze            | Active    | Force compress a Pressure-stacked target; deal damage equal to 15% of current HP per Pressure stack on them (consume no stacks).
  - Pressure Crack     | Passive   | At 5+ Pressure stacks, target's armor begins fracturing: −5% armor per stack above 5 (up to −25% armor at max stacks).
  - Depth Field        | Aura      | Toggle: emit a 6m pressure aura; enemies inside gain 1 Pressure stack every 3s passively.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Bathysphere        | Active    | Encase a target in a pressure bubble for 4s; they are immobilized and take 20 damage/s, and all Pressure stacks on them deal triple tick damage.
  - Crushing Descent   | Active    | Force a target downward (or slam them into the ground); damage equals 30% of max HP plus 20% per Pressure stack. Clears 3 stacks on use.
  - Abyssal Torrent    | Channel   | Channel for 2s; release a stream of pressurized water that applies 2 Pressure stacks per second to all enemies in a 10m line.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Implosion Event    | Active    | Detonate all Pressure stacks on a target simultaneously; each stack deals 20% max HP damage in a 5m burst. Lethal above 8 stacks on most targets.
  - Trench Collapse    | Active    | Create a 10m zone of extreme pressure for 8s; enemies inside accumulate stacks 3x faster and cannot remove stacks while inside.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Mariana's Judgment | Channel   | Channel for 3s, then apply maximum Pressure to all enemies in a 20m radius simultaneously (10 stacks each). Immediately trigger Implosion Event on all of them. Surviving enemies are permanently at 5 Pressure stacks for the remainder of the encounter. 120s cooldown.

>> Your notes on PRESSURE:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Leviathan (Sea Monster Summons / Transform Into Beast / Ocean Horror)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A summoner and shapeshifter who either calls forth ancient sea monsters
to devastate the battlefield or transforms into a leviathan horror themselves —
enormous, terrifying, and difficult to stop.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Tide Spawn         | Summon    | Summon a small deep-sea creature (eel or crab-horror) that attacks nearby enemies for 10s; deals moderate damage.
  - Scale Form         | Transform | Partially shift into a scaled sea-beast; gain +15% armor and melee attacks apply 1 Pressure stack. Duration 10s.
  - Echo of the Deep   | Active    | Emit a low-frequency pulse; startles all summoned sea creatures into frenzy for 4s (+30% attack speed).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Kraken Tendril     | Summon    | Summon a massive tentacle from the ground that grabs and holds one enemy for 4s, dealing damage per second.
  - Leviathan Sight    | Passive/Utility   | Your summons gain +20% damage and +30% HP; you can see through their vision as a secondary camera.
  - Brood Call         | Summon    | Summon 4 small predator fish horrors that swarm a target, each independently attacking and applying 1 Pressure stack on hit.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Deep Behemoth      | Summon    | Summon a massive armored sea-horror that tanks damage and deals AoE slam attacks in a 4m radius. Persists 25s.
  - Leviathan Surge    | Transform | Fully shift into a mid-tier leviathan form for 15s: enlarged size, 3x melee damage, bite attack that devours small enemies instantly, and immunity to displacement.
  - Serpent of the Pit | Summon    | Summon a colossal sea serpent that circles the combat area for 20s, periodically lunging into the center of the enemy group for 300% AoE damage.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Abyssal Hydra      | Summon    | Summon a multi-headed hydra; each head attacks a different target simultaneously. If a head is destroyed, 2 more regrow after 5s. 3 initial heads, up to 7 total.
  - True Leviathan     | Transform | Transform into a full-sized oceanic leviathan for 20s; immense HP pool, all attacks are AoE, can swallow enemies whole (instant kill at <15% HP), generates a Pressure field passively.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Call of the Abyss  | Channel   | Channel for 4s; summon every sea horror bound to your bloodline simultaneously — 1 leviathan, 1 hydra, 2 krakens, and a swarm of lesser horrors — all active for 30s. You enter leviathan form yourself. For the duration, the battlefield is treated as an open ocean: all non-aquatic movement is halved for enemies, and your creatures gain +40% damage. 180s cooldown.

>>

ARCHETYPE C: Bioluminescence (Lure / Blind / Illuminate / Attract)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: A manipulative controller who weaponizes light from the darkest depths
— using beautiful, deadly bioluminescent displays to lure enemies into traps,
blind them at critical moments, and expose hidden foes with light that cannot be
escaped.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Lure Pulse         | Active    | Emit a hypnotic bioluminescent pulse; enemies in a 6m radius are drawn 2m toward the source (compelled movement for 1.5s).
  - Flashbloom         | Active    | Release a burst of intense blue-white light; blinds all enemies facing you in a 4m cone for 2.5s.
  - Trace Light        | Passive/Utility   | Enemies you attack emit a faint bioluminescent trace for 5s; they cannot benefit from stealth and are visible through smoke.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Beacon             | Utility   | Place a floating bioluminescent lure; enemies within 12m have a 35% chance per second to move toward it (will-o-wisp effect). Lasts 15s.
  - Chromatic Blind    | Debuff    | Blast a target with shifting chromatic light; they suffer −40% accuracy and cannot visually identify allies vs enemies for 4s.
  - Living Lantern     | Buff      | Apply bioluminescence to an ally; they become a mobile light source that reveals invisible/stealthed enemies within 6m and grants them +10% damage in dark areas.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Deep Lure          | Active    | Plant an irresistible light source; enemies must make a resist check every 2s or move toward it compulsively. Lethal if combined with traps or cliffs nearby. Duration 20s.
  - Spectral Bloom     | Active    | Release a blossoming explosion of bioluminescent spores in a 8m radius; enemies inside are outlined for 15s (wallhack for allies) and suffer −20% evasion.
  - Photonic Overload  | Active    | Amplify bioluminescence to blinding intensity; all enemies within 10m are blinded for 5s and take 80 damage from the intensity of the light.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Abyssal Lantern    | Toggle    | Toggle: emit a constant 12m deep-light aura; all enemies inside suffer stacking accuracy penalty (3% per second), compelled attention (they find it harder to target your allies), and cannot use stealth or invisibility.
  - Void Bloom         | Active    | Release a massive bioluminescent bloom; 15m radius, blinds for 4s, reveals all hidden units, and marks all revealed enemies with Lure status (they draw all your attacks for 5s).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Angler's Doom      | Channel   | Channel for 2s; project a titanic bioluminescent display across the battlefield (40m radius). Every enemy is instantly compelled toward the center (irresistible lure for 3s), blinded on arrival for 5s, and then the light inverts to absolute darkness — all enemies suffer maximum Chromatic Blind for an additional 6s, and any enemy who was lured to the center takes 300% damage from a pressure detonation as the light collapses. 150s cooldown.

################################################################################
CLASS 89: Pactmaker (Contract/Bargain Magic)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A broker of infernal and celestial agreements, the Pactmaker binds reality itself through signed covenants — offering terrible power in exchange for hidden costs, exploiting contractual loopholes to reshape the battlefield, and imposing unbreakable magical law on those foolish enough to become party to their terms.

ARCHETYPE A: Devil's Deal (Gamble, Power-at-a-Cost, Risk/Reward)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Offer overwhelming short-term power to yourself or allies by secretly embedding punishing delayed costs into every bargain.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Handshake Clause   | Active   | Grant an ally +25% damage for 10s; at the end, they lose 10% of max HP as the cost comes due.
  - Desperate Offer    | Active   | Double your own next skill's damage, but your cooldowns are frozen for 3s after it fires.
  - Small Print Surge  | Passive/Movement  | Whenever you or an ally receives a Deal buff, gain a stacking 3% speed bonus (max 5 stacks) that resets on cost payment.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Blood Signature    | Active   | Sacrifice 15% of your current HP to instantly reset one ally's longest active cooldown.
  - Usurious Rate      | Debuff   | Mark an enemy: they deal 20% more damage for 8s, but receive 30% more damage for the same duration.
  - Escalating Terms   | Passive  | Each consecutive Deal buff you apply increases the power bonus by 5% and the cost by 5% (stacks up to 4x).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Soul Collateral    | Channel  | Over 2s of channeling, bind an ally's soul: they become unkillable for 12s, then are stunned for 3s when the pact expires.
  - Compound Interest  | Reactive | When a Deal cost is paid, trigger a free burst of 40% of the original benefit to all nearby allies within 10m.
  - Renegotiated Terms | Active   | Retroactively alter one active Deal buff — halve its remaining benefit but also halve its remaining cost. 30s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Devil's Masterwork | Active   | Apply a Deal to an enemy instead of an ally: they gain +40% to all stats for 15s, but upon expiry are Stunned and take damage equal to 60% of all bonus damage they dealt during that window.
  - Voided Clause      | Reactive | When any Deal cost triggers on you or an ally, you may instantly void it — absorbing the cost as raw energy that empowers your next skill by 80%. Once per 45s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Grand Bargain  | Transform | For 20s, enter Pact Lord form: all your skills apply simultaneous Deal buffs AND costs to every target they hit (ally or enemy). Allies gain massive temporary power; enemies suffer the costs immediately rather than on delay. On expiry, you pay the aggregate cost of every Deal triggered during the window, reduced by 50%. 120s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Devil's Deal:
>> The central fantasy is a high-stakes gambler who makes everyone stronger but always at a price. The tension between benefit and deferred cost should feel palpable — players must time expirations carefully or chain Compound Interest to convert costs into bonuses. Devil's Masterwork elegantly flips the mechanic onto enemies, making it a lethal setup tool. The Grand Bargain is a dramatic "all in" moment that rewards planning over the 20-second window.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: Fine Print (Loophole, Rule-Twisting, Control)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Read every agreement more carefully than your enemies do, exploiting technicalities and semantic gaps to turn their own actions and buffs against them.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Technicality       | Reactive | When an enemy uses a buff or self-heal, trigger it immediately for 1 additional, unintended free use — then lock the skill for 4s.
  - Ambiguous Wording  | Debuff   | Apply a Clause to an enemy: their next damaging skill deals 50% of its damage to themselves as well.
  - Loophole Search    | Passive  | Reduce all enemy buff durations by 15% passively. Each reduced buff grants you 1% damage for its original full duration.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Retroactive Clause | Active   | Select an enemy buff that is already active and declare it "invalid" — remove it and deal damage equal to 20% of the benefit they received while it was active.
  - Force Majeure      | CC       | Declare a 5s window of "exceptional circumstances" — all enemy Movement skills are classified as Attacks and trigger Attack-reactive defenses. 25s cooldown.
  - Jurisdictional Gap | Active  | Step into a legal grey area: become untargetable for 3s, but you may not deal damage during this time. Cooldown resets if an enemy buff expires while you are in the gap.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Severability       | Active   | Shatter a multi-part enemy buff into its individual components, applying each component's debuff version to the enemy simultaneously. Deals moderate damage per component stripped.
  - Binding Arbitration| CC       | Both you and a target enemy are locked in place for 4s — but you may still cast skills while the enemy may not. 40s cooldown.
  - Double Meaning     | Passive  | Whenever you use a Debuff skill, it simultaneously counts as a Buff on yourself for the purposes of triggering allied Passive bonuses and reactive effects.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Contra Proferentem | Debuff   | Apply to an enemy: for 20s, every skill they use has its ambiguous element (secondary effect) resolved against them — heals become damage, speed becomes slow, shields become vulnerability.
  - Implied Covenant   | Aura     | Passive aura in 15m radius: enemies passively generate "obligation" whenever they damage allies. At 100 obligation, they are forced to spend their next action healing the most wounded ally they hit.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Ultra Vires        | Active   | Declare the enemy team's current combat actions legally void for 6s — all enemy skills used during this window are redirected: damage hits themselves, heals go to your team, buffs become debuffs. Expensive cast time of 1.5s and 150s cooldown. Can be interrupted.

>> Your notes on Fine Print:
>> Fine Print is a cerebral disruptor who punishes enemies for doing literally anything. The loop is: enemies buff/attack/move → Pactmaker twists it against them. Contra Proferentem and Ultra Vires are the fantasy payoffs — a lawyer who makes your own actions hurt you. Force Majeure is particularly clever for countering mobility-heavy enemies. Binding Arbitration has a chess-match feel that rewards players who have strong instant-cast tools.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: Arbiter (Enforcement, Legal Magic, Binding Judgment)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Impose absolute magical law on the battlefield, issuing enforceable decrees that punish violations and reward compliance with brutal efficiency.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Cease and Desist   | Debuff   | Issue a decree against one enemy skill type (Attack/Move/Heal). If they use that type within 6s, they are Silenced for 2s.
  - Sworn Testimony    | Reactive/Heal     | Mark an ally: the next time they are damaged, automatically trigger a counter-effect (heal for 15% max HP or a free dodge). Single use.
  - Precedent Set      | Passive  | The first time each combat you use a skill, that skill's type is "on record" — future uses of the same type deal 5% extra damage or provide 5% extra benefit.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Injunction         | CC       | Legally bar an enemy from moving for 3s. Any movement they attempt deals 8% of their max HP as self-damage and refreshes the duration by 1s.
  - Class Action       | Active   | Extend your current active Decree to affect all enemies within 20m, not just the original target. 20s cooldown.
  - Contempt of Court  | Debuff   | Mark an enemy who has violated a decree this fight: they take 20% increased damage from all sources for 8s, stackable once.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Subpoena           | Active   | Force an enemy to immediately re-use their last-used skill — regardless of cooldown or their intention — targeting their own team.
  - Summary Judgment   | Active   | Execute instant verdict: deal damage equal to the total number of decree violations the target has committed this combat × 15% of their max HP. Max 5 stacks.
  - Protected Witness  | Buff     | Designate an ally as protected: for 15s, any enemy who targets that ally with a single-target skill immediately has that skill reflected back at them once. 35s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Mandatory Sentence | Active   | Issue an unavoidable binding sentence on an enemy: for 20s, every 5s they must perform one of three court-ordered actions (deal damage to themselves, grant a buff to you, or remain still for 1.5s). The game chooses whichever they are currently trying to avoid doing most.
  - Judicial Override  | Reactive | When an enemy skill would reduce one of your allies below 20% HP, override the ruling — cap the HP loss at 1 HP and Silence the attacker for 3s. Once per 60s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Absolute Law       | Aura     | Permanently toggle on a battlefield-wide aura: all skills used by enemies that deal damage also deal 10% of that damage to themselves. All skills used by allies that heal also cleanse one debuff. This effect cannot be Silenced, Dispelled, or interrupted — it is simply law. Can be deactivated voluntarily. 90s cooldown to reactivate after deactivation.

>> Your notes on Arbiter:
>> Arbiter plays like a courtroom judge who actually has the power to enforce rulings with violence. Subpoena is one of the most satisfying designs here — forcing an enemy to nuke their own team. Absolute Law as a capstone is clean and powerful without being flashy; it just quietly reshapes the entire economy of the fight. Mandatory Sentence has great emergent game feel because "the game chooses what they want to avoid" creates genuine decision pressure on the enemy player.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON PACTMAKER]
>
> Pactmaker fills a unique design niche as a "meta-skill" class — it doesn't just fight, it changes the rules of engagement. The three archetypes cover distinct vectors: Devil's Deal is the risk gambler (power spike + time bomb), Fine Print is the reactive judo master (enemy actions become your weapons), and Arbiter is the proactive law-imposer (issue decrees and punish violations). The class rewards players who read enemy skill patterns and anticipate timing windows. It should feel like playing chess while everyone else plays checkers. Key balance concern: Fine Print's Technicality (triggering enemy buffs for free) and Ultra Vires (redirecting all enemy actions) need hard caps or interrupt windows to avoid instant-win scenarios at high level.
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 90: Cipher (Code/Data Sci-fi)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A hacker-mystic who treats reality as a running program, the Cipher manipulates the underlying data-fabric of existence — hardening allies behind cryptographic barriers, tearing enemy defenses apart at the algorithmic level, and unleashing self-replicating viral corruption that cascades uncontrollably through enemy ranks.

ARCHETYPE A: Encryption (Shield, Protection, Data Fortification)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Wrap allies in layered cryptographic defenses that grow more complex with each attack received, becoming exponentially harder to breach over time.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Firewall           | Buff/Shield     | Apply a data-barrier to an ally that absorbs up to 20% of their max HP in damage. While active, their skill cooldowns are reduced by 10%.
  - Hash Lock          | Passive/Shield  | Each time an ally's barrier is hit, the barrier's remaining integrity increases by 2% (stacks up to 5x, resets on barrier break).
  - Packet Filter      | Reactive | When an ally with a barrier is hit by a CC effect, automatically Cleanse the CC and reduce the barrier by 10% integrity instead. Once per 15s per ally.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - 256-Bit Armor      | Buff/Risk     | Apply a heavy encryption layer to an ally: they take 25% reduced damage and are immune to Debuffs, but their movement speed is reduced by 15% for 12s.
  - Redundant Systems  | Passive/Shield  | If an ally's barrier breaks, they automatically receive a secondary "backup" barrier at 30% of the original value for 5s.
  - Handshake Protocol | Active/Shield   | Link two allies with an encrypted channel: they share 30% of all healing and barriers received by either party for 20s.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Zero-Trust Network | Aura     | Passive aura: all allied barriers in 20m radius intercept 10% of damage dealt to unshielded allies nearby, redistributing it across all active barriers.
  - Data Vault         | Active   | Designate an ally as the Vault: for 15s, they take 0 damage from all sources. Every hit that would have landed converts to barrier strength on a second ally you designate. 60s cooldown.
  - Asymmetric Key     | Buff/Shield     | Apply a two-key barrier: only you hold the decryption key. While active, all healing that ally receives is doubled. If you cast a skill on that ally, the key is "used" and the barrier explodes outward for AoE damage equal to 50% of its remaining integrity. 30s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Fortress Protocol  | Channel  | Channel for 2s to encrypt the entire team: all allies gain barriers equal to 40% of their max HP for 20s. Barriers gained this way stack on top of existing barriers rather than replacing them. 90s cooldown.
  - Quantum Encryption | Toggle   | Toggle on: your own HP pool becomes encrypted — incoming damage is delayed by 2s before applying. During that 2s delay, any healing you receive cancels the pending damage proportionally. Toggle off resets all pending damage immediately (takes it all at once). High risk, high reward.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Unbreakable Cipher | Active   | Select one ally. Their HP value is encrypted for 30s — it cannot change in any direction (no damage, no healing, no drain). They become a perfectly stable anchor point. During this window, all damage that would hit them is instead distributed evenly across all enemies. All healing that would land on them empowers your barriers across all allies instead. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Encryption:
>> Encryption is a support-tank hybrid whose barriers have active mechanical texture — not just passive soaking. Asymmetric Key has the best risk-reward feel: you're managing a timed detonation on an ally's body. Quantum Encryption is the scariest toggle in the class — skilled players can cancel massive burst windows, but the penalty for forgetting to heal yourself is lethal. Unbreakable Cipher as a capstone is a dramatic "make one person invincible while making everyone else the shield" — good for raid-style boss fights.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: Decryption (Reveal, Strip, Analyze Weakness)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Tear through every layer of enemy protection and hidden data, exposing raw vulnerabilities and turning their own defensive investments into liabilities.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Vulnerability Scan | Active   | Scan an enemy: reveal all active buffs/barriers on them and mark the weakest one for 8s — marked buff takes 50% more effectiveness from the next dispel.
  - Brute Force        | Attack   | Strike an enemy for moderate damage. If they have any active barrier, the barrier takes triple damage and the excess carries through to HP.
  - Plaintext Read     | Passive  | Enemies with active buffs take 8% bonus damage from all your skills.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - SSL Strip          | Debuff   | Remove all damage-reduction buffs from an enemy. For each buff removed, they take 5% of their max HP as immediate damage. 20s cooldown.
  - Reverse Engineer   | Active   | Analyze an enemy skill used in the last 5s: gain a 10s window where that skill has 0 effect if they use it again. 15s cooldown.
  - Man-in-the-Middle  | Debuff   | Intercept all healing sent to a target enemy for 8s — instead of healing them, each heal packet heals your most wounded ally instead.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Core Dump          | Active   | Force an enemy to instantly expend all of their active buffs at once — triggering each one's expiry effect simultaneously rather than naturally. Deals bonus damage equal to the number of buffs consumed × 8% max HP.
  - Zero-Day Exploit   | Attack   | Identify and attack a critical flaw in enemy defenses: deal damage that completely bypasses all barriers and damage reduction. 25s cooldown.
  - Decompile          | Debuff   | Reduce an enemy's skill damage output by 20% and increase their skill cooldowns by 25% for 12s by fragmenting their ability "code." Can be stacked once with SSL Strip.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Full Decryption    | Active   | Fully strip an enemy: remove ALL buffs, barriers, and damage-reduction effects. For 10s after, they cannot have any applied to them again. Deals damage equal to 15% of their max HP per buff/barrier removed. 70s cooldown.
  - Memory Leak        | Passive  | Each enemy buff you dispel causes that enemy to "leak" — they take 3% of their max HP as damage every second for 3s per buff dispelled (stacks with each buff removed; resets stack duration on additional dispels).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Root Access        | Active   | Gain root-level access to one enemy for 12s. During this window: you see their cooldowns and HP in real time, their skills deal 30% reduced damage, and once during the window you may force them to use any one of their skills of your choice targeting their own team. 150s cooldown. Cannot target bosses more than once per fight.

>> Your notes on Decryption:
>> Decryption is the ultimate anti-buffer — it punishes investment in protective or enhancing skills. Man-in-the-Middle is elegantly cruel: it doesn't just block enemy heals, it redirects them. Core Dump is a massive payoff against enemies who stack multiple buffs. Root Access at capstone feels like full hacker fantasy — seeing their cooldowns and puppet-controlling them briefly. The key design tension is that Decryption becomes stronger as enemies invest more in buffs, creating good counterplay dynamics.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: Virus (Spread, Corrupt, Cascade Infection)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Seed a single infectious data-corruption into one enemy, then watch it replicate and cascade beyond your ability to fully control — a chaotic damage amplifier that rewards patience.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Payload Inject     | Debuff   | Inject a Virus stack into one enemy. They take 3% of max HP as damage per second for 8s. If they move within 5m of another enemy, the stack transfers (not copies) to the closer enemy.
  - Worm Propagation   | Passive  | Each time a Virus stack transfers naturally (not through your skills), it copies 50% of its remaining duration to the original host as well.
  - Trojan Contact     | Attack   | Melee or ranged strike for light damage. On hit, apply 1 Virus stack. If the target already has Virus, apply 2 stacks instead.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Fork Bomb          | Active   | Force an existing Virus stack to split — apply a copy of the stack to all enemies within 8m of the infected target simultaneously. Consumes the original stack. 20s cooldown.
  - Polymorphic Code   | Passive  | Your Virus stacks randomly mutate each time they deal a damage tick: 33% chance to add a 1s Slow, 33% chance to deal an additional burst of 5% max HP, 33% chance to copy themselves to a random nearby enemy.
  - Rootkit            | Debuff   | Apply a deep-seated infection: this Virus stack cannot be Cleansed by any means for 6s, but deals 50% less damage during that window.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - DDoS Burst         | Active   | Overwhelm one enemy with stacks — instantly apply 5 Virus stacks to one target. Any existing stacks on that target refresh to full duration. 30s cooldown.
  - Network Breach     | Active   | If three or more enemies are currently infected with Virus, trigger a mass cascade: all Virus stacks on all enemies deal a burst of their total remaining damage instantly, then are consumed. 45s cooldown.
  - Adaptive Malware   | Passive  | Each time an enemy attempts to Cleanse a Virus stack, the stack resists 50% of the time and, on resistance, doubles its damage rate for 4s.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Ransomware         | Active   | Apply a special Virus to one enemy: their HP pool is "locked." If they fall below 30% HP, instead of dying they are frozen and Silenced for 5s while the Virus "demands payment" — they resume at 30% HP but are Stunned for 2s afterward. You deal full damage during the freeze. 90s cooldown.
  - Botnet Surge       | Aura     | Toggle aura: all Virus-infected enemies deal 15% reduced damage and 10% of all damage they deal is redirected to infect a random nearby ally with 1 Virus stack. Your Virus stacks are now also applied to enemies hit by infected enemies' attacks.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Total System Failure | Channel | Channel 2s. Select one infected enemy — their Virus stacks cascade into a full system failure: they are Stunned for 8s, and every 2s during the stun, one random nearby enemy (within 12m) is inflicted with 3 Virus stacks at full duration. After the stun, the original target takes damage equal to 80% of all Virus damage dealt during this fight. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Virus:
>> Virus is the most chaotic and emergent archetype in the class — a damage-over-time spreader with rogue behavior. The key design challenge is making spread feel exciting rather than frustrating to play: Worm Propagation's copy-on-transfer mechanic ensures you never feel like you "lost" a stack when it moves. Polymorphic Code adds genuine unpredictability that keeps each infection interesting. Ransomware is thematically perfect and mechanically unusual — it's essentially a free 5s execution window on a "dead" enemy. Total System Failure is a massive cathartic payoff for a patient DoT playstyle.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON CIPHER]
>
> Cipher is a sci-fi support/saboteur class with three distinct roles: Encryption (barrier engineer), Decryption (buff destroyer), and Virus (infectious DoT spreader). The class has excellent internal coherence — all three archetypes use the same "data as reality" metaphor while playing completely differently. The main design risk is Virus being either too passive (just watch it spread) or too swingy (Network Breach doing enormous burst). Recommend adding a Virus stack cap per enemy (e.g., max 8 stacks) to prevent Ransomware + DDoS + Network Breach from being a one-shot combo. Encryption's Quantum Encryption toggle is the highest skill-expression skill in the class and should be clearly telegraphed to enemies so it doesn't feel unfair.
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 91: Golemancer (Construct Creator)
################################################################################
Fantasy: An architect of artificial life, the Golemancer sculpts loyal constructs from raw matter and commands them as extensions of their own will — shaping fluid clay into whatever form the moment demands, hammering iron into an indomitable war engine, or cultivating crystalline gemstone into an elemental force of nature.

ARCHETYPE A: Clay Sculptor (Reshape, Adapt, Morph)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Command a single clay golem that can be reshaped mid-combat into radically different forms, trading permanence for total tactical flexibility.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Summon Clay Golem  | Summon   | Summon a basic clay golem (moderate HP, melee attacks). The golem persists until destroyed. Only one golem may exist at a time.
  - Reshape: Brute     | Active   | Reshape your golem into Brute form: gains +40% damage and +20% size, loses 20% speed. Instant. 8s cooldown.
  - Reshape: Runner    | Active   | Reshape your golem into Runner form: gains +60% speed and a knockback on hit, loses 30% damage. Instant. 8s cooldown.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Malleable Body     | Passive/Defense  | When your golem takes damage equal to 15% of its max HP in one hit, automatically absorb and redistribute the excess — the golem takes half damage and gains +5% HP (stacks up to 3x per reshape cycle).
  - Reshape: Shield    | Active   | Reshape your golem into Shield Wall form: it becomes stationary but generates a 180-degree barrier behind it, blocking all projectiles from that direction. 12s cooldown.
  - Clay Snap          | Reactive/Heal | When your golem is below 30% HP, instantly reshape it to its last-used non-combat form and fully restore 40% of its max HP as the clay reconstitutes. Once per golem summon.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Reshape: Siege     | Active   | Reshape your golem into Siege form: it crouches and launches compressed clay projectiles at long range, each slowing hit enemies by 20% for 3s. 15s cooldown.
  - Fission Split      | Active   | Split your golem into two half-sized golems with 50% HP and damage each. They share your reshape commands but act independently. Lasts 20s before merging back. 40s cooldown.
  - Sculptor's Bond    | Passive/Heal  | You and your golem share a sensory link: you gain 5% of all damage your golem deals as healing and see through its eyes as a toggleable view.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Reshape: Colossus  | Active   | Reshape your golem into Colossus form: triple size, 50% bonus HP, and each of its melee attacks creates a shockwave CC that knocks down enemies in a 5m cone. Lasts 15s. 60s cooldown.
  - Living Clay        | Passive/Heal  | Your golem passively regenerates 3% of its max HP per second. Each reshape it undergoes within 10s of the last adds 0.5% to this regeneration rate (stacks up to 4x).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Infinite Canvas    | Transform | For 25s, your golem enters a state of continuous rapid morphing — it cycles through Brute, Runner, Shield, and Siege forms every 3s automatically, applying each form's best effect simultaneously for a brief 1s overlap window. During this time you may manually trigger any reshape for free with no cooldown. If the golem is destroyed during this state, it immediately reforms from the ground at 50% HP after 2s. 150s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Clay Sculptor:
>> Clay Sculptor has the most interesting single-minion design of the three Golemancer archetypes. The core loop of "one golem, many forms" rewards map awareness and anticipating what shape you'll need next. Fission Split is a fantastic mid-tier payoff — two weaker golems create tactical positioning puzzles. The capstone Infinite Canvas is appropriately insane: a golem that is simultaneously everything is visually spectacular and mechanically strong without being broken (the 25s duration is the limiter). Key balance note: reshape cooldowns should be short enough that the game feels fluid, not like you're waiting for the right moment to finally reshape.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: Iron Forger (Armored War Golem, Heavy Tank Construct)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Build and maintain an indestructible iron war golem that holds the line, drawing all fire onto itself while you fortify it further with each passing second.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Summon Iron Golem  | Summon   | Summon a heavy iron golem (high HP, high armor, slow movement). It generates strong threat, forcing nearby enemies to prioritize it over allies.
  - Rivet Strike       | Active   | Command your golem to slam an enemy with an iron fist — heavy damage, 1s stun, 3s cooldown on golem (not you).
  - Forge Plate        | Buff/Shield     | Rapidly add a heat-fused iron plate to your golem: it gains a bonus HP layer equal to 15% of its max HP. Stacks up to 3 times; each stack increases the golem's weight, reducing its speed by 5%. 15s cooldown.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Anvil Stance       | Stance   | Toggle: your golem becomes immovable but gains 40% damage reduction and its threat generation doubles. Enemies within 5m of it take 5% max HP damage per second from radiant heat.
