  - Emergency Weld     | Heal     | Instantly restore 25% of your golem's max HP by welding on scrap material. Golem is briefly halted for 0.5s during the weld. 20s cooldown.
  - Magnetized Armor   | Passive/Defense  | Your golem's iron plates attract enemy metallic projectiles: it intercepts ranged attacks aimed at allies within 8m, absorbing them at 50% reduced damage.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Blast Furnace Core | Active   | Supercharge your golem's internal furnace: for 12s its attacks deal bonus fire damage equal to 20% of the target's max HP and each hit applies a 2s burn stack. 35s cooldown.
  - Indestructible     | Passive/Defense  | When your golem would be reduced to 0 HP for the first time per summon, it instead survives at 1 HP and gains a 5s immunity window. During that window, all incoming damage is stored and dealt back as a burst to surrounding enemies when the window ends.
  - Iron Sentinel      | Aura     | Your golem passively radiates a hardening aura: all allies within 12m gain 10% damage reduction and are immune to knockback while the golem is at above 50% HP.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Juggernaut Charge  | Movement | Command your golem to charge in a straight line at full speed (unusually fast for one moment): it runs through all enemies in its path, dealing massive damage, knocking them aside, and leaving a trail of scorched metal shards that slow movement by 30% for 5s. 50s cooldown.
  - Living Fortress    | Passive/Defense  | Your golem becomes a literal fortress: allies can stand on top of it (elevated position for ranged allies, cover for melee), it gains doors/ports that can be opened/closed as barriers, and it grows a shoulder-mounted impact cannon (auto-fires at your current target every 8s for heavy single-target damage).

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Immortal Engine    | Channel  | Over 3 seconds, you pour your entire forge-craft into your golem. It grows to massive size, gains 200% of its current max HP as a bonus HP layer, and activates all of the following simultaneously for 30s: Blast Furnace Core, Anvil Stance, Iron Sentinel, and Magnetized Armor. If the golem is destroyed during this window, you are Stunned for 4s as the psychic backlash hits you. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Iron Forger:
>> Iron Forger is the most conventional of the three archetypes (tank minion manager) but earns its place through deep mechanical customization. Living Fortress at T4 is the standout design — turning the golem into actual terrain that allies interact with is a genuinely novel mechanic. Forge Plate's stacking speed penalty creates a satisfying tradeoff: do you keep layering armor and make a slow wall, or keep it mobile? Indestructible's stored-damage-burst is a deceptively good passive that punishes burst-damage-focused enemy teams.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: Crystal Grower (Gemstone Golem, Elemental Powers)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Grow a crystalline golem whose elemental affinity shifts with each gem you fuse into it, creating a versatile elemental powerhouse that reflects and amplifies magical energy.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Summon Crystal Core| Summon   | Summon a base crystal golem (moderate HP, moderate damage, immune to the element of its current gem). Begins with no gem fused.
  - Fuse: Ruby         | Active   | Fuse a Ruby into your golem: it gains fire damage on all attacks and applies a 2s burn. Replacing a previous gem launches the old gem as a projectile at the nearest enemy for bonus elemental damage. 10s cooldown.
  - Fuse: Sapphire     | Active   | Fuse a Sapphire: golem gains frost damage, slows hit enemies by 25%, and emits a frost aura that slows nearby enemies by 10%. 10s cooldown.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Fuse: Emerald      | Active   | Fuse an Emerald: golem gains poison damage (stacking DoT), its attacks heal you for 5% of damage dealt, and it sprouts regenerating thorn armor (reflects 15% melee damage). 10s cooldown.
  - Crystal Resonance  | Reactive  | When your golem takes elemental damage matching its current gem type, it absorbs the energy — gaining a 10% damage boost and applying an amplified version of its elemental effect on next hit.
  - Geode Burst        | Active   | Force your golem to detonate its outer crystal layer — AoE blast dealing moderate elemental damage in 8m radius and applying the current gem's effect to all hit enemies. The golem regenerates its crystal layer over 6s during which it deals 30% less damage. 25s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Fuse: Amethyst     | Active   | Fuse an Amethyst: golem gains arcane damage, its attacks apply a 3s Silence on 30% chance, and it passively boosts your skill damage by 15% through sympathetic resonance. 10s cooldown.
  - Prismatic Refraction| Reactive | When a hostile single-target spell hits your golem, it is refracted — a copy of the spell (at 50% power) is fired at the spell's caster. Stacks once with Crystal Resonance.
  - Crystallize        | CC       | Command your golem to exhale a spray of crystalline shards in a cone: enemies hit are encased in crystal for 2s (CC) and take double damage from the next hit that strikes them. 30s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Fuse: Diamond      | Active   | Fuse a Diamond: golem becomes immune to all elemental damage, its attacks deal true damage (bypasses armor), and it emits a Clarity Aura — allies within 10m deal 10% bonus damage of all types. 10s cooldown.
  - Gem Mastery        | Passive/Utility  | You may now have two gems fused simultaneously — the golem gains both gems' damage types and both elemental immunities. Geode Burst triggers both elemental effects. Crystal Resonance activates for either element.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Philosopher's Stone| Transform | Your golem undergoes a 2s transformation: all five gem types are fused simultaneously, making it immune to all elemental damage. It now cycles through elemental types every 2s (Fire, Frost, Poison, Arcane, True), applying each type's effect with 50% bonus potency. For 30s, every time the golem's element cycles, it releases a Geode Burst automatically in all directions at no cooldown cost. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Crystal Grower:
>> Crystal Grower has the highest moment-to-moment decision-making of the three archetypes — choosing which gem to fuse and when to swap (launching the old gem as a projectile!) creates a fun mini-game within combat. Prismatic Refraction is a clever passive that punishes sorcerer enemies specifically. Gem Mastery at T4 opens up beautiful combos: Ruby+Sapphire gives fire/frost which triggers two different on-hit effects. Philosopher's Stone is a worthy capstone — the rotational burst mechanic is visually spectacular and mechanically clear.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON GOLEMANCER]
>
> Golemancer succeeds as a class because all three archetypes are unified by the "commanding a construct" identity while offering radically different feels: Clay Sculptor is a puzzle-solving shapeshifter, Iron Forger is a patient fortress builder, and Crystal Grower is an elemental combo engine. The shared challenge for all three is minion AI — the golem must be smart enough to not require babysitting on simple tasks while responding crisply to player commands on complex ones. Recommend all golems have a "follow and attack my target" default state. Design risk: Fission Split (Clay) combined with Living Fortress (Iron) should be archetype-locked to prevent cross-archetype combinations. Crystal Grower's gem system is the most replayable of the three due to situational gem choices.
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 92: Threadcaster (Fate/String/Weaving)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A weaver of fate's invisible threads, the Threadcaster reaches into the tapestry of destiny to bind enemies together through shared consequences, weave complex buffs and debuffs directly into the fabric of being, and sever the connections that keep others standing — cutting allies free of curses and enemies free of their advantages with equal finality.

ARCHETYPE A: Red Thread (Fate Connections, Link Targets, Shared Consequence)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Tie enemies and allies together with threads of fate so that what happens to one instantly ripples through to the other — creating devastating chain reactions and protective bonds.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Red Binding        | Active   | Connect two enemies with a Red Thread for 10s: 40% of all damage dealt to either target is also dealt to the other. Does not chain infinitely.
  - Fate Link          | Active   | Connect an ally and an enemy: 50% of the healing the ally receives is instead dealt as damage to the linked enemy. 15s cooldown.
  - Tangled Threads    | Reactive  | When a Red Thread connection expires naturally, both connected targets are briefly Slowed by 20% for 2s as the thread snaps taut before breaking.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Triple Knot        | Active   | Extend a Red Binding to connect three enemies simultaneously in a triangle — damage shared between all three at 25% each. 25s cooldown.
  - Sympathetic Pull   | Active   | Yank the Red Thread connecting two targets: pull both targets toward each other simultaneously, dealing collision damage if they meet. 20s cooldown.
  - Resonant Thread    | Passive/Heal  | Each time a Red Thread transmits damage, you gain 3% of the transmitted damage as HP. Threads you control can now be seen as glowing lines by allies.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Life Thread        | Heal   | Connect two allies with a protective thread: they share 60% of all healing received between them for 15s. If either drops below 20% HP, the thread transfers all healing to them exclusively for 5s. 35s cooldown.
  - Fate Convergence   | Active   | Pull all enemies currently connected by your Red Threads toward a single point simultaneously. Enemies who collide take AoE damage and are Stunned for 1.5s. 40s cooldown.
  - Cut on Impact      | Reactive  | When a Red Thread is severed by Cleanse or dispel, it detonates — dealing 20% of the target's max HP as damage to both former endpoints of the thread.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Web of Fate        | Active   | Spin a 20m-radius web of threads: all enemies in range are connected to each other in a full network for 15s. Any damage dealt to any enemy spreads at 15% efficiency to all others in the network. 70s cooldown.
  - Moirai's Knot      | Passive/Utility  | When you have three or more active threads, gain Moirai's Focus: your Red Thread skills cost no resources, have 20% reduced cooldowns, and deal 10% bonus damage. Bonus is lost if threads drop below three.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Skeins of Doom     | Channel  | Channel 2.5s. Weave every enemy in range (up to 20m) into one catastrophic fate-knot: for 20s, all damage dealt to any enemy is split evenly across all connected enemies. All healing any enemy receives is converted to damage against them instead. At the end of the duration, the knot explodes — dealing 30% of each enemy's max HP as AoE damage. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Red Thread:
>> Red Thread is the most "support control" of the three archetypes — it doesn't deal damage directly but multiplies existing damage dramatically. The key skill expression is learning to set up Fate Convergence after Triple Knot, pulling three enemies into a pile for a Stun + collision burst. Moirai's Knot creates a beautiful incentive to maintain thread count rather than letting them expire. Skeins of Doom is appropriately terrifying and requires setup time — good enemies will attempt to break threads during the channel. Cut on Impact is a sneaky punisher for teams who run heavy Cleanse.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: Loom (Weave Buffs/Debuffs, Tapestry, Layering)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Weave intricate patterns of intersecting buff and debuff threads into a living tapestry, layering effects that compound with each new strand added to the design.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Warp Thread        | Buff     | Weave a Warp strand onto an ally: +15% damage for 10s. Warp strands interact with Weft strands to create Pattern effects.
  - Weft Thread        | Buff/Movement     | Weave a Weft strand onto an ally: +15% speed for 10s. Weft strands interact with Warp strands to create Pattern effects.
  - Crooked Weave      | Debuff   | Weave a corrupted strand onto an enemy: -15% damage for 8s. Stacks with a second corrupted strand to become -30% damage.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Plain Pattern      | Passive/Defense  | When the same ally has both a Warp and a Weft strand active simultaneously, they automatically gain a Plain Pattern bonus: +10% to all damage dealt and +10% damage reduction.
  - Darning Needle     | Active   | Rapidly stitch a hole in an ally's protective threads: immediately Cleanse one debuff and reinforce their next 3 incoming hits with 20% damage reduction each. 20s cooldown.
  - Tangled Weft       | Debuff   | Apply a tangled corrupted strand to an enemy: every skill they use triggers a brief 0.3s stutter as the thread snarls their movements. Stacks twice (0.6s stutter on second stack).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Diamond Pattern    | Active   | Weave a complex Diamond Pattern onto an ally with at least two existing strands: they gain +25% to both damage and speed, and emit a 5m aura that applies the Plain Pattern bonus to nearby allies. 35s cooldown.
  - Unravel            | Active   | Pull on all corrupted strands on one enemy simultaneously: remove them all and deal damage equal to 10% of their max HP per strand removed. 25s cooldown.
  - Tapestry Manifest  | Active   | Visualize the current tapestry: for 8s, all strand interactions trigger twice (all Pattern bonuses double, all corrupted strand penalties double). 45s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Master Weaver      | Passive/Utility  | You may now maintain up to 6 simultaneous strand effects (up from 3). Each time you weave a new strand, all existing strands on that target gain +5% potency (stacks up to 3x per target per cast cycle).
  - Cursed Loom        | Active   | Apply the Cursed Loom to one enemy: all Warp and Weft strands you weave for the next 20s are automatically mirrored as corrupted strands on this enemy (they receive the debuff version of every buff you grant an ally). 60s cooldown.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Grand Tapestry     | Active   | Weave the Grand Tapestry across all allies: apply 3 Warp and 3 Weft strands to every ally simultaneously, triggering Plain Pattern, Diamond Pattern, and a new Herringbone Pattern (all allies deal 20% bonus damage to enemies who have corrupted strands). For 25s the tapestry self-maintains — expired strands are automatically reapplied at the start of each 5s interval. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Loom:
>> Loom is the most complex archetype mechanically — it's a buff-weaving system with internal synergy rules. The Warp/Weft/Pattern system rewards players who understand the layering rules while remaining legible ("do I have both strands? Plain Pattern activates"). Cursed Loom at T4 is the best design in this archetype: mirroring buffs as debuffs onto an enemy means you can buff your whole team and simultaneously curse one enemy — elegant and flavourful. Tapestry Manifest as a cooldown doubler is risky but exciting: double-Diamond Pattern for 8s is a serious power spike.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: Scissors (Sever, Cut Connections, Snip Fate)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Cut through anything — buff threads, fate bonds, curses, CC, even enemy attack animations — using razor-sharp fate-scissors that cleanse allies and strip enemies with decisive precision.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Clean Cut          | Active  | Instantly remove one debuff from an ally and deal damage equal to 8% of your max HP to the enemy who applied it (if identifiable). 8s cooldown.
  - Snip               | Debuff   | Remove one buff from an enemy, dealing damage equal to 10% of the benefit they would have received for the buff's remaining duration. 10s cooldown.
  - Frayed Edge        | Passive/Utility  | After each cut (Cleanse or Dispel), your next Scissors skill has its cooldown reduced by 2s and its potency increased by 10%.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Thread Sever       | Active   | Destroy all active Red Thread connections in a 15m radius — both your own and enemy-created ones. Each severed thread damages both its former endpoints for 12% of their max HP. 20s cooldown.
  - Tailored Removal   | Active  | Carefully snip exactly one CC effect from an ally (choose which if multiple are present): they are fully freed and gain a 3s immunity to that specific CC type. 15s cooldown.
  - Anticipatory Snip  | Reactive | When an enemy begins casting a buff or heal, interrupt it by snipping the thread of intent — 25% chance to cancel the cast entirely, 75% chance to reduce the effect by 40% if it completes. 12s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Shredder           | Active   | Remove ALL buffs from one enemy simultaneously. For each buff removed, they are Slowed by 5% for 5s (stacks per buff; max 35% slow). 35s cooldown.
  - Destiny Cut        | Active   | Cut an enemy's fate thread: for 8s they are "fated to fail" — their next 3 skills automatically deal 50% reduced effectiveness, regardless of type. Cannot be Cleansed. 40s cooldown.
  - Guardian Scissors  | Reactive | When an ally would receive a debuff, trigger automatically: 40% chance to snip the debuff before it lands. On a successful snip, the debuff backtracks and applies to the attacker for 3s. Passive.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Unmaking Cut       | Active   | Perform a cut so precise it severs an enemy's connection to a single ability — their lowest-cooldown skill is cut from them and cannot be used for 30s. The severed skill is held as a "stolen thread" — you may activate it yourself once within that window. 70s cooldown.
  - Grand Cleanse      | Heal  | Remove all debuffs from all allies simultaneously. For each debuff removed, heal that ally for 8% of their max HP. The debuffs removed this way cascade onto a random enemy each, applying as their debuff version. 90s cooldown.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Final Cut      | Active   | Make a cut that unravels fate itself: select one enemy. All buffs, connections, and fate threads on them are removed instantly. For 15s, they cannot receive any buff, heal, or protective effect from any source. Every 3s during this window, they also lose one random active skill to a "fate severance" (cannot use it for 6s). At the end of 15s, all severed fate returns at once as a catastrophic backlash dealing damage equal to 60% of their max HP. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Scissors:
>> Scissors is the decisive, surgical archetype — you cut things and things end. The most satisfying skill is Unmaking Cut: stealing an enemy's skill is a power fantasy equivalent to Telekinesis-grabbing someone's weapon. Guardian Scissors as a passive proc-based Cleanse creates genuinely tense moments ("will it proc this time?"). The Final Cut capstone is excellent because it has a slow build-up — the enemy knows they're doomed for 15 whole seconds and can't do anything about it, which creates drama. Destiny Cut is the most flavourful skill in the archetype: "fated to fail" as a mechanic is thematically perfect for a Threadcaster.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON THREADCASTER]
>
> Threadcaster is a class where the word "connection" does all the mechanical heavy lifting: Red Thread creates connections between targets for damage-sharing, Loom weaves connections between buff-layers for compound effects, and Scissors destroys connections of all kinds. The class has outstanding internal thematic coherence. The biggest design challenge is Red Thread's Web of Fate and Loom's Grand Tapestry both being area-wide effects that could trivialize certain encounters — consider adding diminishing returns for targets beyond 5 or so in the network. Scissors' The Final Cut is the cleanest "it's over" capstone of the three archetypes and would benefit from a dramatic visual effect (a massive golden scissor-blade cutting through the air).
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 93: Tideborn (Aquatic Warrior)
################################################################################
Fantasy: Forged by the relentless sea, the Tideborn carries the ocean's power into any environment — commanding water currents to control the flow of melee combat, growing living coral armor and weapons directly from their body during the heat of battle, and diving into the crushing darkness of the abyss to emerge transformed into something far older and more terrible than any surface creature.

ARCHETYPE A: Riptide (Current Control, Pull/Push, Melee Flow)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Weaponize the push and pull of invisible water currents in close-range combat, controlling enemy positioning with tidal force while riding the flow yourself.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Current Strike     | Attack   | Strike with a water-charged fist — deals moderate damage and pushes the target back 4m in the direction of your choosing. 5s cooldown.
  - Undertow           | CC       | Send a low current sweeping under one enemy's feet: pull them toward you 5m and briefly slow them by 30% for 2s. 8s cooldown.
  - Tide Sense         | Passive/Evasion  | You feel all water movement in a 15m radius: gain a passive alert when enemies enter this range and a 5% dodge chance against attacks from behind.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Riptide Surge      | Movement | Ride a burst of current: dash 8m in any direction through enemies (not blocked by them), dealing light damage to each enemy passed through. 12s cooldown.
  - Cross-Current      | CC       | Create opposing currents between two enemies: push them toward each other simultaneously. Collision deals damage proportional to their combined speed of impact. 18s cooldown.
  - Surf the Flow      | Reactive  | After pushing or pulling an enemy, your next melee attack within 3s deals 30% bonus damage and applies an additional 2m push in the same direction.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Maelstrom Stance   | Stance   | Toggle: enter a spinning water-current stance — you become harder to CC (50% resistance) and all your melee attacks push enemies into a slow orbit around you at 6m radius. Enemies in orbit take 8% max HP damage every 2s. Toggle off launches all orbiting enemies outward simultaneously.
  - Tidal Lock         | CC       | Pin an enemy in place using opposing currents from all sides for 3s. They take 150% damage from any attack that comes from behind them during this window. 30s cooldown.
  - Hydraulic Slam     | Attack   | Grab an enemy with a water tendril and body-slam them into the nearest other enemy: both take heavy damage and are knocked down for 1.5s. Requires two enemies within 8m of each other. 25s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - King Tide          | Active   | Call a massive tidal surge: push ALL enemies in a 15m cone back 10m simultaneously, dealing AoE damage. If any enemy collides with terrain during the push, they are additionally Stunned for 2s. 55s cooldown.
  - Current Master     | Passive/Evasion  | You always have a persistent current flowing around you: melee attacks gain +15% damage; enemies who melee-attack you have a 20% chance to be involuntarily pulled 2m off-balance and miss. You are immune to environmental knockback.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Tidal Sovereign    | Stance   | Enter a sovereign tidal state for 20s: all push and pull effects you apply are doubled in distance. Undertow pulls have 0 cooldown. You can freely command which direction the current flows in a 20m radius — enemies moving against the current have 40% reduced speed; enemies moving with the current are accelerated toward you or terrain. Any enemy touching terrain takes 10% max HP damage per second. 150s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Riptide:
>> Riptide is a melee control specialist whose power comes from turning positioning into damage. Hydraulic Slam is the most satisfying single skill in this archetype — slamming one enemy into another is pure physical comedy with mechanical payoff. Maelstrom Stance's orbit mechanic is innovative: maintaining the orbit is rewarding, but the toggle-off launch creates a high-skill "hold them all then explode" moment. Tidal Sovereign's "choose the current direction" at capstone level is genuinely novel — creating a moving debuff zone that rewards terrain awareness.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: Coral Knight (Growing Armor/Weapons, Escalating Defense)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Grow living coral armor, weapons, and fortifications directly from your body mid-combat — escalating defenses that regenerate, spread, and become a mobile fortress with time.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Coral Plate        | Buff/Shield     | Rapidly grow a layer of coral across your torso: gain a barrier equal to 20% of your max HP. The coral slowly grows — barrier increases by 3% max HP per second for 5s before stabilizing. 15s cooldown.
  - Barnacle Grip      | Attack   | Grow coral spines across your fist and strike: deals moderate damage and attaches a barnacle cluster to the target, slowing them by 20% and dealing 3% max HP per second for 5s (the coral digs in). 10s cooldown.
  - Reef Growth        | Passive/Shield  | Coral barriers on you regenerate 2% max HP per second while above 10% of their initial value. Each barrier hit slows the attacker by 5% for 1s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Coral Weapon       | Buff     | Grow a living coral weapon from your arm — your melee attacks gain +25% damage and apply a stacking barnacle slow (5% per hit, max 5 stacks). The weapon persists for 20s and regenerates 10% of its potency every 5s. 20s cooldown.
  - Living Shell       | Buff/Defense     | Grow a coral shell around your entire body: gain 30% damage reduction. The shell cracks incrementally rather than breaking at once — loses 10% reduction per heavy hit. 25s cooldown.
  - Coral Spike        | Reactive | When you are hit by a melee attack, instantly grow a coral spike outward from the impact point: deals 15% of your max HP as damage to the attacker and applies the barnacle slow. Passive, no cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Colony Spread      | Active   | Extend your coral growth to an adjacent ally: grant them a Coral Plate barrier at 60% potency and Coral Spike reactives for 12s. 30s cooldown.
  - Calcification      | Active   | Rapidly calcify your coral growth to stone-hardness for 8s: all your barriers double in value, you become immune to CC, but you cannot move or dash. On expiry, shed the calcified shell as a 10m-radius shrapnel burst dealing heavy AoE damage. 40s cooldown.
  - Reef Fortress      | Active   | Expel coral rapidly in all directions: create 4 stationary coral pillars in a 6m ring around you. Pillars block projectiles, act as terrain for melee pathing, and each deals 5% max HP damage to enemies who touch them. Pillars persist for 20s and can be destroyed (each has 20% of your max HP). 45s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Great Barrier      | Active   | Grow a massive coral wall (20m wide, 4m tall) in a straight line before you: blocks all movement and projectiles for 15s. Enemies touching the wall take 8% max HP damage per second. The wall can be broken by sustained attack (total HP: 150% of your max HP). 70s cooldown.
  - Living Armor       | Passive/Shield  | Your coral constantly regenerates from the sea: all your coral barriers regenerate 5% of their maximum value per second (up from 2%). Each time any coral barrier breaks, you immediately grow a replacement at 30% value.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Coral Colossus     | Transform | Over 3s, you grow a full coral exoskeleton — doubling your size, tripling your max HP temporarily via a massive barrier, and granting all of the following for 30s: Coral Spike on all surfaces, Reef Growth at 3x rate, Living Shell at 30% reduction baseline, and your melee attacks shatter coral fragments onto the ground creating difficult terrain. On expiry, the exoskeleton explodes in all directions dealing 50% of your max HP as AoE damage. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Coral Knight:
>> Coral Knight is a "living fortress" tank whose power scales with time — you get stronger the longer you survive. Calcification is the bravest risk-reward skill in the archetype: rooting yourself for 8s is terrifying but the shrapnel burst payoff is enormous. Reef Fortress creates genuine terrain control, making you a positional anchor rather than just a damage sponge. Coral Colossus as a capstone should have an unmistakable visual — you become a walking reef. Balance concern: Living Armor at T4 may make you effectively unkillable in sustained fights — consider a cap on total active barrier regeneration per second.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: Deep One (Abyssal Transformation, Pressure, Ancient Power)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Descend into the crushing pressure of the deep abyss mentally and physically, awakening ancient aquatic evolutionary features that transform you into something no surface-dweller can understand or predict.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Pressure Adapted   | Passive/Defense  | Passively gain 10% damage reduction. For every 10 seconds you are in combat, gain an additional 1% (up to 15 additional stacks — 25% cap). Pressure resets slowly out of combat.
  - Bioluminescence    | Active   | Emit a burst of deep-sea light: blind all enemies in 8m for 1.5s and reveal all stealthed enemies in 20m for 10s. 15s cooldown.
  - Depth Charge       | Attack   | Release a compressed sphere of deep-sea pressure: deals heavy damage in a 5m radius and creates a 5s field of crushing pressure that slows enemies within it by 30% and prevents jumping/movement abilities. 12s cooldown.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Abyssal Skin       | Passive/Resistance  | Your skin toughens with deep-sea adaptation: gain 15% resistance to all elemental damage. Below 50% HP, this doubles to 30% as your deep-sea survival instincts fully activate.
  - Lure               | CC       | Extend a bioluminescent lure from your body: enemies within 10m are compelled toward it for 3s (irresistible slow pull). Enemies who reach the lure take burst damage and are Stunned for 1s. 25s cooldown.
  - Pressure Wave      | Attack   | Release an expanding shockwave of crushing abyssal pressure: deals AoE damage in a 12m radius and strips one buff from every enemy hit. 20s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Echolocation       | Utility  | Activate deep-sea sonar: for 20s you gain perfect awareness of all enemy positions and HP values within 40m regardless of walls, stealth, or darkness. Your next 3 attacks have 100% hit chance and cannot be dodged. 40s cooldown.
  - Ancient Gills      | Passive/Immunity  | You no longer breathe — immune to all air-based debuffs (poison clouds, smoke, suffocation), and in aquatic environments gain +30% to all stats. Your healing received increases by 20% as your alien biology is supernaturally efficient.
  - Crushing Depth     | Debuff   | Apply abyssal pressure to one enemy: they take 5% of their max HP per second, their skill range is reduced by 30% (as if squeezed), and their cooldowns increase by 20% for 10s. 30s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Abyssal Form       | Transform | Partially transform: your body elongates and darkens, your eyes begin to glow. For 15s: gain +30% damage, your movement leaves a trail of darkened water that blinds enemies walking through it for 1s, and your melee attacks apply Crushing Depth for free (no cooldown). 60s cooldown.
  - Void of the Deep   | Aura     | Toggle aura: emit a field of crushing abyssal pressure in 15m radius. Enemies in range take 3% max HP damage per second, have their movement speed reduced by 20%, and have a 15% chance per second of being forcibly pulled 2m toward you by the pressure gradient.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Abyssal Awakening | Transform | Fully awaken as a Deep One: for 25s undergo complete transformation. Your size increases dramatically, your stats double, you become immune to all CC and elemental damage, and you gain three new instinctual attacks — Abyssal Bite (enormous single-target burst), Pressure Crush (AoE stun in 10m), and Void Gaze (cone debuff applying all current Debuffs simultaneously). At the end of 25s, you revert and are briefly vulnerable for 3s (40% damage increase taken). 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Deep One:
>> Deep One is the most dramatic transformation fantasy in the class — a slow metamorphosis into something alien and terrifying. The Pressure Adapted passive creates a beautiful time-pressure dynamic in long fights (pun intended): the longer you survive, the stronger you get. Lure is wonderfully thematic — extending a bioluminescent lure and watching enemies helplessly swim toward it. Void Gaze in the capstone is a brilliant "apply every debuff at once" payoff for an archetype that has many separate debuffs to collect. The 3s vulnerability on Awakening expiry prevents it from being completely risk-free.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON TIDEBORN]
>
> Tideborn is a class with exceptional environmental identity — all three archetypes feel like different expressions of the same ocean theme: Riptide controls the flow, Coral Knight builds like a reef, and Deep One embodies the alien terror of the deep. The class would particularly shine in environments with terrain features (walls for Riptide push combos, water environments for Deep One bonuses). The main balance concern is stacking: Pressure Adapted's time-scaling passive should not stack with Abyssal Skin's elemental resistance to create a nearly-invincible tank; consider making them share a DR cap. Coral Knight's Calcification paired with Reef Fortress creates a strong "immovable object" window that may need a 30s minimum interval between uses.
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 94: Pyrodancer (Fire Performance)
################################################################################
Fantasy: Part acrobat, part fire mage, the Pyrodancer treats combat as a performance — swallowing flames to turn enemy fire against them, keeping multiple burning projectiles alive in the air with juggler's precision, and spinning into a blazing melee hurricane that scorches everything in range with dancer's grace.

ARCHETYPE A: Fire Eater (Absorb Fire, Convert, Immunity)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Consume incoming fire attacks for power, converting enemy flames into personal fuel while becoming progressively immune to the element that everyone else fears.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Flame Swallow      | Reactive | When struck by a fire-based attack, swallow the flame instead of taking damage: absorb 50% of the damage as HP and gain 1 Ember stack. Passive, no cooldown. (Non-fire attacks deal normal damage.)
  - Smoke Exhale       | Active   | Exhale absorbed fire as a blinding smoke cloud in a 6m cone: blind enemies for 2s and deal light damage. Consumes 1 Ember stack. 8s cooldown.
  - Asbestos Skin      | Passive/Resistance  | Passively reduce all fire damage taken by 20%. For each Ember stack you currently hold, reduce fire damage by an additional 5% (max 5 stacks = 45% reduction).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Fuel Reserve       | Passive/Utility  | Ember stacks now cap at 8 (up from 5) and persist for 30s (up from 15s). While at 5 or more Ember stacks, you emit a faint heat shimmer that causes enemy projectiles (any type) to have 10% reduced accuracy.
  - Flash Spit         | Attack   | Spit absorbed fire as a compressed bolt: deals damage equal to 15% of the total fire damage you absorbed since your last Flash Spit. Consumes all Ember stacks. Minimum damage if no stacks. 10s cooldown.
  - Controlled Burn    | Active   | Deliberately swallow an ember without an incoming attack: deal 5% of your max HP to yourself to gain 2 Ember stacks instantly. Useful when no fire enemies are present. 5s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Pyroproof          | Passive/Immunity  | At 6+ Ember stacks, become completely immune to fire damage (not just reduced). Immunity drops when stacks fall below 6. While immune, enemies who use fire attacks against you have their attacks reflected at them for 30% of original damage.
  - Backdraft          | Reactive | When an enemy fire attack hits you, after Flame Swallow triggers, immediately burst the absorbed heat outward in all directions: AoE damage in 8m equal to 40% of the absorbed attack's damage. Triggers once per fire attack received. Passive.
  - Dragon's Gullet    | Active   | Consume a massive quantity of flame from one nearby fire source (ally fire skill, environmental fire, your own fire attack): instantly gain 4 Ember stacks and restore 15% of your max HP. Range 5m. 20s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Vomit Fire         | Active   | Release all stored Ember stacks in a catastrophic gout of flame: fire a cone of searing flame dealing damage equal to 25% of your max HP per Ember stack consumed. Maximum at 8 stacks = 200% max HP damage in a wide cone. 0 stacks consumed = small fizzle. 45s cooldown.
  - Human Furnace      | Passive/Immunity  | Your body permanently radiates intense heat: enemies within 4m take 4% max HP fire damage per second. All your fire attacks and skills gain +20% damage. You are now immune to both fire and cold damage permanently.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Phoenix Feast      | Transform | Deliberately let yourself be engulfed in fire — absorb all fire damage received over 3s (no harm, all converted). Then detonate in a Phoenix explosion for 30s: you rise with 50% bonus max HP as a temporary overlay, all Ember stack effects are doubled, immunity to fire is permanent for the duration, and every time you take any damage (any type) you convert 30% of it to Ember stacks. At expiry, release all stored heat in one enormous area explosion dealing total absorbed damage across all nearby enemies. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Fire Eater:
>> Fire Eater is the ultimate counter-archetype against fire-heavy enemies — designed to flip the entire damage economy of a fight where opponents lean on fire. Vomit Fire has the most satisfying risk/reward ratio: hoard stacks, time the release. Against fire enemies, you're nearly invincible; against non-fire enemies, you use Controlled Burn to maintain stack generation. Phoenix Feast as a capstone is a true fantasy payoff — deliberately eating a fire attack to become an explosion is spectacular. Backdraft at T3 is the sleeper pick: passive reflect that triggers on every fire hit becomes oppressive against enemies with fast-firing fire attacks.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: Flame Juggler (Airborne Projectile Management, Multi-Target)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Keep multiple blazing projectiles alive in the air simultaneously, juggling them with performer's precision and releasing them in coordinated strikes that reward skillful timing.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Toss               | Attack   | Launch a small fireball into the air: it orbits around you for up to 8s before expiring. While orbiting, you may command it to fire at a target (see: Release). Up to 2 Tosses active at once. 4s cooldown.
  - Release            | Active   | Command one orbiting fireball to fire at your current target. Deals moderate damage, applying a 2s burn. If two fireballs are released within 1s of each other, they collide mid-air and create a larger explosion (AoE 6m, 50% bonus damage). No cooldown.
  - Performer's Grip   | Passive/Defense  | Each orbiting fireball reduces your incoming damage by 3% (stacks; 2 balls = 6% DR). Lose one stack when a fireball expires unused.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Juggle             | Active   | Extend the lifespan of all current orbiting fireballs by 4s simultaneously and briefly accelerate their orbit (visual flourish). They gain +10% damage for the extended duration. 8s cooldown.
  - Fan Toss           | Attack   | Toss 3 fireballs at once (uses 3 of your max orbit capacity if expanded; otherwise requires max available slots). Each has reduced individual damage but grants the Release collision bonus more easily. 15s cooldown.
  - Hot Hands          | Passive/Movement  | When you have 3 or more fireballs orbiting, you gain +20% movement speed and your melee attacks deal bonus fire damage equal to the number of orbiting fireballs × 5% of your max HP.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Expand the Act     | Passive/Utility  | Increase your maximum simultaneous orbiting fireballs from 2 to 5. Each additional fireball added to orbit (3rd, 4th, 5th) has 10% reduced individual damage but triggers a unique collision pattern on Release with other orbiting balls.
  - Shower of Stars    | Active   | Release ALL orbiting fireballs simultaneously: they scatter in a spread pattern covering a 20m-wide cone, each hitting a different target (or the same if only one target is present) for full damage. If 4+ fireballs are released this way, the center of the cone creates an additional 8m-radius Inferno field for 5s (3% max HP fire per second). 30s cooldown.
  - Trick Shot         | Active   | Manually guide one orbiting fireball on a curved path for 2s — it travels around obstacles and can hit enemies behind cover. On hit, deals 150% of normal fireball damage and applies a 3s Stun. 20s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Cascade            | Reactive  | When a fireball hits an enemy who is already burning (any burn stack), it detonates an additional Cascade Burst: a secondary fireball at 30% power bounces off the target and homes in on the nearest other enemy within 8m. Can chain up to 3 times per original fireball hit.
  - Grand Finale Setup | Active   | Mark the next 10s as a "Setup Window": every fireball you Release during this window is stored invisibly as a Grand Finale charge rather than detonating. At the end of 10s (or when activated manually), release all stored charges simultaneously as one massive multi-target barrage. Charges deal 120% of normal damage each, plus 20% bonus per charge beyond the first. 70s cooldown.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Spectacular    | Active   | Launch 8 massive fireballs into orbit simultaneously (overriding your current capacity limit). For 15s, every Release triggers automatically every 1.5s on your current target without your input (the show runs itself). Each auto-release also fires a secondary smaller fireball at a random second target. At the end of 15s, all remaining orbiting fireballs converge on your current target in one final explosion dealing combined total damage of all remaining balls × 150%. 150s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Flame Juggler:
>> Flame Juggler is the most mechanically demanding archetype in the class — managing 5 orbiting fireballs while fighting in melee range requires genuine skill. Trick Shot is the prestige move: manually guiding a fireball around cover. Hot Hands rewards full-capacity juggling with melee bonuses, which creates a satisfying reason to stay near enemies while managing ranged projectiles. Grand Finale Setup has a beautiful "planning" fantasy — spending 10s building up to one enormous multishot. The Spectacular capstone "lets the show run itself" which is a satisfying release from the complexity of manual management.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: Inferno Spin (Melee AoE, Dance Moves, Fire Whirlwind)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Fight as a spinning, whirling fire tornado — melee combat becomes a dance where every footwork pattern and rotation deals escalating fire damage to all surrounding enemies.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Ember Step         | Movement | Take a dancing step in any direction, leaving a 2m fire trail for 3s that deals 5% max HP to enemies who walk through it. Recharges twice. 6s cooldown each charge.
  - Pirouette Strike   | Attack   | Spin in place and deliver a blazing 360-degree strike: deals moderate fire damage to all enemies within 4m. The spin applies a 1.5s dizzy to you (minor aim sway, not a full CC). 8s cooldown.
  - Heated Footwork    | Passive/Utility  | Your movement generates heat — each meter you move builds 1 Heat charge (max 20). At 20 Heat, your next Pirouette Strike or Spin skill deals +50% damage and is free (no cooldown cost). Heat decays at 1 per second while stationary.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Salsa Blaze        | Attack   | Perform a rapid two-step dance sequence: two quick melee hits (left, right) each dealing moderate fire damage with a small spin radius on the second hit (3m AoE on the second strike only). 6s cooldown.
  - Flamenco Stomp     | CC       | Stomp with dramatic flair: create a 6m-radius shockwave of fire that knocks all nearby enemies down for 1.5s and leaves the ground burning for 5s (4% max HP per second). 18s cooldown.
  - Partner Spin       | Active   | Grab a nearby ally and spin them into an enemy: both the ally and the enemy take moderate damage; the ally is repositioned 5m in the spin direction (repositions them, not you). Useful for moving allies out of AoE or into flanking positions. 15s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Whirling Dervish   | Channel  | Channel for up to 3s in a continuous spin: deals rapid fire damage to all enemies within 5m every 0.5s while spinning. Each full second of channeling increases spin speed — dealing 10% more damage per second sustained (max +30% at 3s). 25s cooldown.
  - Tango Inferno      | Attack   | A choreographed 3-hit combo attack: Sweep (5m frontal AoE), Pivot (turn and apply a burn to the previous target's position), Dip (ground slam dealing heavy damage in 3m radius). Must be completed in sequence within 3s. 20s cooldown.
  - Dance in the Flame | Buff/Immunity     | Deliberately stand in fire (your own or environmental): gain +20% damage, become immune to fire damage, and apply fire to all your attacks for 10s while inside the fire. Exiting the fire ends the buff immediately. Passive trigger.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Tornado Form       | Stance   | Toggle: become a spinning fire tornado — continuous movement is mandatory (cannot stand still), speed increases by 30%, and every enemy you pass through takes AoE fire damage equal to 8% of their max HP. While in Tornado Form, Pirouette Strike has no cooldown. Exiting the stance requires a 1.5s "wind-down" during which you are briefly Slowed.
  - Choreography Bonus | Reactive  | When you use 3 or more different Spin/Dance skills within 6s, trigger a Choreography Bonus: release a free Inferno Nova — 12m-radius fire explosion dealing 40% of your max HP to all enemies and knocking them outward.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Grand Finale Waltz | Transform | Perform the ultimate fire dance for 20s: you move in a continuous waltz pattern (movement becomes locked to a sweeping, arcing pattern) that deals automatic fire damage in 8m radius every 0.5s. Every 4s of dancing releases a Choreography Bonus for free. All Heated Footwork stacks are maintained at maximum during the dance. At any point you may end the Waltz early by spinning rapidly in place — the spin detonates all stored heat as a final explosion dealing 100% of your max HP as damage to all enemies in 15m. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Inferno Spin:
>> Inferno Spin is the most kinetic and visually spectacular archetype — a melee fighter who is always moving, always burning. Heated Footwork's Heat mechanic rewards constant movement which fights well with Dance in the Flame (stand in fire) for a push-pull between mobility and positioning. Partner Spin is the most unexpected skill in the archetype: using it on allies is a powerful utility move that repositions them out of danger. Tornado Form at T4 creates a genuinely unique constraint — mandatory movement as a power condition. Grand Finale Waltz's early-detonation option gives players meaningful choice during the capstone window.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON PYRODANCER]
>
> Pyrodancer is the class where fire is a medium of expression, not just a damage type. All three archetypes use fire fundamentally differently: Fire Eater treats it as resource (absorb and release), Flame Juggler treats it as performance objects (toss, juggle, release), and Inferno Spin treats it as the medium of movement itself. The class has excellent cross-archetype identity. The main design consideration is that all three archetypes want to be in fire simultaneously — Fire Eater prefers fire incoming attacks, Dance in the Flame requires standing in fire, and Flame Juggler creates fire fields. Consider making these "own fire field" interactions explicit so players can build around them cross-archetype. Trick Shot (Juggler) and Tango Inferno (Spin) are the two highest-skill-ceiling skills in the class and deserve strong audio-visual feedback.
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 95: Warden of the Wild (Primal Protector)
################################################################################
Fantasy: Ancient guardian of the untamed world, the Warden draws power from the living earth itself — raising reactive thorn-defenses that punish those who dare strike, weaving a vast subterranean root network that shares information and resources across the battlefield, and channeling the primal force of nature into a warrior form of terrifying elemental power.

ARCHETYPE A: Thornguard (Plant Defense, Reactive Punishment, Punish Attackers)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Layer yourself and allies in living thorn defenses that automatically retaliate against attackers, making the act of fighting you more dangerous than not fighting you.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Thorn Skin         | Reactive     | Encase yourself in a layer of thorns: any melee attacker takes 10% of your max HP as damage on each hit. Lasts 15s. 15s cooldown.
  - Briar Patch        | Active   | Rapidly grow a thorn field in a 6m radius at target location: enemies in the field take 4% max HP per second and have their speed reduced by 25%. Lasts 10s. 20s cooldown.
  - Reactive Barbs     | Reactive  | When an ally is hit by a melee attack, the attacker has barbs embedded: they take 5% of your max HP as damage and suffer a 10% slow for 3s. Triggers once per attacker per 5s.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Thorn Mantle       | Buff/Defense     | Apply your thorn defense to an ally: they gain Thorn Skin's retaliation effect for 12s and all damage they take reduces by 10% while the thorns are active. 20s cooldown.
  - Bramblevine        | CC       | Rapidly grow vines that entangle one enemy: Rooted for 2.5s. Thorns deal 8% max HP damage per second while rooted. 15s cooldown.
  - Spike Bloom        | Reactive | When your Thorn Skin is struck more than twice within 3s, automatically trigger a Spike Bloom: thorns shoot outward in all directions in 6m radius dealing 20% of your max HP as damage to all enemies nearby. 12s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Living Wall        | Active   | Grow a 10m-wide thorn wall at a target line: blocks movement and projectiles for 15s. Enemies attempting to pass through take 30% max HP damage. Allies can pass through freely. 40s cooldown.
  - Barbed Retort      | Reactive  | Each time your thorn retaliation deals damage, gain 2% bonus thorn damage (stacks up to 20%). Resets when thorns are not active for 10s.
  - Bone Thorn         | Active   | Launch a volley of hardened thorns: 5-projectile burst, each dealing moderate damage and embedding a barb that deals 3% max HP per second for 5s. 20s cooldown.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Thorn Fortress     | Active   | Transform the area in a 15m radius into a thorn fortress for 20s: Briar Patch effect covers the entire zone permanently, all allies inside gain Thorn Skin, and Spike Bloom triggers automatically whenever any enemy moves within 3m of any ally inside the zone. 70s cooldown.
  - Thousand Thorns    | Passive/Utility  | Your Thorn Skin now triggers on ranged attacks as well as melee (thorns launch back at the attacker). Barbed Retort stacks are maintained between encounters if you re-apply thorns within 30s.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Thornwall      | Aura     | Toggle on a permanent thorn aura: any enemy within 12m of you or any ally is passively dealing damage to themselves at 3% max HP per second just from ambient thorn pressure. Any enemy attack (melee or ranged) against any target in range triggers an immediate thorn retaliation burst for 15% of your max HP. This aura cannot be Dispelled while you are above 50% HP. 90s cooldown to reactivate after deactivation.

>> Your notes on Thornguard:
>> Thornguard's design philosophy is "make attacking anything near me a net negative." This works especially well in long sustained fights where the passive damage accumulates. Spike Bloom at T2 creates a beautiful moment: enemies face a choice between stop attacking (lose DPS) or keep attacking (trigger the spike burst). Bone Thorn at T3 gives the archetype a proactive ranged tool, preventing the playstyle from being purely passive. The Thornwall capstone is unusually simple for a legendary — just a permanent aura — but the simplicity is intentional: it's a statement that fighting your team is simply not an option anymore.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: Root Network (Underground Connection, Shared Resources, Utility)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Extend an invisible underground root network across the battlefield that shares information, resources, and abilities between connected allies — a living supply chain beneath the fight.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Plant Root Node    | Active   | Plant a root node at your current location: allies within 8m of any node gain 5% bonus HP regen and can share skills through the network (see: Pulse). Up to 3 nodes active at once. Nodes last 60s. 10s cooldown.
  - Network Pulse      | Heal   | Send a pulse through your root network: all allies within 8m of any active node are healed for 8% of their max HP. 15s cooldown.
  - Soil Sense         | Passive/Utility  | Through your root network, feel vibrations in the earth: gain passive awareness of enemy positions within 5m of any root node (glow on minimap/radar). Enemies moving near nodes are Slowed by 10% as the roots grip their feet.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Nutrient Flow      | Heal   | Channel nutrients through the root network: for 10s, all allies connected to at least one node regenerate 3% max HP per second. 25s cooldown.
  - Root Transit       | Movement | Teleport yourself to any active root node on the battlefield (range: any distance). After teleportation, you are briefly concealed for 2s as you emerge from the earth. 20s cooldown.
  - Expand Network     | Passive/Utility  | Root nodes now connect to each other if within 15m — creating a visible network web. Allies between two connected nodes gain the benefits of both nodes simultaneously. You may maintain 5 nodes (up from 3).

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Mycelial Relay     | Heal   | Link two allies through the root network: for 15s, any buff one receives is copied at 50% potency to the other. Any healing received is split 60/40 toward the more wounded of the two. 30s cooldown.
  - Emergency Burrow   | Movement | Cause an ally to dive into the root network and emerge at any active node location: teleport any ally to any node position instantly. They emerge with 2s of intangibility. 40s cooldown.
  - Fungal Bloom       | Heal   | Cause a root node to blossom into a Fungal Bloom: for 15s, the node releases spores in 10m radius that apply a stacking heal (1% max HP per stack per second, max 5 stacks) to all allies and a stacking slow (5% per stack, max 5 stacks) to all enemies in range. 35s cooldown per node.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Interconnected     | Passive/Utility  | When the root network has 4 or more nodes active, it becomes sentient: nodes automatically redirect healing pulses toward the most wounded ally in range, automatically slow any enemy that moves within 3m of any node (15% slow), and auto-spawn a new replacement node at the nearest unoccupied position within 5s of any node being destroyed.
  - World Tree Tap     | Channel  | Channel for 2s to tap into primal earth energy through your network: all root nodes supercharge for 20s — they heal for double, their detection range doubles, and Network Pulse heals for 25% max HP (up from 8%). 80s cooldown.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Living Network | Active   | Expand your root network to cover the entire battlefield for 30s: all allies everywhere (regardless of node proximity) gain all node benefits simultaneously. All root nodes pulse automatically every 3s. Enemy movement anywhere on the battlefield applies a 10% slow as the roots cover the ground. You can see every enemy position on the map for the duration. At the end of 30s, the network contracts — dealing 20% max HP damage to all enemies as the roots retract and lash outward. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Root Network:
>> Root Network is the most support-oriented archetype in the entire Warden class — almost all skills benefit teammates. Emergency Burrow stands out as a clutch skill: teleporting a dying ally to safety through the root network is a game-changing moment. Interconnected at T4 is a strong "smart network" passive that reduces micromanagement once the network is established, rewarding early node placement. The biggest design challenge is node placement strategy — the game needs to clearly indicate node radius coverage so players can make informed placement decisions. Root Transit's stealth-on-emerge is a wonderful detail for repositioning yourself aggressively.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: Verdant Champion (Nature-Empowered Warrior, Primal Form)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Shed the complexity of nature magic and become its avatar — channeling the primal force of the wild directly into warrior form, fighting with elemental fury and growing more powerful the longer the battle rages.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Primal Strike      | Attack   | Channel nature's force into a melee strike: deals heavy physical damage and applies a 3s nature mark (subsequent Verdant strikes against the marked target deal +15% damage). 5s cooldown.
  - Wild Charge        | Movement | Charge forward through enemies in a straight line: deal moderate damage to each enemy hit and burst through their formation without stopping. 12s cooldown.
  - Nature's Endurance | Passive/Shield  | Your connection to the wild sustains you: at the start of combat, passively gain 10% bonus max HP. For every 30s of sustained combat, gain an additional 5% max HP (temporary, stacks 3x for max +25%).

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Grapple and Slam   | Attack   | Grab an enemy and slam them into the ground: heavy damage + 1.5s Stun. The impact leaves a patch of churned earth that counts as Difficult Terrain for enemies for 8s. 18s cooldown.
  - Bark Skin          | Buff/Defense     | Channel the resilience of an ancient oak: gain 25% damage reduction for 10s. While active, your HP regenerates at 3% per second. 20s cooldown.
  - Primal Fury        | Passive  | Your melee attacks generate Fury stacks (1 per hit, max 10). At max Fury, your next strike releases the stored primal energy: bonus damage equal to 10% max HP per Fury stack consumed (100% max HP bonus damage) and resets to 0. Stack decay: 2 per second while not hitting.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Verdant Form       | Transform | Enter Verdant Form for 15s: grow in size by 20%, gain +30% damage and +20% speed. Your attacks leave living patches of grass and earth that gradually slow enemies and speed up allies walking through them. 40s cooldown.
  - Thunderclap        | Attack   | Clap both hands together with thunderous force: 8m-radius shockwave knocking all enemies outward. Those hit mid-charge (while using movement skills) take 50% bonus damage. 25s cooldown.
  - Ancient Resilience | Reactive/Heal  | When you are reduced to 30% HP or below, immediately trigger a Resilience Burst: regenerate 20% of your max HP instantly and gain 2s of CC immunity. Once per 60s.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Call of the Wild   | Summon   | Call two Wild Companions — a wolf (fast, flanking, applies a 15% slow on hit) and a bear (slow, tanky, taunts nearby enemies). Both persist for 20s and fight autonomously at your side. 60s cooldown.
  - World Breaker      | Attack   | Deliver a single devastating strike into the earth: create a fissure 15m long in a line, dealing massive damage to all enemies in the line and creating impassable terrain for 8s. The fissure glows with primal energy — allies who jump over it heal for 10% max HP. 50s cooldown.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Avatar of the Wild | Transform | Fully merge with primal nature for 25s: triple your current size, double all stats (HP, damage, speed, armor), and gain access to three legendary primal attacks — Roar (15m silence + fear CC for 3s), Root Grasp (chain-roots all enemies in 10m for 3s), and Sky Smash (leap into the air and crash down dealing 80% max HP AoE damage). Your two Wild Companions become empowered versions during this time. On expiry you return to normal size and take 15% of your max HP as reversion cost. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Verdant Champion:
>> Verdant Champion is the simplest of the three archetypes to understand and the most satisfying to play — it's a nature-warrior who hits things very hard and grows stronger over time. Primal Fury at T2 is the core skill-expression loop: build stacks by attacking, release for massive burst, repeat. Nature's Endurance's time-scaling stacks dovetail beautifully with Ancient Resilience — you survive long enough to get stronger rather than dying early. World Breaker at T4 is a standout: creating impassable terrain that also heals allies who cross it is spatially interesting. Avatar of the Wild gives access to three legendary attacks during the window — players will have 25s to learn which order to use them in for maximum effect.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON WARDEN OF THE WILD]
>
> Warden of the Wild succeeds at being the quintessential "nature protector" class without being clichéd by giving each archetype a distinct relationship to nature: Thornguard treats nature as a weapon that punishes, Root Network treats nature as infrastructure that empowers, and Verdant Champion embodies nature as raw power. The three archetypes have good role clarity (reactive defender, utility support, primal melee) and could function well together in a mixed-team scenario. The main design concern is Root Network's Emergency Burrow + Root Transit creating near-unlimited teleportation — recommend a shared cooldown between the two or a per-fight use limit. Verdant Champion's Avatar of the Wild with three sub-skills during a 25s window needs a clear UI indicator showing which primal attacks are available and ready.
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 96: Echomancer (Sound/Memory)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A manipulator of the echoes that reality leaves behind, the Echomancer reaches into the recent past to replay actions that have already happened, bends sound waves into physical architecture and weaponry, and calls forth the spectral echoes of great warriors from ancient battles — commanding ghost-memories that fight with the skills of legends long dead.

ARCHETYPE A: Replay (Echo Past Actions, Time Repeat, Action Reverb)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Record moments of combat as they happen and replay them — duplicating past attacks, rewinding ally mistakes, and creating layered time echoes that overlap the present with the near past.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Echo Strike        | Active   | Replay your last attack: deal 60% of its damage again at the same target, at the same location, with the same area shape. If the target has moved, the echo hits where they were and may miss. 8s cooldown.
  - Rewind Step        | Reactive | Record your current position. Within 4s, activate to instantly rewind to it — restoring HP you lost and resetting any debuffs applied in that window. Cannot rewind further than the last use. 20s cooldown.
  - Passive Recording  | Passive/Utility  | Passively record the last 5s of your movement and attacks on a rolling buffer. This feeds into skills that require recorded data.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Double Echo        | Active   | Replay the last 2 seconds of all your attacks simultaneously as a burst: every hit you landed in the past 2s repeats at 50% damage at the same positions. 20s cooldown.
  - Rewind Ally        | Heal   | Target an ally: restore their position and HP to where they were 3s ago and Cleanse all debuffs applied since then. The ally's skills are not reset (no cooldown manipulation). 30s cooldown.
  - Loop               | Passive/Utility  | When an Echo skill triggers, 20% chance it automatically triggers again immediately (once per original cast maximum). Only activates when the echo would hit a valid target.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Triecho Burst      | Active   | Replay the last 4s of your attacks three times in rapid sequence, each repetition at 40% damage. If all three echoes hit the same target, the third echo detonates for 80% bonus damage. 30s cooldown.
  - Time Stamp         | Active   | Mark a moment in time: up to 20s later, you may activate this skill to replay everything that happened to you in the 3s after the mark (your attacks, position changes, all echoed at 70% fidelity). 40s cooldown.
  - Resonant Memory    | Passive  | Each consecutive Echo skill you use within 8s adds 5% potency to the next (stacks up to 40%). Reset if you go 8s without using an Echo skill.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Combat Replay      | Channel  | Channel 1.5s to replay the last 10s of the entire combat: every attack made by you and your allies within the past 10s repeats simultaneously at 45% damage. All replays resolve at once — can be extremely chaotic in extended fights. 90s cooldown.
  - Perfect Memory     | Passive/Utility  | Your Rewind Step now records the last 8s (up from 4s) and can be activated at any point within that full window. Additionally, when you rewind, the position you left behind becomes an Echo ghost that continues fighting for 3s before dissipating.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Infinite Echo  | Toggle   | Toggle on: enter a persistent echo loop for 20s. Every skill you use is automatically echoed 2s later at 70% power with no additional cost. Every skill echoed is again echoed at 2s delay at 50% power (a second generation echo). Second generation echoes do not chain further. You hear a growing reverb as echoes stack. Toggle off or 20s expiry ends the loop; all pending echoes fire simultaneously. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Replay:
>> Replay is the most temporally complex archetype in the class — it requires players to think about what they did rather than what they will do. Rewind Ally is the standout support skill: "undo" an ally's death or bad position is enormously powerful and feels magical. Time Stamp at T3 is the most skill-expressive skill in the archetype: mark a good moment, fight for 20s, then replay it for free — massively rewarding spatial memory. The Infinite Echo capstone creates a beautiful audio build-up: players will hear the reverb deepen as layers stack. Key balance concern: Combat Replay at 45% efficiency over 10s of a high-damage fight could be extremely powerful — recommend hard-capping individual hit replays at 200% of the replayed target's max HP total.
>>


ARCHETYPE B: Acoustic Shaping (Sound as Physical Force, Waveform Control)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Sculpt sound waves into physical objects — barriers, projectiles, crushing waves of pressure — treating acoustic energy as both a construction material and a devastating weapon.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Sonic Spike        | Attack   | Launch a condensed sound spike: deals moderate damage and applies a 2s Silence (the target's vocal cords vibrate too intensely to form words). 6s cooldown.
  - Soundwall          | Active   | Construct a solid wall of sound energy (8m wide, 3m tall): blocks projectiles and slows enemies who move through it by 40%. Lasts 8s before the sound dissipates. 15s cooldown.
  - Resonant Frequency | Reactive  | Your sound attacks apply a Resonance stack (max 5) on each hit. At 5 stacks, the target suffers a Resonance Burst: Stunned for 1.5s and takes 20% of their max HP as damage as their body vibrates apart.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Compression Wave   | Attack   | Release a wide cone of compressed sound (12m cone, 60 degrees wide): deals moderate AoE damage and knocks all enemies in the cone back 5m. 15s cooldown.
  - Acoustic Dome      | Buff     | Construct a dome of sound energy (12m radius) around a point: inside the dome, all ally sound-based skills deal +30% damage, and all enemy skills using verbal components (CC shouts, magic words) are Silenced. Lasts 12s. 30s cooldown.
  - Frequency Shift    | Debuff   | Alter the frequency of one enemy's acoustic resonance: they take 20% more damage from all your attacks and 10% more from all sources. Applied Resonance stacks add 2% bonus damage each while Frequency Shifted. 20s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Brown Note         | CC       | Emit a deeply uncomfortable subsonic frequency: all enemies in 10m radius are Stunned for 2s and Silenced for a further 3s as their inner ears are disrupted. 35s cooldown.
  - Acoustic Mirror    | Reactive | Construct a parabolic sound mirror: the next projectile attack aimed at you or any ally in 15m is reflected back at the attacker at 120% of its original damage. Lasts 6s or one reflection. 25s cooldown.
  - Shattering Resonance | Active | Target an enemy with maximum Resonance stacks: instead of a normal Resonance Burst, trigger a Shattering Resonance — 15m radius AoE explosion dealing 40% of that enemy's max HP to all nearby enemies (the enemy themselves take double). 30s cooldown per use, requires 5 stacks on target.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Sonic Fortress     | Active   | Deploy a full acoustic battlefield structure for 20s: four Soundwalls arranged in a diamond shape, an Acoustic Dome covering the interior, and Acoustic Mirrors on each wall face. Allies inside take 30% less damage. Enemies attempting to enter take 15% max HP damage. 80s cooldown.
  - Waveform Mastery   | Passive/Utility  | All Resonance stacks now apply to enemies in a 4m radius of the target (splash resonance). You may maintain Resonance on up to 4 enemies simultaneously. Soundwalls now deal damage (8% max HP) to enemies who pass through them rather than just slowing.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Great Silence  | Active   | Release the full spectrum of sound simultaneously: for 10s, emit a continuous multi-frequency pulse from your body. In a 20m radius all enemies are simultaneously Silenced, Slowed by 30%, and take 6% max HP sound damage every second. Every 2s, one random enemy in range is also hit by a Shattering Resonance-level burst regardless of Resonance stacks. Allies in range are immune to the Silence. After 10s, the sound collapses into a total silence — 2s of absolute quiet where no skill on the field may be cast by anyone, followed by the fight resuming normally. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Acoustic Shaping:
>> Acoustic Shaping is the most "terrain control" heavy archetype in the class — Soundwalls, Acoustic Dome, and Sonic Fortress all modify the battlefield physically. The Resonance stack system creates a satisfying buildup-and-burst loop. Brown Note is unavoidably funny but mechanically solid. Acoustic Mirror's reflect mechanic rewards anticipation over reaction — you place it before you know what's coming, not after. Sonic Fortress at T4 is the archetype's peak fantasy: building an actual acoustic castle mid-combat. The Great Silence's "2s of absolute quiet where nothing can be cast" is the most dramatic capstone mechanic in the class — a literal moment of enforced stillness.
>>


ARCHETYPE C: Memory Echo (Summon Past Warriors, Ghost Combat, Legacy Power)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Pull the echo-memories of legendary warriors from past battles and summon them as spectral combatants who fight with the skills and strategies that made them legendary — growing more powerful the more history you've witnessed in the current fight.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Echo Soldier       | Summon   | Summon a generic Echo Soldier: a spectral warrior who mimics basic melee attacks. Deals moderate damage. Lasts 20s. Up to 2 active simultaneously. 15s cooldown.
  - Memory Imprint     | Passive/Utility  | When an ally uses a skill near you (within 10m), store it as a Memory Imprint (max 3 stored). Your Echo Soldiers use stored imprints as additional attacks.
  - Spectral Recall    | Active   | Choose one expired ally skill from Memory Imprint storage: an Echo Soldier uses it immediately at 70% effectiveness, consuming the stored imprint. 10s cooldown.

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Echo Knight        | Summon   | Summon a stronger Echo Knight: a spectral heavy warrior with high HP and a shield-bash CC (knocks down for 1.5s every 10s). Costs 2 summon slots. Lasts 25s. 25s cooldown.
  - Combat Memory      | Passive/Utility  | As enemies use skills near you during this fight, store them as Combat Memories (max 2 stored). Your Echo Soldiers may use stored enemy skills against that same enemy at 60% effectiveness.
  - Strengthen the Echo| Active   | Pour your own energy into one summoned Echo: grant it +40% damage, +30% HP, and a 5s duration extension. 20s cooldown.

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Echo of the Fallen | Summon   | If an ally has died this combat, summon their Echo — a spectral version of that ally with 50% of their stats and access to 2 of their skills. The Echo fights autonomously for 20s. 60s cooldown.
  - Legacy Strike      | Active   | Channel one of your stored Memory Imprints directly as your own attack (rather than through an Echo): use it at 90% effectiveness as if you had cast it yourself. 20s cooldown, consumes the stored imprint.
  - Battlefield Memory | Passive  | The longer you are in combat, the more you remember: every 15s of combat, your Echo Soldiers gain +5% to all stats (max +25% at 75s). This bonus persists until the end of combat.

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Call a Legend      | Summon   | Summon a specific Legendary Echo chosen at skill selection time (chosen from a roster of 5 historical warrior types — Berserker, Paladin, Archmage, Shadow Dancer, Beastlord). The Legend fights at full combat capability for 15s, using 3 skills appropriate to their type. 90s cooldown.
  - Echo Army          | Passive/Utility  | Your summon slot cap increases to 6 (from 2). All summoned Echoes gain a Cohesion bonus when 3 or more are active simultaneously: +15% damage, +10% HP, and they coordinate attacks — if two Echoes target the same enemy simultaneously, they trigger a Coordinated Strike for 50% bonus combined damage.

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Grand Remembrance | Channel | Channel 2s. Summon every Echo and Memory available to you simultaneously: all 6 summon slots are filled with Echoes drawn from stored Memory Imprints, Combat Memories, any fallen allies, and supplemented with Echo Soldiers to fill remaining slots. All Echoes summoned this way are strengthened by Strengthen the Echo for free, receive Battlefield Memory bonuses at maximum (+25%), and the Cohesion bonus applies to all of them. They fight for 30s. When they expire, each Echo leaves behind a Memory Shard — a static spectral image of their final striking pose that one time deals their strongest attack against the nearest enemy when triggered by enemy proximity within 5m. 180s cooldown.

>> Your notes on Memory Echo:
>> Memory Echo is the most narratively rich archetype in the class — summoning the Echo of a Fallen Ally is genuinely poignant and mechanically powerful. Combat Memory is the standout passive: storing enemy skills and using them against the enemy creates wonderful "turned their own weapon against them" moments. Call a Legend at T4 is the archetype's power fantasy peak — summoning a Berserker or Archmage echo from history is a dramatic moment. The Grand Remembrance capstone is appropriately chaotic: a full army of ghosts for 30s, leaving behind Memory Shards that haunt the battlefield afterward. The Memory Shard mechanic in the capstone is worth careful tuning — five to six shards each triggering on enemy proximity could create enormous burst.
>>


[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON ECHOMANCER]
>
> Echomancer is the most conceptually unified class in this batch: all three archetypes are about "things that were" — Replay echoes past actions, Acoustic Shaping creates physical shapes from invisible sound energy, and Memory Echo summons the ghosts of past warriors. The class has a melancholy, haunted aesthetic that would distinguish it visually from every other class. The main design challenge is Replay's rolling buffer system requiring robust game-state recording infrastructure — every attack, position, and HP value must be stored for the past 10s at minimum. This is a significant technical ask. Combat Replay at T4 (replaying 10s of all allies' attacks) is the most computationally intensive skill in this entire document — recommend a stress-test with 5+ allies in a dense-combat scenario. Memory Echo's Echo of the Fallen is the most emotionally resonant design in the entire class list; it should be given prominent visual treatment.
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 97: Plague Marine (Sci-fi Bio-Weapon)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A post-human soldier whose body has been weaponized at the cellular level, exhaling toxic clouds, seeding the battlefield with engineered contagions, and locking down entire sectors through biological contamination that renders zones uninhabitable without proper protection.

ARCHETYPE A: Biohazard (Toxic Aura, Gas Weaponry, Zone Contamination)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Saturates the environment with persistent toxic gases and auras that punish enemies for occupying the same space, turning the battlefield itself into a weapon.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Vent Toxins         | Aura     | Continuously exhale a 4m cloud of corrosive gas dealing minor damage per second to all enemies within; cloud drifts with you
  - Contaminate Zone    | Active   | Mark a 6m radius area as a biohazard zone for 20s; enemies inside take escalating poison damage per second they remain
  - Hazmat Adaptation   | Passive/Immunity  | You are immune to all poison, gas, and biological debuffs; allied contact with your toxic aura deals 30% reduced damage to them

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Acid Fog Grenade    | Attack   | Hurl a canister that erupts into a 8m acid fog cloud for 15s; enemies inside have armor reduced by 20% and take ongoing damage
  - Lingering Spore     | Active   | Spray a target with adhesive bio-spores; they emit a personal toxic cloud for 12s that follows them and affects nearby allies of the enemy
