  - Atmospheric Bleed   | Passive/Utility  | Your Contaminate Zone clouds persist for 10s after their duration ends as diminishing hazard patches; enemies stepping on patches are briefly poisoned

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Nerve Gas Burst     | Debuff   | Release a 10m radius nerve agent burst; enemies are disoriented, losing 25% accuracy and 20% movement speed for 10s
  - Stack Poison        | Debuff   | Inject a target with a stacking bioagent; each of your subsequent attacks adds a poison stack (max 8), each stack adding 3% more damage per second
  - Aura Surge          | Active   | Briefly expand your toxic aura to 12m radius and double its damage output for 6s; enemies caught in the surge are also slowed by 20%

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Quarantine Fog      | Active   | Flood a 15m radius with an opaque toxic fog for 20s; enemies inside are blinded, continuously poisoned, and cannot call for reinforcements or trigger alerts
  - Compound Toxin      | Debuff   | Expose a target to a compound bioagent that interacts with any other poison on them; if they carry 3+ stacks from any source, those stacks detonate for 400% of their remaining damage instantly

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Dead Zone Protocol  | Active   | Designate a 25m radius as a full biological exclusion zone for 30s; within the zone all enemy healing is negated, poison effects deal double damage and cannot be cleansed, your aura triples in potency, and any enemy who dies within the zone releases their own toxic burst infecting all nearby enemies — the zone also leaves a permanent low-level hazard patch after expiration that lasts for the rest of the encounter

>> Your notes on Biohazard:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Strain Alpha (Engineered Plague, Custom Disease, Adaptive Pathogen)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Engineers bespoke super-diseases tailored to specific targets, building custom plagues mid-combat that mutate and adapt to overcome resistances.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Culture Sample      | Active   | Analyze an enemy, identifying their biological weak points; your next disease skill against them is 25% more effective and gains a bonus effect
  - Basic Pathogen      | Debuff   | Infect a target with a standard pathogen dealing moderate damage over 15s; pathogen can spread on contact with other enemies at 50% potency
  - Adaptive Cell       | Passive/Utility  | Your diseases gain a mutation charge each time a target resists or cleanses them; the next disease you apply ignores that resistance type

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Hemorrhagic Fever   | Debuff   | Infect a target with a fever that reduces their max HP by 15% and causes them to lose additional HP whenever they use an active ability for 12s
  - Neurological Blight | Debuff   | Infect a target with a neural pathogen; they randomly misfire abilities (30% chance each use) and suffer reduced reaction speed for 10s
  - Cross-Strain        | Active   | Combine two active diseases on the same target into a hybrid strain; the hybrid deals the total damage of both and gains the secondary effect of each

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Plague Strain Alpha | Debuff   | Deploy your signature engineered plague; it deals heavy damage over 20s, reduces all stats by 15%, and mutates every 5s to add a new random debuff effect
  - Contagion Vector    | Reactive  | Enemies killed while infected by your custom diseases release an airborne version of the strain; up to 3 nearby enemies are automatically infected at 75% potency
  - Override Immunity   | Active   | Supercharge a disease with a virulence booster; it bypasses all poison resistance and cleansing effects for 8s, making it impossible to remove during that window

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Organ Failure | Debuff   | Administer a catastrophic multi-system pathogen; over 15s the target progressively loses 10% of each stat every 3s until all stats are reduced by 50% — removing the disease reverses this but leaves a 10s immunity window where all your diseases deal 50% extra damage to that target
  - Evolutionary Pressure | Reactive | Your diseases that have been cleansed once evolve; the next application of that same disease type deals 50% more damage and applies an additional CC effect based on the cleansing method used against it

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Extinction Event Strain | Debuff | Synthesize and release the ultimate engineered pathogen against a single target for 25s; the strain begins mild but doubles in all effects every 5s — by 25s it reduces all stats by 80%, deals massive damage per second, and causes all of the target's outgoing abilities to backfire on themselves at 30% effectiveness; if the target dies while carrying the strain it detonates into a mass-infection event, spreading a 60% potency version to every enemy on the battlefield simultaneously, none of which can be cleansed for 10s

>> Your notes on Strain Alpha:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Quarantine (Area Denial, Lockdown, Containment Field)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Seals off sections of the battlefield using biological containment protocols, preventing enemies from moving freely and denying access to key positions.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Biowire Perimeter   | Active   | Deploy 3 bio-sensor wire nodes that form a 10m triangle; enemies crossing the perimeter lines take minor damage and are briefly slowed
  - Seal Exit           | CC       | Target a doorway, corridor, or passage up to 4m wide with a bio-foam seal; the passage is blocked to enemies for 12s
  - Containment Protocol| Passive/Defense  | Enemies inside your designated zones move 15% slower and have 10% reduced damage as the biological pressure affects them psychologically

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Lockdown Beacon     | Active   | Plant a beacon that emits a 12m lockdown field for 20s; enemies inside cannot use movement abilities (dashes, teleports, leaps) and are revealed
  - Bio-Barrier Wall    | Active   | Erect a 8m long bio-contamination wall; it blocks movement and deals moderate damage to enemies who attempt to pass through it for 15s
  - Panic Dispersal     | Debuff   | Trigger an enemy group's containment fear response; enemies in a 10m radius have a 40% chance to move away from you rather than toward you for 8s

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Full Sector Seal    | Active   | Declare a 20m radius as a quarantine sector for 25s; enemies inside cannot leave (exit attempts are physically rebuffed), reinforcements cannot enter, and you gain a notification when enemies inside attempt to use comms
  - Stasis Foam         | CC       | Fire a fast-spreading bio-foam projectile; on impact it hardens around the target, rooting them completely for 4s and reducing their armor by 25% while encased
  - Decontamination Denial | Active | Designate a 15m zone where all healing is suppressed by 60% and cleanses fail; the zone is invisible to enemies until they attempt to heal inside it

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Isolation     | Active   | Encase a single enemy in a personal quarantine bubble for 8s; they are completely unable to act, take 20% of any damage their allies take within 15m of them (transferred to the bubble), and when the bubble pops it deals all transferred damage as an explosion
  - Grid Lock           | Active   | Simultaneously activate all your deployed zone effects and beacons at triple potency for 10s; their radii expand by 50% and all enemies currently inside take a burst of damage equal to 3s of zone damage instantly

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Planetary Quarantine| Active   | Issue the highest biological containment directive for 35s; the entire battlefield (up to 40m radius) becomes a sealed quarantine zone — enemies cannot flee, receive no external support, have all healing reduced by 80%, lose 5% of max HP each time they attempt to cross zone boundaries, and your Contaminate Zones and Lockdown Beacons refresh their durations continuously; at the end of the quarantine, any enemy below 30% HP is automatically suppressed (stunned for 5s) by containment protocols engaging

>> Your notes on Quarantine:
>>
>>

################################################################################
CLASS 98: Time Thief (Temporal Rogue)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A slippery criminal who steals from time itself — lifting cooldowns off enemies before they've fired, snatching away their next move at the last moment, and spending future seconds like borrowed gold, knowing the debt always comes due but betting the fight will be over before it does.

ARCHETYPE A: Stolen Seconds (Cooldown Theft, Duration Piracy, Timing Disruption)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Pickpockets enemy cooldowns and active buff durations, adding stolen time to their own abilities while leaving enemies stranded without their best tools.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Cooldown Lift       | Active   | Target an enemy; identify their longest active cooldown and reduce it by 8s while adding 4s to your own chosen ability's cooldown reduction
  - Duration Snip       | Debuff   | Clip 5s from any beneficial buff currently on an enemy; those stolen seconds extend a buff of your choice on yourself by 3s
  - Light Fingers       | Passive/Utility  | Each time you steal time from an enemy, gain a Tempo stack (max 6); each stack reduces your own cooldowns by 1s on the next ability you use

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Grand Larceny       | Active   | Steal the remaining duration of a single enemy buff entirely, removing it from them and applying it to yourself at 50% duration
  - Cooldown Spike      | Debuff   | Artificially extend an enemy's cooldown by 10s; they are unaware of the change until they attempt to use the ability
  - Fence the Time      | Passive/Utility  | Stolen cooldown reductions and stolen buff durations are 20% more efficient; you extract more value from every temporal theft

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Mass Cooldown Heist | Active   | In a 10m radius, strip 6s from all enemy cooldowns simultaneously, redistributing those stolen seconds as 2s reductions across all your own active cooldowns
  - Duration Laundering | Active   | Collect all Tempo stacks and convert them into a single large cooldown reduction on one ability; each stack converts to 2.5s of reduction instead of 1s
  - Invisible Take      | Passive/Utility  | Your cooldown thefts and duration steals do not generate threat or alert enemies; they remain unaware of the mechanic for 3s after each theft

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Ability Lockout     | Debuff   | Seize an enemy's cooldown system entirely for 8s; all of their abilities are placed on a flat 8s cooldown regardless of their natural timers, effectively locking them to basic attacks only
  - Chrono Heist        | Active   | Steal all remaining buff durations from all buffed enemies in a 12m radius simultaneously; convert the stolen duration into a single powerful 15s buff package that adds 20% to all your stats

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Great Robbery   | Active   | Execute the ultimate temporal heist for 20s; every cooldown tick that passes for any enemy on the battlefield simultaneously ticks down your own cooldowns at double rate — enemies feel their abilities "draining" before they fire while you cycle through your own kit at twice normal speed; all Tempo stacks are converted to permanent cooldown reduction for this encounter (1 stack = 3% CDR), and any ability an enemy fires during this window also briefly reduces its own cooldown for you specifically, as if you had just used it yourself

>> Your notes on Stolen Seconds:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Temporal Pickpocket (Action Theft, Pre-emptive Strike, Turn Stealing)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Intercepts and steals the specific action an enemy is about to take, firing it themselves or simply nullifying it before it can be used.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Read the Tell       | Passive/Utility  | Enemies telegraph their next action with a visible shimmer for 0.5s before executing; you can see this tell clearly, others cannot
  - Interrupt and Pocket| Reactive | When an enemy begins a high-value ability (channel, charge-up, or transform), interrupt it and gain a 6s window to use a weaker version of that ability yourself
  - Stolen Initiative   | Active   | Force an enemy to act last in the current round, delaying their turn to the very end; you act first instead regardless of your speed stat

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Copycat Strike      | Reactive | When an enemy lands an attack on you, immediately counter with an identical copy of that attack against them at 70% damage; triggered automatically
  - Pre-emptive Lift    | Active   | Declare a specific enemy ability; if they attempt to use it in the next 8s, you intercept and nullify it, gaining a 50% power copy of the skill for 10s
  - Action Vacuum       | Debuff   | Surround a target with a temporal void for 4s; any action they attempt during this window is absorbed and lost — they waste their action and nothing happens

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Mirror Heist        | Active   | After interrupting any enemy ability, retain a perfect copy of it at full power for 12s and use it as your own, even if you normally couldn't learn it
  - Stolen Finisher     | Reactive | If an enemy is about to use a finishing or execute-style move, intercept it entirely; you use it on a target of your choice at full power instead
  - Echo Chamber        | Passive/Utility  | Stolen abilities can be used twice before expiring; the second use always applies to a secondary target for 60% of the first use's value

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Full Action Theft   | Active   | Completely steal one enemy's next full action; they skip their turn and instead you gain an additional full turn of your own immediately, usable with any ability
  - Precognition Pocket | Reactive  | You passively predict and intercept one enemy ability per 20s automatically — the interception happens before the ability fires, the stolen action is stored as a free-cast usable within 15s

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Temporal Identity Theft | Transform | For 20s, steal an enemy's entire action economy: you gain access to their full ability kit at 80% power, their passive benefits at full value, and their turn priority — while they are left with access only to your baseline attacks and none of their own abilities; during this window you effectively play as both yourself and your target simultaneously, and any ability you fire from their stolen kit also removes that ability from their kit for 5s after the transformation ends, ensuring they cannot immediately retaliate with their best skills

>> Your notes on Temporal Pickpocket:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Borrowed Time (Future Power, Debt Accumulation, Pay Later)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Draws power forward from their own future, using abilities beyond their current level in exchange for growing debts that punish them later — gambling that the fight ends before the bill arrives.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Advance             | Active   | Borrow 10s of future cooldown; your next ability fires immediately regardless of its current cooldown, but that ability's cooldown is extended by 20s afterward
  - Minor Debt          | Passive/Utility  | Borrowed Time abilities cost Debt tokens (max 10); you begin each encounter with 0 Debt; at 5+ Debt, you take 5% increased damage; at 10 Debt, all your cooldowns increase by 2s
  - Speculative Strike  | Attack   | Attack with power drawn from a future version of yourself; deals 130% normal damage now but reduces your damage by 10% for the next 10s as the future-you is slightly weakened

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Credit Line         | Buff/Risk   | Briefly access a burst of future-self stats: gain +25% to all primary stats for 8s, but afterward suffer -15% to all primary stats for 10s as debt is repaid
  - Time Loan           | Heal     | Borrow HP from your future self; immediately restore 25% of your max HP now, but your max HP is reduced by 15% for 20s as the loan matures
  - Debt Shuffle        | Active   | Redistribute your current Debt; reduce Debt by 3 now but add 20s to the duration of your next Debt-penalty phase when it triggers

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Overdraft           | Active   | Push your Debt past the limit (can exceed 10 for 15s); all your abilities fire at 150% power and all cooldowns are halved, but when Debt resets at the end you take a burst of damage equal to 5% of max HP per Debt token accrued above 10
  - Futures Contract    | Active     | Lock in current buff effects for a future payout; choose an active buff on yourself — it expires now but will automatically reapply at 200% duration after 15s
  - Debt Collector      | Reactive  | Enemies who damage you while you carry 7+ Debt tokens take 15% reflected damage; your suffering becomes a weapon as the pressure of debt sharpens your reflexes

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Accelerated Futures | Active   | Draw 30s of ability power from the future simultaneously; all your abilities come off cooldown instantly and you gain 20% increased damage for 10s, then enter a Debt Crisis where your cooldowns are doubled and you deal 20% reduced damage for 15s as reality balances the books
  - Debt Forgiveness    | Active   | Negotiate with the timestream; remove all current Debt tokens and eliminate all active debt penalties, but this ability itself has a 60s cooldown and leaves you unable to accumulate Debt benefits (borrowed power has 0 bonus effect) for 20s

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Temporal Bankruptcy | Transform | Declare total temporal bankruptcy for 25s; spend every Debt token and every future second you have left in this fight recklessly — you become an uncapped powerhouse with no cooldowns, 50% bonus damage, and access to every ability simultaneously, but you are fully aware that when the 25s ends you enter Debt Reckoning: all your cooldowns lock for 15s, your stats drop by 30%, and you take a lump damage hit equal to 8% of max HP per Debt token spent; the gamble is real and irreversible, and every second of the Reckoning ticks down with absolute certainty

>> Your notes on Borrowed Time:
>>
>>

[YOUR OVERALL NOTES ON TIME THIEF]
>
>
>


################################################################################
CLASS 99: Magnet Knight (Metal Warrior)
################################################################################
Fantasy: An armored warrior who has turned magnetism into a martial art, pulling enemies off their feet and into their blade, hurling them back with enough force to shatter walls, and playing opposing polarities against each other until the entire battlefield is a chaos of involuntary motion.

ARCHETYPE A: Attract (Magnetic Pull, Projectile Catch, Iron Grip)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Draws enemies, weapons, and projectiles toward the Magnet Knight magnetically, controlling positioning and stripping ranged threats while building up for devastating close-range payoffs.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Magnetic Grip       | Active   | Extend your magnetic field; pull any metal-equipped enemy within 12m toward you by 4m against their will
  - Projectile Catch    | Reactive | Automatically catch all metal projectiles fired at you within 4m, negating their damage and storing them for a single use as your own thrown attack
  - Iron Draw           | Aura  | Enemies wearing metal armor are 10% slower when moving away from you as your passive field exerts drag; this effect doubles if they are also carrying metal weapons

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Yank               | Active   | Violently pull a single enemy from up to 18m directly to your location; they arrive staggered and take moderate impact damage
  - Disarm Pull         | Active   | Strip a metal weapon or shield from an enemy's grip within 10m; the item flies to your hand — you can use it or have it float threateningly in your orbit for 10s
  - Lodestone Stance    | Stance   | Enter a magnetic lodestone stance; all metal objects within 8m slowly drift toward you, and enemies in metal armor are pulled 1m toward you each second

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Mass Pull           | Active   | Simultaneously pull all metal-equipped enemies within 15m toward you by 6m; enemies are dragged past each other causing minor collision damage as they clump
  - Caught Round        | Reactive | When you catch a projectile, you may immediately refire it at full power at any target within range as a free action; stored caught rounds can be released all at once as a burst
  - Singularity Point   | Active   | Create a powerful magnetic singularity at a point within 12m for 5s; all metal objects and metal-equipped enemies within 10m of the point are dragged toward it continuously

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Vortex Pull         | Active   | Create a 10m radius magnetic vortex for 8s; everything metal is dragged toward the center at high speed, colliding with each other for heavy damage and leaving enemies clumped and stunned for 2s at the center
  - Total Disarmament   | Active   | Strip all metal gear simultaneously from every enemy within 12m; weapons, shields, and armor pieces all rip free and orbit you harmlessly for 10s before clattering to the ground at your feet

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Gravitational Collapse | Active | Collapse your entire magnetic field inward for 3s of charging, then release in a devastating pull event affecting a 30m radius; every metal-equipped enemy is slammed toward you at extreme velocity, taking heavy impact damage on arrival and collision damage from each other, all their metal weapons and armor are ripped free and orbit you as a lethal debris field for 20s (dealing damage to nearby enemies), and the shockwave of displaced metal creates a 15m shrapnel burst around you that damages everything in range — you stand at the eye, untouched

>> Your notes on Attract:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Repulse (Knockback Field, Deflection, Force Push)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Weaponizes magnetic repulsion to send enemies flying, deflect attacks, and create zones of force that prevent enemies from approaching.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Magnetic Shove      | Active   | Release a repulsive magnetic burst in a 6m cone; enemies within are pushed back 5m and briefly staggered
  - Deflect Field       | Reactive | Raise a magnetic repulsion field for 2s; metal projectiles are deflected away from you at angles, potentially hitting other enemies
  - Push Aura           | Aura     | Maintain a low-level repulsive aura; any enemy who enters within 3m of you is automatically pushed back 2m (does not interrupt their actions, just repositions them)

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Force Launch        | Active   | Focus your repulsion on a single enemy; launch them backward 10m with tremendous force, dealing heavy damage if they impact a wall or obstacle
  - Repulsion Wave      | Active   | Emit a 360-degree repulsion pulse in a 10m radius; all enemies are sent flying outward and take landing damage based on distance traveled
  - Reactive Armor      | Reactive  | When you are struck in melee, automatically release a small repulsion burst on the attacker pushing them back 3m; does not require your action

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Wall Slam           | Active   | Launch a target toward a specific wall within 15m; the impact deals massive damage scaled by distance and briefly stuns them as they crumple
  - Sustained Push      | Channel  | Channel a continuous repulsion beam on an enemy for up to 4s; they are pushed steadily back and take continuous damage, unable to approach you
  - Deflection Mastery  | Passive/Utility  | Deflected projectiles now inherit extra force from your magnetic field; they travel faster than the original and deal 120% of the original projectile's damage to whatever they hit

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Polarity Reversal Burst | Active | Briefly reverse your field polarity then snap it back; the rapid reversal creates a two-stage effect — pull everything within 8m toward you in the first 0.5s, then blast it all outward at extreme speed, creating a collision burst for heavy damage
  - Repulsor Wall       | Active   | Erect an invisible 10m long magnetic repulsion wall for 12s; any enemy or projectile that contacts the wall is blasted back at double the approach speed

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Magnetic Supernova  | Active   | Overcharge your repulsion field to critical levels and release it in a single devastating event; in a 25m radius every enemy is launched at full speed away from you toward the nearest solid surface, taking distance-scaled impact damage plus wall-slam bonus damage, every metal projectile currently in the air is blasted away from the zone, and the residual repulsion field lingers for 15s pushing any enemy who attempts to re-enter the radius back at 50% force — you stand untouchable in the eye of your own storm, and any enemy who was airborne when they land suffers an additional 20% bonus damage from disorientation

>> Your notes on Repulse:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Polarize (Magnetic Tagging, North/South Attraction, Polarity Clash)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Tags enemies with opposing magnetic polarities, causing tagged enemies to involuntarily attract or repel each other in disruptive patterns the Magnet Knight exploits for crowd control and collisions.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - North Tag           | Debuff   | Mark an enemy with North polarity; they are slightly repelled from other North-tagged enemies and attracted to South-tagged ones
  - South Tag           | Debuff   | Mark an enemy with South polarity; they are attracted to North-tagged enemies and repelled from other South-tagged ones
  - Polarity Read       | Passive/Utility  | You can see all polarity tags at a glance and have a mental map of which enemies will attract or repel each other; tagging efficiency increases by 15%

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Snap Together       | Active   | Snap all North and South tagged enemies toward each other simultaneously; they collide at the midpoint, taking collision damage based on distance traveled
  - Force Apart         | Active   | Snap all same-polarity tagged enemies away from each other simultaneously; they fly apart in opposite directions and take impact damage on landing
  - Chain Polarity      | Reactive  | When a tagged enemy collides with another enemy (tagged or untagged), the collision transfers a temporary weaker polarity tag to the unstunned victim at 50% strength

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Magnetic Cage       | Active   | Tag 3-4 enemies alternating North and South in a ring pattern; their opposing polarities lock them in orbit around a central point for 8s, unable to move freely
  - Polarity Detonate   | Active   | Detonate all polarity tags simultaneously in a burst; every tagged enemy takes damage scaled by the number of opposing-polarity enemies within 8m of them
  - Overcharge Tag      | Debuff   | Apply an overcharged polarity tag to a target; their magnetic attraction/repulsion forces are doubled in strength and they visibly spark, warning nearby enemies who nonetheless cannot choose their own polarity

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Polar Lattice       | Active   | Tag up to 6 enemies in an alternating N/S/N/S lattice pattern; the lattice locks them into a grid formation, and damaging any single lattice enemy causes a chain shock through the lattice connections dealing 40% of the triggering damage to all others
  - Polarity Flip       | Active   | Instantly reverse all current polarity tags on every enemy; every attraction becomes repulsion and vice versa, causing immediate involuntary lurching and dealing whiplash damage to all tagged enemies

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Grand Alignment     | Active   | Impose complete magnetic order on the battlefield for 20s; all enemies are automatically tagged with alternating polarities based on their current position, and you gain direct control over the force direction — you can pull, push, spin, or hold every tagged enemy at will as a free action each round; during the alignment any collision deals double damage, any enemy pinned against a surface takes ongoing crush damage, and you may choose to simultaneously snap all opposites together or blast all same-polarities apart once per 5s as a world-altering magnetic event — at the end of 20s the magnetic exhaustion releases all enemies at once in a chaotic scatter

>> Your notes on Polarize:
>>
>>

################################################################################
CLASS 100: Scribe (Written Magic/Scroll)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A scholar who discovered that writing is not merely a record of reality but a blueprint for it — carrying a mobile library of prepared spell scrolls for precise burst deployment, breathing life into text as animated constructs, and editing the properties of objects and creatures as casually as revising a rough draft.

ARCHETYPE A: Scroll Keeper (Prepared Library, Burst Deploy, Scroll Stacking)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Maintains a prepared library of spell scrolls that can be deployed in rapid sequence as a burst, trading preparation for unmatched single-round firepower.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Prepare Scroll      | Active   | Scribe a spell scroll during or before combat from a known spell; prepared scrolls are stored and can be activated instantly with no cast time
  - Quick Deploy        | Active   | Activate one prepared scroll immediately; the spell fires without the normal casting delay, surprising enemies who had no warning sign
  - Scroll Mastery      | Passive/Utility  | You can carry up to 6 prepared scrolls simultaneously (base 3); prepared scrolls are 10% more potent than cast spells due to careful inscription

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Burst Deploy        | Active   | Activate up to 3 prepared scrolls in rapid sequence over 1.5s; each scroll fires in order with no gap between them, creating an overwhelming burst
  - Scroll Augment      | Active   | Before deploying a scroll, augment it with an additional rune; the scroll gains a bonus effect (choose from: wider AoE, longer duration, or 20% extra damage)
  - Efficient Inscription | Passive/Utility | Scroll preparation time is reduced by 40%; scrolls scribed in combat take only 1.5s instead of 3s and maintain full potency

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Grand Library       | Active   | Spend 10s outside of direct combat to prepare your full library of 6 scrolls at once; each scroll is automatically augmented with one bonus rune
  - Chain Scroll        | Active   | Design a scroll chain; fire all 6 prepared scrolls in 2s with each scroll's damage adding to a building multiplier (1st: 100%, 2nd: 110%, 3rd: 120%, up to 150% on 6th)
  - Scroll Duplicate    | Active   | Select a prepared scroll and duplicate it; you now have 2 copies of that scroll at 80% potency; the duplicate shares the original's augment

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Master Inscription  | Passive/Utility  | Your scrolls can now be inscribed with two augment runes each; additionally, scroll spells that share a theme (all damage, all control, etc.) grant a 15% bonus when deployed in sequence as a matching chain
  - Arcane Arsenal      | Active   | Draw on your full prepared library simultaneously; all 6 scrolls detonate at their targets in a 0.5s salvo, no sequencing required — a single overwhelming barrage of prepared magic

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Living Library  | Active   | Unroll your complete scroll library and let them all fly open simultaneously for 20s; every spell you have ever prepared exists in the air around you as floating active scrolls — you may deploy any of them at any time as a free action during this window, and each scroll resets to available every 5s; the library responds to your intent, and any scroll you fire also briefly inscribes itself onto the battlefield as a semi-permanent glyph that repeats its effect at 40% power once every 6s for the remaining duration

>> Your notes on Scroll Keeper:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Living Text (Text Constructs, Animated Words, Linguistic Entities)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Breathes life into written words and sentences, animating them as physical constructs that fight, defend, and carry out their literal meaning on the battlefield.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Animate Word        | Summon   | Write a word in the air (STRIKE, GUARD, or SEEK) and animate it; the glowing construct carries out its meaning as a basic ally for 15s
  - Scribed Shield      | Summon   | Write SHIELD in large letters between you and an attacker; the word physically blocks one hit and then crumbles into letter fragments
  - Linguistic Affinity | Passive/Utility  | Your animated constructs are 20% more durable and 15% more effective as the written word is imbued with greater literal force

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Sentence Golem      | Summon   | Write a full sentence describing a creature (e.g., "A guardian of stone with heavy fists"); the sentence animates into a golem matching its description in size and capability for 20s
  - Burning Letter      | Attack   | Hurl an inflamed letter or word at an enemy; FIRE deals burn damage, COLD deals freeze, SHOCK deals lightning — the element matches the word
  - Erase              | Active   | Literally erase a text construct from existence; if used on an enemy text construct it is destroyed, if used on your own injured one it is recalled and re-usable

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Paragraph Creature  | Summon   | Spend 4s writing a paragraph that animates into a powerful creature with multiple attributes defined by the paragraph's descriptors; longer sentences produce stronger, more capable constructs
  - Living Tome         | Summon   | Release an animated book that fights independently for 25s; it pages through its own contents and deploys a new damaging or buffing effect each round, cycling through a random selection
  - Word Wall           | Active   | Write WALL across a space up to 10m wide; the letters stand as a physical barrier for 15s that blocks movement and deals typographic damage to enemies who attempt to break through

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Manifesto           | Active   | Author a short manifesto declaring your constructs' purpose; all currently active text constructs are empowered by the declaration, gaining 40% stat increases and a new ability matching the manifesto's theme for 15s
  - The Sentence That Walks | Summon | Write a complete compound sentence about a legendary hero; the entire sentence animates as a single large, complex creature that fights for 30s with abilities defined by each clause of the sentence

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Authorial Command   | Active   | For 25s, become the author of the battlefield's reality through text; you write descriptions in real time and they manifest — write "The enemy stumbles" and they stumble, write "An ally regains strength" and they are healed, write "A pillar of fire rises here" and it rises; you can write and manifest one short sentence per 3s as a free action, each taking literal effect at 80% of its described magnitude; additionally all existing text constructs are upgraded to champion tier for this duration and follow authored orders, and enemies who can read are briefly stunned the first time they see a command become real

>> Your notes on Living Text:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Editor (Reality Rewriting, Property Change, Narrative Control)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Edits the properties of objects, enemies, and environments as if revising a manuscript, changing fundamental descriptors to alter what things are and how they behave.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Minor Edit          | Active   | Rewrite a small property of an object or enemy (change "sharp" to "blunt," "fast" to "slow," "armored" to "exposed"); the edit lasts 10s and affects one descriptor
  - Delete Attribute    | Debuff   | Strike through one of an enemy's active buffs or positive attributes; the deleted attribute ceases to function for 8s as if crossed out
  - Editorial Eye       | Passive/Utility  | You instantly perceive the most impactful descriptor of any enemy or object; minor edits to that descriptor are 30% more effective and easier to accomplish

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Revision            | Active   | Rewrite a moderately significant property: change an enemy's damage type, alter terrain from difficult to passable, change a locked door to unlocked, or flip a trap to inactive for 15s
  - Find and Replace    | Active   | Select a property currently present on multiple enemies (e.g., "fire resistance," "high morale," "armored"); replace it simultaneously across all enemies within 10m with an inferior version ("fire weakness," "low morale," "unarmored")
  - Track Changes       | Reactive  | When an enemy attempts to reverse or resist one of your edits, you are notified and may immediately apply a second edit as a free action to reinforce the first

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Major Revision      | Active   | Rewrite a significant property of an enemy for 20s: change "immune to stagger" to "easily staggered," change "regenerating" to "degenerating," or change "massive" to "small" with corresponding stat effects; the edit takes 2s to write but applies completely
  - Overwrite           | Active   | Replace an existing edit of yours on a target with a completely new stronger edit; the new property overwrites the old and gains bonus potency (+25%) from the rewrite
  - Draft Mode          | Toggle   | Toggle draft mode; while active all your edits apply instantly but expire in half the normal time, allowing rapid-fire property changes at the cost of permanence

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Chapter Break       | Active   | Insert a narrative chapter break into the battle; for 5s all enemies are frozen in a scene transition, unable to act, while you may reposition yourself freely, apply two free minor edits, and recover moderate HP as the "author" steps outside the narrative momentarily
  - Whole Cloth Rewrite | Active   | Completely rewrite one enemy's core identity for 15s; change their faction (they become temporarily friendly), change their role (a tank becomes a healer, a caster becomes melee), or change their element (fire becomes ice) — all their abilities shift to match the rewritten identity

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Final Draft         | Active   | Take authorial control of the battlefield narrative for 20s; you may apply edits to any number of entities simultaneously without restriction — there is no limit on the number or significance of changes you can make during this window; you can rewrite terrain, enemy stats, environmental hazards, your own properties, and even abstract concepts like "the ceiling's stability" or "the room's temperature"; each edit you apply leaves a permanent marginal note on the entity that permanently reduces their resistance to your future edits by 15%; at the end of the duration the narrative "saves" and all temporary edits either expire or are optionally made permanent at the cost of 10% of your max HP per permanent edit chosen

################################################################################
CLASS 101: Moth Priest (Cosmic Insect/Light)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A devotee of the vast dark between stars who has taken the moth as sacred symbol — spreading luminescent scale powders that blind and corrupt, retreating into sacred cocoons to emerge transformed, and calling down the cold light of distant suns to lure, trap, and obliterate what the dark has drawn in.

ARCHETYPE A: Dustwing (Scale Powder, Blind, Coating Debuffs)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Sheds luminescent wing scales as a persistent weapon, coating enemies in blinding powder that disrupts their senses and applies compounding debuff effects.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Scale Shed          | Active   | Release a cloud of iridescent scale powder in a 5m radius; enemies caught are coated, suffering 10% reduced accuracy for 12s
  - Blind Dust          | Debuff   | Fling a concentrated packet of blinding scale powder at a single enemy; they are fully blinded for 2s then suffer 20% reduced accuracy for 10s
  - Powder Coat         | Reactive  | Coated enemies who are hit by your attacks shed secondary scale clouds on impact, spreading a weaker coat (50% potency) to all enemies within 3m of them

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Confusing Shimmer   | Debuff   | Release scales with a hypnotic iridescent pattern; coated enemies have a 25% chance to target a random ally instead of their intended target for 10s
  - Adhesive Dust       | Debuff   | Apply a heavy adhesive scale coating to a target; they move 30% slower and any enemy that walks within 2m of them is also lightly coated (10% accuracy penalty)
  - Wing Flash          | Active   | Rapidly spread your wings, releasing a blinding flash of scale powder in a 180-degree arc; all enemies in a 6m semicircle are blinded for 1.5s

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Paralytic Powder    | Debuff   | Dust an enemy with neurotoxic scale extract; their movement speed is reduced by 50% and they cannot use movement abilities for 8s as the powder clogs their joints
  - Prismatic Coating   | Debuff   | Apply a prismatic scale coat that interacts with any light sources; coated enemies take 15% extra damage from light-element attacks and are treated as visible in darkness
  - Scale Accumulation  | Passive/Utility  | Each time you apply a coat to an already-coated enemy, the new coat's potency is increased by 20%; stacking coats combine their debuffs with no diminishing returns

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Total Coat          | Active   | Release a massive discharge of scales in a 12m radius; all enemies are coated with maximum-density powder simultaneously, applying blinding, slowing, and accuracy penalties at full potency in one application
  - Toxic Spore Burst   | Active   | Activate all coatings on all enemies simultaneously, causing them to react with the air and release toxic spore clouds from each coated enemy; each cloud deals moderate damage over 8s to the coated enemy and all within 4m of them

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Great Shedding  | Active   | Enter a 2s wing-spreading ritual and release your entire life's accumulation of sacred scale dust in a 20m radius; every enemy is coated in a divine multi-layer application that simultaneously blinds, paralyzes, confuses, slows, and poisons them — each effect at full potency; the scales glow with cosmic light and cannot be cleansed for 12s; for 20s afterward any enemy who takes damage while coated releases a secondary cloud of inherited scales from the impact, perpetuating the coating effect to nearby enemies indefinitely as long as combat continues; you are left temporarily uncoated but immune to all scale-based effects for the duration

>> Your notes on Dustwing:
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Cocoon (Transformation, Chrysalis Healing, Metamorphosis)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Enters sacred chrysalis states to heal, transform, and emerge with radically altered capabilities — each metamorphosis cycle bringing the Moth Priest closer to their final transcendent form.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Minor Cocoon        | Heal   | Wrap yourself in a gossamer cocoon for 2s; you cannot act but regenerate 15% HP and cleanse one debuff as a brief protected resting state
  - Larval Resilience   | Passive/Evasion  | While in any cocoon state you are immune to all damage and debuffs; outside of cocoon, taking damage has a 10% chance to automatically trigger a 0.5s micro-cocoon that negates the triggering hit
  - Chrysalis Sense     | Passive/Utility  | You sense the optimal moment to cocoon; receive a visual indicator when cocooning would cleanse your most dangerous current debuff with maximum efficiency

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Healing Chrysalis   | Heal   | Enter a full healing cocoon for 4s; you are protected and regenerate 35% of max HP; when you emerge, you shed the cocoon shell as a shrapnel burst dealing minor damage to nearby enemies
  - Metamorphic Step    | Movement   | Briefly enter and exit a micro-cocoon at a second location within 8m; you teleport to the exit point, emerge instantly, and deal a burst of wing-blade damage on arrival as an emergence strike
  - Partial Transform   | Buff     | Enter cocoon for 1.5s and emerge with one body part temporarily transformed (wings for 20% speed boost, claws for 30% melee damage, or compound eyes for 20% increased range and accuracy); lasts 20s

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Ally Cocoon         | Heal   | Wrap a willing ally in a protective cocoon for 3s; they are shielded from all damage and emerge healed for 25% of their max HP; the cocoon can also be placed on an injured ally mid-fall to cushion a killing blow
  - Greater Metamorphosis | Transform | Enter a 5s deep cocoon and emerge in a significantly altered form for 30s; choose from: Predator form (+40% all damage, gain dive attack), Healer form (aura heals allies for 3% HP/s), or Sage form (+35% all magic potency, reduced cocoon cooldowns by 50%)
  - Emergence Burst     | Reactive  | Every time you exit any cocoon state, release a burst of wing energy in a 6m radius dealing moderate damage; the burst is 50% stronger for each second you spent in the cocoon

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Mass Cocoon         | Heal   | Wrap all allies within 10m in protective cocoons simultaneously for 2s; all emerge healed for 20% HP and cleansed; the collective emergence creates a large burst of luminescent energy damaging all nearby enemies
  - Void Chrysalis      | Active   | Enter a 6s deep void cocoon that removes you entirely from reality for the duration; you cannot be targeted, tracked, or detected; you emerge with a 10s window of intangibility (you can still attack but cannot be hit) as your form stabilizes

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Final Metamorphosis | Transform | Enter the cocoon of final becoming for 6s; you emerge permanently transformed into your transcendent moth form for 45s — your wingspan doubles, you gain the ability to fly freely, all scale-powder abilities expand to twice their normal range, all cocoon abilities become available as free actions every 10s, your attacks deal radiant light damage that bypasses physical armor, and you radiate an aura of transformation that causes nearby allies to undergo minor beneficial metamorphoses (one random positive stat increase each) every 10s; when the 45s ends you molt back to base form but retain one randomly chosen transformation stat boost permanently for the rest of the encounter

ARCHETYPE C: Lamp Caller (Light Beacon, Lure, Attracted Summons)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Deploys sacred light beacons that attract both summoned cosmic moths and living enemies alike, using irresistible light as bait for devastating trap-and-summon combinations.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Place Lamp          | Active   | Set a glowing sacred lamp at a location; the lamp persists for 30s and emits warm light that enemies are naturally drawn toward (they will approach it if not actively engaged)
  - Call Moth           | Summon   | Call a single cosmic moth to your lamp's location; the moth orbits the lamp, dealing minor damage to enemies who come near and harassing their attacks
  - Light Affinity      | Passive  | Your sacred lamps emit a frequency that weakens enemies who linger near them; enemies within 6m of a lamp take 8% increased damage from all sources

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Lure Beacon         | Active   | Activate a lamp as a powerful lure; for 12s enemies in a 20m radius are compelled toward it (stronger pull than passive draw), breaking their formation
  - Moth Cluster        | Summon   | Summon 3 moths at once to a lamp's location; the cluster acts as a swarm that obscures vision in a 4m radius around the lamp and deals continuous minor damage to all inside
  - Lamp Chain          | Active   | Connect two of your active lamps; enemies caught between linked lamps take ongoing light damage and are slowed as the light beam between them acts as a snare

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Grand Moth          | Summon   | Summon a large cosmic moth (3x normal size) to a lamp; it actively attacks nearby enemies dealing moderate damage and can be directed to specific targets once per 8s
  - Sacrifice the Lamp  | Active   | Detonate a lamp in a blinding explosion; the explosion blinds all enemies within 8m for 3s and all moths at that lamp's location are recalled and empowered (+50% damage for 15s) by the lamp's released energy
  - Irresistible Light  | Passive  | Enemies below 50% HP who are near your lamps cannot resist the lure; they are compelled toward the nearest lamp on each of their turns unless they are actively engaged in melee

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Moth Legion         | Summon   | Summon 8 moths simultaneously across all active lamps, distributing them evenly; the legion acts as a coordinated swarm that can collectively focus on one target (stacking individual damage) or spread to harass multiple enemies
  - Trap Light          | Active   | Convert a lamp into a trap light; the lamp dims slightly but detonates when an enemy approaches within 4m, releasing a devastating light burst that deals heavy damage and blinds all within 8m for 4s

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Calling Light   | Summon   | Light all lamps with cosmic fire simultaneously and release the ultimate summons for 30s; a massive Celestial Moth (ten times normal size, capable of bearing a rider) descends from the stars to each active lamp location and attacks with devastating light-wing strikes and scale-powder blasts; all smaller moths on the battlefield are absorbed into the Celestial Moths, increasing their power by 10% per moth absorbed; living enemies with the Lure debuff are irresistibly drawn to the nearest Celestial Moth and cannot choose to attack anything else; the combined light of all lamps and Celestial Moths creates a blinding halo that inflicts 15% increased damage on all enemies for the duration

################################################################################
CLASS 102: Wavesmith (Vibration/Frequency Sci-fi)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A frequency engineer who treats the electromagnetic spectrum as a weapon system — dialing in resonant frequencies to crack enemy armor at the molecular level, generating counter-vibration fields that neutralize incoming attacks, and unleashing infrasound waves that hit the nervous system directly, inducing fear and organ disruption no armor can prevent.

ARCHETYPE A: Resonant Frequency (Weak Point Analysis, Structural Crit, Shatter)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Scans enemies for their resonant frequency, then tunes attacks to that frequency for devastating critical hits and structural collapse.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Frequency Scan      | Active   | Analyze one enemy for 1s; identify their resonant frequency and mark it; your next 3 attacks against that enemy deal 25% bonus damage as tuned resonant strikes
  - Hairline Fracture   | Reactive  | Each resonant strike you land builds a fracture charge on the target (max 5); at 5 charges, the next hit applies Shatter, dealing 30% of the target's current HP as a burst
  - Tuned Round         | Attack   | Fire a projectile or melee strike tuned to a target's current resonant frequency marker; deals 140% damage and applies one fracture charge regardless of normal conditions

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Frequency Lock      | Debuff   | Sustain the resonant analysis field on a target for 15s; all attacks against them during this window are automatically tuned, gaining 15% bonus damage without needing the Scan step
  - Resonant Cascade    | Reactive  | When Shatter triggers on a target, it releases a resonant shockwave in a 5m radius; enemies in range with matching armor materials take 20% of the Shatter damage and gain one fracture charge
  - Overtone Strike     | Attack   | Attack at a harmonic overtone of the enemy's frequency (double or half); deals 110% damage but applies two fracture charges simultaneously rather than one

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Mass Frequency Scan | Active   | Scan all enemies within 10m simultaneously in 2s; all receive frequency markers and are vulnerable to tuned attacks for 12s
  - Critical Resonance  | Passive  | Attacks that trigger Shatter always score a critical hit with 200% damage modifier on top of the Shatter burst; the combined hit can one-shot enemies below 40% HP
  - Frequency Shift     | Active   | Rapidly cycle your output frequency through a target's weak band for 3s; deal continuous vibration damage and apply a fracture charge each second, compressing 3s of pressure into what would normally take 6s of tuned attacks

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Catastrophic Resonance | Active | Lock onto a Shattered or nearly-Shattered target and amplify the resonant field to catastrophic levels; the target takes 50% of their max HP as vibration damage and is stunned for 3s as their structural integrity collapses
  - Frequency Fingerprint | Passive/Utility | Once you have Scanned an enemy type, you never need to scan that type again in any future encounter; frequency data is stored permanently, and the first attack against a stored type automatically applies 2 fracture charges

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Total Resonant Collapse | Active | Broadcast the precise resonant frequencies of all enemies on the battlefield simultaneously, tuned to their individual weak points — no scanning required; for 20s every attack you make against any enemy automatically applies two fracture charges, all Shatters trigger at double power, and the resonant field is so intense that enemies within 15m of a Shattering target take full Shatter damage as well (not just 20%); at any point during this window you may trigger a Grand Collapse, detonating all accrued fracture charges on every enemy at once as a battlefield-wide Shatter event

>> Your notes on Resonant Frequency:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Harmonic Shield (Counter-Vibration, Nullification, Absorption)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Generates counter-vibration fields tuned to incoming attacks, nullifying or absorbing them while converting captured wave energy into offensive power.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Counter-Freq Field  | Toggle   | Toggle a personal counter-vibration field; incoming physical attacks are partially dampened, reducing physical damage taken by 12%
  - Null Wall           | Active   | Erect a 4m wide counter-vibration wall for 6s; all physical projectiles that contact it are nullified (deal 0 damage) and absorbed
  - Wave Capacitor      | Passive/Utility  | Absorbed attacks charge a wave capacitor (max 100%); each absorbed hit adds 10% charge; at 100% you may release stored energy as a free action

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Adaptive Counter    | Passive/Defense  | Your counter-vibration field auto-tunes to each incoming attack type within 0.3s; the second hit of any damage type is nullified at 30% efficiency, the third at 50%, and the fourth onward at 70%
  - Release Burst       | Active   | Release all stored wave capacitor charge as a directed burst at a target; full charge deals heavy damage scaled by the total damage absorbed since last release
  - Null Dome           | Active/Defense   | Project a 6m radius null dome for 5s around yourself; all ranged attacks that enter the dome are nullified; melee attacks are reduced by 25%

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Frequency Inversion | Reactive | When struck by a particularly powerful attack, invert its frequency and reflect it back at the attacker for 80% of the original attack's damage; 15s cooldown
  - Resonant Armor      | Passive/Immunity  | Your armor's vibration dampening has been tuned to be completely opaque to sound-based damage; you are immune to sonic, concussive, and infrasound damage types; this immunity extends 2m around you to protect nearby allies
  - Capacitor Overdrive | Active   | Push your wave capacitor past safe limits; double its absorption efficiency for 10s but it vents the excess as a heat field that deals minor damage to enemies within 4m

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Mirror Field        | Active   | Erect a perfect harmonic mirror field for 8s; instead of absorbing attacks, the field returns them at 100% potency to their source with perfect accuracy — ranged enemies effectively shoot themselves
  - Null Zone           | Active   | Designate a 15m radius null zone for 20s; all damaging frequency-based attacks (sonic, vibration, resonant, infrasound) are nullified within the zone for all parties — this includes enemy waveforms but also forces you to rely on physical attacks within the zone

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Grand Harmonic Aegis | Active/Defense  | Erect a perfect harmonic shield system over a 20m radius for 30s; all incoming attacks of any type are met with precisely calculated counter-vibrations — physical attacks are dampened by 50%, energy attacks are fully nullified and absorbed, sonic/resonant attacks are perfectly reflected at 100% power; the capacitor fills continuously at maximum rate and auto-releases every 8s as targeted bursts at the highest-threat enemies; you and all allies within the aegis are completely immune to disorientation, stagger, and knockback effects as the counter-field stabilizes all forces acting upon you; the aegis can be concentrated to a 5m radius for 60% damage reflection if maximum offense is required

>> Your notes on Harmonic Shield:
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Infrasound (Subsonic Disruption, Biological Fear, Organ Disruption)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Deploys frequencies below the threshold of hearing that act directly on the nervous system and organs, inducing terror, nausea, and biological damage that cannot be armored against.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Low Frequency Pulse | Debuff   | Emit a subsonic pulse in a 6m cone; enemies feel intense unease and suffer 15% reduced combat effectiveness for 8s as their concentration fractures
  - Nausea Wave         | Debuff   | Broadcast a nausea-inducing frequency at a single target; they suffer -20% accuracy and must pass a check each time they use an ability or waste it on a recoil reaction
  - Subsonic Aura       | Aura     | Maintain a low infrasound field in a 5m radius; enemies within feel growing unease and after 5s of continuous exposure suffer a moderate fear effect

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Fear Induction      | Debuff   | Broadcast a targeted fear frequency at an enemy; they are gripped by irrational terror for 6s and will not voluntarily approach you during that window
  - Organ Resonance     | Debuff   | Tune your infrasound to a target's organ resonant frequency; they suffer ongoing biological damage (ignores armor) for 10s and cannot regenerate HP for the duration
  - Mass Unease         | Debuff   | Broadcast a wide-band infrasound over a 15m radius for 3s; every enemy in range suffers -15% to all stats for 10s as the psychological pressure mounts

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Panic Cascade       | CC   | Trigger a rapid-escalating fear cascade in a 10m radius; enemies must overcome increasing fear checks each round or spend their action fleeing; the cascade escalates for 3 rounds before plateauing at maximum panic
  - Internal Disruption | Debuff   | Target an enemy's specific vulnerable biology; choose cardiovascular (30% damage increase on all hits as blood pressure spikes), vestibular (100% disorientation, they cannot aim), or neurological (40% chance to misfire abilities for 12s)
  - Sustained Broadcast | Channel  | Channel a continuous infrasound broadcast for up to 6s; each second of broadcast applies increasing biological damage to all enemies in a 12m radius — 1s deals minor, 6s deals catastrophic; you are stationary while broadcasting

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Terror Frequency    | Debuff   | Broadcast a precisely calculated terror frequency at a target; they experience absolute fight-or-flight panic for 10s — they may attack with 40% extra damage from adrenaline or flee at 50% extra speed, but cannot make calm tactical decisions during the window
  - Bio-Disruption Field | Debuff  | Flood a 15m radius with a full-spectrum infrasound burst lasting 15s; all enemies suffer nausea (25% accuracy loss), organ disruption (ongoing armor-penetrating damage), and vestibular damage (movement penalties) simultaneously

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Brown Note      | Active   | Emit a legendary infrasound at the resonant frequency of biological consciousness itself — a 3s buildup followed by a catastrophic wave in a 25m radius; every enemy is simultaneously subjected to maximum fear (frozen for 3s in complete paralysis), maximum nausea (unable to use abilities for 5s after paralysis ends), and maximum organ disruption (taking 20% of max HP as armor-ignoring damage per second for 8s); mechanical enemies, constructs, and the undead are instead subjected to a structural resonance that deals 25% max HP per second for the same 8s; this frequency is so fundamental that no resistance or immunity applies — it works on everything that has a physical form, including bosses

################################################################################
CLASS 103: Gardener of Flesh (Body Horror)
################################################################################
Fantasy: A grotesque naturalist who treats the body as soil — growing bone-bladed weapons directly from their flesh in seconds, grafting harvested enemy parts onto themselves as living upgrades, and decomposing fallen enemies into rich biological fuel that accelerates the next terrible bloom.

ARCHETYPE A: Bloom (Organic Weapon Growth, Body Weapon Arsenal, Living Armament)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Grows weapons, limbs, and organic implements directly from their own body mid-combat, customizing their physical arsenal in real time.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Bone Spur           | Attack   | Rapidly grow a bone spur from your fist or arm; strike an enemy for moderate piercing damage, then retract it — can be grown and used in the same action
  - Thorn Skin          | Reactive  | Grow a layer of organic thorns across your body; any enemy who strikes you in melee takes minor piercing damage automatically
  - Quick Bloom         | Passive/Utility  | Your body has been trained to grow weapons rapidly; all Bloom ability activation times are reduced by 30% and the weapons grown are 10% larger and more damaging

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Tendril Whip        | Attack   | Extend a fast-growing prehensile tendril from your arm up to 8m; strike for moderate damage, pull the target 4m toward you, then retract or leave it extended for 3s as a grappling limb
  - Barbed Carapace     | Buff/Defense     | Grow a hardened organic carapace of interlocking bone and chitin over your torso for 15s; reduce damage taken by 25% and thorns damage triples while it is active
  - Fang Array          | Attack   | Open a bloom of multiple fangs from your hand and bite a target; the array delivers 4 rapid fang strikes dealing light damage each but applying a stacking venom with each hit

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Scythe Growth       | Attack   | Grow a pair of elongated scythe-arms over 1s; for 15s your melee attacks deal slashing damage in a 3m arc and your reach extends to 2.5m; retract on command or after duration
  - Bloom Burst         | Active   | Grow dozens of small organic projectiles across your body simultaneously and fire them all outward in a 360-degree burst; deals light damage to all enemies within 8m but each projectile carries a growth spore that causes brief irritating blooms on targets (slow/accuracy penalty)
  - Living Weapon       | Transform | Spend 2s growing a major organic weapon (choose: bone greatclub for massive damage and knockback, acid gland for corrosive ranged attacks, or wing-membrane for a 20s flight capability); the growth lasts 25s and is fully functional

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Full Body Arsenal   | Active   | Trigger a rapid full-body bloom; simultaneously grow: tendrils on both arms (grapple capability), bone spurs on both legs (stomping attacks deal double damage), thorn skin at maximum density (2x thorn damage), and a jaw extension (bite attack available); all last 20s and function concurrently
  - Adaptive Bloom      | Reactive/Defense | When you take a specific type of damage (fire, cold, acid, etc.), immediately grow a resistance organ for that damage type; reduce that damage type by 30% for the rest of the encounter; can trigger once per damage type

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Apex Bloom          | Transform | Undergo a total body bloom for 30s, growing into your ultimate organic weapons configuration; every body part becomes a weapon — your arms become scythes, your back sprouts a bone bow that fires automatically each round, your legs gain spring-tendons for double jump and kick damage, your spine grows a whip-tail, and your head extrudes a stunning roar organ that releases a 6m cone of biological disruption every 5s as a free action; additionally your thorn skin is at maximum density and every melee attacker is automatically pierced; when the bloom ends you shed all the grown tissue in a 10m explosion of organic shrapnel dealing moderate damage to all nearby enemies

>> Your notes on Bloom:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Graft (Enemy Part Harvesting, Living Upgrades, Chimeric Assembly)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Harvests parts from defeated or living enemies and grafts them onto their own body as functional living upgrades, building a chimeric arsenal from their enemies' best features.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Harvest             | Active   | After defeating an enemy, harvest a useful biological part in 1s; store it as a graft option (max 3 stored grafts at any time)
  - Minor Graft         | Active   | Apply a stored minor graft to yourself; small grafts provide a 10-15% boost to one stat or a minor new passive ability matching the source creature's nature
  - Graft Integration   | Passive/Utility  | Your body integrates grafts rapidly; graft application takes only 1s and multiple minor grafts on the same body location reinforce each other rather than conflicting

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Limb Snatch         | Active   | Tear a limb or significant organ from a living enemy (must be below 40% HP or stunned); immediately graft it for a significant ability gain matching the harvested part's function for 20s
  - Chimeric Build      | Passive  | You can carry up to 5 active grafts simultaneously without conflict; grafts from different creature types have a 20% chance to synergize, granting a bonus effect where the two donor abilities naturally complement each other
  - Graft Siphon        | Passive  | Grafts from living sources slowly drain life from the donor; any enemy whose part you carry loses 2% HP per 10s for as long as they live and the graft remains active

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Major Graft         | Active   | Apply a major harvested organ for a significant ability: an enemy's eyes grant extended darkvision and 30% accuracy bonus, a stomach organ grants regeneration (2% HP/5s), a wing membrane grants gliding, or a venom sac grants a poison bite with the harvested creature's specific toxin
  - Overwrite Graft     | Active   | Replace an existing graft with a superior one from the same body location; the new graft is 25% more effective and the discarded graft explodes as a burst of biological matter dealing moderate damage to nearby enemies
  - Shared Trauma       | Passive/Heal  | When any enemy with your graft (living donor) takes damage from any source, 10% of that damage is reflected to you but converted to healing; you feel their pain but benefit from it

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Complete Chimera    | Transform | Enter a 3s rapid grafting frenzy using all stored grafts simultaneously; apply all 5 graft slots at once, each to the optimal body location; gain all the combined abilities for 30s in a powerful chimeric configuration
  - Living Steal        | Active   | Target an elite or boss enemy; reach into their body and rip out a defining organ or core component while they are alive; they are weakened (lose 25% of their HP and one signature ability for 15s) and you immediately graft their power, gaining their ability at 60% effectiveness for 20s

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Perfect Chimera     | Transform | Graft the finest parts of every enemy type you have encountered in this encounter simultaneously, building the ultimate chimeric form for 35s; you retain: the movement type of the fastest enemy encountered, the regeneration of the most durable, the attack power of the strongest, the elemental affinity of the most dangerous caster, and the senses of the most perceptive — all at 80% of the original's power; additionally your body is now so well-adapted that all incoming damage is reduced by 25% as grafted redundant systems compensate for wounds; when the transformation ends, all grafted parts detach explosively dealing moderate damage to all enemies within 8m

>> Your notes on Graft:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Compost (Decomposition, Biological Fuel, Growth from Death)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Decomposes fallen enemies and organic matter into nutrient-rich biological fuel that accelerates their own growth, healing, and Bloom ability potency.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Decompose           | Active   | Begin decomposing a fallen enemy or organic material over 3s; the decomposition yields 1-3 Nutrient tokens depending on enemy size
  - Nutrient Absorption | Active   | Consume all current Nutrient tokens; restore 8% HP per token and temporarily boost your next Bloom ability's potency by 10% per token consumed
  - Rapid Rot           | Passive  | Your decomposition is accelerated; the Decompose ability completes in 1.5s instead of 3s and small enemies decompose instantly on touch after you kill them

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Compost Spore       | Attack   | Fling a handful of decomposing matter at an enemy; the spores begin breaking them down from the outside dealing moderate damage over 8s and reducing their armor by 15%
  - Bloom Fuel          | Passive  | Each Nutrient token you hold increases your Bloom ability damage by 5% (stacks up to 10 tokens); spending tokens on healing resets the bonus but each heal is immediately reinvested in the next Bloom
  - Worm Farm           | Summon   | Seed a decomposing body with your worms; over 10s the body spawns 4 decomposer worm minions that attack nearby enemies and decompose corpses on contact, generating Nutrient tokens automatically

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Midden Pit          | Active   | Create a 6m radius decomposition zone at a target location for 15s; enemies inside take ongoing acid/rot damage, and enemies who die inside are instantly decomposed for double the normal Nutrient tokens
  - Nutrient Overdose   | Active   | Consume all Nutrient tokens recklessly in one surge; for each token consumed gain 2% bonus to all stats for 15s (stacks with no cap) but suffer 3% of max HP as nutrient toxicity damage; high risk, high reward
  - Mycorrhizal Network | Passive  | Connect all decomposing bodies on the battlefield into an underground network; nutrient tokens flow between network nodes automatically, and any Bloom ability you activate near a network node receives +20% potency from the underground supply

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Mass Decomposition  | Active   | Release a 12m radius spore cloud that begins decomposing all organic matter in range; living enemies take moderate ongoing damage, and any enemy that dies in the zone is immediately converted to maximum Nutrient tokens
  - Fertilized Growth   | Active   | Spend 10 Nutrient tokens to trigger an instant Apex partial Bloom — grow a single major weapon or organ instantly without the normal growth time and at 130% normal potency as the nutrients supercharge the growth process

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Garden          | Active   | Seed the entire battlefield (up to 30m radius) with decomposer organisms for 30s; the battlefield becomes a living garden with enemies being the soil — all enemies are continuously decomposed for ongoing damage equal to 3% max HP per second (ignores armor), every enemy death immediately yields maximum Nutrient tokens and spawns 2 worm minions from the corpse, the Midden Pit effect covers the entire garden area, and your Nutrient token cap is removed entirely; at peak nutrient saturation (30+ tokens) your next Bloom ability automatically becomes an Apex Bloom at 200% potency as the garden pours everything it has into your final growth; the garden leaves behind permanently decomposed terrain patches that halve enemy movement speed in the area for the rest of the encounter

>> Your notes on Compost:
>>
>>
