################################################################################
CLASS 104: Constellation Mage (Star Pattern)
################################################################################
Fantasy: An astronomer who has learned that the stars are not merely lights but a diagram of power waiting to be traced — drawing live constellations across the battlefield to define zones of effect, locking pairs of enemies and allies into binary orbital relationships, and compressing star-energy into critical mass until the release is nothing short of a supernova.

ARCHETYPE A: Star Map (Constellation Drawing, Pattern Effects, Celestial Zones)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Draws constellations across the battlefield by connecting star-points, with the completed pattern determining a powerful ongoing area effect that matches the constellation's mythological meaning.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Place Star Point    | Active   | Mark a point in the world as a constellation anchor; each point glows visibly and persists for 30s; you can hold up to 6 star points simultaneously
  - Connect Stars       | Active   | Draw a line between two existing star points; the line activates a minor effect based on the angle and number of connected stars (more connections = stronger effect)
  - Stellar Cartography | Passive  | You have a perfect memory of all known constellations and can recognize partial patterns; when you form 2+ connections that match a known constellation, you receive a hint of its full effect before completion

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - The Hunter          | Active   | Complete the Hunter constellation (minimum 5 star points in the correct pattern) to activate a 20s tracking field; all enemies in the pattern area are marked and cannot become invisible; your attacks against marked enemies deal 15% extra damage
  - The Serpent         | Active   | Complete the Serpent constellation to create a 15s poison zone where the constellation's body winds; enemies moving through the serpent's path take ongoing poison damage equal to how many "body segments" of the pattern they cross
  - Quick Sketch        | Passive  | Reduce star point placement time by 40%; you can place a star point as a bonus portion of any action, freeing your full action for attacks or other abilities

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - The Shield          | Active   | Complete the Shield constellation over an area; for 20s, allies within the constellation boundary take 25% reduced damage and cannot be displaced from the shielded area by knockback effects
  - The Storm           | Active   | Complete the Storm constellation (irregular zigzag pattern) over an enemy cluster; call down star-lightning at each connection point once every 3s for 20s, dealing heavy damage to enemies near those points
  - Layered Map         | Passive  | Two different constellations can be active simultaneously without interfering; both effects operate in their respective drawn areas, and if they overlap, the overlapping region gains 50% of each constellation's effect on top of the primary

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - The Dragon          | Active   | Complete the Dragon constellation (the longest pattern, requiring 7 points); for 25s a celestial dragon traces the constellation outline as an active creature, attacking any enemy that crosses into the constellation's boundary for heavy damage and applying fire damage-over-time
  - Constellation Nova  | Active   | Detonate all currently active star points simultaneously; each point releases a moderate burst of stellar energy, and all connection-lines detonate as beams of stellar fire dealing heavy damage along their length — more connections means more beams

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Living Sky      | Active   | Project the entire night sky as a 35m radius star map that hovers above the battlefield for 30s; you may place star points anywhere in the area instantly as a free action each round and complete any constellation in 2 connections rather than the full pattern; all active constellations operate at double potency and their effects pulse every 2s rather than being static; any constellation you complete during The Living Sky also creates a permanent ghostly copy of itself that continues to function at 50% potency for the rest of the encounter after The Living Sky ends; the sky itself deals minor ongoing stellar damage to all enemies in the radius as the concentrated starlight is simply too intense to stand under unprotected

>> Your notes on Star Map:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE B: Binary Star (Paired Orbital System, Linked Buff/Debuff, Gravitational Pull)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Establishes binary relationships between pairs of allies or enemies, linking them in orbital dynamics so that what happens to one influences the other — powering allies at the cost of weakening linked enemies.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Binary Link         | Active   | Link two targets (ally-ally, enemy-enemy, or ally-enemy) within 15m into a binary pair; linked targets are aware of each other's position and share 15% of stat changes
  - Orbital Boost       | Buff     | Designate two linked allies as a binary pair; for 20s they orbit each other's position in movement, and both gain +15% damage when attacking the same target simultaneously
  - Stellar Empathy     | Passive/Heal  | Your binary links convey 20% of healing received by one linked target to the other; allies linked together heal together, enemies linked together share poison and debuff durations

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Mass-Energy Link    | Active   | Link an ally and an enemy as a binary pair; for 15s, 25% of the damage you deal to the enemy is simultaneously restored as healing to the ally as starlight energy transfers between the pair
  - Gravitational Tide  | Active   | Intensify the gravitational bond of a binary pair; pull both linked targets 3m toward each other simultaneously, regardless of their intentions, then release them — if they collide, deal collision damage
  - Orbital Momentum    | Passive  | Linked binary pairs that move generate orbital momentum; each round one member of the pair moves, the other gains a 10% damage bonus on their next action as angular momentum transfers through the link

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Tidal Lock          | CC       | Lock a binary enemy pair into tidal synchronization for 8s; they cannot move independently — if one moves, both must move in the same direction and distance, making tactical positioning impossible and often beneficial to you
  - Ecliptic Transfer   | Active   | Trigger a mass-energy transfer through an ally-enemy binary link; transfer 30% of the enemy's current HP directly to the ally as healing, and transfer 30% of the ally's damage bonus directly to debuff potency on the enemy
  - Roche Limit         | Passive  | Binary pairs that are pulled too close together (within 3m) hit the Roche Limit and partially destabilize; both members take burst gravitational stress damage and are staggered for 1.5s as the orbital mechanics become violent

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Triple System       | Active   | Extend your binary into a triple-star system, linking three targets; the three-body system is chaotic by nature — each target's actions randomly influence one of the other two, making enemy triple-links highly disruptive and ally triple-links explosively synergistic (each action has a 40% chance to trigger a bonus action from one of the other linked members)
  - Stellar Collapse    | Active   | Destabilize a binary link violently; the two linked targets are slammed together at extreme speed, dealing massive collision damage to both equal to 30% of each target's max HP, then the link shatters, releasing a burst of stellar energy dealing moderate AoE damage to all enemies within 8m of the collision point

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - The Grand Orrery   | Active   | For 30s, link every entity on the battlefield (ally and enemy alike) into a single grand orbital system; all stat increases to any entity flow at 20% efficiency to all allied entities simultaneously; all debuffs applied to any enemy flow at 20% efficiency to all enemies simultaneously; every action taken by any binary-linked pair generates orbital momentum that grants a bonus free-action to another linked pair of your choice; the orrery is a living machine of cause and effect — the more active the battlefield, the more the system amplifies everything, and at the end of the 30s the orrery collapses in a grand gravitational implosion that deals damage to all enemies equal to the total damage dealt during the orrery's duration at 30% efficiency

>> Your notes on Binary Star:
>>
>>

ARCHETYPE C: Supernova (Star Energy Build, Critical Mass, Explosive Release)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Identity: Methodically builds star energy toward critical mass through sustained combat, then releases it in a cataclysmic supernova explosion whose power scales directly with the energy accumulated.

Tier 1 (Common)
  - Stellar Accumulation| Passive  | Each attack you land generates 2 stellar energy; each ability you use generates 5 stellar energy; stellar energy builds toward critical mass (max 100, displayed as a star-brightness meter)
  - Solar Flare         | Attack   | Release a small burst of built stellar energy as a targeted flare; deals damage equal to 1% of your current stellar energy per hit and costs 10 stellar energy; useful for controlled bleeding of the meter
  - Compress            | Active   | Actively compress your stellar energy for 2s without attacking; gain 15 stellar energy during the compression and your next attack deals bonus damage equal to 5% of your stellar energy meter

Tier 2 (Uncommon)
  - Stellar Density     | Passive  | Your stellar energy meter gains 25% more energy per source; attacks generate 2.5 and abilities generate 6.25 (effectively, energy builds 25% faster across the board)
  - Protostar Burst     | Attack   | Release a moderate stellar energy burst at 20 stellar energy cost; deals damage equal to 3% of your current stellar energy and applies a burning stellar fire effect for 6s
  - Event Horizon       | Aura     | Spend 15 stellar energy to activate a brief 4s aura; enemies within 8m are dragged 1m per second toward you as your compressed stellar mass creates gravitational pull — a taste of the black hole you are becoming

Tier 3 (Rare)
  - Stellar Wind        | Active   | Vent stellar energy outward as a solar wind in a 10m cone; deals damage equal to 2% of current stellar energy per enemy hit, cost equal to 15% of current stellar energy; efficient area pressure with partial energy preservation
  - Red Giant Phase     | Transform | At 60+ stellar energy, enter the Red Giant phase for 10s; your size and reach increase by 30%, all your abilities deal 25% extra damage as you expand outward, and stellar energy generation doubles during this phase
  - Critical Mass       | Passive  | At 80+ stellar energy, you enter Critical Mass; your spells deal 20% extra damage, you emit a damaging stellar field in a 6m radius, and the supernova threshold decreases to 90 energy (instead of 100), making the release slightly easier to control

Tier 4 (Epic)
  - Controlled Detonation | Active | At 70+ stellar energy, release a controlled supernova in a 15m radius dealing damage equal to 70% of your current stellar energy as a base damage value; energy meter resets to 20 after use — sacrifices power for repeatability
  - Magnetar Pulse      | Active   | At 50+ stellar energy, release a powerful magnetar-level pulse in a 12m radius; knocks all enemies back 8m, stuns them for 2s, and costs 50 stellar energy; good for creating breathing room while building toward full supernova

Tier 5 (Legendary)
  - Supernova           | Active   | At 100 stellar energy, trigger the true supernova; a 3s buildup pulse (during which you are invulnerable) precedes the detonation — the supernova releases in a 30m radius dealing damage equal to 300% of your current stellar energy as base damage (effectively 300 × 3% = 900% weapon damage equivalent), knocking all enemies to the edge of the blast radius, applying a stellar burn that deals 10% of their max HP per second for 8s, and creating a gravity well at the center that pulls them back in during the burn; after the supernova you retain 25 stellar energy rather than resetting to 0 (the remnant core begins accumulating again immediately); the supernova's damage is halved against the very edge of the radius and doubled at the epicenter, rewarding precise positioning to catch enemies at close range

>> Your notes on Supernova:
>>
>>
